With 10 independent 75% rolls ~2.5 factions defect every time, so the flat per-faction roll does **NOT** reliably hold; `ted1772`'s near-misses survived only via the 2/3 Articles threshold (RevWar floor #2), which the **plurality-override path bypasses entirely**. **The fix (re-scoped ‚Äî the flat 75% roll is no longer recommended):** EITHER **(a) a HARD VETO** (in a solo/CPU-majority game, `triggersGameEnd`/surrender responses are removed from the CPU vote menu / unselectable) OR **(b) a points-based anti-peace ideology bias** tuned so a CPU plurality can't form ‚Äî **PLUS, either way, a non-plurality-overridable game-ending-peace guard** (the Pres's reject must not be beaten by a mere plurality; a separate guard on the override path). **S‚ÄìM** (the veto/bias is S; the override-path plurality guard adds the M part). **This is a SOLO-PLAY BLOCKER (the DH-29-of-the-CPU/war-track) and ONE OF THE THREE RevWar floors (debt #34a below ‚Äî floor #3, now known LEAKY) ‚Äî build it WITH the #155 war-balance pass (E3) AHEAD of the rest of the CPU/war track** + the #75 CPU event-vote handler (E9). Bears on #114 (solo-app). game-mechanics ¬ß13.2 (the STRENGTHENED #158 block) + ¬ß21.1 + ¬ß25.7. **‚òÖ Batch-19 provenance: `fixes2022` is the EARLIEST source for the CPU anti-game-over rule (#88/OC-3/#158)** ‚Äî the Carlisle Peace Treaty episode + vcczar's *"if a decision will result in the game automatically ending‚Ä¶ the CPU will vote nay 75% of the time"* (`POST 622, 663`), predating `ted1772` #158 by ~a year ‚Äî designer intent from the start. game-mechanics ¬ß30.10. |

| 55 | **‚òÖ #175 `Legislation` has NO `repealable` flag and NO law-class tag ‚Äî a small data-model add (batch 23 / `pop2012b`, MrPotatoTed designer-authoritative)** | the `Legislation` interface (`types.ts:1506-1520`) has `id/year/title/description/sponsor*/committee/status/effects/votes` and **NO `repealable` and NO `lawClass`**; the floor runner `runPhase_2_6_3_Floor` (`phaseRunners.ts:3498`) resolves only `passed_committee` bills with no repeal/replace branch; **grep `repealable\|lawClass` in `src/` = ZERO** (the existing `Challenge-Legislation-can't-target-REPEAL` filter #132 is a separate guard) | MrPotatoTed (game co-author) ruled (`pop2012b#POST 687-688): *"Every law is marked with whether or not it can be repealed"* ‚Äî a per-row flag ("Column P", LegisActive sheet) with **three classes**: **repealable** (a Repeal bill removes it through the normal pipeline ‚Äî most policy bills); **replace-only** (non-repealable but **replaceable** ‚Äî you cannot remove it, only supersede it with a new same-kind bill; **tax + immigration** laws); **permanent** (irreversible ‚Äî **statehood**, *"once a state is a state, it's a state"*; no state can be abolished). This is the authoritative resolution of the `pop` ¬ß5.5 data-tag hole and explains why some passed policies are "downgrade-only" and why no Repeal-Statehood bill exists. **The fix: add `Legislation.repealable: boolean` + a `lawClass: 'repealable' \| 'replace-only' \| 'permanent'` tag; gate Repeal proposals on the flag; for replace-only laws expose a Replace action that supersedes (not removes) the prior law; mark statehood (and other one-way structural bills) `permanent` so no repeal/replace is offered.** **Pairs with #42 (the bill-relationship graph, ¬ß12.9 ‚Äî `Not repealable`/replace-by-X/amendment-tier) + ¬ß27.5 (statehood-by-bill: stamp every admit-state bill `permanent`).** **S ‚Äî a small data-model + pipeline-guard add inside the legislation/bill-relationship epic; needs an authored repealable/replace-only/permanent per-bill list (open Q, `game-context.md` #175).** game-mechanics ¬ß12.9. |

| 68 | **‚òÖ‚òÖ #186 a late/mid-cycle scenario boot must be DETERMINISTIC ‚Äî the 1960 boot (a FIFTH BootSheet shape) was entirely improvised; consolidates the boot cluster from the modern era (batch 27 / `redbutton`, the single most build-relevant cluster in the source)** | NO generic boot abstraction (re-verified batch 27): `GameContext.tsx:5-6` imports `build1772Scenario`/`build1856Scenario`; `startNewGame(factionId, scenarioId?: '1772'\|'1856')` (`:24`) dispatches to hand-authored data files; **no BootSheet, no scenarioBoot, no year-keyed incumbent/office table** (`grep scenarioBoot\|BootSheet\|bootScenario in src/` = ZERO); #115 (debt #46-area) captures the 1820 boot-procedure gap, #86 the boot-data shape | The 1960 MP is the **FIRST modern-era boot in the corpus** and the cleanest late-start failure. The 1960 scenario was authored for the **1960-62 GOVERNANCE turn (JFK president, offices for 1961)** but players forced an **Elections-Phase start one turn early** ‚Üí cascading manual patches: offices/incumbents wrong (Senate seeded 50-50 vs real 66-D Jan-1960); no Blue Party Leader (game requires the Leadership trait ‚Üí GM hand-picked LBJ); the **party-leader on-election rolls** (+Command/lose-Obscure/+3-primaries/3-term party-pref penalty) skipped; **party-fatigue (#187) never applied**; governor terms-served unknowable (blanket "2 two-year terms / ex-govs 8 yrs"); **delegate weights + primary calendar hand-assigned** (#185); DC EVs invalid until 1961; **mid-cycle = NO Command-earning runway** ‚Üí roster frozen out of high office, players asked to back-date the last 2-4 draft classes into career tracks (the DH-25 fix from a modern angle) (game-mechanics ¬ß28.1, ¬ß28.6; `redbutton#POST 64, 73, 84-87, 522, 528, 543-560, 645, 931-938`). **‚òÖ Designer verdict:** eras are *"the stoniest"* thing in the game, can't be retrofitted at a button-press ‚Üí a clean per-year boot TABLE is the only path (`#POST 528`). **The fix (the deterministic late/mid-era boot pipeline, as a 5th `BootSheet` shape):** for any chosen start year ‚Äî (a) year-correct incumbents/offices/chamber balances (pick the ¬ß26.1 #164 election-start vs president-in-place fork EXPLICITLY per scenario, with a verifier); (b) auto-elect a Leadership-gated Party Leader AND apply the on-election rolls (not skip them); (c) **retro-apply party-fatigue (#187)** to whichever party is dominant at the start year; (d) seed delegate weights (Cat 1-5) + the primary calendar (#185) so the first convention is reproducible; (e) back-date recent draft classes into career tracks (DH-25/#163, ACUTE at a mid-cycle start); (f) handle year-keyed EV validity (DC 1961); **(g) ‚òÖ‚òÖ BATCH-35 NEW REQUIREMENT ‚Äî a deterministic per-office, per-era CABINET/ADVISOR STAT-SEED table.** The `redbutton1960` RESTART is the cleanest LIVE PROOF that boot-stats are LOAD-BEARING, not cosmetic: the original 1960 run was STOPPED because the cabinet was seeded with STARTER-LEVEL stats ("all at starter levels and it tanked all the meters since we didn't have any Cabinet members above 3", `redbutton1960#POST 1`) ‚Äî the low-Admin officers drove the #179 lingering meter-roll (debt-area, the admin-ladder‚Üímeter coupling, re-confirmed at WHOLE-CABINET scale) into crisis at turn 1, and the era was UNPLAYABLE until the GM hand-RE-STATTED the entire cabinet to mid/peak (one 5-Admin, four 4s, rest 3s; ambassadors a 5 [Kissinger] + four 4s; a Key Advisor 2‚Üí4). **VERIFIED the gap:** scenario boots pull cabinet members from whoever already holds the office in the hand-authored seed roster, or leave the seat `null` (`scenario1856.ts:127-142`) ‚Äî there is **NO per-office/per-era stat-seed table** guaranteeing era-appropriate Admin/competence for the sitting government. So a deterministic late/mid-era boot MUST seed cabinet/ambassador/advisor officers at era-appropriate mid/peak strength (not draftee starter levels), or the #179 engine drags every meter into crisis on turn 1. The per-era cabinet-strength curve is still an OPEN Q (the mid/peak reset was eyeballed by the GM). **Where it binds:** the `scenarioBoot(BootSheet)` pipeline + `BootSheet` schema (K4, ¬ß9.1.9) ‚Äî extend with a `startMode: 'electionPhase'\|'governance'` discriminant + an `asOfYear` incumbent table + `seededLeaderTenure` (for #187) + `delegateWeights`/`primaryCalendar` fields + `careerTrackBackfill` + **a per-office/per-era `cabinetStatSeed` table (the batch-35 requirement)**. **M-L; RIDES K4's `BootSheet` + the `scenarioBoot` pipeline (#115); the modern boot is the most-demanding instantiation, so it SHARPENS the K4/scenarioBoot spec rather than adding a keystone.** **Open Q (designer): the per-year incumbent/office tables AND the per-era cabinet-stat curve must be AUTHORED (research-bound, like all era content).** game-mechanics ¬ß28.1, ¬ß28.6; `redbutton1960#POST 1, 2`. |

| 87 | **‚òÖ #218 Rule-of-Four / Chief-Justice case-refusal (Gov-Action state cases) + #219 US-Rep PROPORTIONAL representation (each influential US Rep carries a SHARE of the state's real seat count) ‚Äî both ABSENT (batch 30, `summer2021`, DESIGNED)** | **Verified ABSENT:** `grep -i 'filibuster\|cloture\|nuclear.?option\|Rule of Four\|ruleOfFour\|caseRefusal\|refuseCase' src/` = **ZERO** (same scan that confirms DH-76); the SCOTUS docket has no case-refusal / Rule-of-Four / CJ-discretion step. For #219, US Reps are a discrete id list ‚Äî `State.representativeIds: string[]` (`types.ts:1327`) ‚Äî with NO proportional share of the state's apportioned seat count (no `votingPower`/`repShare` derivation; corroborates #34/#62/#74). | **(#218):** a **Chief Justice (‚â•4 Judicial)** may **refuse a state case** brought via Gov Actions, but the **Rule of Four can override** the refusal; an **Iron-Fist** CJ ‚Üí Rule of Four doesn't apply (refusal stands); **Jurisprudence** ‚Üí may refuse unlimited cases; **Passive** CJ ‚Üí can't refuse at all (`summer2021` POST 236-239). Add a CJ case-refusal step on the Gov-Action‚Üícourt-case path; **#218 and #217 must share the SAME trait reads** (Passive/Jurisprudence/Iron-Fist are gated identically). **S; pairs with #20/#52/#217.** **(#219 ‚Äî ‚òÖ‚òÖ BATCH-33 RESOLVED, ORIGIN FOUND, scope SHARPENED (`housepoll` aed35c6e, the design poll behind it):** the community VOTED to **KEEP the focus-Rep abstraction; modeling every real ~435 seat was REJECTED** (perf/tedium/authoring; >10k historical Reps would need a pol-generator) ‚Äî explicitly parked as **"AMPU 2."** vcczar then ADOPTED + BUILT an expanded apportionment ladder (the "3.0.16 Determining US Reps" rule). The CANONICAL build (NOT "every seat"): **(A)** US Reps a state has = **historical EVs ‚àí 2** that year, recomputed at a **census election = first election after a decade** (decade year skips, N+2 midterm applies; ahistorical drift carries forward; early-join +3EV/+1Rep, late-join erase EVs). **(B)** US Reps ‚Üí Focus Reps via an **EV-derived 1-10 ladder** (‚â§5‚Üí1 ¬∑ 6-10‚Üí2 ¬∑ 11-15‚Üí3 ¬∑ 16-20‚Üí4 ¬∑ 21-25‚Üí5 ¬∑ 26-30‚Üí6 ¬∑ 31-35‚Üí7 ¬∑ 36-40‚Üí8 ¬∑ 41-45‚Üí9 ¬∑ ‚â•46‚Üí10) ‚Äî **SUPERSEDES the flat `(EV‚àí2)/5` focus-Rep count of #55**. **(C)** vote power = `floor(stateUSReps / focusReps)`, odd **REMAINDER ‚Üí the highest-Legislative rep gets the extra vote(s)** (randomized if tied) ‚Äî EXACTLY the #219 rule, confirming this thread is its origin. **The shipped state is a flat `representativeIds: string[]` (`types.ts:1327`) with NO size enum, NO EV-ladder, NO vote-weight, NO census recompute** ‚Äî so the build is: the 1-10 ladder (replacing #55's flat count) + the census-recompute pass + a per-focus-Rep `votingPower` derivation w/ remainder, on a (new) per-seat struct. **S-M; pairs with #55 (which it supersedes)/#34/#62/#110/#191 (the per-seat deviant bias rides the same per-seat struct).** No new "every-435" gap ‚Äî that stays AMPU-2 parking. **Both fold into the legislative/SCOTUS clusters; neither is a new keystone.** game-mechanics ¬ß22.7.z, ¬ß15.5(b), ¬ß29.5, ¬ß30.20, ¬ß30.23. |

| 101 | **‚òÖ #241 THE ELECTION CADENCE IS HARDCODED `year % 4` / `year % 2` ARITHMETIC, NOT DATA ‚Äî a variable presidential term length (a passed 6-yr/2-yr-presidency Amendment) would break every predicate; the cadence must become a term-length FIELD the phase scheduler reads (batch 33, `ampuelections`, vcczar's own voice; DESIGNED, 0% shipped; `GM‚áíApp`)** | **Verified hardcoded.** `isPresidentialYear`/`isDraftYear` = `year % 4 === 0`, `isElectionYear` = `year % 2 === 0` (`phases.ts:53-58`); `shouldRunPhase` (`phases.ts:62+`) gates the 2.9.x election phases off these. **There is NO term-length field anywhere in `src/`** (`grep -i 'termLength\|presidentialTerm\|termYears'` = ZERO); `ConstitutionalArticles.termLimits` (`types.ts:1396`) is a binary `'two_terms'\|'no_limits'` = a term-COUNT, not a term LENGTH; the 4-year length is baked into a LABEL STRING only (`constitutionalConvention.ts:19`). | **vcczar flagged this in his own voice** (`ampuelections` 92f0659b POST 1, 6): he added "6-year term presidency" + "2-year term presidency" Amendments, then warned they "might upset how the election cycles are programmed." The fix: **replace the year-predicates with a variable election-cadence model** ‚Äî a per-office term-length field (default 4-yr pres / 2-yr House / 6-yr Senate-class) read by the phase scheduler, so a passed term-length Amendment reschedules the presidential cycle deterministically. **‚òÖ This is the SECOND, INDEPENDENT reason the hardcoded cadence must go** ‚Äî #92's era-as-content-band model ALSO wants the year-predicates replaced (era-advance keys off game-state, not calendar; debt #5, ¬ß9.1.5). | 104 | **‚òÖ‚òÖ DH-79 SHIPPED BUG: "one naval win ends naval forever" ‚Äî the naval tally is RECORDED but NEVER READ by the win-check (batch 34, `rethinkwar`, VERIFIED against code; SMALL STANDALONE QUICK-FIX; `GM‚áíApp`)** | **VERIFIED in code.** `runRevWarBattles` runs the naval phase and records the result ‚Äî `if (win) war.navalWins++; else war.navalLosses++;` (`revolutionaryWar.ts:202`) ‚Äî but the war-over check reads **ONLY the ground tallies**: win at `war.currentGroundWins >= war.groundWinsNeeded` (`:254`), loss at `war.currentGroundLosses >= war.groundLossesRemaining && !war.frenchAlliance` (`:259`). `navalWins`/`navalLosses` are **dead state** ‚Äî they affect NOTHING in the outcome (the war can be won with the navy losing every battle ‚Äî exactly the `ted1772` "Navy won ZERO at sea, war still won" trace). | **This is the shipped confirmation of the complaint that ORIGINATES the #45 redesign** (`rethinkwar` 0fd0f2e5 POST 1, 16). **‚òÖ TECH-LEAD CALL: this is a SMALL STANDALONE QUICK-FIX, not a wait-for-#45 item.** The #45 War-Score rewrite (debt #105) is POST-LAUNCH, but naval being purely cosmetic is a visible "naval never matters" defect that should not sit until then. **Two cheap-fix options (pick one):** (a) **the #45-aligned minimal slice** ‚Äî make a naval WIN give the next ground general **+1 Mil for that battle only (75% chance), a naval LOSS ‚àí1 Mil** (the `revolutionaryWar.ts:212-214` `baseTarget` already sums `general.military`-derived terms, so a per-phase ¬±1 nudge on the general's effective Mil drops in cleanly and is forward-compatible with the #45 naval-as-difficulty-modifier design); or (b) **feed the naval tally into the win-path** (a small naval contribution toward `groundWinsNeeded` / against the loss cap). if granted SC **affirms or reverses**. **‚òÖ Scoring rules:** points/meter effects do **NOT** change on affirm vs reverse (only aye-vs-nay matters; reversing has zero point effect); **cert DENIED** ‚Üí effects computed as if the Focus Judge's ruling were the SC's, EXCEPT SC justices get no points and the Focus Judge gets the case-law points; the same fork `pop`/`dem1820`/`oopscpu` leave unsettled. **GM-REPLACEMENT LENS:** this is the REFEREE's-court-half ‚Äî the engine adjudicating who hears a case (Focus Court vs SC), whether cert is granted (Rule-of-Four), and the ruling outcome + its point/meter effects ‚Äî all work a GM does by hand today. | **MOSTLY AMPU-2 (a deferred epic ‚Äî do NOT scope pre-launch):** (A) per-justice/judge **Judicial Philosophy** (Judicial Ideology base-table + trait-mods + CPU re-normalize past 100% / Judicial Doctrine per-tagged-case ¬±%, hidden-until-revealed via Career-Track/Focus-Court/direct-appt); (B) **Focus Courts** (4 regional appellate courts + Original/Appellate routing + Rule-of-Four cert + affirm/reverse-points-unchanged + cert-denied‚ÜíFocus-Judge-scores + deny-Landmark‚ÜíSC-loses-points wiring into #249 + appointing-Pres enthusiasm-drop roll). **‚òÖ ONE SMALL SHIPPABLE NEAR-TERM SUB-ITEM:** port the forum-AMPU-1 partial ‚Äî a **confirmed-justice 10% immediate ¬±1 ideology shift + per-10-yr further shifts + 5% leftward bias** (XS-S; a tighter variant of #52/drums' 10-yr drift; **MUST reconcile with #52's 10-yr drift rule (25%/10%/5% mid/left/right) so the two do NOT double-fire** ‚Äî pick ONE drift system: either the #52 per-decade rule OR this confirmation-anchored variant, not both). Resolve the "State Judicial Career Track"‚Üí"Judicial Career Track" rename. **Sizing: AMPU-2 epic = L-XL (Focus-Court routing + the two-axis Philosophy model are net-new subsystems, gated behind the SCOTUS docket #25/E25); the 10% confirmation-drift sub-item = XS-S standalone.** Pairs #52/#218/#249/#25/E25/#6/#354/#112/#136. `GM‚áíApp`. game-mechanics ¬ß22.7.v (#251), ¬ß11.6. |

gated DomStab-worst + MilPrep<slightly-prepared + a Controversial/Leadership trait gate); **(B) Populist Coup = incumbent-President CHOICE** (the Jan-6 analogue ‚Äî an incumbent who LOST re-election launching to stay in power; **treason trials** for opposition not up for reelection (Senate/military/SCOTUS) ‚Üí losers removed-from-game + replaced ‚Üí supermajority in all branches; +3 in ALL federal elections for the President's tenure (or ~10 yrs) with opposition challengers who lose removed-from-game; + DomStab + foreign-relations penalties. **Countercoup ruling:** in a Populist Coup the opposition leader launches a countercoup; if it succeeds, the **ACTUAL Presidential-election WINNER is reinstated (NOT the countercoup launcher)** + added to the treason/impeachment rolls (worked example: 2020 ‚Üí Trump coup ‚Üí Pelosi countercoup ‚Üí BIDEN reinstated). **‚òÖ ENGINEERING TAKE: this rides EVERY foundational system at once** ‚Äî the DomStab/EconStab/MilPrep meters (gap #88/#92, mostly shipped) for the GATE; the treason trial = a coup-driven removal+appoint, so impeachment must be authored/built FIRST or the two co-build).** Plus a NEW turn-loop hook (coup resolves AFTER Lingering, BEFORE Legislation, so a treason trial runs the same turn). **‚òÖ THE OPEN PRODUCT QUESTION GATES THE BUILD: full two-type subsystem vs. a single lightweight Era-of-Populism scripted event** ‚Äî vcczar floated scrapping it (POST 1); | 130 | **‚òÖ‚òÖ #266 "METER-AT-FLOOR = NO DETERRENT" systemic balance flaw ‚Äî a penalty against an already-floored meter is a SILENT NO-OP; the SMALL, high-value balance fix that can land EARLY and INDEPENDENTLY (batch 40, `a2312dd2`-foreign-affairs/treaty; VERIFIED against code; the FLAW *is* the shipped behavior)** | **‚òÖ VERIFIED ‚Äî the flaw is the build.** All 7 `NationalMeters` (`types.ts:1399-1407`: revenue/economic/military/domestic/honest/quality/planet) are bare numbers, and EVERY application path clamps them to **`[-5, 5]`** via `clamp(n,min,max)=Math.max(min,Math.min(max,n))` (`rng.ts:49-51`): era-event/legislation effects `applyEffect` (`phaseRunners.ts:3213` `clamp(meters[k]+v,-5,5)`; domesticStability `:3219`), anytime-events (`:2588-2591` via `ANYTIME_EVENTS_RULES.meterClampLow/High`), era-event modulation (`:2991`), Pres-action stub (`:3637-3640`). So a `-1` against a meter sitting at `-5` is absorbed by the clamp and registers **nothing** ‚Äî `#266` confirmed real, shipped, game-wide. **‚òÖ CRITICAL CORRECTION to the digest's fix-attachment claim:** the digest (and #266/#88) assumes a *multi-year game-over COUNTDOWN clock* exists that Largo's "8yr‚Üí6yr" fix would modify ‚Äî **it does NOT.** `game.gameEnded` (`types.ts:1635`) is a one-shot `{year,reason,templateId}` set INSTANTLY by `event.triggersGameEnd` (`phaseRunners.ts:2871`); there is NO meter-driven loss ROLL and NO countdown clock in `src/` (debt #28 / #88 = the FIRST proven loss state is still UNBUILT). Two floated fixes (design spitballs, no tier-1 pick in-thread): **(a) SPILL-OVER** ‚Äî a penalty against an at-minimum score redistributes into other scores; **(b) SHORTEN THE LOSS-COUNTDOWN** ‚Äî a floored meter that would drop has a % chance to accelerate the game-over window (DomStab=1 ‚Üí 50% 8yr‚Üí6yr). **‚òÖ ENGINEERING TAKE: this is a SMALL, high-VALUE, INDEPENDENT balance fix that can land EARLY (QW-to-S) ‚Äî IF fix (a) spill-over is chosen.** Spill-over is a localized change at the ~4 clamp call-sites: detect "delta would push below floor AND meter already at/near floor ‚Üí redirect the un-absorbed remainder to a chosen other meter (or a generic dissatisfaction sink)". It does NOT need any unbuilt system. Fix (b) countdown-shorten is BLOCKED on the unbuilt #88 meter-driven game-over (debt #28) and should co-build there. **The home is the meters/game-over subsystem, NOT the war/treaty engine** ‚Äî it will recur anywhere a penalty hits a floored meter (notably the #45 loss-debuff package, which dumps onto already-floored party-pref ‚Äî exactly why it was toothless in the Butler playtest). **‚òÖ SIZE: QW‚ÄìS (spill-over, independent, do early); the countdown-shorten variant folds into debt #28 (#88).** ‚òÖ OPEN (human): one gap or a sub-row of #88; pick spill-over vs countdown-shorten. ### 8.1 Confirmed shipped bugs + GM-confirmed design holes

Code defects (`BUG-*`: BUG-0 from `hd`, BUG-1/2/3 from `fed`) and GM-confirmed
design holes (`DH-*`: DH-1/2 from `modern`, DH-3..DH-11 from `hd`,
**DH-12..DH-23 from `drums`**, **DH-24..DH-28 from `pop`**, **DH-29..DH-35 from
`rep1800`**, **DH-36..DH-44 from the two batch-8 1772 threads: DH-36..DH-40 +
DH-43/DH-44 from `new1772`, DH-41/DH-42 from `tea1772`**, **DH-45..DH-57 from the
batch-9 `nuke` Cold-War/modern corpus**, **#115/#115a/#115b + the
DH-24/DH-25/DH-27/DH-36/DH-53 re-corroborations from the batch-10 `dem1820`
1820-start**, **#116/#119/#61-extended + DH-59/DH-60 from the batch-11 `arkzag`
1820‚Üí1840 full-arc**, **‚òÖ #120..#142 from batch 12 ‚Äî the `tedchange` +
`smallbugs` designer rulings (now AUTHORITATIVE ‚Äî `tedchange` is the official
rules-doc rewrite channel; the rulings SUPERSEDE prior GA calls where they
conflict). Many batch-12 items CLOSE long-standing OPEN items: QW0 closes,
#51's #18 sub-fork settles its algorithm half (state-scope still user-gated),
Pres-blunder rule canonicalizes the cf82a7d3 ¬ß5a #3 fuzzy "hybrid," CC
composition is rewritten, Hale/Frail/ex-Pres death schedule pins #85**.
**`BUG-*` are fixes,
not features** ‚Äî small and high-value;
**BUG-0 (the relocation-cap stale constant) is the cheapest win in the roadmap**
and BUG-1 is a hard blocker the moment a federalism/1800 scenario ships
(**batch 7 LIVE-confirmed it** + merged the era-events-predating-start hole into
it). **`DH-*` are *rules gaps / balance flags*, not crashes** ‚Äî the forum
rulebook had no answer and a human improvised; many **need rules *authored*
(a PM/design task) before they can be built**, several are **balance dials**
for an existing-but-unbuilt system, **DH-19/20/21/22 are architectural CPU
gaps that need K5's persistent state**, and **DH-34 (static-vs-policy-reactive
era-biases) is a ROADMAP DECISION, not a quick fix**. Verified against the
codebase where quick.

**Classification by where each `DH-*` lives:**
- **CPU-AI handler (folds into the K5 handler suite, ¬ß6.6.1):** DH-19 (gov
  menu industry-stack-aware), DH-20 (reciprocity), DH-21 (meter-guarding),
  DH-22 (cascading scandals), DH-23 (cabinet 36% bug ‚Äî but smaller than
  expected because the system isn't built yet). **‚òÖ Batch-13 OC-* sub-rules fold
  into specific handlers as field-validated sub-rules (NOT new epics):** **OC-1**
  (scandal-resignee re-appointment cooldown ‚Äî instance of DH-22) + **OC-5**
  (court-as-firing loophole gate) ‚Üí **handler #4 (cabinet, ¬ß25.5.4)**; **OC-2**
  (one canonical `chamberControl` definition shared by leadership-select + the
  ‚â•60% proposer gate) ‚Üí **handler #3 (leadership, ¬ß25.3)**; **‚òÖ OC-3
  (BALANCE-CRITICAL ‚Äî crisis-vote ideology-floor + secession check)** ‚Üí
  **handler #2 (legislation, ¬ß25.6)** as its **highest-priority sub-rule**;
  **OC-6** (kingmaker multi-prot√©g√© pairing tiebreak = highest Com+Leg+Gov) ‚Üí
  the kingmaker handler (¬ß25.11). All six are now spec-complete + field-validated
  (`oopscpu`); see ¬ß6.6.1 + ¬ß9 batch-13 lead.
- **Per-subsystem owns it (the subsystem epic must address it):** DH-5
  (Kingmaker-pairing dissolution on flip ‚Äî conversion epic), DH-7 (R/D
  threshold asymmetry + Iron-Fist unilateral lower ‚Äî convention epic), DH-8

  war-end roll's odds are so low wars ran two decades. Build a real

  enthusiasm REWORK** ‚Äî a teardown of E16's existing lobby‚Üíenthusiasm path.
  **(a)** LOBBY satisfaction now writes POINTS (to Pres + matching-lobby
  factions, via the `Faction.score?` ledger), NOT enthusiasm; **(b)**
  IDEOLOGY COMPOSITION drives enthusiasm (‚â•50% cabinet of an ideology =
  +enth; ‚â§20% = ‚àíenth); **(c)** the 3-shifts/half-term cap holds. Land
  AFTER K2 + K5 (the rework requires the conditional-vote-rules primitive
  + the new score path); **re-scope E16 from "build the confirmation
  system in the right shape" to "build confirmation + #124 enthusiasm
  rework TOGETHER in the right shape from day one."** The actual
  percentages are DESIGNER-GATED OPEN.

| **DH-29 (`rep1800` ‚Üí ‚òÖ STRUCTURAL reframe `hd1`)** ‚Äî ‚òÖ‚òÖ Reconstruction readmission DEADLOCKS ‚Üí solo (AND deadlocked-human) Reconstruction UNRESOLVABLE | GM-verified (`rep1800` POST 9170): *"only 3 factions would ever consider voting for it‚Ä¶ in a single player game it basically can never pass."* **‚òÖ Batch-16 (`hd1` POST 2678) ESCALATES this from CPU-only to STRUCTURAL:** the FIRST run with HUMANS on both sides still deadlocked ‚Äî *"The Congress could never agree on a Reconstruction guideline‚Ä¶ NEITHER PASSED and states just started coming back into the Union with NO reconstruction plan at all."* Mutual filibuster ‚Üí null drift. So it is NOT a CPU-heuristic gap; it is a both-chambers-must-agree paralysis. | **DH-36 (`new1772`)** ‚Äî ‚òÖ‚òÖ GM/manual-upkeep burnout ‚Äî now a **4-thread cluster + a designer-run SUCCESS that proves the ceiling** (the META-justification for the entire build) | `new1772` POST 3607: the first captured multiplayer 1772 campaign (10 humans, ~12 turns) collapsed when the first-time GM could not sustain the manual bookkeeping. **‚òÖ‚òÖ Batch-24 raises this to FOUR documented burnout deaths in the recent corpus** + sharpens the CAUSE: **(1)** `new1772` (the original), **(2)** `dem1820#POST 900` (2nd), **(3)** `modernday`/`pop2012b` (Rodja resigns ‚Äî *"freaking hard to be the literal computer"*), and **(4) the two batch-24 1772 runs, BOTH of which died to upkeep load, NOT a loss/win:** `grass1772` died SPECIFICALLY because 2 humans couldn't hand-run 8 CPU factions ("didn't have the time to run the two player factions AND all 8 CPU factions", `#POST 328`) ‚Üí fixed by ADDING humans to offload the CPU work (`#POST 348`); `rookie1772` found the solo run "a part time job" (`#POST 36`), opened it to 4-5 helpers, then died to GM time-burnout (`#POST 1444`). **‚òÖ The NEW angle the batch-24 1772 pair adds: the load-bearing cost is specifically the MANUAL CPU-FACTION SIMULATION** (run 1 faction, not ten) ‚Äî the exact work #114/E9 must own. **‚òÖ‚òÖ Batch-25 (`principle1772`) ADDS THE OTHER END OF THE ARGUMENT ‚Äî a designer-paid SUCCESS that quantifies the ceiling.** It is the FIRST 1772-cluster run to reach a natural ~1800 endpoint WITHOUT a burnout death ‚Äî but ONLY because **@MrPotatoTed (the game co-author) himself hand-simulated all NINE CPU factions** (cabinets, draft lists, leadership votes, event responses; he even REWROTE the CPU career-track heuristic mid-run, POST 323) while @vcczar live-ruled. So the cluster now carries BOTH ends: 4 burnout deaths (the cost of NOT having the CPU-AI) AND a designer-paid completion (the proof that the per-faction sim is exactly what makes the shipped solo mode completable). **Not a code bug ‚Äî the META-justification for the entire build.** | **NOT a discrete fix.** The strongest single "why build AMPU at all" datum: the computer must own all deterministic upkeep AND the per-faction CPU sim (the human GM cannot). Cite it as the motivation behind scaling walls #19/#20 + the **CPU handler suite (E9/#114) + the DH-69 rules/legal-move surface (debt #53)** ‚Äî batch 24 makes these the load-bearing "make it playable solo" investments, and **batch 25 ESCALATES E9/K5/#114 from "reduces upkeep" to "the LOAD-BEARING PREREQUISITE for the shipped solo mode to be playable to completion"** (the designer-run reached its end only because a human did the CPU-AI's job). | **Motivation, not a roadmap row. ## 9. Build-sequencing advice

> **‚òÖ Framing: this whole section sequences the GM-replacement program.** Per the
> north-star (`game-context.md` ‚Üí "The app must subsume the human GM"), every
> `GM‚áíApp`-tagged gap is the engine taking over work a human GM does by hand ‚Äî
> the referee/calculator half (election/platform math, conventions, scenario
> boot, lingering rolls, decision-event/treaty adjudication, end-conditions,
> in-app rules) and the opponent half (the E9/K5 CPU faction AI). Order below is
> dependency-first; the `roadmap.md` "GM-replacement lens" groups the same gaps
> by which half of the GM's job they retire.

> **This section is written for the roadmap-planner to lift directly.** It is my
> engineering opinion on order, dependencies, and rough size/risk for the
> game-pm gap log (~176 rows across 5+ eras + A1‚ÄìA9 presentation + the CPU-AI
> cluster #70‚Äì#85 + the **batch-6 scenario-boot / endgame cluster #86‚Äì#91** + the
> **batch-7 early-republic cluster #92‚Äì#99** + the **batch-8 founding/era cluster
> #100‚Äì#105** + the **batch-12 designer-rulings cluster #120‚Äì#142** + the
> **batch-13 `oopscpu` CPU-validation cluster #143‚Äì#146 + OC-1‚Ä¶OC-8 + DH-61/DH-62**
> + the **batch-14 `gild1868` gilded-era content cluster #147‚Äì#150 + DH-63**),
> the design divergences (mechanics ¬ß19.1, now **#1‚Äì#21**), the confirmed bugs
> (incl. BUG-0), and the GM design holes (DH-1/DH-2 + DH-3..DH-11 + DH-12..DH-23 +
> DH-24..DH-28 + DH-29..DH-35 + **DH-36..DH-44 + DH-61/DH-62 + DH-63**). Source: codebase +
> `gilded` + `fed` + `1772s` + `modern` + `hd` + `drums` + `pop` + `rep1800` +
> **`new1772` + `tea1772` + `dem1820` + `arkzag` + `tedchange` + `smallbugs` +
> `oopscpu` + `gild1868` + `hd1` + `terror2000` + `ted1772` + `ideo1928` +
> `fixes2022` + `biden2021` + `planb` + `nixon1972`/`cpufull`/`solo1916`/`trump2024` +
> `modernday` + `pop2012b` + `grass1772` + `rookie1772` + `principle1772` +
> `summer2021` + **`redbutton`** + **`to-do` + `fixes` + `completions` + `vic3` (batch 28)**.
>
> **‚òÖ‚òÖ‚òÖ Batch-28 changes to the plan (FOUR designer META-DOCS ‚Äî `to-do` [the
> authoritative pre-build dev backlog, 2021-22], `fixes` [vcczar's designer-authoritative
> fix-queue, 2022], `completions` [‚òÖ the *done-half* of the backlog ‚Äî what the **2022
> reference/spreadsheet game** considers DONE], `vic3` [aspirational Victoria-3 borrows,
> mostly "AMPU 2" parking-lot]; a DISCUSSION/CATALOG + **RECONCILE** batch, no era playtest.
> **This is the pivotal RECONCILIATION batch:** the `completions` list lets us convert
> 2022 "done" claims into VERIFIED truth about our `src/`. 17 NEW gap rows #189‚Äì#205; ~50
> existing rows annotated in-place; NO new keystone, NO re-sequence of the top of queue):**
>
> > **‚òÖ Read this block if you only read one for batch 28 ‚Äî the RECONCILIATION RESULT is the
> > headline.** A 2022 spreadsheet "completion" is NOT grounds to close any gap (our "Built" =
> > present in our React/TS `src/`, a different universe). I verified the flagged claims at
> > `file:line`:
> >
> > **(a) ‚òÖ‚òÖ THE 2 "CONFIRMED-SHIPPED" CLAIMS ‚Äî ONE REFUTED, ONE CONFIRMED.**
> > **‚ëÝ Election die D6 ‚Üí REFUTED.** `completions`/game-mechanics ¬ß15.5(a) tagged the swing die

> > `calcStateVote`, NOT a die). The REAL election randomizer is **`(Math.random()-0.5)*8` at
> > `phaseRunners.ts:3711`** ‚Äî a continuous **¬±4 band of raw `Math.random`, NEITHER d6 nor d3, NOT
> > seeded.** Our build ships **NO election die at all**; the batch-27 finding stands. **‚ë° Trait-conflict
> > d6 ‚Üí CONFIRMED-BUILT, and via the RNG seam.** `tryGrantTrait` (`traits.ts:41-59`) resolves a
> > held-conflict on `d(6) >= TRAIT_LIFECYCLE_RULES.conflictD6Threshold` (`:53`; `d` from `rng.ts`,
> > imported `:3`). One delta: the threshold is **`4`** (`types.ts:638` ‚Üí **1-3 cancel / 4-6 keep**),
> > not the sheet's "1-2/3-6" (a tuning gap, not structural). **So I MARK trait-conflict-d6 CONFIRMED

> **‚òÖ‚òÖ‚òÖ Batch-26 changes to the plan (`summer2021` / `fe15db25` ‚Äî "Summer 2021

>
> > **‚òÖ Read this block if you only read one for batch 26.** Federalism was played
> > forward for the first time ‚Äî and EVERYTHING in the federalism president/cabinet/
> > diplomacy spine had to be hand-rolled because the build deliberately refuses to
> > run it. The load-bearing moves:
> >
> > **(a) ‚òÖ‚òÖ #177 ESCALATED ‚Äî federalism is now DESIGNED-AND-PLAYED-FORWARD, not
> > just designed-and-walled. LIFT THE TWO `eraGraph.ts` WALLS.** Prior batches had
> > the federalism foreign-affairs spine as *static designed content* (the `fed`
> > spec + `principle1772`'s endpoint). `summer2021` is the **first source to run
> > the whole federalism TURN LOOP forward** (cabinet appointment ‚Üí era/decision
> > events ‚Üí cabinet-driven lingering ‚Üí diplomacy ‚Üí legislation+veto+override ‚Üí
> > presidential action; ¬ß20.9), proving the walled content is not hypothetical ‚Äî it
> > is the *actual gameplay* of the next era. **‚òÖ Shipped-state CONFIRMED unchanged
> > (re-verified):** `eraGraph.ts::validate()` still throws via the same TWO guards ‚Äî
> > `:153-155` rejects any node with `chartIndex >= 49` ("French Revolution /
> > Federalism is out of scope") and `:150-152` rejects any `president`/`cabinet`
> > decider ("no President/Cabinet pre-1789"); **every event in ¬ß20.9-¬ß20.15 is a
> > post-1788 president/cabinet decision-event.** Authoring federalism STILL requires
> > lifting both walls (gate president/cabinet deciders on `year >= 1789` instead of
> > forbidding them) + per-power diplomacy-relation meters on the snapshot (none
> > exist ‚Äî `grep relation|diplomacyMeter|foreignRelations in src/` = ZERO; the
> > shipped `snap.game.diplomacy` is a flat 8-power score map mutated only by the
> > ¬ß11.1 lingering drift, not a relation-event meter). **No re-sequence; this is
> > the highest-confidence "federalism is the next-era build" signal in the corpus.**
> > game-mechanics ¬ß20.9, ¬ß20.3.1. debt #57.
> >
> > **(b) ‚òÖ #179 (NEW) ‚Äî the cabinet LINGERING-ROLL meter engine (the per-turn
> > input to the economy; designed, hand-rolled).** The federalism counterpart to
> > the shipped ¬ß11.1 lingering: each lingering phase, **every cabinet member rolls
> > to move their portfolio meter ¬±1, and it can BACKFIRE** (Treasury rolled badly ‚Üí
> > EconStab dropped to Great Recession; War+Navy both succeeded ‚Üí MilPrep maxed).
> > **‚òÖ Shipped-state CONFIRMED unbuilt-as-designed (verified):** the shipped
> > `runPhase_2_5_1_Lingering` (`phaseRunners.ts:3260`) is a **deterministic,
> > skill-keyed DRIFT** ‚Äî `drift(skill)` (`:3266-3273`) maps a cabinet member's
> > `admin`/`military` skill to a fixed ¬±delta with NO roll, NO backfire, NO
> > Efficient/Pliable/regional hook; the cabinet pick `runPhase_2_3_1_Cabinet`
> > (`:2158-2224`) is a flat score-sort with NO ideology gate, NO Speaker/PPT/advisor
> > decider override, NO bill-coupling. So #179 is a **redesign of the existing
> > deterministic drift into a roll-based engine** PLUS five sub-systems. It bundles
> > **‚â•6 sub-rules that are ‚â•2 distinct systems** (¬ß20.10): (a) per-portfolio ¬±roll
> > w/ backfire + **Efficient = auto-+2-if-it-moves** [vcczar verbatim, ch99 POST
> > 8949]; (b) **Pliable Pres ‚Üí Speaker/PPT/Key-Advisor decider override** incl.
> > SCOTUS nomination; (c) **cabinet-bill double-points coupling** [propose-in-
> > portfolio ‚Üí carried to law = 2√ó for both, dropped = SecXX ‚àí50]; (d) **ideology-
> > gated confirmation by office** [RW-Populist SecWar rejected]; (e) **regional-
> > representation meter penalty** [DomStab fell ‚Äî no Deep-South Sec]; (f)
> > **executive-actions-implemented-by-cabinet-with-BLUNDER rolls** [the ¬ß10.4.1/#22/
> > #126 implementation-blunder layer in a cabinet context]. **‚òÖ Flag for the human:
> > SPLIT #179** ‚Äî (i)+(f) is the meter/blunder engine, (b)+(d) is the appointment/
> > confirmation layer, (c) is a legislation-coupling, (e) is an election-feedback
> > input; they have different dependencies. **Where it binds:** `phaseRunners.ts`

> > (`:2158`, the gate+decider-override) + the ¬ß10.4.1 multi-decider/blunder layer +

> > **(c) ‚òÖ #182 (NEW) ‚Äî `command` = the President's PER-TURN EXECUTIVE-ACTION
> > BUDGET.** Verbatim (ch95:159): *"Osgood can do up to 4 actions each turn because
> > he has 4 command."* So a President's `command` value = his executive-action count
> > per Presidential-Action phase ‚Äî generalizing the ¬ß24.1/#61 "0-command acting
> > president is inert" read into a positive budget (0 = inert, N = N actions/turn),
> > each action then implemented by the relevant cabinet member with a blunder roll

> > **(f) ‚òÖ DH-71 (NEW BUG) ‚Äî faction-handover DUPLICATE-politician data-integrity

>
> > **‚òÖ Read this block if you only read one for batch 24.** Two 1772 runs, both
> > of which DIED to manual-upkeep/CPU-bookkeeping load, not a loss/win. Nothing
> > re-sequences the keystones; the load-bearing moves:
> >
> > **(a) ‚òÖ #176 ‚Äî founding MilPrep hard-capped at 2 for the whole Era of
> > Independence (the ONE net-new gap, surfaced INDEPENDENTLY by both runs).**
> > The forum's spreadsheet wires MilPrep tiers 3-4 to the Militia Act (a
> > federalism-era ~1792 bill) while the auto-forced 1774 war bills (Continental
> > Army / Continental Navy) point at the HIGHER tiers (5-6) ‚Üí every forced-war
> > MilPrep roll is wasted, every game, and the founding era sits in a permanent
> > Military-Preparedness crisis (`grass1772#POST 86-90, 121`; `rookie1772#POST
> > 26, 32-33`). **‚òÖ Shipped-state nuance (verified):** the build does NOT have
> > this bug yet because **it does not have the tier-prerequisite system at all** ‚Äî
> > `scenario1772.ts:9-17` boots `military: -2` as a RAW numeric `NationalMeters`
> > field clamped to `[-5,5]` (moved by bill `effects.meters` deltas, e.g.
> > `phaseRunners.ts:3638`); `revolutionaryWar.ts` models NO MilPrep ladder and
> > NO per-tier legislative prerequisites (`grep meterPrereq|meterTier|MilitiaAct|
> > StandingArmy in src/` = ZERO). So #176 is **a content/authoring constraint to
> > honor WHEN founding war-content + a meter-prereq ladder are built, not a
> > regression to patch**: whatever founding MilPrep ladder we author, the
> > auto-forced Continental Army/Navy bills MUST be able to raise the meter
> > (cap ~4-5 pre-federalism, per Cal's reverse-the-prereqs fix), else the forced
> > war legislation is dead rolls. **Where it binds:** the founding bill/meter
> > data (`scenario1772`/`eraEvents1772`) + a (new) meter-prereq predicate +
> > `revolutionaryWar.ts`'s `milPrepMod` battle term. **Size: S; folds into the
> > founding/RevWar content (E1) + #67 meter caps.** Open Q (designer-gated):
> > adopt the reverse-prereq fix or keep the founding military crisis as intended.
> > game-mechanics ¬ß17.4. debt #56.
> >
> > **(b) ‚òÖ‚òÖ THE ONBOARDING / SOLO-APP / CPU-AI CLUSTER IS NOW VERY STRONGLY
> > EVIDENCED ‚Äî the meta-roadmap headline (NO new scope; it RAISES the
> > justification of already-scoped items).** Both runs are the cleanest KB proof

> > `playerFactionId` gates scattered through `phaseRunners.ts`, e.g. `:89, :253,

> > bearing "make it playable solo" investments are (1) the app's CPU-AI owning

> > French-alliance-unloseable / scripted-autonomy-event); **the generic `War`
> > model the war engine builds (Phase-1 #3, #155/#56) must support an
> > event-driven win, not only a battle threshold.** Folds into the war-engine

> > `tedchange` BEFORE building** (open Q logged). Size S‚ÄìM. **(b) ‚òÖ #175** is a **small
> > law-repealability DATA MODEL** (MrPotatoTed designer ruling, `#POST 687-688`): add

> > spreadsheet artifact). **XS, pairs with #120 dataset-balance + DH-51.** **‚òÖ DH-69 no
> > in-app rules / legal-move surface (UX/onboarding):** grep `rulebook|legalMove|
> > availableActions|helpPage|tutorial|onboard` in `src/` = **ZERO hits** ‚Äî there is no
> > rules page, no legal-move enumeration, no onboarding. Players "winging it" without a
> > rulebook (POST 342-356) is the friction an **in-app rules/legal-move surface** fixes;
> > it sharpens #115's boot-procedure gap and **serves the CPU cluster too** (a legal-move
> > enumerator is the same primitive a CPU action-picker needs) and the GM-burnout theme
> > (DH-36 family). **UX/onboarding item, no engine size of its own; cite under the
> > #115/CPU-AI work.** **Decision-gated RECOUNT: 0** (the #172 boot-flag-vs-derived
> > question is designer-gated TUNING within E16/E14c, not a new bucket entry). **No NEW
> > keystone, NO re-sequence; top of queue UNCHANGED** (QW0 ‚Üí K0/K2 ‚Üí K3/K4 + scenarioBoot
> > ‚Üí E1).
> >
> **‚òÖ‚òÖ Batch-21 changes to the plan (FOUR playtests ‚Äî `nixon1972` [`4853cf4d`,
> 1972-start modern MULTIPLAYER, GA-run, one half-term then GM-burnout stall],
> `cpufull` [`1f72600c`, all-CPU 1772 founding traversal, GA-run, ENDED on a
> scripted CPU game-over], `trump2024` [`51dfaef1`, **Ted-run designer-authoritative**,
> 2024/Jan-2025-start modern MULTIPLAYER, SETUP-ONLY], `solo1916` [`5027f0f3`,
> 1916-start SOLO Progressive-Era/WWI, GA-run, one half-term then stall]. TWO new
> era-keyed gaps (one PARTLY SHIPPED), ONE escalation that re-prioritizes a debt
> item HIGHER, ONE DH bug; NO new keystone, NO re-sequence; TOP-OF-QUEUE UNCHANGED.):**
>
> > **‚òÖ Read this block if you only read one for batch 21. The load-bearing move is
> > **‚òÖ #158 ESCALATED** ‚Äî `cpufull` is the **2nd live CPU game-over in the KB** and
> > **field-falsifies the flat-75%-oppose patch** (the patch was applied and the game
> > ENDED anyway: 4/10 factions rolled ‚â§25 and a game-ending peace passed at a **4-5-4
> > plurality** after the CC-President's reject was overridden). The fix is **no longer**
> > the flat 75% roll ‚Äî it needs a **HARD VETO** (CPUs can't select a `triggersGameEnd`
> > option in a solo/CPU-majority game) **or** a **points-based anti-peace bias**, **plus
> > a non-plurality-overridable game-ending-peace** (the Pres's reject must not be beaten
> > by a mere plurality). This **raises debt #32's priority** within the CPU/war track and
> > makes the **#155 RevWar floor #3 LEAKY** (debt #34a). The other three items are
> > smaller: **#170** era-keyed offices is **PARTLY SHIPPED** (the founding-seat half is
> > LIVE in `cabinetSeatsForYear`, `types.ts:1196`; only the modern departments + the
> > **DNI‚áíCIA-Director supersession** are unbuilt) ‚Üí **extends the boot/offices work
> > (¬ß3 item 2 / E16)**, S‚ÄìM; **#171** the era-keyed draft-ideology TOGGLE (OFF in the
> > modern present) ‚Üí **folds into the #4/#108 draft-profile work**, S; **DH-68** (1916
> > successor-state events fired before WWI ended) ‚Üí **folds into the DH-60 era-event-
> > precondition work** (now multi-era), S. **`cpufull` re-validates the CPU suite
> > (#70‚Äì#79) end-to-end; `nixon1972` corroborates the crisis/war/Watergate mechanics +
> > is ANOTHER GM-burnout stall** (the upkeep-automation argument grows). The top of the
> > queue does NOT move: QW0 ‚Üí K0/K2 ‚Üí K3/K4 + scenarioBoot ‚Üí E1 still lead.**
> >
> > **Verified shipped-state of every batch-21 item (grep/Read-confirmed):**
> > **(1) ‚òÖ‚òÖ #158 ESCALATED ‚Äî the flat-75% anti-game-over patch is BOTH UNBUILT and now
> > FIELD-FALSIFIED; re-prioritize HIGHER (debt #32).** The shipped consumer is bare:
> > `runPhase_2_4_3_Era`'s terminal handler at `phaseRunners.ts:2871` does
> > `if (event.triggersGameEnd && !snap.game.gameEnded) snap.game.gameEnded = {‚Ä¶}` ‚Äî it
> > simply SETS `game.gameEnded` (`types.ts:1635`) with **NO CPU veto, NO anti-peace
> > bias, and NO plurality guard.** The CPU resolver `pickAIResponse` (`eraGraph.ts:88-103`)
> > is an `aiBias`-map lookup keyed by faction personality with **NO anti-game-over term
> > whatsoever** (grep-confirmed; same as batch-17). So a CPU-voted terminal peace node
> > (1772 `lost_war`/`dominion_autonomy`/`confederation_remains`, `eraEvents1772.ts:296,
> > 308` + the Carlisle/Conciliatory `chose('carlisle_commission','b')`/`chose(
> > 'conciliatory_resolution','b')` chain `:227,184,308`) resolves by point-math, which
> > leans FOR peace. **‚òÖ `cpufull` is the LIVE counter-example to the flat-75% patch
> > (`cpufull#POST 62-68, 73`):** the all-CPU Continental Congress accepted the Carlisle
> > Peace Commission and ENDED the game ‚Äî *"75% to vote no, but four factions triggered
> > it"* (two rolled 1/100, two rolled 9/100, meeting the ‚â§25 support threshold), and
> > after CC-President John Adams's REJECT was overridden, a **4-5-4 plurality** carried
> > the game-over. With 10 independent 75% rolls ~2.5 factions are EXPECTED to defect
> > every time, so the flat per-faction roll does NOT reliably hold. **‚òÖ Why `ted1772`'s
> > near-misses survived but this one did not:** `ted1772`'s votes were saved by the **2/3
> > Articles peace-vote threshold** (RevWar floor #2); here the game-over needed only a
> > **plurality** after the Pres's reject was overridden ‚Äî **the plurality-override path
> > bypasses the 2/3 floor entirely.** **‚òÖ The fix (re-scoped, designer-gated which-way):**
> > (a) a **HARD VETO** ‚Äî in a solo/CPU-majority game, any response that sets
> > `triggersGameEnd`/surrender is **removed from the CPU vote menu** (unselectable), OR
> > (b) the **points-based anti-peace ideology bias** Ted floated (*"give points to most
> > ideologies to be opposed to peace and 90% of the time that's the way the CPU will
> > swing"*) tuned so a CPU plurality **cannot** form; **PLUS, either way, the Pres's
> > reject of a game-ending peace must be NON-plurality-overridable** (a separate guard on
> > the override path, not just the per-faction roll). **Binds at:** the `triggersGameEnd`
> > decision in `pickAIResponse`/`runPhase_2_4_3_Era` (`eraGraph.ts:88-103` +
> > `phaseRunners.ts:2871`) for the veto/bias, and the CC/Congress OVERRIDE path
> > (`continentalCongress.ts` `voteCC` + the era-event `decider:'cc-president'`/`congress`
> > resolution) for the plurality guard. **Bears on #114 (solo-app is the target mode) +
> > #155 RevWar floor #3 (now known LEAKY, debt #34a).** **Size: S‚ÄìM** (the veto/bias is
> > S; the non-overridable-plurality guard adds the override-path work). **‚òÖ This is a
> > SOLO-PLAY BLOCKER** ‚Äî the same class as DH-29 was for Reconstruction: a CPU-majority
> > game can end itself prematurely against the human's survival wish. **Build it WITH the
> > #155 war-balance pass (E3) + the #75 CPU event-vote handler (E9), AHEAD of the rest of
> > the CPU/war track.** game-mechanics ¬ß13.2 (the STRENGTHENED #158 block) + ¬ß21.1 (the
> > #155 floors) + ¬ß25.7. debt #32 (escalated) + #34a.
> > **(2) ‚òÖ #170 era-keyed offices/departments ‚Äî PARTLY SHIPPED (founding seats), DESIGNED
> > (modern offices + the DNI‚áíCIA-Director supersession).** **‚òÖ The founding-seat half is
> > ALREADY LIVE:** `cabinetSeatsForYear(year)` (`types.ts:1196`) era-gates the cabinet seat
> > list exactly as #170 wants for the early seats ‚Äî `[]` before 1789, +Navy at `year>=1798`
> > (`:1203`), +Postmaster at `year>=1829` (`:1206`), +Interior at `year>=1849` (`:1205`) ‚Äî
> > confirming the game-master's read. **What's MISSING (the unbuilt half):** the
> > `OfficeType` union (`types.ts:1111-1134`) has **NO modern departments at all** (no
> > Energy/DHS/DNI/CIA-Director/Commerce/Labor/HHS/HUD/Transportation ‚Äî grep-confirmed:
> > zero `DNI|CIADirector|DepartmentOfEnergy|HomelandSecurity|supersedes|foundedYear|
> > createdByBill` hits in `src/`), there is **no `foundedYear`/`createdByBill`/`supersedes`
> > schema** on the office model, and there is **no office-supersession** (so a modern board
> > can neither seed the right offices nor map a later office onto an older slot). **‚òÖ The

> > a 1972 energy bill is *"Wrong Era"*, `#POST 392-394`; `terror2000`: the DNI is *created*
> > mid-game in 2004). **‚òÖ Where it binds:** extend the `OfficeType` enum with the modern
> > departments + add a per-office existence table (`foundedYear`, optional `createdByBill`,
> > optional `supersedes`), and have **`cabinetSeatsForYear`'s successor ‚Äî the BOOT-SEED
> > `GameState.cabinetSeats: SeatSpec[]` from ¬ß3 item 2 / E16** ‚Äî seed only offices whose
> > founded-year ‚â§ the board's start year (or that a bill has created), applying the
> > supersession at the creating event. **‚òÖ This is the SAME mutable-cabinet-seat refactor
> > the build already plans** (`cabinetSeatsForYear` is confirmed the WRONG long-term model

> > bill); #170 is the **era-keyed-EXISTENCE + supersession layer** on top of it ‚Äî NOT a
> > new epic. **Size: S‚ÄìM** (the enum + table is S; the supersession + boot-seed wiring is
> > the M part, but it folds into the E16 cabinet-seat refactor that is already scoped).
> > **Open Q (`trump2024`):** is DNI‚áíCIA-Director a permanent stopgap, or should the modern

> > **(3) ‚òÖ #171 era-keyed draft-ideology TOGGLE ‚Äî DESIGNED, not built; FOLDS INTO the

> > 15-16, 102, 173`):** *"There are no draft ideology restrictions for the future/present

> > (`phaseRunners.ts:107`) picks via `pickBestForFaction` (`:33`), which scores by PV +

> > **(4) ‚òÖ DH-68 ‚Äî Progressive-era successor-state Era Evos lack a WWI-end prerequisite (a

> > In a 1916-start first half-term (`solo1916#POST 31, 34`) the GM fired *"Czechoslovakia
> > Gains Independence from Austria"* and *"Hungary Gains Independence from Austria"* **before
> > WWI had ended** ‚Äî in the SAME events phase as the WWI-entry decision ‚Äî flagging it: *"I
> > wonder if this should have a requirement that ww1 has ended"*; community: *"These two
> > should not trigger until after WWI is over."* **‚òÖ CODEBASE-VERIFIED ‚Äî this is exactly
> > DH-60's gap, and the two era-event builders DIVERGE on it:** the **1772 graph HAS the
> > machinery** ‚Äî every node carries an optional `precondition: Predicate` (`types.ts:1487`)
> > and `evalPredicate` (`eraGraph.ts:12`) interprets `eventCompleted` (`:18`), `warActive`
> > (`:31`), `warOutcome` (`:32`), and `eventChose`, so founding successor-style chains gate
> > correctly (the Treaty of Paris gates on `warOutcome`/`yearAtLeast`; the Carlisle/
> > Conciliatory game-over chain gates on a prior `chose`). But the **1856 builder is
> > CALENDAR-ONLY** ‚Äî `buildEraEventsForYear` (`eraEvents1856.ts:4`) emits events purely by
> > `year >= X && year <= Y` (e.g. `:6`, `:30`) with **NO `precondition` field on any row** ‚Äî
> > the exact DH-60 shape ‚Äî and a **Progressive (1896-1932) builder does not exist** (1916
> > runs on `modern`/`progressive` tuning, UNBUILT). **‚òÖ The fix:** add a WWI-end predicate
> > (`worldWarOneEnded` / `eventCompleted:'wwi_end'`) to the Czechoslovakia/Hungary nodes
> > (and other post-war beats ‚Äî League of Nations, post-war treaties), and **port the
> > 1772-graph `precondition` layer into the 1856/Progressive builders** so era content can
> > gate selectively on a prior event, not just the calendar. **This is the SAME selective-
> > precondition ask as DH-60** (`dem1820`/`arkzag`/`smallbugs`: "Stubborn Cherokee" /
> > "Force Open Trade with Japan" firing without a prerequisite) ‚Äî now corroborated from the
> > Progressive band, so **DH-60 is multi-era confirmed.** **Size: S** (a few keyed
> > preconditions + the precondition layer ported to the non-1772 builders; SAME surface as
> > BUG-1 + K3's `territoryOwned`). **Open Q (`solo1916`):** which other Progressive-band
> > events need a WWI-end (or other prior-event) gate? game-mechanics ¬ß10.4.8. debt #49.

> **‚òÖ Batch-20 changes to the plan (FOUR meta/design threads ‚Äî `planb` [`094cc3a2`,
> the build-FINISHING PROCESS plan, NOT a playtest], `dbomit` [`4be5a005`, a
> missing-pol REQUEST thread ‚Üí #120], `biden2021` [`24061ad6`, a modern era-CONTENT
> brainstorm], `ampu2wish` [`888ba777`, the OUT-OF-SCOPE AMPU-2 wishlist]. LIGHT
> batch: ONE new runtime mechanic, ONE authoring-process gate, the rest dataset /
> quarantine. NO new keystone, NO re-sequence, TOP-OF-QUEUE UNCHANGED.):**
>
> > **‚òÖ Read this block if you only read one for batch 20. Almost nothing here is new
> > code. The single runtime delta is **#169** ‚Äî the "Elderly President Drops Out of
> > Reelection ‚Üí endorses VP" mid-campaign replacement event (the Biden-2024 ‚Üí Harris
> > analog) ‚Äî a **small addition to the era-event epic (E15)**, size S, that gates on the
> > age roll the engine ALREADY has and swaps the VP onto the ticket inside the existing
> > presidential general resolver. **#168** is a **pre-build AUTHORING-quality pass** (a
> > terminology contract + a branch-path/meter/% sanity-audit + a trait/interest
> > compilation) ‚Äî it is **process/authoring work, NOT a code epic**; the roadmap NOTES it
> > as an authoring gate, it does not SCHEDULE it as a code task. **`biden2021`** is
> > modern era-CONTENT that extends the modern band past 2020 (folds into the ¬ß28.13
> > modern content tail / #92/#41; the pardon pres-actions block on #122). **`dbomit`** is
> > pure dataset work ‚Üí **#120** (no per-pol rows). **‚òÖ `ampu2wish` is OUT OF SCOPE ‚Äî do
> > NOT roadmap ANY AMPU-2 wishlist item for AMPU 1** (day-by-day Paradox rebuild, full
> > House, dynamic regions/biases, etc.); quarantine only. The one cross-cutting process
> > constraint to carry forward: **all content authoring proceeds CHRONOLOGICALLY** (Anthony
> > imports pols/events in chronological order; all changes route through vcczar). The top
> > of the queue does NOT move: QW0 ‚Üí K0/K2 ‚Üí K3/K4 + scenarioBoot ‚Üí E1 still lead.**
> >
> > **Verified shipped-state of every batch-20 item (grep/Read-confirmed):**
> > **(1) ‚òÖ #169 mid-campaign drop-out ‚Üí endorse-VP event ‚Äî DESIGNED, not built; FOLDS
> > INTO E15 (era-event epic); the age roll it gates on ALREADY EXISTS.** grep for
> > `dropOut|replaceOnTicket|endorseVP|midCampaign|forcedOut|stepAside` across `src/`
> > returns **ZERO** hits ‚Äî there is **no mid-campaign presidential-replacement / ticket-
> > swap path** anywhere. **But the trigger substrate ships:** the age-penalty die roll the
> > event gates on is the **old-age ability-decay roll** (`ABILITY_LOSS_RULES.oldAge`,
> > `minAge: 70`, `types.ts:521`; fired at `phaseRunners.ts:2384-2393`) plus the
> > `MORTALITY_RULES` death/retire brackets (70/80, `types.ts:488-498`) and the PV age
> > penalty (`pv.ts:85`, `age > 70`). **‚òÖ Note on the digest's "70-or-75 age roll": the
> > candidate-relevant roll is keyed at 70, not 75** ‚Äî the only 75-gated roll is the SCOTUS
> > retirement roll (`age >= 75`, `phaseRunners.ts:3655`), which is unrelated to the
> > president. **Where the drop-out event binds:** a new `EraEvent` (the shipped data model,
> > `types.ts:1466`) firing during an aged incumbent's reelection campaign, whose effect (i)
> > checks the president was hit by the ¬ß10.1 age roll AND is running for reelection; (ii)
> > rolls **50% to pull** him; (iii) injects a flat **‚àí1 party malus** into the ¬ß21.9
> > presidential-vote modifier stack ‚Äî **landing on the VP even when the pres is pulled**;
> > (iv) **swaps the VP onto the ticket** by replacing `blueCand`/`redCand` inside
> > `runPhase_2_9_4_PresidentialGeneral(snap, blueCand, redCand)` (`phaseRunners.ts:3752`,
> > which already takes the ticket candidates as params; the VP id is at `types.ts:1568`);
> > (v) a fallback to the ¬ß15.3 pre-primary/compromise-candidate convention machinery (+ a
> > pre-12A "designate a successor" path) if the VP can't/won't step up. **Designed-but-
> > unsettled guards:** a VP-younger-than-pres check before defaulting to the VP; the
> > alternative stiffer trigger (80+ / Frail-Easily Overwhelmed-Incoherent / older-than-VP /
> > Party-Pref-against). **Era-of-Populism-scoped until it fires twice** (the twice-before-
> > generalizing rule). **‚òÖ Distinct from #37 / debt #29 (defeat-then-retire / the war-
> > defeat President-loss package): #169 removes the candidate DURING the campaign, not
> > after a loss.** **Size: S ‚Äî a small addition to E15 (or the election epic E20b).** debt
> > #45; game-mechanics ¬ß10.4.7.
> > **(2) ‚òÖ #168 ‚Äî a PRE-BUILD AUTHORING-QUALITY PASS, NOT a roadmap code epic.** There is
> > **NO code surface.** It produces (a) a **terminology contract** the build must honor
> > (ideology short forms `LW Pop/Prog/Lib/Mod/Cons/Trad/RW Pop`, matching the `types.ts`
> > 7-point scale; the Skills/levels/Experience/Interests vocabulary buckets; the military-
> > Experience ‚Üí **"Army" rename** ‚Äî currently incomplete: "Army" is mislabeled as a
> > *starting expertise* and should be Military, a `dbomit` corroboration; **human-rights ‚Üí
> > "criminal reform"**; demographic-category standardization ‚Äî add Middle Eastern
> > ethnicity, drop the no-op Protestant/White defaults); (b) a **branch-path / meter-
> > direction / percentage-multiplier sanity audit** of the AUTHORED content (+budget meter
> > must move + when it makes money = the **DH-53 effect-SIGN family**; the Afghanistan-War-
> > Phase-I multiplier; alt-state event enter/exit columns swapped; legislation
> > repealability; the half-broken Split-Electoral-Votes gov action); (c) a **trait/interest
> > compilation** (how each is gained + what each does). **This is an AUTHORING-PROCESS GATE
> > the roadmap NOTES but does NOT schedule as a code task** ‚Äî it standardizes + audits the
> > content the era-content/dataset epics consume, and it pairs with the
> > `scripts/seedDataset.mjs` author-time pipeline (¬ß7) + the **#120 dataset umbrella**.

> **‚òÖ‚òÖ Batch-18 changes to the plan (`ideo1928` / `e45a756c` ‚Äî "Era of Ideologies",
> the FIRST 1928-start interwar campaign; GA = @10centjimmy [a GAME-ADMIN, NOT the
> designer ‚Äî GA rulings are useful but NOT designer-authoritative; ONE Ted-authored
> ruling, flagged]; 8 human + CPU factions; plays ~1928‚Üí~1936 (Hoover landslide ‚Üí
> the Great-Depression event fires LATE ‚Üí FDR wins 1932). Runs on `modern`/

> top-of-queue.):**
>
> > **‚òÖ Read this block if you only read one for batch 18. The headline is the
> > ECONOMIC ENGINE: this is the FIRST interwar source, and it is the SECOND era to
> > exercise the E4c economic-engine epic (after the `arkzag` Bank-War / Panic-of-1837
> > arc, batch 11) ‚Äî so E4c is now WELL-SPECIFIED ACROSS TWO ERAS (the 1820s Bank War
> > AND the 1930s Great Depression both want the SAME `game.economy` state machine +
> > EconStab crisis + crisis-gated bills). This batch (a) EXTENDS E4c with the
> > Great-Depression META-event + the EconStab‚Üíindustry cascade + crisis-gated
> > New-Deal bills (#160) and the DH-67 crash-gated-party-modifier fix; (b) CONFIRMS
> > the era-band model at a SIXTH start year (1928, #161) ‚Äî pure confidence, no
> > scope; (c) surfaces a REAL FIX for the ¬ß25.5 CPU cabinet pathology ‚Äî the
> > confirmation AUTO-PASS gate (the one Ted-authored ruling this batch); (d)
> > 3-thread-confirms the impeachment subsystem is BROKEN (DH-66). The top of the
> > queue does NOT move: QW0 ‚Üí K0/K2 ‚Üí K3/K4 + scenarioBoot ‚Üí E1 still lead. #160 +
> > DH-67 fold into E4c (subsystem track, ON TOP of E2+E6); the confirmation gate
> > folds into E16/E9-handler-9d; #161 is K3/K4 confidence; DH-66 stays parking-lot
> > awaiting Ted's pending rewrite. The marquee single takeaway is DH-67: event-gate
> > the era's BLUE party bias to the crash ‚Äî a clean, high-value fix that makes the
> > whole crash meta-event load-bearing instead of cosmetic-on-top-of-a-static-band.**
> >
> > **Verified shipped-state of every batch-18 item (grep/Read-confirmed):**
> > **(1) #160 interwar economic engine / Great Depression ‚Äî UNBUILT; EXTENDS E4c.**
> > `Era` is STILL the 4-value enum `independence|federalism|nationalism|modern`
> > (`types.ts:1337`) ‚Äî there is NO `progressive` value and no 1928 scenario. The
> > ONLY economy token is the **generic 7-step `economic` meter** (`meters.economic:
> > number`, `types.ts:1401`), whose UI band descriptors LITERALLY include
> > `'Depression'‚Ä¶'Severe Recession'‚Ä¶'Recession'‚Ä¶'Roaring'` (`Meter.tsx:15`) ‚Äî so the
> > tier NAMES the cascade needs exist as cosmetic strings, but there is **no logic**.
> > grep for `economicStability|greatDepression|econStab|requiresCrisis|requiresCrash`
> > across `src/` returns **ZERO** hits ‚Äî no Great-Depression meta-event, no
> > EconStab‚Üíindustry cascade, no `Bill.requiresCrisis`. The era-event runner that the
> > meta-event would bind to is `runPhase_2_4_3_Era` (`phaseRunners.ts:2796`); its
> > `triggersGameEnd` consumer is at `:2871` (the multi-decider (a)/(b) presidential
> > choice rides the same `EraEvent` decider surface). **Where the new surface binds
> > (all on TOP of `arkzag`'s E4c #116 `game.economy` machine):** (i) a
> > **Great-Depression META-event** = one era-event row carrying a multi-meter shock
> > bundle `{economic:‚àí4, revenue:‚àí2, quality:‚àí1, military:‚àí1, partyPref:‚àí3}` + the
> > (a)/(b) decider (NB: the ‚àí4 EXCEEDS the ¬ß21.8 ¬±3 swing cap ‚Äî a meta-event override
> > to flag); (ii) the **EconStab‚ÜíRecession cascade** = 2 random industries ‚àí1 in
> > EVERY state ‚Üí an **EV-reflow roll** (couples to the ¬ß11.5 industry-leadership +
> > ¬ß28.9 census/EV surfaces) + a DomStab-drop chance + **EconStab-in-Recession gates
> > other meters' gains**; (iii) **crisis-GATED bills** = a `Bill.requiresCrisis:
> > 'economic'` flag so Fed-currency-in-crisis / worker-bailout are INVALID until the
> > crash fires (binds E2's Bill-type taxonomy). **Size: M as an E4c extension** (the
> > meta-event + decider is S; the cascade is M; the crisis-gate flag is the SAME
> > `Bill.type`/crisis-bypass primitive E2 already builds). game-mechanics ¬ß29.7.1.
> > **(2) DH-67 crash-gated party-modifier ‚Äî UNBUILT; the CENTRAL #160 takeaway.**
> > The era's party-preference / realignment bias is a **static era-band constant,
> > DECOUPLED from the crash event** ‚Äî so the era's BLUE-favorable bias fired in the
> > 1930 midterms WITH A HEALTHY ECONOMY (the crash had not yet fired; the GOP losses
> > were legislation penalties, not a depression), and the GA's post-mortem (ch18 POST
> > 8) is that *"these era bonuses are SUPER STRONG‚Ä¶ maybe too large‚Ä¶ the Depression
> > wasn't nearly as bad."* **The fix (Umbrella, POST 545):** EVENT-GATE the era's
> > BLUE party-modifier bias to the Great-Depression event having fired ‚Äî not a static
> > band constant. **Where it binds:** the party-preference bias that feeds
> > `calcStateVote` (`phaseRunners.ts:3709-3711`, the `partyPref*5` term) must read a
> > `game.crashFired`-style flag set by the meta-event, instead of an unconditional
> > era-band value. **Size: S ‚Äî and the highest-value single fix in the batch** (it is
> > the switch that turns the meta-event from cosmetic into load-bearing). game-mechanics
> > ¬ß29.7.1(f). **Build it WITH #160 (E4c extension).**
> > **(3) Confirmation AUTO-PASS gate ‚Äî UNBUILT; folds into E16/E9-handler-9d; a REAL
> > fix for the ¬ß25.5 36%-pass pathology.** `runPhase_2_3_1_Cabinet`
> > (`phaseRunners.ts:2158-2223`) is a flat scored pick that goes straight to
> > `cabinet[seat] = pick.id` (`:2191`) + `addLog("confirmed as ${seat}")` (`:2198`) ‚Äî
> > **NO Senate vote, NO Controversial gate, NO skill threshold** (re-verified: the pick
> > body has no `vote`/`nay`/`Senate`/`Controversial`/`skill`-gate logic). The
> > `'Controversial'` trait DOES exist as a `Trait` union member (`types.ts:103`), so the
> > gate has the trait to read. **Ted's authoritative rule (RULED in-thread, POST
> > 213-214):** ALL nominees AUTO-CONFIRM **EXCEPT** {State / Treasury / AG / Defense}
> > OR Controversial OR <3 relevant skill (unless Integrity); a Senate-Majority-Leader
> > with Iron Fist can force a vote on any. This directly fixes the `drums` ¬ß25.5
> > pathology (only **36% of 88 nominees passed** ‚Äî the CPU confirmation was
> > over-rejecting) by ensuring **most picks NEVER REACH A VOTE.** **Where it binds:**
> > insert the auto-pass gate at the confirmation step inside `runPhase_2_3_1_Cabinet`
> > (the contested-pick path then routes through E9-handler-9d's CPU Senate vote).
> > **Size: S ‚Äî a Ted-authoritative ruling** (DESIGNER class, not a GA call). **Folds
> > into the E16 cabinet rework + E9 handler 9d** (the ¬ß25.5 confirmation work both
> > already scope). game-mechanics ¬ß25.5 + ¬ß30.9.
> > **(4) #161 era-band SIXTH start (1928) ‚Äî CONFIRMATION ONLY, no scope.** A 1928
> > start is the **6th independent start-year** confirmed for the K3/K4 era-content-band
> > model (after 1772 / 1800 / 1820 / 1856 / 1948 / 2000). The bands key off
> > `game.eraBand` + game-state/territory, NOT the calendar (the ¬ß27.1 / divergence-#18
> > finding), and the 1928 thread re-emits the same start-independent band labels +

> **‚òÖ‚òÖ Batch-16 changes to the plan (`hd1` / `c015a0cb` ‚Äî "A House Divided" PART 1,
> a SEPARATE EARLIER run from the Part-2 `hd` thread; an 1856-start, partly-HUMAN
> antebellum ‚Üí Civil War ‚Üí Reconstruction ‚Üí restart run; GM matthewyoung123, GAs

>
> > **‚òÖ Read this block if you only read one for batch 16. The headline: this run
> > REFRAMES DH-29 from a CPU-only artifact to a STRUCTURAL deadlock, and hands the

> > Reconstruction unwinnable." `hd1` is the FIRST run to play the
> > Civil-War‚ÜíReconstruction arc with HUMANS ON BOTH SIDES of the tug-of-war (a
> > human Southern-Dem wanting it short vs a human Radical-Republican wanting it
> > prolonged) ‚Äî and the marquee result (GM-stated, `POST 2678`) is that **even
> > with humans the Ironclad-vs-10% choice DEADLOCKED and states drifted back into
> > the Union with NO plan at all** (mutual filibuster ‚Üí null result). So the
> > blocker is NOT a CPU heuristic gap; it is STRUCTURAL (a both-chambers-must-agree
> > paralysis). vcczar's authoritative RESTART rewrite (`POST 2692-2694`) is the
> > fix: a **4-plan Reconstruction model (No-plan / 10% / Ironclad-Wade-Davis /
> > Military-district) available to BOTH the President AND Congress**, gated by a
> > single prerequisite ‚Äî **"there is a reconstruction plan adopted by Congress OR
> > by the President"** ‚Äî so the **President can adopt a plan UNILATERALLY**,
> > guaranteeing resolution in solo play AND in deadlocked-human play. This is the
> > #156 build target, and it FOLDS INTO the existing E3b Reconstruction epic as the
> > definition-of-done for its readmission half (¬ß9.1.6 ‚Äî which is hereby upgraded
> > from "author the rule, then build" to "build vcczar's authored rule"). This is
> > the **3rd Civil-War run** (after `hd` Part 2 and the all-CPU `drums`) and the
> > **5th antebellum source** ‚Äî HIGH corroboration confidence on the
> > war/Reconstruction/secession cluster. **It does NOT move the top of the queue:
> > QW0 ‚Üí K0/K2 ‚Üí K3/K4 + scenarioBoot ‚Üí E1 still lead** (these are subsystem-track
> > items on the already-placed E3/E3b epics).
> >
> > **Verified shipped-state of every batch-16 item (grep/Read-confirmed):**

> **‚òÖ Batch-14 changes to the plan (`gild1868` / `bf590684` ‚Äî the first dedicated

>
> > **‚òÖ Read this block if you only read one for batch 14. The big picture:

> > Reconstruction regime, AfAm enfranchisement, imperialism). But the Gilded Age is
> > UNBUILT ‚Äî the `Era` enum is `independence | federalism | nationalism | modern`
> > (`types.ts:1337`, NO `gilded`), only `scenario1772.ts` / `scenario1856.ts`
> > exist, and the era is hand-run on `modern` tuning (gap #41). The thread is
> > almost entirely the unbuilt design. It surfaces FIVE new deltas ‚Äî #147‚Äì#150 +
> > DH-63 ‚Äî that are ONE "gilded-era content" epic (¬ß9.1.10 below), and it
> > corroborates the standing Gilded cluster (#3/#5/#6/#21/#41/#57 + the convention/
> > amendment/SCOTUS/spending-cap/gov-action machinery) from a native start. **It
> > does NOT move the top of the queue: QW0 ‚Üí K0/K2 ‚Üí K3/K4 + scenarioBoot ‚Üí E1
> > (`scenario1788`) still lead.** Gilded content rides on top of K3/K4 (the
> > era-content-band model), the economic engine (#116 / E4c), the bill-relationship
> > graph (#42), and the Reconstruction epic (#57 / E3b ‚Äî which inherits the DH-29
> > solo-blocker). DH-63 folds into #42 + #147's MonetaryRegime; #148 EXTENDS #57,
> > it does not open a new epic. **3rd GM-burnout death** (`gild1868`, after
> > `new1772`/`dem1820`) ‚Äî cite for the automation-reduces-upkeep argument; do not
> > queue.**
> >
> > **‚òÖ POLARITY FLIP (load-bearing for any `gilded` scenario data):** by 1868 the
> > parties have INVERTED from the founding era ‚Äî **RED = Republicans** (Grant,
> > bloody shirt, Reconstruction, tariff/business, Stalwart/Half-Breed/Mugwump) and
> > **BLUE = Democrats** (Solid South, Tweed/Tammany, soft-money/Free-Silver),
> > `gild1868` POST 6. This is the OPPOSITE of the founding-era BLUE = Dem-Rep
> > frame, so a `scenario1868` `BootSheet` faction roster + nickname table is
> > red/blue-flipped vs `scenario1772`. The era-content-band model (K3/K4) must
> > carry party-label-by-era, not assume a fixed RED/BLUE‚Üîparty mapping.
> >
> > **Verified shipped-state (every item below is DESIGNED, not built):**
> > **(1)** `Era = 'independence' | 'federalism' | 'nationalism' | 'modern'`
> > (`types.ts:1337`) ‚Äî **NO `gilded` value**; only `scenario1772.ts` /
> > `scenario1856.ts` exist (no Gilded/1868 scenario). **(2)** the tariff is a
> > **¬±0.5 meter-nudging flavor bill** ‚Äî `BILL_TEMPLATES` "Tariff Increase"
> > `meters: { revenue: 1, economic: -0.5 }` / "Tariff Reduction"
> > `{ revenue: -0.5, economic: 0.5 }` (`phaseRunners.ts:3421-3422`) ‚Äî there is **NO
> > `game.tariffRate` national integer and NO `MonetaryRegime` enum**. **(3)** there
> > is **NO Reconstruction subsystem** in code (#57 is the readmission/war-victory
> > SPEC; the KILL exists but `vacateOffice` just nulls the office,
> > `phaseRunners.ts:2446`) ‚Äî no auto-expiry timer, no appoint-by-Speaker/PPT,
> > no Solid-South bias-sunset. **(4)** there is **NO civil-service / merit-vs-spoils
> > axis** (no merit-reform bill, no State-Gov-Jobs spoils lever). **(5)** there is
> > **NO special-election branch** (every state resolves via `calcStateVote`,
> > `phaseRunners.ts:3752`). **(6)** currency/regime bills have **NO exclusivity
> > guard** ‚Äî `Legislation` (`types.ts:1506`) has no `type`/`replaces` field, so two
> > contradictory currency regimes can hold at once (DH-63).
> >
> > **The load-bearing build items this batch sizes (one epic; each binds in code as
> > noted) ‚Äî see ¬ß9.1.10 for the dependency table:**

> >    flipped, 10 factions / 2 parties, `gild1868` POST 6), the Gilded nickname

> >    (¬ß9.1.9). **L** (a whole era's content + a scenario boot).
> > 2. **#147 tariff-as-%-rate + the mutually-exclusive `MonetaryRegime`.** Add

> >    (begins 1864, auto-ends ~1884), appoint seceded-state Gov/Sen/Rep seats by
