> **‚òÖ‚òÖ‚òÖ Batch-13 changes to the plan (`oopscpu` ‚Äî Ted-run all-CPU 1788 stress-test;
> the K5/E9 VALIDATION source; designer-authoritative, same class as `tedchange`.
> NO new keystone, NO re-sequence ‚Äî `oopscpu` DE-RISKS the existing E9 plan):**
>
> > **‚òÖ Read this block if you only read one for batch 13. The big picture:
> > `oopscpu` is the first batch that systematically validates a whole subsystem's
> > spec (the K5 CpuController + the E9 ¬ß25 handler suite) BEFORE it is built. Ted
> > ran every faction on the CPU rules and stopped, live, at each spot where the
> > rules are vague, contradictory, or impossible without human judgment. The
> > result: it does NOT change the K5/E9 build ORDER, but it turns the E9 handler
> > specs #70/#72/#73/#74/#75/#76 from "designed" into "designed + field-validated
> > with concrete failure modes + Ted's fixes." Higher build-confidence; lower
> > spec-risk. The OC-1‚Ä¶OC-8 sub-gaps fold into the relevant handler rows as
> > sub-rules (the handler-order table in ¬ß6.6.1 is updated below). Two NEW
> > prerequisites for ANY 1788 boot ‚Äî DH-61 (boot-seed active wars) + DH-62 (pre-12A
> > EC mode) ‚Äî must land WITH `scenario1788` (E1). Two NEW open design calls ‚Äî
> > OC-4 (off-ideology draft gate) + OC-8 (define "office") ‚Äî go to the
> > designer-gated parking bucket.**
> >
> > **Verified shipped-state of the load-bearing CPU items (re-grounded for this
> > batch):** The engine has CPU code ONLY for the meta-passes ‚Äî draft auto-pick
> > (`pickBestForFaction` `phaseRunners.ts:33-53`; pick loop `:241-262`), relocation
> > (`RELOCATION_ODDS.cpuGate`), ideology shifts (`IDEOLOGY_SHIFT_ODDS.cpu`),
> > conversions (`CONVERSION_ODDS.cpu`), kingmaker auto-assign, cabinet auto-fill,
> > era-event `pickAIResponse` (`eraGraph.ts:88-103`), and CC consensus
> > (`autoFillCPUVotes`, `constitutionalConvention.ts:81`). **There is NO
> > leadership-IRV / legislation-vote / event-vote / SCOTUS-vote handler.** Spot-
> > verified the load-bearing ones: **(1) cabinet auto-fill** (`runPhase_2_3_1_Cabinet`,
> > `phaseRunners.ts:2158`) is a **flat highest-score pick** (`scoreCabinetCandidate`
> > = admin¬∑w + governing¬∑w + expertise bonus) ‚Üí seats immediately with `cabinet[seat]
> > = pick.id` ‚Äî **NO Senate vote, NO ladder/refuse-roll, NO scandal-guard, NO
> > firing-precedent gate**; **(2) leadership** (`runPhase_2_2_1_CongressLeadership`,
> > `:1844`) computes `senateMajority`/`houseMajority` INLINE TWICE (`:1863-1864`,
> > `blue >= red`) and picks Speaker/PPT as the top-PV member of that majority party ‚Äî
> > **NO IRV bloc-vote, NO closest-ideology cross-party rule, NO single shared
> > "chamber control" definition** (the OC-2 collision surface); **(3) legislation**:
> > no per-CPU NAY/AYE/NAY pass exists on the floor at all (the only `autoFillCPUVotes`
> > is the CC path); **(4) `loseCommand`** (`abilities.ts:15`) is called ONLY at
> > `phaseRunners.ts:2410` (old-age/Anytime loss) and `:2709` (event effect) ‚Äî **NO
> > post-election Command decay** (note: the battle/defeat penalty at
> > `revolutionaryWar.ts:137` uses `loseSkill`, not `loseCommand`); **(5) succession**:
> > `vacateOffice` (`:2446`) just sets `presidentId = null` on death ‚Äî **NO VP-inherits
> > branch, NO acting state, NO leadership re-run**; **(6) `scenario1788` does not
> > exist** (only `scenario1772` / `scenario1856`), and no boot seeds active wars except
> > the 1772 Rev War; `calcStateVote:3711` still carries the raw-`Math.random` jitter
> > (debt #1).**
> >
> > **The load-bearing build items this batch sizes (each binds in code as noted):**
> > 1. **OC-3 crisis-vote gate (‚òÖ BALANCE-CRITICAL).** Maps to the **legislation-vote
> >    handler (¬ß25.6 / E9 handler 9b / #74)**. Without it, CPUs vote AYE on crisis
> >    bills that hurt their own ideology/lobby cards (the all-CPU run abolished
> >    slavery peacefully by 1792, no secession, `oopscpu#POST 162-180`). Two sub-checks:
> >    an **ideology-floor gate** (don't AYE a crisis bill that costs your own cards)
> >    + a **secession/slavery-active check** before an Abolish-Slavery crisis bill
> >    can pass. **S** (two predicates on the handler the epic already builds); the
> >    exact weighting is a design dial. **This is the single highest-priority sub-rule
> >    inside handler 9b.**
> > 2. **#143 post-election Command-decay pass.** NEW, RULED, standalone. After every
> >    presidential cycle close, every living pol NOT on a Pres/VP ticket has a **40%
> >    chance of ‚àí1 Command** ("shit or get off the pot", `oopscpu#POST 1, 224`).
> >    Binds in **`runPhase_2_10_End` (`phaseRunners.ts:4171`), gated on
> >    `isPresidentialYear`, after the 2.9.4 ticket roster is known**; applies via the
> >    existing `loseCommand` (`abilities.ts:15`). **XS** ‚Äî one new pass, one roll,
> >    needs the ticket roster from the election phase. Pairs with #85/#130
> >    death-schedule discipline (same `runPhase_2_10_End` site).
> > 3. **#61 death-succession branch.** Reconciled spec (game-mechanics ¬ß24.1 +
> >    ¬ß29.9): a Presidential **DEATH** ‚Üí VP becomes **FULL President** (refuses
> >    "Acting", **no action-divert roll**), is **NOT auto party/faction leader** ‚Üí
> >    **re-run the leadership IRV**. This is **distinct from** the incapacity /
> >    0-Command / ineligible-substitute case, which keeps the `arkzag`
> >    acting-president + action-divert read. **Extends E10b** (already re-scoped in
> >    batch 11 for the kill‚Üísucceed‚Üífill chain) ‚Äî `oopscpu` supplies the reconciled
> >    death-branch spec. Binds at `vacateOffice`/the death path
> >    (`phaseRunners.ts:2446`) + the leadership runner (`:1844`). **S** within E10b.
> > 4. **OC-2 single "chamber control" definition.** Maps to **¬ß25.3 / E9 handler 9c
> >    (#70)**. The marquee collision bug: closest-ideology leadership can hand chairs
> >    to the minority party, then the "‚â•60%-chamber ‚Üí may propose" gate reads chamber
> >    control differently ‚Üí minority chairs propose under the majority's threshold
> >    (`oopscpu#POST 151`). Fix: **ONE canonical `chamberControl(snap, chamber)`
> >    helper** shared by leadership-select AND the proposer gate. The inline
> >    `senateMajority`/`houseMajority` duplication at `:1863-1864` is exactly the
> >    surface to consolidate. **S** (one helper + two call-site migrations).
> > 5. **OC-1 scandal-resignee re-appointment cooldown + OC-5 court-as-firing gate.**
> >    Both map to the **cabinet/appointment handler (¬ß25.5.4 / E9 handler 9d / #73)**.
> >    OC-1 (`oopscpu#POST 65`): a scandal-resignee is immediately re-appointed to
> >    another appointed office ‚Äî the CPU has no scandal-smoother memory. It is the
> >    concrete instance of **DH-22 / ¬ß25.15.3** and reads the K5-introduced
> >    `recentScandalIds?` state. OC-5 (`oopscpu#POST 184-187`): the CPU dumps
> >    un-fireable cabinet members onto SCOTUS to evade the firing-precedent rule ‚Äî gate
> >    a cabinet-vacating SCOTUS appointment behind the firing-precedent gate (¬ß21.4).
> >    **XS each** within handler 9d; OC-1 needs the scandal-cooldown persistent field.
> > 6. **Pre-12A election mode (DH-62) + boot-seed active wars (DH-61) ‚Äî BOTH 1788-boot
> >    prerequisites.** DH-62: a pre-12A two-votes-per-state / no-ticket EC mode (top
> >    EV = Pres, runner-up = VP, no separate VP ballot) + same-state-EV exclusion
> >    (`oopscpu#POST 197`) + the throwaway-tie defense (the ¬ß25.1 pre-12A nomination
> >    trio incl. VP retention). An **era-keyed election-mode variant** for
> >    1788/1792/1796 ‚Äî a genuinely new resolution branch alongside the per-state EC
> >    method (#5 / divergence #20) and the `electorsByLegislature` flag. DH-61: the
> >    `scenarioBoot` pipeline must read a start-year **active-wars table** and
> >    instantiate each historically-running war (1788 ‚Üí Northwest Indian War, 20%-loss
> >    / WS ‚àí2) ‚Äî they boot at peace today except the 1772 Rev War. **Both gate ANY
> >    `scenario1788`**: DH-62 is **M** (new EC resolution branch; lands with E1 / the
> >    per-state-EC epic #4); DH-61 is **S** (a `BootSheet.activeWars` field + a boot
> >    hook in `scenarioBoot`, lands in K4 / the boot pipeline with E1).
> >
> > **OPEN design calls (NOT ready-to-build ‚Äî designer-gated):** **OC-4** (off-ideology
> > CPU draft gate ‚Äî Ted wants "a better third way" than draft-strong-pol-off-ideology
> > vs. uncontested; `oopscpu#POST 227-228, 234`) and **OC-8** (define "office" for the
> > Scandalous-Non-Office-Holder forced-out event + rewrite the event text; flagged to
> > `@vcczar`, `oopscpu#POST 334, 336`). Add both to the **designer-gated** parking
> > sub-bucket (alongside the 9 open `tedchange` items). The all-CPU run also RESOLVES
> > **#52 for the all-CPU case** (CPU SCOTUS by ideology-distance) ‚Äî the player-vs-CPU
> > fork stays user-gated for human games ‚Äî and **could NOT reach the convention CPU
> > (#71)** because 1788 predates conventions: a **post-12A all-CPU run would validate
> > #71** the same way `oopscpu` validated #70/#72/#73/#74. **Recommend the
> > all-CPU-test as a repeatable spec-validation methodology** (see ¬ß9.1.3).

> **‚òÖ‚òÖ‚òÖ‚òÖ‚òÖ Batch-12 changes to the plan (`tedchange` + `smallbugs` ‚Äî TWO DISCUSSION
> THREADS, not playtests; the DESIGNER's official rulings channel. NO new keystone,
> NO re-sequence of the keystones; ‚òÖ THE AUTHORITY HIERARCHY IS NOW EXPLICIT
> (Ted > GA > inference), QW0 CLOSES, ONE major rework lands (cabinet‚Üíenthusiasm
> #124), 19 sized Ted-rulings slot into existing epics + an ~100-item dataset pass.):**
>
> > **‚òÖ Read this block first if you only read one. The big picture: `tedchange` is
> > the OFFICIAL designer rules-doc cleanup channel ‚Äî its rulings SUPERSEDE prior GA
> > calls where they conflict. Most rulings are XS‚ÄìS code/constant changes that bind
> > at known sites; the **cabinet enthusiasm rework #124 is the one M-sized re-architecture**
> > (a teardown of the lobby‚Üíenthusiasm path); the **Lingering 7-step ordering #134**
> > is M as a re-spec of the meter-decay/volatility carry-forward path. **QW0
> > relocation-cap=4 is now SETTLED** (`smallbugs#POST 734-735`, vcczar 12-30-25
> > approved) ‚Äî promote it from "open-design / build" to "ready-to-build with
> > const=4" and ship FIRST. **The amendments-NOT-SCOTUS-challengeable ruling**
> > simplifies the E5‚ÜîE25 interaction (Govs can't challenge amendments ‚Üí drop the
> > ¬ß21.3 SCOTUS-overturns-amendment branch from E25's docket scope, keeping only
> > the strike-a-statute / mutable-threshold faces). **The ~100-item `smallbugs`
> > dataset umbrella (#120) is ONE coordinated `scripts/seedDataset.mjs` pass**,
> > not 100 backlog rows. Add a **NEW Decision-gated sub-bucket "designer-gated"**
> > (the 9 open `tedchange` items are gated on Ted/vcczar, not the user).**
>
> 1. **‚òÖ‚òÖ‚òÖ QW0 relocation-cap CLOSES ‚Äî promote it to top-of-queue with const=4.**
>    Verified shipped: `RELOCATION_ATTEMPTS_PER_TURN = 5` at `src/types.ts:247`.
>    `smallbugs#POST 734-735` settled the cap on **12-30-25** ("Approved by vczar"):
>    *"A faction is limited to FOUR total attempted moves per half-term. A politician
>    that moves to an ALT-STATE does NOT count against the FOUR total moves."* This
>    closes the long-running BUG-0 / QW0 / batch-10 top-of-queue item from
>    "open-design + ready-to-build" to **fully settled and ready-to-ship**. The fix
>    is **a one-line constant edit** + a guard at the relocation accumulator so
>    alt-state moves don't decrement the budget (verify the alt-state accounting in
>    `runPhase_2_1_4_Relocations`). **Recommendation: this is now the cheapest +
>    safest win in the whole roadmap; do it FIRST.** No human review-gate; no
>    designer-gate; the cap value is authoritative.
> 2. **‚òÖ‚òÖ‚òÖ The cabinet ‚Üí enthusiasm REWORK (#124) is the one M-sized teardown
>    this batch ‚Äî strategy call required.** Verified shipped: the cabinet
>    confirmation step doesn't yet exist (`runPhase_2_3_1_Cabinet`,
>    `phaseRunners.ts:2158-2223`, is a one-shot scored pick with no Senate vote
>    and no enthusiasm side-effect at all). Ted's rework (`tedchange#POST 1-4`)
>    **fundamentally re-architects the lobby‚Üíenthusiasm path** that was already
>    on the roadmap as debt #17 / DH-23 / E16 cabinet-confirmation:
>    - **(a) LOBBY satisfaction now gives bonus POINTS** to Pres + factions with
>      matching lobby cards (NOT enthusiasm ‚Äî moves the cabinet-lobby coupling
>      from the `enthusiasm` table to the per-faction `score?` ledger).
>    - **(b) IDEOLOGY COMPOSITION drives ENTHUSIASM** ‚Äî ‚â•50% cabinet of an
>      ideology = +enth that ideology; ‚â§20% representation = ‚àíenth.
>    - **(c) 3-shifts-per-half-term cap holds.**
>    - **(d) Big-4 / rest-of-cabinet / cabinet-level potentially weighted
>      differently** ‚Äî the actual percentages are **DESIGNER-GATED OPEN** (Ted
>      RULED IN CONCEPT but the numbers are TBD).
>
>    Engineering call: **this is a CPU-action with significant state-write changes
>    + a known designer-open numeric**. **Land it AFTER K2 (ActionRegistry) +
>    AFTER K5 (CpuController)** ‚Äî cabinet picks are CPU actions, and the rework
>    requires the conditional-vote-rules primitive (`pop` POST 1111) +
>    the lobby‚Üíscore path that K2's action registry mediates. **It lands in E16
>    (cabinet retention refactor + dynamic seat list)** as a sibling beat ‚Äî the
>    existing E16 row already plans the cabinet teardown; #124 changes WHAT the
>    cabinet writes (POINTS to factions + IDEOLOGY-COMPOSITION-driven enthusiasm,
>    not the current lobby-card-driven enthusiasm). **Re-scope E16 from "add
>    confirmation system in the right shape" to "BUILD the confirmation +
>    enthusiasm rework together in the right shape from day one"**, because
>    building the today-shape confirmation only to tear it apart for #124 is
>    waste. Size: M (was XS-S). **The numeric percentages stay designer-gated
>    until Ted closes them** ‚Äî ship a const table that can be re-tuned post-design.
> 3. **‚òÖ‚òÖ The Lingering 7-step strict ordering + tax/tariff carry-forward (#134)
>    is a re-spec of the meter-decay path.** Verified shipped: `runPhase_2_5_1_Lingering`
>    (`phaseRunners.ts:3260-3377`) runs cabinet-drift-driven meter writes
>    plus per-trait modulation + national-debt update ‚Äî but it does NOT have an
>    explicit 7-step ordering and has NO tax/tariff volatility-vs-decay
>    distinction. Ted's `tedchange#POST 397-408` rules **strict 1‚Üí7 ordering with
>    NEVER re-doing a step + volatility = THIS-phase-only + tax/tariff decay
>    propagates forward to NEXT phase's step 3 (carry-forward across half-terms).**
>    Size: M as a re-spec of the meter-decay/volatility path (depends on the
>    meter-bank work / E6 named-ordinal meter model). **Re-scope E6/Phase-1 #6
>    (meter-model generalization + APOCALYPSE) to also carry the Lingering
>    step-order + carry-forward semantics** ‚Äî same surface (Lingering is where
>    meters get written), and the step-order is the discipline the bank model
>    needs.
> 4. **‚òÖ Pres implementation 2-step Admin-then-Command blunder rule (#126) is now
>    the CANONICAL 5-tier wording.** Verified shipped: no Pres-implementation
>    path exists as a discrete code site ‚Äî bill effects apply directly via
>    `applyEffect` (`phaseRunners.ts:3209`) with no Pres roll, no Admin step, no
>    blunder. Ted's `tedchange#POST 159-164` publishes the AUTHORITATIVE 5-tier
>    Command-modifies-blunder table that **supersedes the cf82a7d3 ¬ß5a #3 fuzzy
>    "hybrid" wording**:
>    - **Step 1:** Pres rolls Admin for impl (same as cabinet).
>    - **Step 2 (Blunder check):** Pres Command modifies the blunder ‚Äî Cmd 5
>      avoids; Cmd 4 = 50% avoid; Cmd 3 = +1 to blunder; Cmd 2 = 50% +1; Cmd 1 =
>      normal; no Cmd / no expertise = ‚àí2 (unless Efficient on impl team);
>      Incompetent = ‚àí3; Easily Overwhelmed **skips step 2 entirely**.
>
>    Where it binds: **the Pres-implements-bill code in the legislative-depth
>    epic (Phase-2 #29 / `runPhase_2_6_*` extension)**, NOT in `applyEffect`
>    itself (which stays the post-roll write). Size: S ‚Äî a roll-table + a
>    trait-gate; the trait readers exist. **Lands in E29 (modern legislative
>    depth) but the rule is now READY** (was OPEN as cf82a7d3 ¬ß5a #3).
> 5. **‚òÖ Death/retirement schedule (#130) ‚Äî REPLACES the current era-mult
>    schedule.** Verified shipped: `MORTALITY_RULES` (`src/types.ts:485-516`)
>    has 4-bracket death + 3-bracket retire + 4-era-keyed `eraConfig` with
>    `deathMult` + `retireMult` ‚Äî close in spirit but NOT identical to Ted's
>    schedule. Ted's `tedchange#POST 89-100, 137-148, 195-197, 396` rules:
>    - **Hale = 1/2 death chance** (RESTORED). Verified: `Hale` is NOT in the
>      `Trait` union today ‚Äî needs adding (parking lot or PR-with-the-schedule).
>    - **Frail rolled FIRST in death roll; retirement rolls oldest-to-youngest
>      (no Frail priority).** Verified: today the death loop walks
>      `snap.politicians` in array order (`phaseRunners.ts:2358`) ‚Äî order-aware
>      refactor needed.
>    - **Retired ex-Presidents ONLY roll for DEATH, not retirement.** Closes
>      the "John Adams immortal" loop.
>    - **Cabinet members retire at END of half-term, not on appointment.**
>    - **5%/half-term retire-death + era-scaled %s** (corroborates cf82a7d3 #4 /
>      Orange's formula).
>    - **Auto-retire at 100** (already in 2.10, confirmed pre-existing).
>
>    Where it binds: **`runPhase_2_4_1_Deaths` (`phaseRunners.ts:2341-2444`) +
>    `MORTALITY_RULES`.** Size: S ‚Äî rules-const refinement + order-aware loop
>    refactor + Hale trait addition. **Lands as a coordinated PR with the #85
>    5%/half-term retire/death rate** (already queued under Phase-1 #19). The
>    Hale-half-rate + Frail-first ordering + ex-Pres-only-death are net-new
>    discipline; the era-scaled percentages refine existing `eraConfig`.
> 6. **‚òÖ Draft re-rules (#136 / #137 / #138) ‚Äî small XS each, fold into the
>    draft path.** Verified shipped: `runPhase_2_1_1_Draft` (`phaseRunners.ts:
>    107-267`) uses raw `Math.random` for random-skill picking (`:187-197`)
>    AND random-skill-boost (`:196`) ‚Äî both can roll any of the 6 skills incl.
>    Command. Ted's RULED draft rules (`tedchange#POST 7-10, 47-50`):
>    - **Random skill from 6, NO Command** (#136). Restrict the skill pool at
>      `:196` to exclude `'command'` from the boost. (Note: Command isn't in
>      the 6 base skills today ‚Äî it's a separate `command` field on
>      `Politician`. Verify whether the boost was at one point Command-eligible
>      via the dataset / a separate path; if not, this is a no-op verification.)
>    - **No cross-party draft** (#137). Pols enter at IRL party at age 25; flip
>      via 2.1.6 only. Today's CPU pick (`pickBestForFaction`, `:33-53`) picks
>      by faction-personality-fit, not by historical party. Add a draft-time
>      assignment gate that pins a rookie's `partyId` to the dataset's IRL
>      party (and excludes cross-party drafting in the picker).
>    - **3 random traits + 3 random alt-states per draft** (#138 ‚Äî SUPERSEDES
>      5/5). This is the rookie-grant pair that was already partly captured by
>      #69 (1856-native re-rule). Goes in the era-config table (¬ß6.1 pattern).
>      Size: XS each.
> 7. **‚òÖ Conversion / ideology-shift rules (#127) ‚Äî refine existing tables.**
>    Verified shipped: `CONVERSION_ODDS` (`src/types.ts:268-291`) has poach
>    matrix `same: { notInOffice: 0.2, inOffice: 0.05 }` and `cross:
>    { notInOffice: 0.1, inOffice: 0.02 }` ‚Äî **the 33% party-flip Ted RULED
>    (`tedchange#POST 34-39`) is NOT a single constant**; it's the *target
>    success rate* after the willingness-multipliers stack. The shipped
>    `cross` rate of 0.1-0.02 base * willingness amplifiers is *roughly*
>    aligned, but the **same-party adjacency rule (`tedchange#POST 38, 53` ‚Äî
>    same-OR-adjacent ideology eligible for same-party conversion)** is the
>    behavioral change that binds:
>    - **Adjacency** binds at the conversion-target eligibility filter
>      (`phaseRunners.ts:993-1003` distance check); migrate to the new
>      `ideologyDistance(a,b,circular)` helper (#99/¬ß9.1.7) so the wrap +
>      adjacency unify.
>    - **LW‚ÜîRW Pop 25% cross-circle shift + Two-Faced** (`tedchange#POST 24,
>      28-29, 51`) is the IDEOLOGY-AS-CIRCLE rule (#99/¬ß9.1.7) at 25% rather
>      than 50% with auto-Two-Faced trait grant.
>
>    Size: S total ‚Äî adjacency is a one-site filter change; the LW‚ÜîRW Pop rate
>    + Two-Faced grant rides #99's circle helper + a special-case shift table.
>    **Folds into the #99 ideology-circle helper PR (Phase-1 #5b).**
> 8. **‚òÖ Kingmaker / Master Kingmaker scope (#128) ‚Äî pin the +1 binding sites.**
>    Verified shipped: the Kingmaker bonus is **NOT YET a state-vote bonus** ‚Äî
>    `calcStateVote` (`phaseRunners.ts:3685-3722`) does NOT consult Kingmaker
>    state to add a per-state +1. The prot√©g√© chain exists (`KINGMAKER_RULES`
>    `src/types.ts:295-307`), but the +1-in-state / +1-everywhere bonus is
>    DESIGNED-not-shipped. Ted's `tedchange#POST 316`:
>    - **Basic Kingmaker = +1 in own state ONLY** (incl. state's Pres primary
>      + state's general).
>    - **Master Kingmaker = +1 in EVERY state** (all Pres primaries + all
>      generals).
>    - **SUPERSEDES** Matt's "state OR national, pick one" reading.
>
>    Where it binds: **`calcStateVote` (`phaseRunners.ts:3711` adjust the
>    scoring sum to include `kingmakerBonus(snap, c, stateId)`)** + a new helper
>    `kingmakerBonus(snap, candidate, stateId): number` that returns +1 if any
>    Kingmaker in the candidate's faction is from the same state (basic) or
>    anywhere (Master). Size: S. **Pin the bonus binding site in the
>    election-math epic (Phase-1 #20 bill-scoring leaderboard / Phase-1 #6
>    meter-model where state-vote modifiers live).**
> 9. **‚òÖ 50/50 House split (#135) ‚Äî XS, inverse-control rule.** Verified shipped:
>    `runPhase_2_2_1_CongressLeadership` (`phaseRunners.ts:1844-1889`) computes
>    `senateMajority` and `houseMajority` as `BLUE if blue>=red else RED`
>    (`:1863-1864`), which is **wrong for 50/50** ‚Äî today the tie defaults to
>    BLUE silently. Ted's `tedchange#POST 65`: **a 50/50 House ‚Üí leadership
>    goes to the party NOT in control of the Senate** (inverse-control rule).
>    Where it binds: **`phaseRunners.ts:1864`** ‚Äî replace `houseMajority`
>    with: `houseMajority: PartyId = houseBlue === houseRed ? (senateMajority
>    === 'BLUE' ? 'RED' : 'BLUE') : (houseBlue > houseRed ? 'BLUE' : 'RED')`.
>    Size: XS one-line edit. **Ship with QW0 + the small consistency PRs**
>    (Phase-1 #19).
> 10. **‚òÖ CPU Chief Justice selection ladder (#142) ‚Äî XS, replaces the coin-flip
>     court today.** Verified shipped: `runPhase_2_5_3_Court` (`phaseRunners.ts:
>     3397-3415`) is a `chance(0.5)` coin-flip on 4 hardcoded titles +
>     conserv-vs-liberal ideology majority ‚Üí ¬±0.1 `partyPreference`. **No CJ
>     selection logic at all today.** Ted's `tedchange#POST 387-390` rules
>     the explicit CPU CJ ladder: **highest Judicial ability from their party
>     ‚Üí multi-faction tie: own faction ‚Üí Pres-ideology match ‚Üí lowest-scoring
>     faction ‚Üí multi-candidate tie: matching-appointer-ideology ‚Üí random.**
>     Where it binds: **the SCOTUS docket epic (Phase-2 #25 / E25)** where the
>     CJ selection lives. Size: XS as the ladder spec is fully authored.
>     **Sharpens debt #18 (SCOTUS as a stub) + the #52 player-SCOTUS fork** ‚Äî
>     the CJ ladder is CPU-side regardless of which fork wins.
> 11. **‚òÖ Amendments NOT SCOTUS-challengeable (`smallbugs` POST 250-269) ‚Äî

>     `smallbugs#POST 250-269` ruling: **Govs CANNOT challenge amendments via

> 12. **‚òÖ 1st / 2nd Continental Congress composition (#133) ‚Äî rewrites the
>     CC composition rules.** Verified shipped: `continentalCongress.ts`

>     NOT in code today** (the firstCC builder uses faction-by-pol-count, not
>     the size table). Ted's `tedchange#POST 211, 217-236`:
>     - **Big states (PA/MA/VA/MD) = 4 delegates** each.
>     - **Medium states = 3** each.
>     - **Small states (GA/RI/DE/NH) = 2** each.
>     - **1st CC (pre-DoI):** faction with most pols in a state picks delegates.
>     - **2nd CC (post-DoI):** Gov picks delegates.
>     - **Articles of Confederation:** prohibit consecutive election + require
>       2/3 of states for legislation + UNANIMOUS for amendments.
>     - **PMG appointment goes through Domestic Committee in CC era**
>       (`tedchange#POST 352-355`).
>
>     Size: S (data table + builder rewrite + Articles-of-Confederation gating
>     rule). **Lands in the existing Continental Congress era system

> 18. **‚òÖ NEW Decision-gated SUB-BUCKET: designer-gated.** Batch 12 makes the

> **‚òÖ‚òÖ‚òÖ‚òÖ Batch-11 changes to the plan (`arkzag`, the 1820‚Üí1840 FULL-ARC
> continuation of `dem1820` ‚Äî the FIRST capture of the late-game systems. NO new
> keystone, NO re-sequence of the keystones; THREE new content systems that EXTEND
> existing epics, ONE fork RESOLVED + promoted, ONE NEW fork to gate, and two sized
> bug-fixes. This batch is the planner's grounding for where the late-game content
> lands.):**
>
> > **‚òÖ Read this block first if you only read one. The three new content systems
> > (#116, #119, #61) are EXTENSIONS of already-planned epics, not new epics ‚Äî
> > re-scope E4b/E5/E10b, do not add E-new rows. The #51 enthusiasm engine is now
> > SETTLED (promote the reshuffle + ¬±3 cap to ready-to-build; keep ONLY the #18
> > state-scope behind the human gate). One NEW fork (delegate-class) gates the
> > convention loop. Two sized bugs (DH-59 XS, DH-60 S).**
>
> 1. **‚òÖ The three new late-game CONTENT SYSTEMS all EXTEND existing epics ‚Äî re-scope,
>    do not create new epics.** Verified vs shipped: NONE of these state shapes exist
>    in `types.ts` today (grep-confirmed ‚Äî `GameState` has `nationalDebt`,
>    `meters.economic`, `isSlaveState`, but no `amendments`, no `economy`/`secondBank`,
>    no `actingPresident`/`successionOrder`; `Legislation` has no `type`/`replaces`/
>    `lockedUntilYear`). Where each lands:
>    - **#116 Bank-War ‚Üí Independent-Treasury economic engine ‚Üí a NEW item that sits
>      ON TOP of E4b(b) + E2 + E6.** This is the one genuinely-new *engine* this batch,
>      but it is **not a standalone epic** ‚Äî it is the long-run **content state machine**
>      that drives the **¬ß27.6 Second-Bank institution object already scoped in E4b(b)**
>      (the President-of-US-Bank seat + 20-yr recharter clock + Bank-War "Remove
>      Deposits" exec action). It needs: (a) **E2** (`Bill.type` crisis tag + crisis
>      bypass ‚Äî the Bank/Treasury bills are CRISIS bills); (b) **E6** (the named EconStab
>      meter + crisis entry/exit + cascade ‚Äî EconStab IS `meters.economic`; the
>      Panic-of-1837 CRISIS state is an E6 band-entry); (c) **E4b(b)** (the Bank
>      institution it recharters/replaces). NET-NEW fields: a `game.economy?` state
>      machine, **`Bill.replaces?`** (Independent-Treasury removes the Bank), and a
>      **`Bill.lockedUntilYear?`** per-bill-class change cooldown (the "no tariff until
>      1836" cadence). **Recommendation: add it as a small epic `E4c` (or an E4b(d)
>      sub-PR) that lands AFTER E2 + E6 + E4b(b).** Open design Q (carry it as a
>      decision, not a blocker): is the Bank‚ÜíTreasury arc a **scripted branch** or
>      **emergent** from recurring EconStab-crisis legislation? The save played it
>      **emergent** ‚Äî so build it emergent (recurring CRISIS bills that resolve an
>      EconStab CRISIS), which falls out of E2+E6+E4b(b) with the smallest new surface.
>    - **#119 amendment lifecycle ‚Üí RE-SCOPE E5.** Verified: no `amendments` field
>      today. E5 already specs `amendments?: { id; passedYear; data? }[]` + governor-
>      ratify (¬ß24.4) + the *Pollock*‚Üíincome-tax bill-class-gate hook. #119 adds two
>      things E5's row does not yet fully carry and **must be folded into E5's scope**:
>      (1) an **explicit lifecycle state** (`proposed ‚Üí inCommittee ‚Üí onFloor ‚Üí
>      pendingRatification ‚Üí active`), and (2) an **active-amendment ‚Üí blocks-a-whole-
>      legislation-class** effect (excise-tax ban, suffrage class) checked **at proposal
>      time** ‚Äî the *proactive* face of the *Pollock* gate (which today is framed only
>      as a repeal/until-ratified constraint on ONE bill class). Plus the **un-bundleable**
>      flag. **No new epic ‚Äî widen E5's field + add the block-hook + the un-bundleable
>      rule.** E5 stays `ready`; its dependency on E25 (the SCOTUS bill-class-gate hook)
>      is unchanged.
>    - **#61 succession/acting-president chain ‚Üí RE-SCOPE E10b (it currently scopes only
>      the leaf).** Verified end-to-end: the KILL already happens (`assassination-killed`
>      anytime event, `anytimeEvents.ts:232`, fires `{ kind: 'death' }`) but death only
>      sets `presidentId = null` (`vacateOffice`, `phaseRunners.ts:2449`) ‚Äî there is NO
>      succession at all. E10b today scopes `successionOrder?`/`bornForeign?`/
>      `actingPresident?` (the *0-Command-inert* acting state from `hd`). #61 here adds
>      the **rest of the chain**: assassination/death-Evo ‚Üí **VP succeeds** ‚Üí **must fill
>      the VP vacancy** (gated on the ¬ß29.8 VP-Vacancy amendment being `active` ‚Äî so this
>      is **dependent on E5**) ‚Üí **acting-president ACTION-DIVERT roll** keyed to traits
>      (Easily Overwhelmed ‚Üí 50% the VP acts in his stead), layered on top of E10b's
>      Command-gates-actions rule ‚Üí + the **trait-acquisition side-effect** (overwhelmed
>      successor GAINS Easily Overwhelmed + Pliable). **No new epic ‚Äî widen E10b to the
>      full chain.** Note: the kill TRIGGER is the cheap part (it ships); the
>      **succession ENGINE + acting-divert** is the work. E10b's existing parking-lot
>      caveat (DH-54: impeachment/succession ruleset never authored) still applies to the
>      *line-of-succession/impeachment* half ‚Äî but the **VP-succeeds-on-death + acting-
>      divert** path here is ready to spec (the rule is now documented end-to-end, ¬ß29.9).
> 2. **‚òÖ‚òÖ #51 enthusiasm engine is RESOLVED ‚Äî PROMOTE the reshuffle + the ¬±3 cap to
>    ready-to-build; keep ONLY #18 behind the human gate.** Batch 10 left this a
>    three-way fork (Ted "every state unless penalized" vs V "ideology-leaning only"
>    vs Matt "primaries only"). **`arkzag` SETTLES it:** the final chunk published the
>    4-step faction-performance ‚Üí enthusiasm rule **verbatim and it MATCHES `drums`**
>    (¬ß29.10), so the GAs converged on the canonical model. Two concrete consequences
>    the planner can act on now:
>    - **The per-Congress 4-step reshuffle pass is ready-to-build** ‚Äî a NEW phase runner
>      that, after a Congress's bills are scored by-card ‚Üí by-faction, fires the four
>      stacking enthusiasm shifts (MOST-for-dominant +1 toward dominant; LEAST-for-
>      dominant ‚àí1 away; MOST-for-opposition +1 toward **dominant**, *only if it gained
>      points*; LEAST-from-opposition +2 toward **opposition**, *even if it gained*) plus
>      the **‚àí100 crisis-bill-failure / ideology-conflict-waiver ‚Üí +1 enthusiasm** rule.
>      **This lands in E23 (the Enthusiasm/Party-Pref engine + Score economy), and it is
>      now `ready` ‚Äî the algorithm is no longer in dispute.** Placement nuance: E23 was
>      Phase-2 (it rides E6's meter bank); the reshuffle itself is small once E6 exists,
>      and you may pull the reshuffle pass forward as soon as bill scoring (E20) + E6 are
>      done, ahead of the rest of E23.
>    - **The ¬±3 cap is ready-to-build and binds at `calcStateVote` (`phaseRunners.ts:
>      3709-3711`)** ‚Äî it clamps the `enthusiasm*2` + `partyPref*5` terms. This is the
>      XS clamp already queued under E6/#80; batch 11 confirms the binding site and
>      removes the "wait for the fork" caveat batch 10 put on it. Ship it with E6.
>    - **The ONE remaining open piece is #18 ‚Äî the state-scope sub-question** ("which
>      states does a card's enthusiasm apply to": every-state-unless-penalized vs
>      ideology-leaning-only). The ¬ß29.10 model expresses shifts **per-ideology-card**
>      and leaves the state-application step open. **Keep #18 behind a human DECISION-
>      GATE** ‚Äî the cap and the reshuffle can ship; the state-scope cannot until the
>      human picks. (This is the reconciliation the planner asked for: batch-10's QW3 ¬±3
>      cap is now unconditionally ready; only #18 stays gated.)
> 3. **‚òÖ NEW unsettled fork ‚Äî delegate-class assignment ‚Äî gate it BEFORE the convention
>    loop (E10).** `arkzag` opened a NEW fork distinct from the resolved #51: **who sets

> 4. **‚òÖ Two sized bug-fixes (no re-sequence):**

> 5. **‚òÖ The META-signal flips POSITIVE this batch ‚Äî but it ARGUES FOR the same items.**
>    Unlike batch 10 (`dem1820`, the **2nd** GM-burnout death in the KB) and `new1772`
>    (the 1st, DH-36), **`arkzag` did NOT collapse** ‚Äî it ran the full 1822‚Üí1840 arc.
>    The reason is decisive for prioritization: the GAs **heavily SCRIPTED the upkeep**
>    (Zagnut/Ark automated deaths/retirements, conventions, and delegate classes). So
>    the contrast with DH-36's two prior burnout deaths is **direct evidence that
>    automating the deterministic upkeep is what makes a long campaign survivable** ‚Äî
>    i.e. the exact thing the build IS. **This STRENGTHENS (does not weaken) the
>    automation-reduces-upkeep argument behind the CPU handler suite (E9), the focus-Rep
>    House abstraction (#55), the scenario-boot pipeline (#115), and the death/retire
>    + delegate-class automation that #61/#85 + E10 deliver.** Cite it behind those
>    items; it is now a **3-thread signal** (2 burnout deaths + 1 survived-by-scripting).
> 6. **CORROBORATION (confidence only, no movement):** #115 scenario-boot (the
>    continuation-boot is re-confirmed ‚Äî `arkzag` boots the `dem1820` mid-government save;
>    **this does NOT change #115's priority ‚Äî it stays at the front of the subsystem
>    queue per ¬ß9.1.9**, and the continuation-boot is just another `BootSheet` instance);
>    #13/#111 convention + 8-stage general + presidential-promise buyouts + party-specific
>    thresholds (2/3 vs 50%+1) + VP-rubric (all multi-campaign confirmed ‚Äî ¬ß29.11a, feeds
>    E10); #44 per-state EC (DE+SC by legislature in 1828); #52 SCOTUS docket (Amistad/
>    Barron/Antelope + min-age amendment + docket-runs-dry ‚Äî feeds E25); #59 sectional
>    crisis (recurring Abolish-Slavery CRISIS amendments always fail ‚Äî feeds E3b(b)); #11
>    crisis bills + named-CRISIS states (feeds E2/E6); #10 filibuster (Puritan ‚â•2 legis ‚Äî
>    feeds E14); #1 multiplayer + CPU fallback + transferable factions; #9 bill packaging;
>    #25 cabinet rules (5-region); #54 investigations (same-half-term Controversial); #85
>    death/retire = 3%+2%; #40 per-(party,era) nicknames (RED fragments into ~6
>    micro-factions); #101 era-gated office creation (Minister to Japan). None move the
>    keystones.
>
> **‚òÖ‚òÖ‚òÖ Batch-10 changes to the plan (`dem1820`, the first 1820-START scenario ‚Äî a
> short stalled campaign that is mostly CORROBORATION, but it PROMOTES the
> scenario-boot pipeline and SHARPENS two unsettled forks into decision-gates. NO
> new keystone; ONE promotion + two decision-gates + a handful of sized fixes):**
> 1. **‚òÖ‚òÖ #115 scenario-boot is PROMOTED to the FRONT of the queue (a hard
>    prerequisite, NOT a new keystone ‚Äî it folds into K4's `BootSheet` schema, but
>    its priority rises above everything except the determinism/registry
>    keystones).** The game-master frames it correctly: *"the build's scenario-boot
>    pipeline IS the missing setup spec"* and it is **the single most-requested
>    missing item** across the corpus (the forum has "**no rules for how to CREATE
>    a game**," `dem1820` POST 92 ‚Äî Ted: *"rules‚Ä¶ on how to create a playtest is
>    maybe our greatest need but to date nobody has stepped up to write one"*).
>    **Verified vs shipped (the reason this is foundational, not cosmetic): there is
>    NO shared boot abstraction today.** `startNewGame` (`GameContext.tsx:264`)
>    branches to `build1772Scenario` / `build1856Scenario`; each builder
>    (`scenario1856.ts:44-214`) **hand-assembles everything from scratch** ‚Äî seats
>    senators/reps/governors in nested loops, wires cabinet/court, stamps a 47-field
>    `GameState` literal ‚Äî and **uses raw `Math.random` at boot** (`scenario1856.ts:83,99,113`,
>    the K0 blast radius). A 1820/1788/1800/1948 scenario today means **copy-pasting
>    that ~200-line builder**, which is exactly how the undocumented house-rules
>    (strip-Command-at-boot, inaugural career-track seeding, Senate-class assignment,
>    vacant-seat fill) proliferate ‚Äî none of them are in code. **The build target is
>    the canonical `scenarioBoot(BootSheet)` pipeline + a typed `BootSheet` schema
>    (mechanics ¬ß26.1 / ¬ß29.1) that 1772/1856/1820/1788/1948 all share** ‚Äî so era
>    identity is **data**, not a hand-authored code path. **This stays inside K4
>    structurally** (the `BootSheet` schema was already K4's cross-cutting
>    constraint) but **rises in priority to "do it with the first new scenario, and
>    factor the shared pipeline THEN, not after the third copy-paste."** See ¬ß9.1.9
>    (new) for the exact factoring + the boot-sheet field list + the
>    undocumented-setup-rules the pipeline must encode. **This is the headline of
>    batch 10.**
> 2. **‚òÖ The TWO unsettled forks are DECISION-GATED ‚Äî flag them NOT-ready-to-build.**
>    `dem1820` surfaced both as live multi-party design arguments that the forum did
>    **not** settle; each needs a **human design decision before any build**, so the
>    roadmap-planner must place them behind a decision gate, not in the build queue:
>    - **Player-SCOTUS (#52, the fork).** `dem1820` (POST 420-443) resolved the
>      forum's *own* working rule as **votes-by-ideology-chart (ties roll a die),
>      player gets delay/dismiss only** (dismiss only Gov-Action cases; must hear
>      ‚â•1) ‚Äî but the designer (`vcczar`) wants it **trait-gated** (only
>      Integrity/Disharmonious/Predictable justices auto-vote; Controversial/Pliable/
>      Lackey let a Manipulative leader's player decide), and two players want a
>      **fully player-controlled court**. **Three live models, no decision.**
>      Verified shipped: the court is a `chance(0.5)` coin-flip + ¬±0.1 partyPref
>      (`phaseRunners.ts:3397`) + age-75 retire (`:3648`) ‚Äî **no docket, no case
>      data, no player surface AT ALL** (debt #18). So this is not "pick a fork on
>      existing code" ‚Äî it is "design the fork, THEN build the from-scratch docket."
>      The docket data structure lands in `src/data/scotusCases<Era>.ts` + a
>      `GameState.scotusDocket` ledger regardless of which fork wins; the fork only
>      changes the *resolution* + the player-action surface (delay/dismiss is one
>      ActionRegistry row; trait-gated player-vote is a different one). **Gate:
>      human picks CPU-by-ideology vs trait-gated vs player-controlled ‚Üí THEN the
>      SCOTUS docket epic (Phase-2 #25) builds it.**
>    - **Meter‚Üíenthusiasm‚Üíelection (#18/#51, the fork).** `dem1820` (POST 569/575/618)
>      published a concrete processing rule ‚Äî **non-enthusiasm meters shift the
>      per-(ideology) enthusiasm boxes; the boxes then apply to EVERY state UNLESS a
>      state penalizes that ideology; hard ¬±3 cap on ideology + party-pref bonuses**
>      ‚Äî but it is **explicitly a GA-vs-designer fork**: Ted's "every state unless
>      penalized" vs V's "ideology-leaning states take the hit" vs Matt's "primaries
>      only." **Verified shipped: `calcStateVote` (`phaseRunners.ts:3709-3711`) reads
>      enthusiasm raw (`enthusiasm[ideology][party] * 2`), applies it UNIFORMLY to
>      every state, with NO ¬±3 cap and NO per-state ideology penalty** ‚Äî so the ¬±3
>      cap is a missing clamp and the state-scope decision is the unbuilt fork. The
>      ¬±3 cap itself is XS and **already queued** (the meter-model ¬±3-clamp, Phase-1
>      #6 / #80) ‚Äî but **WHERE the cap binds and WHETHER enthusiasm is state-scoped
>      depend on which fork wins.** **Gate: human picks the state-scope model ‚Üí
>      THEN the meter-model/election-math epic binds the cap + the per-state penalty.**
> 3. **‚òÖ Sized, corroborated quick-fixes (all fold into existing rows; no
>    re-sequence):**
>    - **DH-53 (bill EV-effect sign) ‚Äî XS-to-S, but NOT a sign-flip in existing
>      code.** Verified: `EraEventResponseEffect` / `Legislation.effects`
>      (`types.ts:1448` / `:1515`) has **NO per-state EV field**, and `applyEffect`
>      (`phaseRunners.ts:3209`) **cannot mutate `State.electoralVotes`**. The
>      "until-passed ‚àí1 EV vs when-passed +1 EV" bug is about a per-bill EV-effect
>      mechanic that **does not exist yet** ‚Äî so the fix is **author the structured
>      per-bill effect tables with sign-checked "when-passed +N" semantics** (the
>      SAME surface as DH-14 era-aware bill impacts + DH-48 structured era-event
>      `evDelta`). Lands in the bill-catalog / era-content work (Phase-1 #20 +
>      K4), not as a standalone patch.
>    - **DH-24 (Senate-class boot validator) ‚Äî XS, now 2-thread.** Verified: the
>      boot assigns classes naively (`scenario1856.ts:86`, `classId =
>      senators.length + 1` ‚Üí only ever 1/2, never 3) and the election rotation
>      **hard-codes the 1856 base year** (`phaseRunners.ts:3885`,
>      `senateClass = ((year - 1856) / 2) % 3 + 1`) ‚Äî both **wrong for an 1820
>      start** (the `dem1820` GA ran the 1822 midterm as the wrong class and had to
>      resubmit every appointment). Fix = a Senate-class assigner+verifier that runs
>      **in the scenario-boot pipeline** (#115) and a start-year-relative rotation
>      (replace the literal `1856`). Folds into the `BootSheet` boot-pipeline hooks.
>    - **Focus-Rep House (EV‚àí2)/5 (#55) ‚Äî M, DESIGNED-not-shipped.** Verified: the
>      House is modeled as **full (EV‚àí2) reps per state** (`scenario1856.ts:93`,
>      `electoralVotes - 2`; re-elected per-seat at `phaseRunners.ts:3913-3939`) ‚Äî
>      the abstracted **(EV‚àí2)/5 focus-Rep model (POST 643) is NOT in code.** This
>      is the scaling-wall-(b) surface (#55 / A9): collapsing per-seat reps to
>      focus-Reps is the change that makes the "congressional election from hell"
>      (10 forum pages / 8 real days in `dem1820`) tractable. Folds into
>      **scaling-wall-(b) House-slate persistence (Phase-1 #7)** + the census/
>      apportionment epic (#34/#55). The seat-specific-incumbency-except-census-year
>      rule (POST 682/696) is a balance dial on top, not a separate build.
>    - **Statehood‚Üísectional-crisis coupling (#59) ‚Äî S additive, DESIGNED-not-shipped.**
>      Verified: `State.isSlaveState` (`types.ts:1329`) exists + is populated but is
>      **only READ in `StatesPage.tsx`** (display); `admitState` (`territories.ts:8`)
>      does **no free/slave balance check**. The `dem1820` "Era of Good Feelings
>      ends" crisis (14 slave / 12 free ‚Üí DomStab ‚àí2 + Party-Pref +2 + faction
>      penalties, POST 521) fired from a 1820 start ‚Äî **earliest observation of
>      #59**, but it is a GM hand-computation. The build is a **cheap additive
>      derived-count crisis** hooked at `admitState` (and at era-event resolution
>      that flips a flag): same code area as the 1856-arc sectional crisis (#59) ‚Äî
>      **note it is NOT 1856-only; it must fire from 1820/Nationalism-era starts
>      too.** Folds into the Civil-War/Reconstruction epic's cheap-additive front
>      (Phase-1 #3b, secession #58 + sectional #59).
>    - **Appointment-order ladder + replacement-gains timing ‚Äî XS each, author-into
>      the boot/appointment rules.** `dem1820` settled two rules mid-thread the
>      build should adopt: the **Senate-appointment-eligibility ladder** (POST 859:
>      your-party-not-on-CT ‚Üí your-party-on-CT ‚Üí opp-party-not-on-CT ‚Üí opp-party-on-CT
>      ‚Üí generate a 1-skill pol; can't leave a full-term Senate seat vacant; can't
>      pull a CT pol if any non-CT same-party pol is eligible; appointees 30+) ‚Äî a
>      concrete spec for the vacancy-fill order the boot pipeline + the cabinet/
>      Senate phases need; and **replacement-gains timing** (POST 291: a new
>      replacement ‚Äî incl. faction leaders ‚Äî gets **no stat gains until they hold
>      the post through the next appointment/election phase, EXCEPT military
>      positions which are eligible on appointment**) ‚Äî a one-rule guard on the
>      ability-grant path (`ABILITY_EARN_RULES` area). Both are XS rules-table
>      additions; the eligibility ladder pairs with DH-25's career-track-eligibility
>      decision (still PARKING-LOT ‚Äî the "CT pols can't run/be appointed" vs "CT
>      pulled last" contradiction is re-corroborated here, POST 850/859).
> 4. **‚òÖ DH-36 (2nd GA-burnout death in the KB) ‚Äî the META-signal, reinforced.**
>    `dem1820` is the **second campaign in the corpus killed by GM burnout** (after
>    `new1772`): Ted quit (POST 900) ‚Äî *"these used to be a lot of fun to run and
>    now it just feels argumentative"* ‚Äî under the compounding weight of
>    undocumented-rule friction + relentless manual bookkeeping (10-page elections,
>    constant spreadsheet repair, a demographic-corruption data bug that rendered
>    every pol Black/LGBT from a column mismatch). His tell: *"I'm currently helping
>    run an all-CPU game for a reason."* **This is NOT a row to schedule ‚Äî it is the
>    prioritization argument for the items that REDUCE manual upkeep:** the
>    scenario-boot pipeline (#115, eliminates the boot/data-corruption class), the
>    focus-Rep House abstraction (#55, kills the election tedium), the CPU handler
>    suite (K5/E9, owns the bookkeeping a human GM can't sustain), and the
>    disclosed-deterministic-ruleset theme (the engine must SHIP the rules the
>    forum improvises ‚Äî that is the whole point of #115). Cite it behind those
>    items; the **automation-reduces-upkeep argument is now 2-thread.**
> 5. **CORROBORATION (no movement, confidence only):** #92 era-bands (a **THIRD
>    start year, 1820** ‚Äî "Era of Democracy (1820-1840)" / "Manifest Destiny
>    (1840-1856)" printed, POST 946 ‚Äî joining 1772/1800/1948); #1 multiplayer +
>    faction-handover + CPU fallback (2 mid-thread handovers); #76/#108
>    enthusiasm-gated party-flips (RED‚ÜíBLUE at boot, POST 72); #24 card-distribution

> **‚òÖ‚òÖ Batch-9 changes to the plan (`nuke`, the Cold-War/modern half ‚Äî a big
> NEW-ERA gap-fill, but mostly CONFIDENCE + NEGATIVE-SCOPE + small placements;
> NO re-sequence, NO new keystone):**
> 1. **‚òÖ The K3/K4 era model gains a TWO-LEVEL refinement + a STRUCTURED-era-event-
>    data requirement.** The band model is now confirmed across a THIRD start year
>    (1948), and `nuke` (¬ß27.1) **splits it into two separately-fired mechanics that
>    MUST both be built**: (a) point-banked **Historical Eras** (content-bands with
>    `advanceWhen` + per-era `{bills, eraEvents, draftees, biasTable}` + **rule-deltas**
>    ‚Äî the Era-of-Terror cabinet rework proves bands carry rule changes, not just
>    content); and (b) **within-era decade/census boundaries** = a SEPARATE
>    schedule-fired mechanic doing **bulk EV reallocation + state-bias re-lean +
>    content rotation** (the per-decade `AMPU Census`, distinct from #68's per-era
>    bias swap). **Do NOT collapse the two.** AND: **era-event data needs STRUCTURED
>    `evDelta`/census fields** (DH-48) ‚Äî the Neocon census events were LOST in the
>    spreadsheet because they were free-text flavor; the typed field + per-era
>    completeness validation is a real K4 requirement. **Logged correction: "Neocons"
>    is a FACTION REBRAND, NOT an era band** ‚Äî do not model it as a band. No new
>    keystone ‚Äî refines K3/K4. See ¬ß9.1.5 (updated).
> 2. **‚òÖ NEGATIVE SCOPE ‚Äî the Cold War is NOT a subsystem to build (the single most
>    important scope-control finding this batch).** Despite the "Nuclear Age" title
>    there is NO purpose-built nuclear/MAD/NATO/space-race/military-branch engine in
>    the design (¬ß28.2). Verified shipped: only `src/engine/revolutionaryWar.ts`
>    exists ‚Äî no generic war engine, no Cold-War engine. The "Cold War" is the
>    **generic war engine (#6) RELABELED + EraEvos/A-B events + the diplomacy
>    subsystem (#107)**. **The roadmap must NOT scope a Cold-War subsystem.** The
>    REAL build items underneath are: (i) the **generic war engine** (ONE engine, all
>    eras, ideally army/navy/air branches + a **resolution/peace path** ‚Äî DH-47:
>    wars NEVER resolve today, Korea ran ~2 decades); and (ii) the **diplomacy
>    subsystem** (8 relation meters + ambassador actions, #107). Cold-War "content"
>    = data (events/bills/lobby-cards/a couple of legislative √ó2 multipliers).
> 3. **‚òÖ Mutable cabinet extends to CREATE-AND-ABOLISH (divergence #15 / E16).**
>    `cabinetSeatsForYear` (verified still year-keyed at `types.ts:1196`) must become
>    fully data-driven. Confirmed END-TO-END now: founding (Bank/Navy/AG by bill) ‚Üí
>    modern (DOE/DHS created; **Postmaster General ABOLISHED**; HEW split). The
>    batch-6 spec only added a CREATE path ‚Äî **add the ABOLISH path** too
>    (`Legislation.abolishesCabinetSeat?`). Folds into E16; no re-sequence.
> 4. **‚òÖ CPU-faction AI is LOAD-BEARING ‚Äî elevate the handler suite to a first-class
>    system (#114 / ¬ß28.12).** The designer's strongest corpus statement: the **APP =
>    1-human-vs-9-CPU** (multiplayer "goes off the rails"; the points system is for
>    CPUs, not humans). The forum game is team-MP with CPU backfill; **the app is a
>    solo adaptation of a multiplayer game** ‚Äî so the ENTIRE multiplayer apparatus
>    (party-leader elections, conversions, kingmaker pairings, committee assignment,
>    cross-faction endorsements, forced 3rd-party runs) must be CPU-AI-driven. This
>    **validates solo-first sequencing AND elevates K5 + the handler suite (E9) from
>    "force-multiplier" toward "the load-bearing system that makes the product
>    playable solo."** No re-sequence (K5 stays after K0+K2), but its *priority*
>    rises ‚Äî see ¬ß9.1.3.
> 5. **‚òÖ Senate pass-threshold ‚Äî RESOLVED IN CODE: simple majority.** The digest
>    flagged a SOURCE conflict (¬ß28.5/¬ß28.12: ~60%-to-pass vs simple-majority-pass-
>    with-60%-cloture). **The shipped code settles the engine side:**
>    `runPhase_2_6_3_Floor` (`phaseRunners.ts:3562`) passes a bill iff `house.yea >
>    house.nay && senate.yea > senate.nay` ‚Äî **simple majority in both chambers, NO
>    cloture/supermajority step** (the only supermajority anywhere is the
>    Articles-of-Confederation 2/3-of-states in the independence-era CC path,
>    `continentalCongress.ts:224` ‚Äî a different mechanism). **Code = simple majority;
>    the DESIGN question (should the Senate require 60% / a cloture step?) stays OPEN
>    for the human** ‚Äî when the filibuster/cloture epic lands, the human decides and
>    the constant goes there. See debt #27.
> 6. **‚òÖ Parking-lot additions (batch 9):** **DH-49 ‚Äî a POPULATION MODEL + House cap**
>    (the Wyoming Rule + per-decade census EV reallocation are un-implementable
>    without one; none exists ‚Äî `State.electoralVotes` is a static seed; author
>    before build OR size into the census epic #34/#55). **DH-54 ‚Äî impeachment /
>    VP-vacancy fill was NEVER in the rules doc** (author the impeachment + succession
>    ruleset before the institutional layer #112/#61; corroborates DH-33). **DH-56**
>    conflicting career-track rule-sets (with DH-25). The other batch-9 DHs classify
>    into existing epics: DH-45/46/47 ‚Üí diplomacy + generic-war (#6/#107); DH-48 ‚Üí
>    structured era-event data (K4); DH-50/53 ‚Üí era-event scheduling + bill-catalog;
>    DH-52 ‚Üí election-math balance; DH-51 ‚Üí modern dataset balance (`scripts/`);
>    DH-55 ‚Üí 3rd-party trigger #48; DH-57 ‚Üí convention epic / handler #5.
> 7. **‚òÖ NEGATIVE RESULT confirmed (carried from batch 8) ‚Äî no "future" era to
>    scope.** `nuke` spans only Nuclear Age (1948‚Äì2000) ‚Üí Era of Terror (2000‚Äì~2012);
>    it stalls at ~2005 and seams into the already-documented `modern` (2004‚Äì2020)
>    thread. **The KB now spans a CONTINUOUS 1772‚Üí2020 timeline** (this 1948 thread
>    is the predecessor of the `modern` 2004-2020 thread, joined at the 2004 election).

>    `progressive`.

> **‚òÖ Batch-8 changes to the plan (the lead ‚Äî mostly CONFIDENCE + small
> placements; NO re-sequence, NO new keystone):**
> 1. **‚òÖ The era-model reframe (K3/K4 / divergence #18) is now MULTI-SAVE PROVEN
>    ‚Äî confidence, not structure.** Two independent saves emit the identical
>    era-band sequence at identical in-game dates: `tea1772` (1772-start solo
>    all-CPU) and `rep1800` (1800-start multiplayer), 28 in-game years apart
>    (game-mechanics ¬ß27.1/¬ß27.2; `tea1772` POST 21/62/91/130/153). The bands are

> 2. **‚òÖ The dynamic cabinet-seat refactor (divergence #15 / E16) is reinforced ‚Äî
>    `cabinetSeatsForYear` is now confirmed the WRONG model END-TO-END.** Verified
>    still year-keyed at `types.ts:1196` (its docstring `:1190-1195` hard-codes the

>    timeline: founding (`new1772` ‚Äî SCOTUS/Bank/Navy/AG/academies built by bills,
>    ¬ß24.6) ‚Üí modern (`pop` ‚Äî Climate-bill creates a cabinet seat). So the seat-
>    list-as-mutable-state refactor is **foundational to the offices-as-data theme,
>    not a modern-only nicety.** No new work ‚Äî the existing E16 item's justification
>    is strengthened to founding+modern.
> 3. **‚òÖ DH-41 ‚Äî a genuine new author-before-build PARKING-LOT item.** The general
>    SCOTUS-ruling ‚Üí downstream-statute cascade (a ruling that *contradicts a law on
>    the books* auto-voiding it) is **UNBUILT and was explicitly DEFERRED by
>    `vcczar`** (`tea1772` POST 124-126, the Prigg‚ÜíFugitive-Slave-Act case). Today a
>    contradicting ruling leaves the law operative. Distinct from the *built*
>    strike-a-single-law + overturn-an-amendment-on-review. Author the cascade
>    policy before building it; lands in the SCOTUS docket epic once decided.
> 4. **DH-36 (‚òÖ GM-burnout abandoned a 12-turn game) is the META-justification for
>    the whole build, not a row.** `new1772` POST 3607: the first multiplayer 1772
>    campaign collapsed under manual bookkeeping. The strongest single "why build

> 6. **‚òÖ NEGATIVE RESULT ‚Äî do NOT scope an "Era of the Future."** No thread in the

>
> **Batch-6 changes to the plan (the lead ‚Äî three concrete moves):**
> 1. **APOCALYPSE meter-driven endgame is Phase-1, sized M.** Verified shipped:
>    only event-driven endgame exists (`EraEvent.triggersGameEnd` ‚Üí
>    `phaseRunners.ts:2871` ‚Üí `game.gameEnded`); no meter-watcher, no
>    countdown clock, no `endgameClocks` array. The forum adds a NEW model:
>    bottom-tier band entry ‚Üí 10-game-year countdown ‚Üí mandatory game-end
>    (recovery clears it). **Phase-1, NOT Phase-2.** Rationale: the `planet`
>    meter ships today and ticks every era; the model is **meter-agnostic**
>    (the Populism Planet Health clock is one configured row per era, and
>    analogous bottom-tier clocks may apply to other meters/eras). **Folds in
>    with the meter-model generalization** (Phase-1 #6 ¬±3-clamp + crisis/cascade)
>    ‚Äî same code area, same tick site (`runPhase_2_5_1_Lingering`), shared
>    `game.gameEnded` sink with the event-driven path. **Size: M.**
>    Architecturally significant ‚Äî it's a new endgame surface, not a balance
>    dial ‚Äî but cheap because the meter and the sink both ship.
> 2. **K4 `BootSheet` schema is THE cross-cutting build constraint of K4.**
>    Three documented mid-government boots ‚Äî 1788 (designed) / 1856 (shipped) /
>    2012 (designed in `pop`) ‚Äî share ONE shape: pre-built faction roster
>    (5 Blue + 5 Red) + per-faction archetype politicians + era-tuned
>    ideology/interest/lobby decks + sitting government keyed to start year +
>    **state roster keyed to `{era, startYear}`, NOT era alone** (divergence
>    #17 ‚Äî same `modern` enum has the 50+DC fresh-modern roster AND the
>    53-state Wyoming-Rule continuation roster) + EXPLICITLY EMPTY at boot
>    (no faction leaders, no career-track pols, no inherited PV/legacy/
>    Kingmaker pairs). **Build the schema ONCE in K4**, instantiate per era.
>    **Era identity is data configuration, not code paths** ‚Äî R1's "Trumpism"
>    deck is the seed configuration of one faction, not a "Trumpism mechanic."
>    Also: **Senate-class verifier (DH-24)** + **`TRAIT_CONFLICTS` validator
>    (DH-27)** run at boot pipeline as guardrails on seed data.
> 3. **K2 + cabinet + amendment refactors are the divergence-#15/#16 calls.**
>    Two refactors fold cleanly into already-planned work:
>    - **K2 gains `requires?: AmendmentPredicate`** on the `GameAction<Ctx>`
>      shape from day one (divergence #16) ‚Äî one field + one filter step in
>      the picker. **Cheap if early, expensive if retrofit** across 6 libraries.
>      The canonical instance ("Send VP to Shore Up Support" requires 12th
>      Amendment) ships with the general-election library. Same `requires:`
>      mechanism also gates bill catalog entries (income-tax category) and
>      gov action entries ‚Äî predicate field belongs at the registry-row
>      level, not the library level.
>    - **E16 cabinet refactor gains the dynamic seat list** (divergence #15).
>      Verified: `cabinetSeatsForYear(year)` (`types.ts:1196`) is **pure
>      derived with NO mutable state**; `phaseRunners.ts:2162` recomputes it
>      each turn. Refactor: shipped function becomes the **boot seed only**;
>      runners read `GameState.cabinetSeats: SeatSpec[]`; bill-sign handler
>      appends `Legislation.createsCabinetSeat?: SeatSpec`. **Folds into the
>      cabinet retention refactor** ‚Äî same code area, marginal additional
>      cost.
> 4. **CPU handler 9b legislation now consumes the conditional-vote-rules
>    primitive** (`pop` POST 1111). Iron-Fist controllers publish declarative
>    `Predicate ‚Üí {AYE/NAY}` policies stored at `Faction.factionLeader.
>    compelledVoteRule?`. Handler #2 (legislation) consults this BEFORE the
>    ¬ß25.6 heuristic; handler #4 (cabinet) consumes the same primitive for
>    auto-AYE-own-picks AND SCOTUS within-1-step auto-AYE (¬ß26.6.1). Promotes
>    a ¬ß25.9 sub-effect to a **first-class CPU primitive** ‚Äî subsumes per-vote
>    Iron-Fist compulsion + auto-AYE under one shape. Folds into E17 (Iron-Fist
>    split) + CPU handler suite (#2, #4).
> 5. **DH-25 (career-track bootstrap unresolved) is a PARKING LOT BLOCKER on
>    any modern scenario shipping.** The 3-yr-stale design discussion has no
>    canonical rule for which existing pols start on career tracks at a
>    mid-game boot. Author the rule (Zagnut's "1996+, 1/track" houserule is
>    on the table) before `scenario1948` or `scenario2012` ships. DH-24 / DH-27
>    are XS validators that run at the boot pipeline.
>
> **Batch-5 changes to the plan (carried, still the lead for CPU work):**
> 1. **K5 ‚Äî a `CpuController` scaffold ‚Äî is a NEW keystone.** The shipped engine
>    has **no agent-decision pass at all** (only three thin stubs:
>    `pickBestForFaction` `phaseRunners.ts:33`, `pickAIResponse` `eraGraph.ts:88`,
>    `autoFillCPUVotes` `constitutionalConvention.ts:81`). The ¬ß25 spec doesn't
>    slot into any existing place ‚Äî it needs its own home. K5 lives **after K0
>    (seed) and after K2 (ActionRegistry)** because handlers route action picks
>    through K2; but K5 is **not on the critical path for scenarios** ‚Äî
>    federalism + the 1856-arc can ship with stubbed handlers and upgrade
>    together. **K5 unlocks ~15 lightweight per-subsystem handler PRs that
>    parallelize.** See ¬ß6.6.1 + ¬ß9.1.3 + ¬ß9.2.
> 2. **DH-23 (cabinet 36% pass-rate bug) is XS-S, not the M many digests imply.**
>    Verified: **the engine ships NO Senate confirmation step at all** ‚Äî
>    `runPhase_2_3_1_Cabinet` (`phaseRunners.ts:2158-2223`) is a one-step scored
>    pick. So the fix is **build the confirmation in the right shape from day
>    one** (default-AYE baseline + Iron-Fist Maj-Leader auto-AYE-own-picks +

> **‚òÖ‚òÖ‚òÖ Batch-29 change (TWO timeline-edge playtests ‚Äî `wilsons1916` / `a0b7ef49`
> "Wilson's Vision: 1916, Era of Hollywood" [first multiplayer 1916] + `welcome2future`

> **‚ëÝ ‚òÖ‚òÖ #206 ‚Äî the `Era` enum is DOUBLY too coarse, confirmed from BOTH timeline

> level: `Era = 'independence' | 'federalism' | 'nationalism' | 'modern'`
> (`types.ts:1337`) ‚Äî **no `future`, no `populism`, no `progressive`/`gilded`.**
> `currentEra` is boot-set (`scenario1772.ts:97` / `scenario1856.ts:177`) and advanced

> `eraGraph.ts:153-155`); the only year‚Üíera map is a UI-only `eraForYear`
> (`AnytimeEventsPage.tsx:20-25`) collapsing ‚â•1933 to `modern`. The forum's "Era of the
> Future"/"Era of Populism"/"Era of Hollywood" are content-band labels mapping onto this

> this batch:** the fix is exactly the K3/K4 era-keystone work already specced (¬ß9.1.5 /
> debt #5) ‚Äî **either grow the enum (`future` + the Hollywood/Populism labels) OR adopt

> path: with the Future/Populism/Hollywood/Gilded/Progressive labels all needed, a flat

> **REFRAMES (annotate, no re-sequence):**
> - **‚òÖ DH-14 reframe ‚Äî NOT a hard block, a CPU-AI artifact.** The 19th Amendment ("Equal Voting
>   Rights for Women") PASSES in the 10-human 1916 game (House 374-61, Senate 79-17, ratified) ‚Äî so
>   the `drums` all-CPU "this amendment will never pass with CPUs" is a **CPU-AI / era-ideology-weighting
>   artifact, NOT a mechanical block.** The fix is TWO things, both already-queued: the era-keyed
>   bill-ideology table (debt #5-adjacent ‚Äî move Mods off suffrage's negative side in 1916) AND a CPU
>   vote model (#208/E9 9b) that can reach 2/3 like a human field. No standalone work ‚Äî folds into the
>   era-content table + the CPU legislative engine.
> - **#106 / WWI optionality** corroborated (the US never entered WWI in `wilsons1916`; the war engine
>   is dormant unless the war-entry event fires) ‚Äî no change.
> - **#114 / #110** corroborated as the load-bearing CPU-AI requirement (`welcome2future` = the most
>   complete modern CPU-turn sample) ‚Äî reinforces E9/K5, no re-sequence.
> - **#173 / #186 METHODOLOGY CONVERGENCE ‚Äî era-band OPENINGS, not mid/tail.** Both threads independently
>   re-affirm: start at an era's OPENING, not its edges. `wilsons1916` opened with the explicit decision
>   *"starting at the beginning of an era, rather than the middle, was better"* (the deliberate fix to the
>   1960 #186 failure ‚Üí it booted cleanly); `welcome2future` confirms the INVERSE ‚Äî a 2022 TAIL-of-band
>   start has thin scripted-event density (*"only one scripted event capable of occurring right now"*,
>   `post 378`). So BOTH era edges (mid-band AND tail) are thin-content friction zones ‚Üí the #173 14-band
>   era-start presets (debt #51) should snap New-Game starts to band OPENINGS. **Engineering principle for
>   any future scenario boot: author/test at era-band openings; treat mid/tail starts as a separate,
>   higher-risk class.**
>
> **Bottom line: NO new keystone, NO re-sequence; top of queue UNCHANGED** (QW0 ‚Üí K0/K2 ‚Üí K3/K4 +

> **‚òÖ‚òÖ‚òÖ Batch-30 change (FIVE DESIGN/DATA/changelog threads, NO playtest ‚Äî `revampPV` [the PV-system
> overhaul] / `summer2021`-changelog / `ampuData` [authoring source] / `presidents` [corpus-coverage

> touches `pv.ts` ‚Äî which DRIVES elections, so it is a balance change, not a free win. Lead with the
> PV cluster (#214/#215/#216/#220 + DH-77), then #217, the era-content registry fields (#221), the
> SCOTUS/apportionment pair (#218/#219), the small fold-ins (#222‚Äì#227), and the DH-76/#115 sharpening.**

> **‚ëÝ ‚òÖ‚òÖ THE PV-SYSTEM OVERHAUL ‚Äî #214 + #215 + #216 + #220 + DH-77 (debt #85; the headline; `GM‚áíApp`).**
> All four shipped sites VERIFIED in `pv.ts`'s `computePV`: the flat per-trait loop `+4`/`‚àí5` (`pv.ts:75-77`);
> flat `skill √ó officeWeight √ó 4` (`pv.ts:70-73`); flat `command √ó 10` (`pv.ts:74`); the magic `Kingmaker +6`
> (`pv.ts:79`); the `Math.max(0, Math.round(total))` clamp (`pv.ts:88`); office/age/FF terms at `:80-87`.
> The `revampPV` thread (Feb 2023) re-valued every trait/ability on a fixed TIER scale (25/10/7/3/‚àí3/‚àí7/‚àí10,
> ¬±25 extremes; vcczar POST 33 = the authoritative ~65-item column) and DIED UNRESOLVED on a GM handoff ‚Äî
> vcczar disowned the exact numbers (*"the RANKING is the point"*) and the fitted formula was deferred to
> OrangeP47 (never delivered). **‚òÖ‚òÖ #216 is the BLOCKING PREREQUISITE ‚Äî counted at code level:** of the ~57
> traits in the P33 table, **~15 are absent** from the shipped `Trait` union (`types.ts:62-117` ‚Äî Teflon,
> Military Leader, Bookkeeper, Geostrategist, Everyman, Lawful/Cop, Jurisprudence, Union Loyalist,
> Disharmonious, Lackey, Late Bloomer, Illicit, Pliable, Incoherent, Easily Overwhelmed), **2 are pure
> renames** (`Flipflopper`‚Üí`Flip-Flopper`, `Two-faced`‚Üí`Two-Faced`), and **12+ shipped traits were NEVER
> valued in 2023** (Nationalist/Globalist/Reformist/Loyal/Ambitious/Opportunist/Impressionable/Scandalous/
> Corrupt/Traitor/Outsider/Ideologue) ‚Äî so **~17 of ~57 line-items (~30%) mis-key or drop without the
> remap.** A literal transcription is wrong; the table is **tiers to RE-MAP, not literal keys.**
> **‚òÖ‚òÖ ELECTION COUPLING ‚Äî the "change carefully" part:** `calcStateVote` reads `pv = c.pvCache`
> (`phaseRunners.ts:3699`) and adds **`pv * 0.1`** to every state-vote score (`:3709`), so a PV reweight
> DIRECTLY shifts election outcomes ‚Äî this is the same election-balance surface as the cluster #18 / #184 /
> DH-72 / QW19-21 (debt #1/#64/#65/#71). **After the reweight, re-tune the `pv*0.1` weight (and the bias/
> jitter terms) or the new PV ceiling distorts margins.** **‚òÖ SIZING (the key call):** this is NOT one
> task ‚Äî it splits into a **quick-win front-half** and a **balance-epic back-half:**
>   - **#216 the trait-name remap = S quick-win** (an explicit old‚Üínew map, ~17 line-items + value the 12+
>     never-valued shipped traits); it is the PREREQUISITE and is independently useful (clarifies the trait
>     vocabulary). **Do this FIRST.**
>   - **DH-77 the floor-offset clamp fix = XS quick-win** ‚Äî replace `Math.max(0,‚Ä¶)` (`pv.ts:88`) with a
>     constant floor-OFFSET so sub-zero pols keep ordering for the CPU's draft/leadership heuristic (the clamp
>     is the most clearly *wrong* line today). Independently shippable.
>   - **#214 the per-trait tier table = M-L balance EPIC, GATED** on the un-ingested **"Groupthinking the
>     Political Value"** thread (which holds the trait EFFECTS ‚Äî this thread sets only POINT VALUES) + an
>     OrangeP47-style coefficient re-fit; fold Kingmaker + Master-Kingmaker into the table (drop the `:79`
>     `+6`); keep office/age/FF ADDITIVE and do NOT double-count `flipFlopperPenalty` (`:86`).
>   - **#215 the per-ability curve + Command re-weight = M** ‚Äî first-point-worth-more (Command ‚âà25/level
>     decreasing; Legislative 10 then +3) + Admin/Military low-floor thresholds (Admin +10 from the 2nd
>     point); leave the UNRESOLVED restrict-born-Command fight OPEN (a toggle ‚Äî forcing it breaks modern
>     starts + 3rd-party rules).
>   - **#220 the display-scale = S decision** ‚Äî raw ("pink", revampPV lean) vs 0-100/100-normalized
>     (summer2021 POST 200-205); normalized only if PV is hidden/AI-only. UNRESOLVED in both sources; the
>     build is free to calibrate.
>   **So: ship #216 + DH-77 as quick-wins NOW; treat #214/#215 as a single PV-reweight balance epic that is
>   gated on the un-ingested effects thread AND must re-tune `calcStateVote`'s `pv*0.1` in the same PR.**
>   game-mechanics ¬ß3.4.1.
>   **‚òÖ‚òÖ BATCH-33 SUPERSEDES TWO calls in this item (see the batch-33 block + debt #85):** (i) the "GATED on

>   #214/#215/#220 are **READY-after-#216**, with direction RULED (Command√ó15 ¬∑ Legis/Gov/Admin√ó10 ¬∑
>   Mil/Jud√ó5; display 0‚Äì100/50-avg) + a NEW trait-EFFECTS-in-PV layer to build (0% built). (ii) the "re-tune

> **‚ë° ‚òÖ #217 the president/cabinet IMPLEMENTATION sub-engine + ~30 governing traits (debt #86; deepens
> #179; `GM‚áíApp`).** A DISTINCT system layer beyond the flat `traits[]`: after legislation/actions pass, a

> + Crisis-{Manager,Admin,Gov} (the Crisis-Admin 10/20% Lingering crisis-pull ON TOP of #179's rolls) +
> Passive/Predictable + Passive+Pliable PUPPET + Magician (`summer2021` POST 128, 139-156, 167-184, 239). The

> adding; and these traits need PV values (#214) + the remap (#216). **L (‚â•2 systems ‚Äî rides #179's roll

>
> **‚ë¢ ‚òÖ #221 the era-content registry needs TWO schema fields (debt #88; sharpens K4 / #92 / #206).** Verified
> the `EraEvent` shape (`types.ts:1466-1482`) lacks both: (1) a **scripted-vs-flavor tier flag** (flavor events
> are narration-only, post-launch scope ‚Äî vcczar declines flavor suggestions, `ampuData` POST 4, 41), and (2)
> **per-category first-active-era metadata** (Pres-Actions/SC-Cases/Gov-Actions are *"Not functional"* pre-1789,

> `"‚Ä¶ confirmed as Associate Justice"` (`:3667`), no vote. `grep -iE 'nuclearOption|cloture|filibuster|topFour'
> src/` = ZERO. The game-master pinned the **complete 12-rule procedure at game-mechanics ¬ß9.3.11** (auto-confirm
> gate Admin‚â•3 ‚àß ¬¨Controversial ‚àß ¬¨IronFist-SML ‚àß ¬¨Top-Four ‚Üí full hearing at the #172 era-keyed threshold ‚Üí
> SML party-wide block [5 reasons] ‚Üí Puritan filibuster + Manipulation-cloture [the DH-76 half] ‚Üí president's

> promise #181). **‚òÖ Build flag:** the two "Top-Four" sets DIFFER (always-vote = State/Defense/Treasury/**War**;

> block + filibuster/cloture + fallout + vacancy cascades + promise ‚Üí **re-size to M-L, and re-scope E16 to own

> **‚ë° ‚òÖ THE 15-ERA TRIGGER-YEAR TABLE ‚Üí K4 (debt-row corroboration on #92/#206; the data-model input).** The
> rulebook (`rulebook#¬ßA POST 2`) prints the **canonical 15-era trigger-year calendar** (Independence 1774 ‚Ä¶
> Populism 2012 ‚Üí Era-of-the-Future = after the current real-world cycle) and states eras "auto-update when their
> trigger date is reached," driving legislation/event availability + state populations + preferred ideologies +
> the statesman death rate. The shipped `Era` enum is the 4-value `independence/federalism/nationalism/modern`
> (`types.ts:1337`); the 15 named bands collapse into those 4 mechanical buckets. **‚òÖ How the table feeds K4:**
> K4 already widens the enum (add `gilded`/`progressive`) and introduces the per-era CONTENT-BAND registry (¬ß9.1.5)
> ‚Äî the 15-row table is the **authoritative band-label spec K4's registry keys on** (the `{era, startYear}` /
> trigger-cycle map), and it RECONFIRMS the batch-29/#206 push toward the #92 two-level content-band model (the
> 15 labels become content-band DATA, not 15 enum values; the transition predicate stays game-state-gated per #92,
> with "trigger date reached" as the EARLIEST a band opens). **Reinforces K3/K4; no re-size ‚Äî it is the data the
> registry consumes.** Reconciles with the modernday 14-band map (#2) and the nuke "Neocons = rebrand, not a
> point-banked band" finding via the two-level model. game-mechanics ¬ß27.1.2, ¬ß30.21.
>
> **‚ë¢ ‚òÖ #182 Command = the per-half-term EXECUTIVE-ACTION BUDGET ‚Äî CONFIRMED, reinforces K2 (debt #92;
> `GM‚áíApp`).** Re-confirmed `command` is ONLY a PV input today (`pv.ts:74` flat `command*10`) + VP/eligibility
> gates; the exec phase is a flat `chance(0.5)` (`phaseRunners.ts:3635`) over 4 hardcoded one-shots
> (`:3636-3642`). The rulebook makes it authoritative (`rulebook#POST 30`): *"The President may take as many
> executive actions per half-term as he has Command Points."* The build = replace the flat coin-flip with a
> **command-sized loop over an `ActionRegistry` exec-action library** (the K2 keystone serves this as one of its
> 6 libraries) + the persistent active-action set + green/yellow/white lifecycle (debt #23/#62). **REINFORCES K2**
> (it is no longer a single-thread inference) and pairs with #179/#217 (cabinet IMPLEMENTS the actions via blunder
> rolls). **S for the budget loop; M for the library/lifecycle surface.** game-mechanics ¬ß11.2.1, ¬ß30.21.
>
> **‚ë£ ‚òÖ #179/#134 LINGERING + #50/#51 CARD-ASSIGNMENT ‚Äî canonical algorithms pinned (debt #93; `GM‚áíApp`).**
> **#179/#134:** the shipped lingering pass is a flat deterministic drift ‚Äî `drift(skill)` fixed by Admin tier
> (`phaseRunners.ts:3266-3273`), applied once per meter (`:3284-3290`); the canonical `rulebook#POST 20` algorithm
> is a per-meter positive%-vs-negative% PIT ‚Üí roll-if-it-moves ‚Üí roll winning-direction (top-2/bottom-2 = 25%
> no-change, else 50%) + the cabinet-save admin ladder (Efficient doubles; Incompetent can't roll positive) +
> the officer‚Üímeter map + the **decay numbers** (tariffs/income-tax decay 10%/2yr once age‚â•10, DO NOTHING at age
> 20 ‚Üí crisis; volatility at 2/4/8-yr marks; crises/booms never >10 yrs). **M (a real roll engine for debt
> #59/#72).** **#50/#51:** the card layer is partial (`Faction.*Cards`, `types.ts:1298-1300`, no assignment
> engine); the canonical `rulebook#POST 10` algorithm is plurality-of-own-pols assignment (every faction MUST
> hold ‚â•1 ideology card) + interest/lobby compatibility-ban tables + a 5-pol FLOOR + a cascade to the
> next-eligible faction. **M (the card-assignment pass feeds the enthusiasm reshuffle, debt #30/E16).** Both fold
> into existing clusters; neither is a keystone. game-mechanics ¬ß30.21.

> **‚òÖ‚òÖ‚òÖ Batch-32 change (FIVE MIXED DATA/DESIGN/CHANGELOG threads ‚Äî `tomorrowlist` [vcczar's to-do + the

> re-sequence, top of queue UNCHANGED [QW0 ‚Üí K0/K2 ‚Üí K3/K4 + scenarioBoot ‚Üí E1]). This batch is **mostly NEW
> designed-but-unbuilt surfaces + one DATASET-fill action** ‚Äî almost nothing is shipped. Lead with the #240
> dataset action (most ACTIONABLE ‚Äî but gated on the #216 remap), then the two new system surfaces (#236
> government-form, #237 policy-genre ‚Äî both ride the unbuilt #221 content-primitive registry), then the
> #238/#239/#194 data-model cluster. Three of the four design threads are vcczar-authored; this is the strongest

>
> **‚ëÝ ‚òÖ‚òÖ #240 ‚Äî THE `CURATED_ROWS` DATASET FILL (debt #100; the most ACTIONABLE item this batch; a STANDALONE
> author/DATA task, NOT an engine change ‚Äî but GATED on the #216 remap).** **‚òÖ FIRST, a correction to a claim
> circulating in the source digests: `CURATED_ROWS` is NOT empty at runtime.** `seedDataset.mjs:275`
> `export const CURATED_ROWS = [];` is only the INITIALIZER ‚Äî the loop at `:276-289` immediately populates it
> from the `ROWS` tuple-array (`:38-233`), giving **143 curated entries at runtime** (verified by `node -e` import
> ‚Üí `CURATED_ROWS.length === 143`; `ERA_FIGURES` = 23, from `ERA_ROWS` `:242-267`). What is actually true: `ROWS`
> holds the **1772 + 1856 marquee in-game roster, NOT the 47 presidents** ‚Äî so vcczar's presidential statlines
> (`histpres`, Washington‚ÜíBiden) are simply NOT authored in. The #240 action is therefore "**ADD ~47 president
> tuples to `ROWS`**" (which the loop folds into `CURATED_ROWS` as same-named OVERRIDES, beating the
> `congress-legislators` real-served rows per `CLAUDE.md` merge precedence), then regenerate the dataset
> (`bash scripts/fetchLegislators.sh && node scripts/legislatorsToDataset.mjs && npm run build`). **‚òÖ HARD PREREQ
> ‚Äî the #216 trait-name remap (the already-queued (c) quick-win of debt #85, cheap-fixes lane):** 14/50 `histpres` trait names mismatch the
> shipped `Trait` union (`types.ts:62-117`) ‚Äî `Charisma`‚Üí`Charismatic` (`:63`) + **13 with NO shipped analog**
> (`Bookkeeper, Cop, Disharmonious, Geostrategist, Illicit, Incoherent, Jurisprudence, Lackey, Late Bloomer, Low
> Brow, Military Leader, Pliable, Teflon`, each 0 hits). An importer CANNOT author the tuples until the
> rename/cut map exists, or they carry invalid `Trait` values. **‚òÖ The Misc/Demographics/Dynasty lines of each
> statline need debt #99's NEW FIELDS first** (so the FULL statline waits on the schema cluster ‚Äî but the
> skills/traits/ideology CORE can land as soon as the #216 remap does). **‚òÖ Balance:** every president maxes a single
> ability at **3** (no 4s/5s ‚Äî expect 0-3 for marquee curated pols, consistent with #204); the dataset is
> 2021-vintage (pre-#214 PV revamp) ‚Äî OPEN Q whether to trust raw skills or re-derive under the #214/#215 curve.
> **S-M; a pure author/DATA task; NOT a build-queue change ‚Äî runs standalone once the #216 remap lands.** game-mechanics
> ¬ß30.22.C.
>
> **‚ë° ‚òÖ‚òÖ #236 ‚Äî THE ALTERNATE-GOVERNMENT-FORM SCENARIO AXIS (debt #97; DESIGNED, 0% shipped; `GM‚áíApp`).**
> VERIFIED wholly unbuilt: a `src/` scan for `fascist|communist|theocra|governmentForm` returns ONE hit ‚Äî the
> `Theocrats` pressure-group faction STRING (`factions1856.ts:28`), NOT a USA-government-form state. The country
> can *transition INTO* a non-democratic form ‚Äî **Fascist / Communist / Theocratic** ‚Äî each unlocking its own
> Pres-Action/Legis-Prop/Scripted-Event menu + candidate pool, with a reusable cross-form **"authoritarian kit"**
> (ban-opposition-parties / empower-executive-via-amendment / ban-politicians-by-class / state-media /
> nationalize-sector / dissolve-Congress); Theocracy branches √ó5 by denomination. The fix: a
> `game.governmentForm` flag (Theocracy sub-keyed by denomination) that swaps in the form's menu + pool.
> **‚òÖ TWO HARD DEPENDENCIES that keep this OFF the near-term queue:** (a) it RIDES the **unbuilt #221
> per-administration content-primitive registry** ‚Äî there is NO Pres-Action/Legis-Prop/Gov-Action/Scripted-Event
> CONTENT SYSTEM in `src/` at all (`grep -iE 'presAction|legisProp|govAction|scriptedEvent' src/` = ZERO; shipped
> content is the differently-shaped `EraEvent`/`EventEntry` records), so there is nothing to attach a regime menu
> to; (b) **Theocracy is BLOCKED on the #239 religion demographics field** (debt #99 ‚Äî the engine must assign a
> denomination per pol + form denominational party-splits). The one kit primitive with a coupling target today is
> "empower-executive-via-amendment" (the `ConstitutionalArticles` core-params it mutates DO ship, `types.ts:1390-1397`
> / ¬ß24.x). **‚òÖ DISTINCT from #6/#354** (mid-era social-MOVEMENT factions / the `Theocrats` lobby ‚Äî a
> movement-faction is a lobby; a government-form is a scenario branch). **‚òÖ OPEN ‚Äî the transition TRIGGER + the
