> E1 still lead); these are mid-tier modern-engine items. Dependency-ordered:

**(A) #88 AUTOCRATIC-COUP terminal ‚Äî the FIRST PROVEN LOSS STATE. Build it as a
small end-condition item; HIGH priority for giving the game a real end-condition.**
Verified: the only game-over path is event-driven (`triggersGameEnd`,
`phaseRunners.ts:2871`); there is NO meter-driven per-phase game-end roll. The
terminal surface (`game.gameEnded` ‚Üí `GameOverScreen`, `App.tsx:341`) already
exists; only the trigger is missing. **`terror2000` is the FIRST record of any
game-over firing** (`POST 827, 829`) ‚Äî Honest-Gov at floor ‚Üí 20%/event-phase
"Autocratic Coup Ends America" ‚Üí GAME OVER ‚Üí resolves to the standard cumulative
winner. **Where it sits:** a small **end-condition epic** that builds the
per-event-phase game-end roll TABLE (game-mechanics ¬ß26.4's `rep1800` enumerated
set: Standard Coup / Economic Collapse / **Autocratic Coup (HonestGov=1, ~20%/phase,
CONFIRMED modern)** / Enemy-Takes-Defenseless-US / Civil-War-secession). It is the
**third shape of meter-driven endgame** alongside the APOCALYPSE 10-year countdown
clock (`pop` ¬ß9.1.5) ‚Äî build them as ONE end-condition module (a meter-watcher in the
Lingering phase that arms countdown clocks AND rolls the per-phase game-end table).
**Meter-driven (Honest-Gov), reachable in the modern era** (Honest-Gov is far easier
to crater than late-game MilPrep ‚Äî the "coups are dead once MilPrep is maxed" balance
note does NOT neutralize the Honest-Gov coup). **Size: S** (the roll table + one
`gameEnded` write + a HUD warning); pairs with ¬ß22.1 meter bank + the APOCALYPSE clock.
**It EXTENDS the existing endgame surface (debt #28), it does not need a new keystone.**

**(B) #152 WAR-DEFEAT resolution ‚Äî extends the war-engine epic (Phase-1 #3 /
#56/#106).** Verified: the resolver ends a war at `warScore ¬±50` but the defeat
branch (`phaseRunners.ts:3618-3620`) applies NO loss package. The loss package
(officers ‚àí1 Mil + ‚àí1 all-elections; **President ‚àí1 ALL future elections**;
Party-Pref crater) is the symmetric inverse of the victory bundle; multi-phase wars
(naval‚Üíground; "Invasion"‚Üí"Counter-Terrorism"; Afghanistan‚ÜíPhase II) carry across
half-terms. **Epic home: the generic `War` model (Phase-1 #3)** ‚Äî this is a REQUIRED
completion of the "war must RESOLVE" finding (DH-47: wars never end today), not a new
epic. The President-loss term COUPLES to the #18 election scorer (the ‚àí1 rides into
`calcStateVote` for all future elections, the inverse of the victory +1) ‚Äî so build it
AFTER the war engine has a real resolution path, and wire its President/officer terms
into the election-math epic. **Size: M within E3.**

**(C) #18 CANONICAL election scorer ‚Äî now fully SETTLED (V's 2-layer model); PROMOTE
to ready-to-build.** Verified: `calcStateVote` (`phaseRunners.ts:3685`) applies the
enthusiasm box raw with no ¬±3 cap and no universal-meter layer. Ted reversed his own

per-ideology METER modifier** (flat, BOTH parties, EVERY state, primary+general ‚Äî
"unless penalized" caveat GONE) **+ (b) the per-PARTY enthusiasm box** (moved by the
#51 reshuffle), composed additively then ¬±3-capped. **This removes a 3-batch-old
decision gate** ‚Äî #18 was user/state-scope-gated since batch 10; it now LEAVES the

**(D) Cabinet cluster #124 + #151 ‚Äî RE-SCOPE E16 (third time).** Verified:

#### 9.1.12 ‚òÖ‚òÖ The Reconstruction-fix + war-balance cluster (batch 16, `hd1`)

> **For the roadmap-planner: lift this section directly.** `hd1` ("A House
> Divided" Part 1) is the first HUMANS-on-both-sides Civil-War‚ÜíReconstruction run.
> It adds NO keystone and does NOT move the top of the queue (QW0 ‚Üí K0/K2 ‚Üí K3/K4 +
> scenarioBoot ‚Üí E1 still lead), but it hands the **canonical fix for the DH-29
> solo-blocker** that has gated every antebellum/CW/Reconstruction scenario since
> batch 8 ‚Äî turning DH-29 from an open blocker into a designed build target inside
> E3b. Dependency-ordered:

**(A) #156 ‚Äî the 4-plan Reconstruction model + the UNILATERAL-ADOPT prerequisite:
the canonical DH-29 fix; HIGHEST-VALUE Reconstruction target; folds into E3b.**
Verified: the entire Reconstruction subsystem is unbuilt (`Reconstruction|Ironclad|
readmission|Confederate|CSA` ‚Üí 0 hits in `src/types.ts`; only the Secession-Winter
loyalty scaffold exists, `types.ts:981/1149-1157/1481`). vcczar's restart rewrite
(`POST 2692-2694`) is an **authored design**, not just an open gap: **4 plans
(No-plan / 10% / Ironclad-Wade-Davis / Military-district) usable by BOTH the
President AND Congress**, gated by *"a plan adopted by Congress OR by the
President"* ‚Äî the President can adopt UNILATERALLY, so solo AND deadlocked-human
Reconstruction always resolve. **This is the single highest-value Reconstruction
build target** because it UNBLOCKS DH-29 (the named solo-blocker). **Where it
lands:** add `game.reconstruction = { plan; adoptedBy: 'congress'|'president'|null;
startYear }`; the 4 plans become ActionRegistry rows (K2) callable from BOTH the
exec path (`runPhase_2_8_1_Executive`, `phaseRunners.ts:3632`) AND the legislation
pipeline; the plan-adopted gate fronts every per-state readmission; the 15th-Amd
**+2 Deep-South / +1 other-seceded time-boxed bias-while-active** rides the
`calcStateVote` bias term (`phaseRunners.ts:3709-3711`, distinct from static
`State.bias`). It SUPERSEDES the ¬ß9.1.6 options menu and **removes the K5
soft-dependency for the solo case** (the player-President adopts directly; CPU
handler #2 is still wanted for a CPU-President / Congressional adoption but is no
longer a HARD gate). It composes with #148 (`gild1868`'s 20-yr Reconstruction
timer + Solid-South sunset ‚Äî same `game.reconstruction` block) and #57 (the onset
cascade). **Size: M‚ÄìL within E3b.** game-mechanics ¬ß23.4.1.

**(B) #155 ‚Äî war-balance pass: enemy-strength + battle-size + Officer-Mil cap +
per-theater scoring; EXTENDS the war engine (E3 / #56/#152), bounded by the
1772-RevWar-winnable floor.** Verified: the shipped resolver
`runPhase_2_7_2_Military` (`phaseRunners.ts:3593-3627`) is flat `milPower*10 + d100`
(`:3602-3605`) with `enemyPower = 1 + Math.random()*4` (`:3603`, a random
placeholder ‚Äî NOT a real enemy-strength term), `warScore ¬±50` end (`:3615-3620`);
the generic `War` shell (`types.ts:1532-1546`) is single-`warScore`, no theaters.
`hd1`'s critique (BOTH human players + the GA, `POST 1000-1004`): the end-multiplier
is too high (changed LIVE 1.0‚Üí0.5), Officer-Mil dominates (a 5-Mil officer > all
other terms combined), there is no enemy-strength term, no battle-SIZE weighting,
and a ‚àí1 loss is too small; scoring was made per-theater LIVE. **The hard constraint
(`POST 1004`): do NOT over-harden** ‚Äî the 1772 start fights the Revolutionary War,
which is a GAME-OVER on loss (`revolutionaryWar.ts`), so a harder engine risks
ending games before they begin; war "isn't the main focus." **Where it lands:** the
balance terms bind on the generic `War` model the war engine (#56) already builds ‚Äî
**route enemy-strength through `rng.ts`** (this also closes the `:3603`
determinism leak, debt #2/#16). It PAIRS tightly with #152 (the batch-15 war-DEFEAT
loss package) on the same model. **Resolve the three cross-run OPEN QUESTIONS while
here** (all human DECISION-GATES, all tuning): the war-end multiplier (1.0 vs the
LIVE-adopted 0.5); the naval phase (a 3-naval-wins HARD gate per #56/Part 2 vs the
naval-then-land continue-roll chain `hd1` ran); the war-hero presidential bonus
(permanent +1-all-elections per Part 2 vs the one-term +1 `hd1` applied in 1864 ‚Äî
this term also feeds the #18 election scorer). **Size: M within E3.** game-mechanics
¬ß23.3.

**(C) #157 ‚Äî CSA-government seeding: small; folds into the #58 secession + war
epic; needed for the CSA-victory branch.** Verified: no CSA government in code ‚Äî
`startWar` (`phaseRunners.ts:2978-2981`) + `applyEffect` (`:3240-3253`) only spin up
the generic `War` shell; no CSA Pres/VP/cabinet/general seeding, no Secessionists
pool. `hd1` rules define only CSA Pres/VP/Sr-General (Pres = random among seceded
Command-holders; Comm-Gen = sole seceded Military-Leader); the GM improvised a full
cabinet "for flavor" (`POST 893-894`). **Where it lands:** a CSA-government seeding
spec (cabinet + multiple generals/admirals from the seceded Command/Military pool)
inside the #58 epic ‚Äî required for vcczar's CSA-victory branch (`POST 2692`: seceded
pols removed, Unionists move to the nearest loyal state, eventual reintegration).
**Depends on the per-pol Southern-Unionist/Secessionist gate being built (also
unbuilt ‚Äî DH-64). Size: S.**

**(D) DH-64 / #158 ‚Äî `Southern Unionist` dataset audit: XS; joins the #120 dataset
umbrella.** Verified: `Southern Unionist` is NOWHERE in `src/` as a trait (the only

(`POST 1446, 2682`): Union-officer-settled-South, Black-Republican, and Northern-

drafts. **Where it lands:** a `scripts/seedDataset.mjs` CURATED_ROWS audit (the trait

#120 dataset-umbrella pass (the `Southern Unionist` column on VA/MS/FL/Border
draftees).

**Does the DH-29 fix change priority?** DH-29 has been the named solo-blocker for
every antebellum/CW/Reconstruction-bearing scenario. With #156 now a **designed fix
in hand**, the Reconstruction epic (E3b) becomes **more attractive and less risky** ‚Äî
its hardest open question (how does solo Reconstruction ever resolve?) is answered,
so E3b's readmission half moves from "design-then-build" to "build the authored

scenarioBoot ‚Üí E1.** What changes is E3b's *internal confidence*: build the war
engine (#56 + #155 + #152) first, then the Reconstruction half (#57 onset + #156
4-plan model) with the solo-blocker already solved.

**Net for the planner:** DH-29 changes CATEGORY (CPU-only ‚Üí structural; open-blocker
‚Üí designed-fix) but stays inside E3b; #156 is the new highest-value Reconstruction
target (M‚ÄìL); #155 is an M war-engine extension bounded by the RevWar floor, pairing

open questions are tuning, not blockers). **3rd Civil-War run + 5th antebellum

cluster. **Top of queue UNCHANGED.**

are deep-modern subsystems that build last.

### 9.3 Design divergences ‚Äî keep shipped or refactor to forum?

Rules where the forum and the engine genuinely disagree (mechanics ¬ß19.1, now
**#1‚Äì#21**, plus the separate meter-model item). These are **decisions, not
feature-adds**. My defensible call per item. **Numbering matches mechanics ¬ß19.1:**
#1‚Äì#3 batch 1, #4‚Äì#6 batch 2, #7 (SCOTUS) and #8 (cabinet) batch 3, **#9
(relocation cap) is batch 4**, **#10‚Äì#13 batch 5**, **#14‚Äì#17 batch 6**,
**#18‚Äì#21 batch 7**; the meter model is its own unnumbered item (mechanics
¬ß21.8). **#9 is also BUG-0.** **#13 is structural** ‚Äî it points at ¬ß25.15 (the
architectural CPU-AI gaps) and is the call to make K5 a keystone. **#14 is the
NEW endgame model** (meter-driven APOCALYPSE clock ‚Äî Phase-1). **#15-#17 are all
refactor calls that fold into existing planned work** (E16, K2, K4 respectively).
**#18 is the batch-7 era-model reframe ‚Äî the biggest one ‚Äî but it folds into
K3/K4** (it is a refinement of the keystone specs, not a new keystone; see
¬ß9.1.5). **#19 (ideology-as-circle) is a foundational refactor behind an
era-gated flag** (¬ß9.1.7). **#20 (legislature-chosen electors) extends #5.**
**#21 (procedure-bills-bypass-veto-misrouted, DH-31) is a small verify-and-fix.**

| # | Divergence | Recommendation | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Bill scoring** ‚Äî shipped: `factionCenter` √ó `cardBiasPerDelta 0.03` per matching card. Forum: ¬±50/¬±100/¬±150 per card per bill, summed across **every** ideology+lobby+interest card. | **Refactor to forum**, in two phases. **Phase A** (small): keep `cardVoteBias` as the **vote-probability** input (well-tuned for floor votes); add a separate `legislativeScoring(bill, faction)` points number on a new `Faction.score` ledger. Voting odds vs. leaderboard scoring are different jobs. **Phase B** (later): decide whether to re-tune `cardVoteBias` to be per-card-aware. | Forum ¬±50/100/150 is *scoring*, not *voting*. Shipped `cardVoteBias` is fine for probability and invasive to rebalance. Land the leaderboard first. **Size: M. Risk: low** (additive). |
| 2 | **Carpetbagger trait grant** ‚Äî shipped: 4-step probability ladder. Forum: auto-grant on alt-state moves. | **Refactor to forum (auto-grant).** Same PR as ladder removal; add the 10-year expiry + alt-state-cap exemption (`1772s`). | More legible; removes a dial that doesn't carry weight. **Size: XS. Risk: low.** |
| 3 | **Conversion targeting** ‚Äî shipped: multiplicative willingness table. Forum: hard gates on `Can Party Flip` (cross-party) / `Pliable + adjacent ideology` (same-party) + 5/10/15% rates. | **Keep shipped for now; revisit after the faction-personality drift rules are formalized.** | Both defensible. If the rule-driven personality system lands and emits `Can Party Flip` cleanly, the forum model gets attractive. Until then the shipped table works. **Size: M if changed. Risk: medium** (load-bearing dial). |
| **4** | **Era-event scheduling** *(batch 2, biggest 1772 fork)* ‚Äî shipped: `coreSpine` precondition graph (`eraEvents1772.ts:23`; `selectEraGraphNode` `eraGraph.ts:107`) fires spine nodes regardless of roll. Forum: historical-year sort + per-half-term roll (`‚â§ %-to-fire`) up to a per-era cap, with spill into anytime-events. | **Decision for the human ‚Äî but if undecided, keep the graph and *layer* the cap.** Concretely: keep `coreSpine` for the *causal* backbone (Gaspee‚ÜíCommittees‚ÜíTea Act), and add the forum's **per-half-term cap + probabilistic minor-event roll + spill** *on top* for non-spine nodes. This is closer to additive than a rewrite and preserves the strongest property of each model. | The two genuinely produce different sequences (`1772s` B1). A full switch to year-sort discards the graph's authored causality and the `Predicate` infrastructure (`evalPredicate`, counterfactual branches) that already works. The hybrid keeps both. **But flag it loudly: resolve before authoring federalism graphs**, or the federalism spine is authored twice. **Size: M (hybrid) / L (full switch). Risk: medium.** |
| **5** | **Per-state EC method** *(batch 2)* ‚Äî shipped: `calcStateVote('presGeneral')` (`phaseRunners.ts:3752`) resolves every state by popular vote. Forum: per-state legislature-vs-popular elector selection. | **Refactor to forum (add the mode).** It is a genuine historical mechanic that is *decisive* in early elections (CT/GA/MA/NJ/NY/RI/SC in 1796). Add `State.electionMethod`; legislature-states award EV by seated-legislature majority. | Not a balance dial ‚Äî a fidelity feature federalism *requires*. Additive: popular-vote states are unchanged. **Size: M. Risk: low‚Äìmedium** (touches the EC tally path). |
| **6** | **Generic war resolver** *(batch 2)* ‚Äî shipped: flat `milPower√ó10 + d100` one-roll (`phaseRunners.ts:3593-3627`); rich battle system is Rev-War-only. Forum: additive Chance-of-Success + warscore/momentum/√ó2 + confirmation cascade, for *every* war. | **Refactor to forum (one generic `War` model).** Generalize the rich path; fold the 1772 Rev-War loop in as one configured instance. The flat resolver is a placeholder, not a designed alternative. | The forum model is corroborated across 2 eras and the Rev War already implements most of it ‚Äî this is *consolidation*, not new design. **Size: M‚ÄìL. Risk: medium** (touches both war paths; do after K0 so the rolls are seeded). |
| **7** | **SCOTUS model** *(batch 3)* ‚Äî shipped: 2.5.3 picks 1 of 4 hardcoded titles + nudges `partyPreference ¬±0.1` (`phaseRunners.ts:3398-3414`); 2.8.2 age-‚â•75 retire + same-party backfill (`:3648-3671`). Forum: a named-Justice docket with compel-vote/retire, dynamic court size + packing, 64/60% confirmation, ideology reveal + 10-yr drift, ruling‚Üílaw-deactivation. | **Build the forum subsystem from scratch** ‚Äî there is **no balance-tuned court to preserve** (the shipped court is a stub, debt #18). Mechanics ¬ß19.1 calls it "a replacement"; verified, it is *net-new over a stub*, which is the easier case (nothing to migrate, just superseded). | Corroborated only in `modern` (1 era) but deeply specified. **Far-end** (deep-modern); case content is per-era data. Gates BUG-2. **Size: M‚ÄìL. Risk: medium** (compel mechanics are intricate). |

### 9.4 The presentation track (A1‚ÄìA8) ‚Äî a parallel workstream

Batch 2's `1772s` thread adds a **net-new product/UI surface** (`game-context.md`
A1‚ÄìA8): procedural portraits, an ideology‚Üícolor palette, sport-card infoboxes, a
styled scoreboard, battle-cards, election maps, era-correct office titles, legacy
lines, and a narration-voice bar. **Architectural opinion:**

> **A9 is NOT on this track.** The batch-3 `A9` (persist/auto-fill House slates +
> committee rosters) reads like a presentation item but is a **state-shape + engine
> UX requirement** (scaling wall b) ‚Äî it persists new snapshot fields and changes
> the staffing phases. It lives on the **engine track** (¬ß9.2 near-term row), not
> here. Batch 3 also *extends* A1 (the procedural-portrait system must cover
> *generated* pols, tying P2 to procedural pol generation) and A7 (the map renderer
> must auto-produce the 53-state map + per-state popular-vote atlas the modern GM
> hand-builds).

- **This is mostly independent of the engine queue and should run as a SEPARATE
  TRACK by a different workstream.** Almost all of A1‚ÄìA8 are **read-only views
  over snapshot data that already exists** (roster, congress, war state, election
  results, office history). They don't depend on the keystones, the action
  libraries, or the era work ‚Äî they depend on the *data already being there*,
  which it is. The single exception is A4 (battle-card additive-odds breakdown),
  which is most useful *after* the generic-war epic (#6) surfaces the itemized
  odds; build the card shell early, wire the real numbers when #6 lands.
- **It is several epics, not one.** Group them by surface and dependency:
  - **P0 ‚Äî ideology‚Üícolor palette (K1.5).** The cross-cutting foundation. **Do
    first** ‚Äî A2/A3/A7 all consume it. XS.
  - **P1 ‚Äî politician card + roster/congress restyle (A2, A3, A5, A6).** The
    "sport-card" infobox, the persistent scoreboard, era-correct office titles,
    honorific-memory/legacy lines. Small additive `Politician` fields (A6) +
    components. M.
  - **P2 ‚Äî portrait pipeline (A1).** The chunkiest and most *different*: a
    procedural-portrait system for the ~18.5k long tail, **hard-constrained to
    CK2-style generated assets ‚Äî no AI imagery or text in the shipped product**
    (`game-context.md` A1; AI only as a throwaway proof-of-concept). This is an
    asset-pipeline + layered-sprite renderer epic, closer to the dataset-pipeline
    (¬ß7) in spirit than to the engine. It can proceed fully in parallel; the
    `Politician.portrait` field is the only engine-track touchpoint. L.
    **Batch-3 linkage:** P2 must cover **procedurally-generated** pols, not just
    the real dataset ‚Äî the dataset runs out in deep-modern (scaling wall a), so
    P2 and the engine-track procedural-pol generator share the demographic model.
    The portrait system should consume the generator's demographic output. Don't
    build P2 assuming a closed real-person set.
  - **P3 ‚Äî maps + iconography (A7).** Election-result maps + era-correct flags.
    Best *after* more states exist (gilded/modern), but the renderer can be
    prototyped on 1856. **Batch-3 raises the bar:** the modern GM hand-builds
    53-state yapms maps + a per-state popular-vote % atlas every presidential
    election ‚Äî the build should **auto-generate** these. M.
  - **P4 ‚Äî narration voice (A8).** A `log.ts` output-quality bar, not a schema
    change. Smallest; ongoing. XS‚ÄìS.
- **Hard constraint to encode now:** the **no-AI-in-product** stance (A1) is a
  ratified-pending design call but should be treated as a constraint on the
  portrait pipeline from day one ‚Äî building an AI-runtime portrait system and
  then ripping it out is the wasteful path.

> **Why a parallel track is the real lever.** The engine track (keystones ‚Üí
> subsystems) is a long dependency chain bottlenecked on a few people who know the
> engine. The presentation track shares almost no code with it and can be staffed
> independently. Running them concurrently remains the single biggest schedule
> win. The only sync points are a handful of small additive `Politician`/`Party`
> fields (portrait, honorific/legacy lines, formedYear, homeStates) and two
> deeper handoffs: the **A4 battle-card ‚Üî generic-war** odds breakdown, and the
> **P2 portrait pipeline ‚Üî engine procedural-pol-generation** demographic model
> (P2 must render *generated* pols, not just the real dataset).

### 9.5 Multiplayer ‚Äî last, with one caveat

**Multiplayer is still last**, but the action-library refactor (K2) changes the
planning. Specifically:

- **Hot-seat / sequential** multiplayer reuses the existing `needsPlayerInput`
  mechanism (`engine.ts:9`). It pauses mid-phase per decider ‚Äî extend to
  round-robin across human factions before `advancePhase`. **But the
  player-input modalities are exactly the (now six) action libraries** + the
  primary/convention loops. Hot-seat needs those libraries to *exist as picker
  UIs* before it's coherent. **So: hot-seat goes after K2 + the libraries land.**
  `fed` + `gilded` + `modern` + **`drums` (all-CPU)** confirm solo and
  multiplayer are **two modes of one engine**, not two games (`modern` had a
  player take over a CPU faction mid-campaign and win; `drums` flips it to the
  other extreme ‚Äî all factions CPU, with humans rolling dice and patching
  rules). **K5's handler suite serves both cases identically**: a CPU faction
  in a hot-seat game uses the same handlers as a CPU faction in a solo game,
  and a human taking over a CPU faction mid-campaign just disables the handler
  for that faction. **K5 + the handlers are the load-bearing piece that makes
  the take-over-a-CPU-faction mode work** at all.
- **Async forum-style** is a backend epic. IndexedDB is per-browser (`db.ts`);
  shared state needs a server (or CRDT/host-authoritative sync) the repo does not
  have today. Scope it as its own L‚ÄìXL epic.
- **Determinism (K0) is hard-blocking for either model** ‚Äî clients/turns must
  derive identical roll outcomes from the same seed.
- **Whole-snapshot clone + save** (debt #6) becomes a real bottleneck once
  multiple players are poking state. Expect to move to per-store/delta writes.

### 9.6 One-line ordering (for `roadmap.md`)

> **Two tracks run in parallel.** The **engine track** is the long dependency
> chain; the **presentation track** (A1‚ÄìA8, *not* A9) shares almost no code and
> should be staffed separately (¬ß9.4). Within a track, order is top-to-bottom.
> **Batch-3 change:** three cross-cutting items pulled forward into the engine
> track + a far-end modern epic appended after gilded.
> **Batch-4 change:** BUG-0 added to the FRONT; the Civil-War / Reconstruction
> epic inserted right after generic war; 1856-arc election additions slotted
> next to the convention work; #54 marked READY; per-era point-banking folded
> into K3.
> **Batch-5 change:** **K5 (CpuController scaffold) added as a late-keystone**
> (after K0 + K2, parallel with K3/K4 + federalism); **the CPU handler suite
> added as a peer subsystem epic** (~15 lightweight PRs once K5 lands); **the
> cabinet-confirmation system (DH-23) sized down to XS-S** because the system
> doesn't exist yet (build in the right shape from day one); **Iron-Fist split
> (¬ß25.9) added as an independent M epic**; **divergences #10-#13 added**;
> **contingent-election rules #84/#10 moved to PARKING LOT** until authored.
> **Batch-6 change:** **APOCALYPSE meter-driven endgame added at Phase-1 #6**
> (folds in with meter-model generalization ‚Äî same code area, M-sized);
> **K4 gains the `BootSheet` schema as the cross-cutting build constraint**
> (single shape for 1788 / 1856 / 2012 boots; per-`{era, startYear}` state
> roster keying); **K2 gains `requires?: AmendmentPredicate` from day one**
> (divergence #16, cheap if early); **E16 cabinet refactor gains dynamic
> seat-list** (divergence #15); **modern era epic splits into TWO scenarios**
> (`scenario1948` continuation + `scenario2012` fresh-boot); **CPU handler #2
> consumes the conditional-vote-rules primitive** (`pop` POST 1111);
> **divergences #14-#17 added**; **DH-25 career-track bootstrap moved to
> PARKING LOT ‚Äî BLOCKS modern scenario** until authored.
> **Batch-7 change:** **‚òÖ K3/K4 re-specced for the era-model reframe**
> (`advanceEra` becomes CONDITION-DRIVEN ‚Äî game-state/meter/territory, per
> half-term ‚Äî NOT year-boundary-driven; content gated on `eraBand` + territory
> ownership, not calendar; divergence #18; reconciles #68 + ¬ß26 + ¬ß27.1 ‚Äî see
> ¬ß9.1.5); **‚òÖ‚òÖ the Reconstruction SOLO-BLOCKER (DH-29) added as a hard BUILD
> REQUIREMENT on E3b** (the Strict/Ironclad plan can NEVER pass with CPU ‚Üí
> solo Reconstruction unwinnable; E3b must ship a CPU-passable readmission path
> ‚Äî see ¬ß9.1.6); **ideology-as-CIRCLE added as a Phase-1 foundation refactor**

> #20; slavery-flag + Cohens #94; Second Bank recharter clock #95; statehood-by-
> bill + unorganized-territory gating #95); **era-events-predating-start MERGED
> into BUG-1** (LIVE-confirmed by the LA-Purchase-dropped-at-1800-start episode)

> classified**; **`scenario1800` noted as an optional later boot sheet**.
> **Batch-8 change (mostly CONFIDENCE + small placements ‚Äî NO re-sequence):**

> military-branch subsystem** (¬ß9.1.8 / ¬ß28.2; verified: only `revolutionaryWar.ts`
> exists) ‚Äî the Cold War is the **generic war engine (Phase-1 #3) RELABELED +

> simple majority** (`phaseRunners.ts:3562`, `yea > nay` both chambers, no cloture;
> the only supermajority is the Articles-2/3-of-states CC path at

> **impeachment + succession ruleset** (DH-54 ‚Äî never in the rules doc; corroborates
> DH-33). Other batch-9 DHs classify into existing epics (DH-50/53 ‚Üí era-event
> scheduling + bill-catalog; DH-52 ‚Üí election-math; DH-51 ‚Üí modern dataset balance;
> DH-55 ‚Üí 3rd-party #48; DH-57 ‚Üí convention/handler #5). **‚òÖ The KB now spans a
> CONTINUOUS 1772‚Üí2020 timeline** (this 1948 thread is the predecessor of the
> `modern` 2004-2020 thread, joined at the 2004 election).
> **Batch-11 change (`arkzag`, the 1820‚Üí1840 FULL-ARC continuation of `dem1820` ‚Äî
> the first late-game capture; THREE new content systems that EXTEND existing epics,
> ONE fork RESOLVED + promoted, ONE new fork, two sized bugs; NO new keystone, NO

> **‚òÖ‚òÖ‚òÖ Batch-27 change (`redbutton` / `8bc0231c` ‚Äî "The Big Red Button: 1960 Playtest", the corpus's
> FIRST Cold-War / Civil-Rights source and the first MODERN-ERA scenario boot stress-tested at scale [a

> richest forward-played election spec):**
> **‚òÖ‚òÖ #184/DH-72 PLATFORM-OVERPOWER NERF ‚Äî the HEADLINE finding, designer-authoritative (vcczar +
> MrPotatoTed both played + agreed *"platform is way too powerful"*, `redbutton#POST 538-540`).** A
> hand-driven 1960 run produced a **Bricker (R) 492-40 landslide over JFK** off a `+1`-rule-read-as-`+3`
> board-wide misapplication (DH-72) on top of a bogus `+3` platform bonus; recompute ‚Üí 284-251 ‚Üí ~281-129
> near-tie (`#POST 532, 587, 603-607`). **‚òÖ The fix splits into TWO items with DIFFERENT availability:**
> **(A) DH-72 = a TUNING PATCH at `calcStateVote` that is INDEPENDENTLY SHIPPABLE TODAY and a QUICK-WIN.**
> Re-verified shipped-state (UNCHANGED): `calcStateVote` (`phaseRunners.ts:3709-3711`) is the single
> election scorer = `50 + baseLean*5 + partyPref*5 + enthusiasm*2 + pv*0.1 + factionBias + traitBonus +
> (Math.random()-0.5)*8`, with NO ¬±3 cap, NO per-state ideology gate, raw `Math.random` (a continuous ¬±4
> jitter, NOT a die). The designer-blessed sub-fixes that bind HERE and need NO new system: **narrow the
> jitter (the d6‚Üíd3 intent ‚Äî compress the luck band) AND route it through `rng.ts`** (folds the debt #1/#3
> determinism leak); **re-weight the historical state-bias term `baseLean*5`‚Üí the +3/+5 band behind a
> `game.biasMode?` toggle** (historical-plausible vs "go-crazy"); **HIDE the raw formulas in the UI**
> (anti-collusion ‚Äî show only which ideologies like a position + polls/maps). These pair with the
> already-queued **QW3 ¬±3 cap (debt #1)** + the **#18 2-layer scorer** and belong in the QUICK-WIN bucket ‚Äî
> **add the jitter-narrow+seed, the bias-toggle, and the hide-the-math as a QW alongside QW3/#18.** **(B) the
> +2/+1/‚àí1 PLATFORM meter-cap (#184) clamps a term that DOES NOT EXIST yet** ‚Äî `grep platform\|plank in
> src/engine` = ZERO; the platform engine (5 planks √ó per-card faction scoring √ó 4-step comparison) is the
> SAME unbuilt object as #181 (`summer2021`). So the cap is a **BUILD-INTO constraint on the convention
> epic, not a shippable patch** ‚Äî build the platform scorer with the +2/+1/‚àí1 cap + hide-the-math from day
> one; never ship the uncapped forum scorer. debt #64 (DH-72), #65 (#184). game-mechanics ¬ß15.3.5, ¬ß28.6.

> a player gerrymandered GOP weights to bury MacArthur ‚Äî Ted holds rigging is INTENDED), kingmakers-affect-
> conventions-not-primaries (+1), 67%-first-ballot (mutable), **two-leaders force a KINGMAKER-WEIGHTED
> rule-change vote** (‚Üí50%+1/WTA/proportional), **least-delegates-first interballot order**, the **100%-
> delegate rule + WALKOUT** (persisted into 1964), and the **KINGMAKER-WEIGHTED VP VOTE** (PL+nominee each

> sibling that UNBLOCKS the PV epic] / `housepoll` [the House-model design poll = #219 origin + #191 engine] /

> one XS item and the PV epic moves gated ‚Üí ready). The two headlines are a TINY high-leverage REMOVAL and an
> EPIC becoming buildable.**
>
> **‚ëÝ ‚òÖ‚òÖ REMOVE `pv*0.1` FROM ELECTIONS ‚Äî the headline call (debt #85; XS quick-win; `GM‚áíApp`).** `calcStateVote`
> scores each candidate `50 + baseLean*5 + partyPref*5 + enthusiasm*2 + pv*0.1 + factionBias + traitBonus +
> (Math.random()-0.5)*8` (`phaseRunners.ts:3709`, `pv = c.pvCache` `:3699`). The designer + room RULED *PV must
> NOT feed elections* ‚Äî pricing PV into the vote DOUBLE-COUNTS the traits/abilities already in BOTH PV and the
> election roll (`groupthinkpv` c6f6dcc7 POST 36-38; a highest-PV election-bonus proposal was REJECTED). **This
> SUPERSEDES the batch-30/31 "re-tune `pv*0.1`" note** ‚Äî the term should not be there at all. **Delete `+ pv * 0.1`
> at `:3709`.** It is **XS and high-leverage twice over:** (a) it pairs with **QW19's election-determinism work at
> the SAME line** (route the `:3711` `Math.random` jitter through `rng.ts` in one touch ‚Äî debt #1/#71); (b) it
> DECOUPLES the #214 PV reweight from elections, so the reweight can re-fit freely without distorting margins.
> **‚òÖ Tech-lead judgment-call (one line, flagged not auto-done):** the 2.9.1 primary scorer also adds raw
> `pvCache` (`:3733`); the primary is candidate *selection* (the CPU picking its nominee = the AI-utility use the
> ruling ENDORSES), so I read `:3733` as defensible and remove ONLY `:3709`. ‚Üí **`pv*0.1`-removal JOINS the
> cheap-fixes lane** (below) alongside #216 + DH-77.
>
> **‚ë° ‚òÖ‚òÖ THE PV EPIC IS NOW BUILDABLE END-TO-END ‚Äî #214 + #215 + #220 UNBLOCKED (debt #85; `GM‚áíApp`).** The
> long-flagged gate ‚Äî "the trait EFFECTS live in an un-ingested thread" ‚Äî is LIFTED: `groupthinkpv` IS that

> flat ¬±4/‚àí5 (`pv.ts:76-77`), `command√ó10` (`:74`), `Math.max(0)` (`:88`); the **trait-EFFECTS-in-PV layer is 0%
> built** (verified ‚Äî `pv.ts` has NO trait‚Üíoffice-conditional logic). Direction now RULED: ability curve
> **Command√ó15 ¬∑ Legislative/Governing/Admin√ó10 ¬∑ Military/Judicial√ó5** per level (+ expertise +5 each); display
> **0‚Äì100, 50 = average pol**. **Sequence the epic:** **#216 trait-name remap FIRST** (S quick-win, already
> queued, BLOCKS the rest) ‚Üí then **#214 tiers (M-L re-fit) + #215 curve (M) + #220 0‚Äì100/floor-offset (XS+S) +
> remove `pv*0.1` (XS, item ‚ëÝ) + BUILD the trait-EFFECTS-in-PV layer (M-L:** office-conditional ability buffs,

> the ¬ß14.1.y #217 implementation engine). **The ONLY remaining human-call across the whole epic = the
> office-magnitude ‚Üî 0‚Äì100 display-scale reconciliation.** **NET: the PV epic moves from gated ‚Üí READY-AFTER-#216;
> office-PV is scoped to the active-draft/persuasion AI, NOT the vote (consistent with item ‚ëÝ).**

> Actions** (president-only, inactive pre-Constitution) / **Governor Actions** (state-scoped), with era/date

> parked "AMPU 2."** vcczar then built an expanded apportionment ladder. Shipped state: flat
> `representativeIds: string[]` (`types.ts:1327`) ‚Äî NO size enum, NO EV-ladder, NO vote-weight, NO census. **The
> build (#219):** US Reps = historical EVs‚àí2 (census recompute = first election after a decade) ‚Üí Focus Reps via
> an **EV-derived 1-10 ladder** (‚â§5‚Üí1 ‚Ä¶ ‚â•46‚Üí10, SUPERSEDES #55's flat `(EV‚àí2)/5`) ‚Üí **vote power =
> `floor(stateUSReps / focusReps)`, remainder ‚Üí highest-Legislative rep**. **#191 (now authored, `housepoll`
> POST 17-19):** a per-seat STATIC `deviantPartyBias` (overrides only the party term) + the minority-seat
> guarantee (5+ Focus Reps ‚Üí a Bias+2 minority seat) + the 25/25/50 new-seat / seat-gain / seat-loss rolls keyed
> to **era-gated growth lobbies**. Both ride a NEW **per-seat struct** (the vote-power remainder + the deviant

> **‚ë§ ‚òÖ #241 VARIABLE TERM LENGTH + #242 AMENDABLE ARTICLES + DH-78 TRAIT-COMBO (debt #101/#102/#103;

> (`phases.ts:53-58`), NO term-length field anywhere; a passed 6-/2-yr-presidency Amendment would break every
> predicate. vcczar flagged this in his own voice. **This is the SECOND independent reason to replace the
> year-predicates** (the FIRST is #92's era-advance, debt #5/¬ß9.1.5) ‚Äî so build the predicate-replacement ONCE
> inside K3's `advanceEra` work and a per-office term-length field serves both. **M, but the cost is shared with

> Amendment rewrites an article on 75%-state ratification) + NEW fields (suffrage/EC-toggle/houseSizeCap/term-
> length). **M; downstream of #221 (an Amendment IS a Legis-Prop) + #241; the data shape already exists, the
> build is the mutation PATH + new fields + a `DB_VERSION` default.** **DH-78:** `TraitElectionRule`

> a consume-side check in `applyTraitElectionBonus` `electionEffects.ts:16`); GATED on #216 (which adds
> `Military Leader` to the union); verify it wasn't deliberately cut first.**

> The two MOST ACTIONABLE headlines are (‚ëÝ) #247 ‚Äî the state-lean realignment finding that is #92's TWIN (folds

> **‚ëÝ ‚òÖ‚òÖ #247 EVENT-DRIVEN STATE-LEAN REALIGNMENT = #92's TWIN (debt #109; M; FOLDS INTO K3/`advanceEra`; `GM‚áíApp`).**
> VERIFIED: `State.bias` is a single STATIC scalar (`types.ts:1324`; VT 1.6 / MA 1.4 / ME 1.2 `states1856.ts:5-10`,
> header "positive = Red lean, negative = Blue lean" `:3`) with **NO decade/era bias schedule and NO realignment-event
> layer.** The only dynamics are the ~30%/term governor drift `(governing‚àí1)*0.05` clamped ¬±5 (`phaseRunners.ts:3388-3389`)
> + a region anytime-event `stateBias` effect **clamped ¬±1, un-time-boxed** (`phaseRunners.ts:2608-2616`); `bias` feeds
> the √ó5 election lever `baseLean` (`:3697`) + ideology-drift (`:903-906`). Draft party-assignment is ALSO static ‚Äî
> `recordDraftPick` sets `p.partyId = faction.partyId` (`:55-60`) ‚Äî so landmark events CANNOT re-key incoming-pol party
> today. **The design (the 1928 exhibit): year/era-keyed bias modifiers mis-fire when the timeline diverges (crash never
> fired in-game, yet bias swung blue ‚Üí Dems unrealistically swept 1930).** Fix = #92 projected onto state-lean: an
> era/decade base-lean schedule PLUS an event-keyed, TIME-BOXED (‚âà10-yr) realignment modifier selected by region OR by
> the underutilized `State.industries` map, stacking on the base; lift the `:2608` region effect beyond ¬±1 + add expiry;
> make the draft pipeline read landmark-event flags. **‚òÖ SAME CLASS as #92 (debt #5, era-as-content-band) and #241
> (debt #101, year-predicate replacement) ‚Äî game-state-keyed, NOT year-keyed.** Build it EVENT-KEYED FROM THE START
> (per #92) INSIDE the K3 era-system work ‚Äî do NOT replicate the spreadsheet's year-tab pinning. **Supersedes the
> DH-34 "ship static biases" stance for the alt-history aspiration; DH-34 stays the cheap fallback if balance permits.**
> Keep realignment DISTINCT from the meter inputs (no double-count). game-mechanics ¬ß11.4.1, ¬ß15.1.
> **‚ë° ‚òÖ‚òÖ #186 BOOTSHEET STAT-SEED PROOF (debt #68; SHARPENS the K4/scenarioBoot spec; `GM‚áíApp`).** The `redbutton1960`
> RESTART is the cleanest LIVE PROOF a boot requirement is load-bearing: the original 1960 run was STOPPED because the
> cabinet was seeded with STARTER-LEVEL stats ("all at starter levels and it tanked all the meters since we didn't have
> any Cabinet members above 3", POST 1) ‚Üí the low-Admin officers drove the #179 lingering meter-roll into crisis at turn
> 1, UNPLAYABLE until the GM hand-RE-STATTED the whole cabinet to mid/peak. VERIFIED: scenario boots pull cabinet from
> the hand-authored seed roster or leave the seat `null` (`scenario1856.ts:127-142`) ‚Äî **NO per-office/per-era stat-seed
> table.** **NEW K4 REQUIREMENT: a deterministic per-office, per-era `cabinetStatSeed` table** so the sitting government
> boots at era-appropriate mid/peak strength, not draftee starter levels (the per-era curve is an OPEN Q ‚Äî the reset was
> eyeballed). Folds into the `BootSheet` schema (debt #68 g; ¬ß9.1.9) alongside the existing #187/DH-25/asOfYear fields.
> **#179 cabinet‚Üímeter coupling re-confirmed at WHOLE-CABINET scale.** game-mechanics ¬ß28.1, ¬ß28.6.

> because the type + interpreter exist; the M-part is auditing era-event call sites for regression. **Schedule it as the
> content-engine track's FIRST item, with/just-ahead of K4, so registry content carries preconditions from day one.** debt

> **‚òÖ TWO DIGEST CLAIMS the codebase CONTRADICTED ‚Äî flag for the human:** **(1) #219 weighted House votes is NOT shipped.**
> The `8f12ac05` digest asserts `housepoll` (Aug 2022) "RESOLVED + built #219." **FALSE in code:** `runPhase_2_6_3_Floor`
> (`phaseRunners.ts:3541-3560`) tallies legislation with `yea++`/`nay++` = **exactly 1 vote per member** in BOTH chambers;
> `representativeIds: string[]` (`types.ts:1327`) is a flat list with **no delegation-size / weighted-share field**; the
> Speaker/committee-majority math (`:1858-1864`) also counts HEADS. There is **NO `floor(stateReps/focusReps)`, no
> remainder-by-legislative, no per-era named-rep ladder, no census recompute anywhere.** So #219 is a DESIGNED, **0%-shipped**
> delta ‚Äî NOT a settled "provenance + remainder-direction reconcile." The remainder-direction discrepancy (drop-off-vote from
> LOWEST-legis rep [this thread] vs add-remainder-to-HIGHEST-legis rep [`housepoll`]) is still worth pinning, but it is a
> spec-choice to make WHEN the weighted-vote feature is built, not a one-line reconcile to an existing system. **(2) the coup
> subsystem (#264) is ~0% built AND there is NO impeachment plumbing to reuse** ‚Äî grep `coup\|treason\|insurrection\|
> countercoup` and grep `impeach` are BOTH empty in `src/`; `EraEvent.triggersGameEnd?` (`types.ts:1476`) is a one-shot LOSS
> flag, not a coup procedure. The treason-trial's assumed "reuse impeachment plumbing" therefore has **nothing to reuse** ‚Äî
> impeachment must be authored/built first or co-built (DH-29..DH-35).
>
> **(‚ëÝ) ‚òÖ #258 PREDICATE FIELD (debt #120) REMAINS the content-engine keystone ‚Äî this batch is the 2nd + 3rd witness, no
> re-sequence.** Civil Service (`fa452d38`, ~40 L / ~12 P / 2 G rows, heavily prereq-gated) and Diplomacy (`b8aecb83`, ~55
> rows) WIDEN the predicate vocabulary the era-event enum can't express: institution-exists, multi-office COMPOUND (War AND
> Navy ‚Üí merge, via the existing `all`), **cross-primitive mutual-exclusion** ("Not Already Created via Legis Prop / via Pres
> Action" ‚Äî EOP/CEA: created by either an L- or P- row, one path disables the other), scenario-mode (Constitutional-Convention
> -Only), **per-nation diplomatic-state** (recognized / trade-open / at-war / relations-threshold), **war-outcome flags**
> (WWII-won / any-WW-active / WWI-won), 3rd-party-status, anti-prereq. The FIELD-add is unchanged (debt #120: add `precondition?:
> Predicate` to all four content primitives + gate via `evalPredicate`); two of the NEW vocab arms have explicit DEPENDENCIES ‚Äî
> the per-nation classes need #107 (debt #128) first; the cross-primitive class needs the #66 office registry (debt #127)
> first. **Schedule #258 WITH/just-ahead of K4 as the content-engine track's FIRST item** (debt #120/#129). game-mechanics

> only) with a DATA-DRIVEN office/department registry on `GameState` that content rows mutate via 6 lifecycle verbs (create /

> flat scalar) into `Record<string, NationDiplomacy>` = `{relations, recognized?, aidLevel?, trade?, atWar?}`, and move war

> **(‚ë§) ‚òÖ #182 is SETTLED (NO trait-gate build) and #219 is NOT shipped (see the CONTRADICTION flag above); command-GAIN

> direction-keyed faction-effect payload, NO generator, NO Landmark/tier field, NO precedent overturn/reinstate links.** The

> `standardDraftClasses.ts:13`) + the scenario state roster. This is the **BIGGEST SCOTUS-content deliverable** ‚Äî it replaces the

> path exists; justices are appointed+confirmed only. `ElectionResult.type` is the closed union `'presidential' | 'senate' |
> 'house' | 'governor'` (`types.ts:1525`) ‚Äî a retention ballot is a genuinely NEW type (single-candidate up/down tally, distinct
> from the multi-candidate margin model). Court SIZE is also fixed (`supremeCourtIds: string[]`, no court-packing variable) and

> restriction ACTUALLY SHIPS (the `8189b724` digest's "no ideology gate / grep ‚Üí ZERO" claim is WRONG).** `Faction`
> carries an `eligibleIdeologies: Ideology[]` field ‚Äî **typed locally in `factions1772.ts:7` (NOT on the `Faction` interface,
> `types.ts:1293`)** ‚Äî populated for all **10 1772 factions** (e.g. `fact_blue_lw_1772 ‚Üí ['LW Populist','Progressive']`) and
> ZERO in `factions1856.ts`. The draft CONSUMES it: `pickBestForFaction` (`phaseRunners.ts:33-53`) applies it **ONLY when
> `scenarioId==='1772' && year===startYear`** (`:38-39`, the inaugural/expansion draft), as a **SOFT two-tier preference** ‚Äî
> `strict = eligible.filter(in eligIdeos); pool = strict.length>0 ? strict : eligible` (`:42-43`) + a `+50` score nudge (`:49`).
> **That IS exactly #4's "priority pool first, fall back when exhausted" shape ‚Äî so the mechanic is partially built, just
> 1772-inaugural-ONLY, NOT era-keyed, NOT on 1856, NOT a per-era "FactNumber" table, and the field isn't on the canonical type.**

> **(1) ‚òÖ‚òÖ DH-81 war-end cadence ‚Üí the SHIPPED engine is ALREADY per-term (NOT per-battle); the digest's instant game-over is the MANUAL SIM, not the code (debt #141, S).** `runRevWarBattles` (`revolutionaryWar.ts:175`) runs ONCE per Military Phase 2.7 (`phaseRunners.ts:3593-3596`), and the win/loss check (`revolutionaryWar.ts:254-264`) is evaluated ONCE after the battle loop, NOT inside it; the per-battle do-while (`:211-236`, `d100()<=66` continuation) only increments tallies, and `revolutionaryWar.ts` never sets `gameEnded`/`triggersGameEnd` (grep=0). **So a single lost battle CANNOT immediately lose the war in shipped code** ‚Äî the `06fbb2e5#POST 75` "United States has failed‚Ä¶ after Trenton" is the Google-Sheets run (VOIDED live), GM+designer-confirmed against the SHEET. The REAL shipped gap: the count-threshold win-path (7 wins / 16 losses, `:16-18,254-264`) is the **stale per-battle-count model Ted says was REPLACED** by the war-score+momentum chart (¬ß21.1.A). Fix = (a) [S, cheap-fix] swap the count-threshold WIN/LOSS conditions for the war-score chart + delete the stale columns (the per-term cadence is already correct; the 50/50 "another battle?" roll already exists as the `:236` continuation, ~66% ‚Äî a tuning delta); (b) [M, post-launch] the full ¬ß21.1.A war-score model. game-mechanics ¬ß21.1.A (authoritative home).
> **(2) ‚òÖ DH-82 officer cap ‚Üí an unclamped DERIVED battle-target, not a stored-stat violation; a one-line clamp QW (debt #142, XS).** No-SecWar "Planning" = `general.skills.military * 2` (`revolutionaryWar.ts:212`) feeds the d100 target unclamped ‚Üí reads 6 with a 3-Mil officer (`06fbb2e5#POST 73-74`); the naval sum at `:187` is similarly unclamped. The PERSISTED 0‚Äì5 skills ARE clamped (`abilities.ts:6-30`) ‚Äî so this is a derived combat coefficient over-buffing the no-cabinet case, not a corrupted stat. Fix = wrap in `Math.min(5,‚Ä¶)` (+ the one-line design call: does the no-SecWar fallback INTEND to exceed 5?). game-mechanics ¬ß21.1.A.
> **(3) ‚òÖ‚òÖ #273 impeachment ‚Üí 0% shipped AND DECISION-GATED; a LARGE subsystem that vcczar must RATIFY first (debt #143, L, BLOCKED).** `impeach` (case-insensitive) = 0 occurrences in `src/`; no proposal-to-impeach, no Judicial-Committee article step, no House/Senate trial pipeline, no resign-at-any-stage, no Controversial gate, no `game.impeachment` state. vcczar DEFERRED the entire `ae435b5f` thread (POST 47/48: *"I'm not going to have time. Remind me when Anthony gets closer"*) ‚Üí the spec is DESIGN-INTENT-IN-PROGRESS, **NOT canon ‚Äî DO NOT build before ratification.** ‚òÖ THE DEPENDENCY EDGE: #273 is the plumbing **#264 (coup‚Üítreason-trials) reuses** and the system **#112 (modern institutional layer) instantiates** ‚Äî so #273 GATES both; it also rides #221 (event auto-fire) + #258 (trait/precedent gates). The broken-impeachment flags DH-33/DH-54/DH-66 (¬ß24.1.1) now CONSOLIDATE under it as symptoms of the same null subsystem. game-mechanics ¬ß24.1.1a (#273 spec home), ¬ß20.12.

The TRAP is the SEAT-gated paths around it: CPU track assignment shunts a floor-stat pol to its least-bad skill (`bestAvailableTrack`, `:392-399`), tracks cap at 5/faction (`CAREER_TRACK_CAP`, `types.ts:237`) so good tracks fill, and the committee/cabinet skill-ups (`OFFICE_ADMIN_GRANT`, `types.ts:597-605`) need a WON seat that PV gates behind the missing skills ‚Üí ShortKing/Euri's chicken-and-egg. > **GM-replacement lens:** the changelog is the app's spec source-of-truth ‚Äî it pins the cabinet-enthusiasm %s, the era-keyed retirement/death charts, and the Pres-implementation 2-step that the manual sim runs by hand and the build must encode deterministically. The 2.3 rework + the Kagan backlog + the Canada conquest are all the manual-process pain the app subsumes once appointments, the content engine, and a territory-acquisition mechanic ship.
> **‚òÖ CODE-VERIFIED BUILD-STATE (this batch's headline verifications ‚Äî all codebase-confirmed; the digests assert, I confirm):**
> **(1) ‚òÖ‚òÖ Appointments 2.3 = a thin DETERMINISTIC CPU auto-fill ‚Äî NO confirmation vote, NO lobby‚Üíenthusiasm, cross-party gate is a FLAT 10%-relax / ‚àí3-penalty (NOT the designed ¬±1-ideology gate).** `runPhase_2_3_1_Cabinet` (`phaseRunners.ts:2158-2224`) iterates `cabinetSeatsForYear` (`types.ts:1196`), and for each empty seat does: one `chance(CABINET_CROSS_PARTY_RATE)` roll (= `0.1`, `types.ts:1233`) to *relax* the same-party filter, then a pure `scoreCabinetCandidate` (`:2229-2241`, admin/governing/secondary/expertise, no RNG) with `CABINET_CROSS_PARTY_PENALTY` (= `-3`, `types.ts:1234`) added to cross-party scores, then takes `scored[0]` and writes `cabinet[seat]=pick.id` + `addLog("confirmed as ${seat}")` (`:2191-2198`). **There is NO Senate/committee vote, NO NAY/AYE, NO lobby‚Üíenthusiasm channel, and the cross-party gate is the flat 10%/‚àí3 ‚Äî NOT the ¬±1-ideology window the designer specced.** (Same site as debt #24/#30/#38 ‚Äî this batch PINS the constants.) Both 2.3 phases are **skipped entirely in the independence era**: `phases.ts:89-90` (`if (phaseId === '2.3.1' || phaseId === '2.3.2') return false;`). **2.3.2 Military** (`phaseRunners.ts:2246+`) is a separate flat GiC auto-pick. game-mechanics ¬ß30.34, ¬ß25.5; gaps #124/#229/#275/#112.
> **(2) ‚òÖ‚òÖ Canada ‚Äî structure SHIPS, real data does NOT; no war-acquisition mechanic exists.** `src/data/expansionStates.ts` registers every Canadian province as a `region:'Canada'` annexable seed (ALB/MAN/NBR/NFL/NCN/NSC/ONT/QBC/SAS/VAN + PEI; "Central Canada" correctly ABSENT) ‚Äî but the seed factory (`expansionStates.ts:173-183`) stamps **EVERY** expansion seed with placeholder `electoralVotes: 4, bias: 0, industries:{agriculture:2,trade:1}, isSlaveState:false` (uniform, not per-province). `admitState` (`territories.ts:8-23`) is a straight `{...seed, admissionYear, governorId:null, senators:[], representativeIds:[], isColony:false}` copy ‚Äî **NO census/EV/bias computation at all.** There is **no acquisition-by-war/event path** (admit is via the Territories page / God Mode / era graph only). The curated draft fallback has **ZERO** Canada-region entries. Confirmed exactly as the digest claims. game-mechanics; gap #277.
> **(3) ‚òÖ The mid-game EV-MUTATION path any Canada-EV fix must not break ‚Äî LOCATED.** The shipped EV-writes are: the **federalism slave-compromise plank** `constitutionalConvention.ts:210` (`s.electoralVotes = Math.max(3, (s.ccDelegateSlots ?? 2) + 1)` ‚Äî the one engine path that REWRITES EVs at an era transition), the per-state EV *reads* in `calcStateVote`/EC tally (`phaseRunners.ts:3692` turnout scale, `:3767-3768` `blueEv/redEv += s.electoralVotes`), and the **God-Mode editable EV** in `StatesPage.tsx:14`. The Kagan thread's "events that change EVs mid-game" (`f64a522c` POST 6) are **authored EraEvent content on the forum census sheet, NOT shipped engine** ‚Äî `EraEvent`/`applyEffect` (`phaseRunners.ts:3223` clamps meters `-5..5`) has no typed EV-delta effect today. So the EV-mutation "primitive" is design-intent (content), and the only live EV-write to preserve is the CC plank + the God-Mode editor. game-mechanics ¬ß17.3.y; gaps #34/#277.

`Politician` has NO demographic field of any kind [grep race/religion/gender/sexuality/citizenship = 0]; #238 owns gender‚Üísuffrage, #239 owns the race√óreligion√ósex tuple ‚Äî SHARPEN both, extended with sexuality + citizenship as concrete columns) + **#4/#171** (**Can-Party-Flip + Flip-Date-Year-1/2/3 + Ideology-Shift-Date-1 + New-Ideology-1** as PER-POL SCHEDULED data ‚Äî the authored dated schedule behind the party-shift/ideology-drift system; shipped `partyId`/`ideology` `:1265-1266` have NO flip/shift-date field) + **#38** (**3 Alternate States + When-Relocated-to-each** = multi-alt + relocation TIMING; shipped is a single `altState?` `:1256`, one-shot seeded/consumed) + **#189** (the **Election Bonus/Penalty point ladder 20/10/7/5/3/‚àí3/‚àí5/‚àí7/‚àí10/‚àí20** as authored per-pol data; shipped is the deterministic `applyTraitElectionBonus` bands iterating `TRAIT_ELECTION_EFFECTS` `electionEffects.ts:16`, NOT a per-pol authored ladder) + **#194** (**Dynasty + Politician Father/Mother/Spouse** columns = the first-class-FIELD form of the dynasty graph #194 already owns; shipped `Politician` has NO family/parent/spouse field [grep dynasty/father/mother/spouse = 0, excluding `fatherOfConstitution`] ‚Äî RESOLVES to OWNED, not a mint) + **#319** (a 3rd authoritative witness omitting `backroom` ‚Äî the model scores Command + 5, NO backroom, matching the canonical reference AND the 7,349-roster; the shipped `SkillKey` invents the 7th ability) + **#338** (Bio in BOTH datasets ‚Äî Vstyle col 179 + the Pols-2024 `Bio` col ‚Äî re-confirms the unhomed bio field; the stale-nominee-bio hygiene item) + **#339** (splits **Historic Value vs Draft Value** ‚Äî two scores: realized weight vs draft-time prestige, extends the stored-prestige finding); documented game-mechanics ¬ß30.57 (.1/.2). NO new keystone, NO re-sequence ‚Äî top-of-queue framing UNCHANGED: QW0 ‚Üí K0/#296 ‚Üí K2 ‚Üí K3/K4 + scenarioBoot ‚Üí E1; the keystone queue does NOT change; ¬ß9.6 is UNCHANGED. ‚òÖ This is a DATASET-RECONCILIATION ingest (the "source" is a spreadsheet artifact, not a discussion): the intended-DATA-MODEL statement of *where the politician data model is meant to go* ‚Äî a relational, time-aware, demographically-tagged characterization vs the shipped flat static stat block ‚Äî plus the 146-figure roster-expansion pass. It re-confirms/sharpens a large cluster of existing DATA-MODEL gaps (#240/#216/#238/#239/#4/#171/#38/#189/#194/#319/#338/#339) while surfacing ONE genuinely-new dimension (a temporal skill arc, #342) and ONE genuinely-new gate (citizenship ‚Üí presidency, #343). ‚òÖ‚òÖ THE LOAD-BEARING pol-additions-2024 FINDING for the planner: **the entire 182-col model is ~0% shipped as a data model ‚Äî of every dimension, only TWO are genuinely UNOWNED mints (the temporal skill ARC #342 and the natural-born ‚Üí presidency GATE #343); EVERYTHING ELSE MAPS to an already-owned gap** (demographics‚Üí#238/#239, dynasty‚Üí#194, flip/shift/relocate schedule‚Üí#4/#171/#38, point ladder‚Üí#189, two-value‚Üí#339, bio‚Üí#338, backroom-omission‚Üí#319, name-decomp/Army‚Üí#240/#216). And of the two mints, NEITHER adds a keystone: #342 rides the data-model/scenarioBoot work behind a DESIGNER-DECISION on the arc form, and #343 is a per-pol field + an enforcement path directly analogous to the b63 #159 finding (the ConCon articles are STORED-BUT-NEVER-READ). ‚òÖ‚òÖ‚òÖ The build-state CORRECTIONS + designer-decision-vs-near-term calls the planner needs: (1) **‚òÖ #342 TEMPORAL SKILL ARC ‚Äî 0% shipped; a `Skills` type RE-MODEL + an engine skill-arc pass, gated on a DESIGNER-DECISION on the arc FORM.** `skills: Skills` (`types.ts:1271`) with `Skills = Record<SkillKey, number>` (`:41`) is a STATIC flat map ‚Äî there is NO time dimension, NO Peak-Abilities-Year, NO First/Midway/Historic stage anywhere on `Politician` or in the engine (grep confirms 0). The model encodes an 18-column career ARC (First-Year ‚Üí Midway ‚Üí Historic √ó {Command, Legislative, Governing, Judicial, Military, Administrative}) + a `Peak Abilities Year` + dated trait-gain years (Year-Gains-Celebrity/Leadership/Military-Leader/Controversial) ‚Äî a skill value that GROWS over a career, unrepresentable by the flat map (e.g. Silas Talbot First-Year Legislative 1.0 ‚Üí Midway 0.5, peak 1792; Truxtun First-Year Admin 1.0 ‚Üí Midway 0.5, peak 1796). **‚òÖ SIZE: the `Skills` re-model is the crux and it turns on the OPEN designer question (game-pm OQ1, game-context #342): does the runtime (a) INTERPOLATE a live skill value by year between the three anchors, (b) STEP skills at 3 discrete life stages, or (c) use the anchors only to SEED a single at-draft value + a growth SCHEDULE? Each implies a different shape ‚Äî (a)/(b) replace `Skills` with a time-keyed structure (`Record<SkillKey, {first,midway,historic}>` + a `peakYear` + a per-tick derive pass) touching every `skills.*` read across the engine (`pv.ts`, `abilities.ts`, the draft/appointment/election paths) + a `repair()` migration; (c) keeps `Skills` flat and adds a growth-schedule sidecar. This is a `Skills`-type re-model (M-L pure-engine, wide blast radius) + an engine skill-arc pass ‚Äî it RIDES the data-model/scenarioBoot work (K3/K4 + the #115 scenario-boot pipeline is where at-draft seeding + the growth schedule would be established) and is a designer-decision on the arc form ‚Üí route to a roadmap B#. Distinct from the career-track class-progression (#150) which advances an OFFICE track, not the underlying skill values. NOT near-term (blocked on the arc-form decision), NOT a keystone.** (2) **‚òÖ #343 NATURAL-BORN-CITIZEN ‚Üí PRESIDENCY GATE ‚Äî 0% enforced, directly analogous to the b63 #159 "ConCon-articles-never-read" finding; needs BOTH a per-pol citizenship FIELD and an ENFORCEMENT path (+ the source-data flag bug fixed first).** ‚òÖ THE KEY CORRECTION the ingest is right to flag: the ONLY citizenship concept that ships is the scenario-wide Convention article `presidentialEligibility: 'natural_born' | 'any_citizen'` (`types.ts:1395`) ‚Äî it is AUTHORED (the ConCon question `constitutionalConvention.ts:52-58`, CPU-picked `:118-119`, applied `:142`), SEEDED in 1856 (`scenario1856.ts:188` = `'natural_born'`), and LABELED in `ConventionModal.tsx:10` ‚Äî but it is **NEVER READ to gate a candidate**: grep of `src/engine` for `presidentialEligibility` finds ONLY the ConCon WRITE path (`constitutionalConvention.ts:118` is the CPU-*picks-the-article* switch), and the presidency is set UNCONDITIONALLY (`snap.game.presidentId = winner.id`, the presidential-general path) with no citizenship check ‚Äî exactly the #159 pattern (the whole `ConstitutionalArticles` record is stored-but-never-read). So there are TWO gaps: (a) a **missing per-pol citizenship field** ‚Äî `Politician` (`:1251-1291`) has NO race/religion/gender/sexuality/citizenship field of any kind (grep = 0), so even IF the article were enforced there is no per-pol attribute to test against; and (b) a **missing enforcement path** ‚Äî no eligibility gate anywhere reads citizenship (the `eligible`/`eligiblePoolFor` machinery is draft-pool/delegate/faction-leader eligibility, unrelated to Art. II). ‚òÖ AND (c) the SOURCE-DATA citizenship-flag BUG that feeds it: confirmed foreign-born figures are mis-tagged ‚Äî Matthew Lyon (Irish-born, neither flag set), Robert Dale Owen (Scottish-born, `Natural Born Citizen = 1`), Bernie Moreno (Colombian-born, tail reads Natural-Born) ‚Äî so importing the flag as-is would wrongly model naturalized citizens as president-eligible; the flag must be verified per foreign-born figure BEFORE wiring the gate. **‚òÖ SIZE: the per-pol field = XS additive type-add (`citizenship?: 'natural_born'|'foreign_born'` on `Politician`, `repair()`-backfillable, part of the fuller-demographics tuple #238/#239); the ENFORCEMENT path = S ‚Äî gate the presidential nomination/general on `article.presidentialEligibility === 'natural_born' && pol.citizenship === 'foreign_born'` (a hard bar or heavy penalty ‚Äî OPEN, game-context #343); the import-hygiene flag fix = an S `scripts/` prerequisite (folds on #240). ‚òÖ This is the SAME SHAPE as the #159 ConCon-articles-read prerequisite (b63 debt #252): enforcing `presidentialEligibility` is one instance of "make the stored ConCon articles actually READ" ‚Äî sequence it WITH that ConCon-enforcement epic (#159/#252). OPEN (game-context #343): does the gate cover presidency ONLY (Art. II) or also VP / other offices; hard bar vs heavy penalty. NOT near-term (needs the field + the ConCon-enforcement path + the flag fix), NOT a keystone.** (3) **‚òÖ DEMOGRAPHICS TUPLE (#238/#239) + DYNASTY (#194) + FLIP/SHIFT/RELOCATE SCHEDULE (#4/#171/#38) ‚Äî all data-model FIELD additions riding their existing gaps; the ingest is the intended-DATA-MODEL confirmation, not new work.** `Politician` (`:1251-1291`) has NO demographic field (grep race/religion/gender/sexuality/citizenship = 0), NO dynasty/father/mother/spouse field (grep = 0, excluding `fatherOfConstitution`), NO flip/shift-date field (`partyId`/`ideology` `:1265-1266` are single current values), and a single `altState?` (`:1256`, one-shot seeded at `phaseRunners.ts:626-641`, consumed-when-used `:588`) with NO multi-alt / relocation-DATE schedule. The Vstyle model supplies all of these as concrete columns (race√ó6/religion√ó9/gender/sexuality/citizenship [135‚Äì154]; Dynasty + Father/Mother/Spouse [7‚Äì10]; Can-Party-Flip + 3 flip-dates + Ideology-Shift-Date + New-Ideology [155‚Äì167]; 3 Alternate States + when-relocated). **‚òÖ SIZE: each is an additive field cluster on `Politician` (XS-S type-adds, `repair()`-backfillable) owned by its existing gap ‚Äî demographics tuple on #238/#239, dynasty FIELDS on #194 (resolves the "likely NEW family model" question to OWNED), the dated flip/shift schedule on #4/#171, the multi-alt+timing on #38. The VALUE (and the real work) is the ENGINE that CONSUMES them ‚Äî suffrage-gating (#238), dynasty‚Üíkingmaker/prot√©g√© bonuses (#194), scheduled party-flip/ideology-drift (#4/#171), scheduled relocation (#38) ‚Äî which rides each system's epic, not this ingest. NO new rows. NOT near-term (ride the owning epics), NOT keystones.** (4) **‚òÖ THE POINT LADDER (#189) + TWO-VALUE (#339) + BIO (#338) + BACKROOM (#319) + NAME/ARMY (#240/#216) ‚Äî sharpen/corroborate, NO new rows.** #189: the authored 20/10/7/5/3/‚àí3‚Ä¶‚àí20 ladder is the per-pol DATA behind the election-trait scoring; shipped `applyTraitElectionBonus` (`electionEffects.ts:16`) iterates `TRAIT_ELECTION_EFFECTS` bands deterministically (no per-pol authored ladder) ‚Äî owned by #189. #339: Historic Value vs Draft Value = two authored scores; `computePV` (`pv.ts`) DERIVES PV at runtime, no stored authored value ‚Äî owned by #339 (the store-vs-derive fork). #338: Bio in BOTH datasets ‚Äî owned by #338 (with the stale-nominee hygiene). #319: Command + 5, NO backroom ‚Äî a 3rd authoritative witness, owned by #319. #240/#216: name-decomposition (First/Middle/Surname/Suffix + PoliticianID as a duplicate-name key) folds on #240; the "Army"‚Üí"Military" naming reconcile folds on #216/#240. ‚Äî DATASET-INGEST, no source ships behavior, no determinism/persistence/migration change (the additions are un-ingested data + the two mints are additive optional fields; old saves load ‚Äî no `DB_VERSION` bump).**

> **‚òÖ‚òÖ SEQUENCING RECOMMENDATION FOR THE ROADMAP-PLANNER (pol-additions-2024 deltas) ‚Äî the block to lift directly:**
> **(a) NO keystone, NO re-sequence.** Top of queue stands UNCHANGED: **QW0 ‚Üí K0/#296 ‚Üí K2 ‚Üí K3/K4 + scenarioBoot ‚Üí E1.** This is a DATA-MODEL / dataset INGEST ‚Äî no source ships behavior, the two mints are additive optional fields (old saves load, no `DB_VERSION` bump), and both RIDE existing keystones/epics rather than reordering them. ¬ß9.6 is UNCHANGED.
> **(b) ‚òÖ ZERO near-term-buildable.** The ONE genuinely-new DIMENSION ‚Äî the temporal skill ARC (#342, row #260) ‚Äî is a `Skills` type RE-MODEL blocked on a DESIGNER-DECISION on the arc form (interpolate vs step vs seed+growth); it cannot ship correct-by-construction before the human calls the form, and its wide-blast-radius (every `skills.*` read) makes it a data-model/scenarioBoot rider (K3/K4 + #115), not a standalone quick win. Route the arc-form to a roadmap B#. The other mint ‚Äî the citizenship‚Üípresidency gate (#343, row #261) ‚Äî is NOT standalone either: its enforcement half is ONE instance of the b63 #159 "make the stored ConCon articles actually READ" prerequisite (debt #252), so fold it into that ConCon-enforcement epic; its data half is a per-pol `citizenship?` field that rides the #238/#239 demographics tuple; and it has a `scripts/` flag-bug prerequisite (fix the mis-tagged foreign-born figures before wiring the gate).
> **(c) ‚òÖ DESIGNER-DECISIONS (route to a roadmap B#, do NOT schedule as a build):** **#342 arc form** ‚Äî interpolate-by-year vs step-at-3-stages vs seed+growth-schedule (determines whether `Skills` becomes a time-keyed structure or stays flat with a sidecar); **#343 gate scope** ‚Äî presidency-only vs also-VP/other-offices, and hard-bar vs heavy-penalty. Both are model forks; nothing ships until called.
> **(d) DEFERRED / PARKED (ride existing epics; do NOT schedule as their own units):** the demographics tuple (#238/#239), dynasty FIELDS (#194 ‚Äî resolves the family model to OWNED), the dated flip/shift schedule (#4/#171), and multi-alt+relocation-timing (#38) are all additive `Politician` field clusters whose VALUE is the CONSUMING engine (suffrage-gating / dynasty bonuses / scheduled party-flip+ideology-drift / scheduled relocation) ‚Äî build the fields WITH each owning system's engine work, not ahead of it. The 145+221 additions import (#240) is a `scripts/` PIPELINE task (M-L ‚Äî ingest the 3 tabs, exclude playtesters [done] + filter `#VALUE!` [done] + ‚òÖ fix the citizenship-flag SOURCE-DATA bug + refresh stale nominee bios + reconcile "Army"‚Üí"Military" + key on row-index, sub-floor the never-served/Reps-elect-who-died cohort); the pipeline does NOT ingest these files today (grep `scripts/`=0) ‚Äî rides the dataset epic on #240, NOT near-term, NOT a keystone. The trait add-or-cut (#216, +3 Everyman/Lawful/Union-Loyalist), the point ladder (#189), the two-value split (#339), bio (#338), and the backroom-omission (#319) all ride their existing owners. **‚òÖ The single most useful planner takeaway: of the whole 182-col intended model, only #342 (temporal skills) and #343 (citizenship gate) are UNOWNED ‚Äî and NEITHER is near-term-buildable (one is a designer-decision-gated re-model, the other folds into the #159 ConCon-enforcement epic); the rest is a large but ALREADY-OWNED data-model field-addition backlog whose real cost is the consuming engines, not the fields.**

> **‚òÖ‚òÖ SEQUENCING RECOMMENDATION FOR THE ROADMAP-PLANNER (era-events deltas) ‚Äî the block to lift directly:**
> **(a) NO keystone, NO re-sequence.** Top of queue stands UNCHANGED: **QW0 ‚Üí K0/#296 ‚Üí K2 ‚Üí K3/K4 + scenarioBoot ‚Üí E1.** This is a content/data-model INGEST ‚Äî no source ships behavior, the two mints are additive optional struct-adds (old saves load, no `DB_VERSION` bump), and both new mechanics RIDE existing keystones/epics rather than reordering them. ¬ß9.6 is UNCHANGED.
> **(b) ‚òÖ NEAR-TERM-BUILDABLE from this batch: NONE stands alone; the closest-to-ready is #340 (row #258), but it GATES on K0/#296.** The Resolve-ability struct-add is XS, but its resolution step is a seeded roll ‚Äî it CANNOT ship correct-by-construction before the seeded-RNG keystone (K0/#296) lands, since adding a `Math.random` roll to the resolve path would DEEPEN the determinism debt (¬ß8 #1) this project is trying to pay down. So sequence #340 as an EARLY rider on K0/#296 (once the seeded substrate exists, it is a small, self-contained, high-flavor add ‚Äî the graded era-event response is a clean first customer for the seeded-roll machinery, alongside #56/#330).
> **(c) ‚òÖ RIDES THE ELECTION EPIC: #341 office-points + carry (row #259).** The office-points CHANNEL is a cheap XS-S effect-struct add, but its VALUE is the next-election carry, which mutates the election-scoring path (`calcStateVote`/the presidential general) ‚Äî that path is owned by the #184/#292 election epic and is ALSO where the pre-existing `Math.random` jitter (debt #1) lives. Fold #341 into that epic so the carry, the jitter-determinism fix, and the enthusiasm/partyPreference reads are reworked together ‚Äî do NOT bolt a per-officer carry onto the unseeded scoring path in isolation.
> **(d) ‚òÖ THE CONTENT-ENGINE EPIC (#258/#221/#262) ‚Äî the corpus is the BASE-CORPUS import; the merge rule is the AUTHORING MODEL going forward.** The 1,664 `Requires` clauses are IMPORTABLE DATA against the already-shipped `Predicate` union, but they need (i) the `evalPredicate` reach extended from 1772-graph-only to all 16 bands + 1856 migrated off its hardcoded year-gate, and (ii) the forum `8f7ae0b9` chain-DSL topology (multi-edge / blocker-group) which is ADDITIONAL authoring the flat sheet cannot express. ‚òÖ Record the merge rule as the authoring model: **the sheet is the authoritative base era-event table (existence + preconditions + base-effects); the forum threads are ADDENDA (chain-wiring + response-detail + modern/future/SCOTUS content the 2022-terminating sheet lacks); neither subsumes.** This is the #221/#258 content-engine epic, gated behind K3/K4 + the #206 enum-expansion for the unshipped bands; NOT near-term.
> **(e) BUILD-STATE CORRECTIONS (code vs the ingest claims ‚Äî all CONFIRMED, one nuance):** (i) `EraEventResponse`/`EraEventResponseEffect`/`decider`/`Predicate`/`evalPredicate`/`Era`-enum all verified at the cited lines ‚Äî the two mints (#340/#341) are genuinely unrepresentable. (ii) ‚òÖ NUANCE the ingest is right to flag: `EraEvent.triggersGameEnd` (`types.ts:1476`) DOES ship and IS consumed (`resolveEraEvent` `phaseRunners.ts:2871` sets `game.gameEnded`) ‚Äî the "Game Ends" special rule (Conciliatory-Resolution, SpaceBotUSA) maps cleanly; it is NOT a gap. (iii) ‚òÖ The 1772-vs-1856 asymmetry is the sharpest #258 datum: 1772 runs on the `Predicate` graph (`eraEvents1772.ts` + `eraGraph.ts`), 1856 runs on a hardcoded `if(year>=)` push-list (`eraEvents1856.ts:6-180`) with NO `Predicate` ‚Äî so even the ONE other shipped era does not use the interpreter, confirming "wired 1772-graph-only." (iv) ‚òÖ debt-id disambiguation for the planner: **debt row #258 ‚Üí gap #340 (Resolve-ability); debt row #259 ‚Üí gap #341 (office-points+carry); gap #258 (predicate/eraGraph reach) is SHARPENED here but adds NO debt row** ‚Äî it is already owned by the content-engine epic.

already owned) + cross-refs #214 (the trait‚ÜíPV TIER table ‚Äî the #216 completion UNBLOCKS its transcription); documented game-mechanics ¬ß30.55 (.1/.2/.3). NO new keystone, NO re-sequence ‚Äî top-of-queue framing UNCHANGED: QW0 ‚Üí K0/#296 ‚Üí K2 ‚Üí K3/K4 + scenarioBoot ‚Üí E1; the keystone queue does NOT change; ¬ß9.6 is UNCHANGED. ‚òÖ This is a DATASET-RECONCILIATION ingest (the "source" is a data artifact, not a discussion): the hand-authored roster the game has actually been PLAYED from, and the SOURCE-OF-TRUTH the shipped generated dataset + the trait/skill/PV systems are supposed to reconcile against ‚Äî it SETTLES three long-open reconciliation questions the KB has been circling from the playtest side for dozens of batches (#216 trait ground-truth, #319 pre-backroom skill ground-truth, #240 the curated superset source) AND adds two net-new data-model gaps (#338 bio, #339 Draft Value). ‚òÖ‚òÖ THE LOAD-BEARING politician-dataset FINDING for the planner: **the two new gaps (#338 bio, #339 Draft Value) are the only fields the authored ground-truth carries that the shipped `Politician`/`ImportedDraftee` type CANNOT hold ‚Äî everything else in the record maps.** The record is `{ Full Name, Draft Value, Initial State, Initial Team Color (Red/Blue), Initial Ideology, Draft Year, Year Gains Celebrity, Celebrity, skills{Command + 5, 0-5}, traits[] (55-boolean), bio (prose) }`; field-by-field against `Politician` (`types.ts:1251-1291`) / `ImportedDraftee` (`:1780-1793`): skills‚Üí`Skills`+separate `command`, team‚Üí`partyId`, ideology‚Üí7-point scale, `Draft Year`‚Üí`draftYear` ‚Äî all MATCH; the three misses are `bio` (#338, `grep bio|biography` on the type = 0), `backroom` (#319, the source has NO 7th ability), and a stored `draftValue` (#339, `computePV` re-derives it). ‚òÖ‚òÖ‚òÖ The build-state CORRECTIONS + designer-decision-vs-near-term calls the planner needs: (1) **‚òÖ #338 PER-POLITICIAN BIO ‚Äî 0% shipped, a net-new data-model + UI + PIPELINE surface.** `Politician` (`types.ts:1251-1291`) and `ImportedDraftee` (`:1780-1793`) carry NO `bio`/`description`/`biography` field (the `description` fields at `types.ts:1435/1462/1471/1510/1552` belong to legislation/era-event types, NOT `Politician`); the generated fallback rows (`defaultDraftClasses.ts`, `ImportedDraftee[]`) likewise carry none. **‚òÖ PIPELINE CORRECTION:** the source-game bio prose is DROPPED by the pipeline today ‚Äî `legislatorsToDataset.mjs:63` reads `person.bio` but that is the congress-legislators YAML bio OBJECT (birthday etc., consumed only at `:70` for `birthYear`), NOT prose; the emitted JSON header (`:319`) has NO `bio` column, so `public/standard-draft-classes.json` carries no biography at all. Adding `bio?: string` is a SMALL additive type-add (optional field, old saves load) BUT it is INERT until surfaced (a politician detail/portrait card, ties the #235-family presentation rows) AND the authored bios must be IMPORTED via `scripts/` ‚Äî per CLAUDE.md the dataset is GENERATED, so the bio-carry goes through `scripts/legislatorsToDataset.mjs` + a regenerate, NOT a hand-edit of the generated files. **‚òÖ SIZE: `bio?: string` on `Politician`+`ImportedDraftee` = XS type-add; the pipeline carry (import the authored prose + emit a `bio` column) = S; the detail-card UI = S; INERT until surfaced. Near-term-BUILDABLE as a data-model add, but low-value until the presentation surface exists. NO keystone.** (2) **‚òÖ #339 DRAFT VALUE = STORED prestige/PV vs the RUNTIME-DERIVED `computePV` ‚Äî a DESIGNER-DECISION, and a FLOOR CONFLICT the code makes concrete.** `computePV` (`pv.ts:67-89`) DERIVES PV at runtime (skills√óoffice-weights √ó4 `:71-73` + `command`√ó10 `:74` + trait +4/‚àí5 `:76-77` + `Kingmaker` +6 `:79` + office prestige `:81` + faction-leader +8 `:83` + age curve `:85-86` ‚àí flip-flopper√ó5 `:87`), caches it as `pvCache` (`refreshPv` `:91-93`), and `Politician`/`ImportedDraftee` carry NO stored `draftValue` field. The authored Draft Value is a STORED hand-ranked prestige score: min **‚àí25**, median 13, p90 30, **max 241** (Napoleon), 110 negatives ‚Äî the marquee tier (Clay 140, Franklin 140, TR 139, Washington 120, Lincoln 118, FDR 114, Jackson 113) sits far above the long tail (one-term relatives 1-20) and the negative floor is the fictional "Duopoly Patine-Mambo". **‚òÖ THE CONFLICT the code makes concrete: `computePV` ends `return Math.max(0, Math.round(total))` (`pv.ts:88`) ‚Äî a NON-NEGATIVE floor ‚Äî while the authored DV floor is `‚àí25`. So "store the authored DV" is not a drop-in: the two scales disagree at the bottom AND a marquee figure's authored DV need not equal its computed PV. This is a DESIGNER-DECISION (game-context #339 OPEN): (a) import DV as a stored `draftValue?` and let `computePV` SEED/CLAMP toward it (authored override ‚Äî but then reconcile the ‚àí25 floor against the `Math.max(0,‚Ä¶)` floor), or (b) keep deriving PV and use DV ONLY as the draft-ORDERING key at import time (never stored). Route to a roadmap B# decision; NOT a build until the fork is called. The Spoils order, if built, would force that 10% window to ZERO for a half-term ‚Äî which requires the standing-effect-class plumbing that does not exist (the only expiry precedent is the trait-scoped `failedBidExpiresYear`, `types.ts:1289`). "Ministers" ‚âà cabinet seats (`game.cabinet: Partial<Record<OfficeType,string|null>>` `types.ts:1580`, era-gated by `cabinetSeatsForYear` `types.ts:1196`); no separate minister entity. **‚òÖ SIZE: the half-term-timer standing-effect class = S-M NEW primitive (own row #255; the `failedBidExpiresYear` year-expiry pattern is the model to generalize to a game-level rule scope); the Spoils order itself + the cabinet party-lock = XS-S CONTENT on top of it, riding the exec-action library epic (debt #173/#23) + the cabinet-composition debt #222. What is missing is a PEACETIME/standard-military Admiral appointment (2.3.2 is `GeneralInChief`-only). Both the Rev-War Admiral path and the peacetime General path ignore `partyId`, so the non-political-by-omission finding holds across both. **‚òÖ SIZE: codifying "non-political, excluded from all party-composition limits" = an XS type-add (one `nonPolitical`/non-partisan flag on the military officer path) that makes the incidental rule INTENTIONAL; it is INERT (the rule is already satisfied ‚Äî nothing applies a party limit to the military) and RIDES the military-tier epic (debt #179/#49). The designed removal set is impeachment OR resignation; the resignation-analog ships (age-retire), but IMPEACHMENT is UNBUILT (`grep impeach src/` = 0) ‚Äî the sole designed removal mechanism absent, already owned by the impeachment debt #156. **‚òÖ SIZE: ZERO build for the "no-executive-removal" fact (the model already forbids it ‚Äî the ruling elevates incidental behavior to intentional constitutional rule); the impeachment-as-removal path is the DESIGNER-DEFERRED "3.0 last" subsystem on debt #156. NO new row (sharpens #250/#225/#156).** (4) **‚òÖ THIRD-PARTY SPAWN-GATE FALSE-POSITIVE (#48) ‚Äî the near-term fix is a spec refinement on a ~0%-built gate, and the deferred 2-D rework is a REWIRE of already-2-D data, not a rebuild.** Phase 2.9.3 is a hardcoded no-op ‚Äî `case '2.9.3': addLog(snap, '2.9.3', 'election', 'No third-party challenge this cycle.'); return {};` (`engine.ts:70`) ‚Äî it never reads a meter, never rolls, never spawns; `PartyId = 'BLUE' | 'RED'` is a hard binary (`types.ts:3`), and `calcStateVote` (`phaseRunners.ts:~3696-3709`) has NO third-party/spoiler vote-drain term (cross-ref #228). The designed primary is a governor-activated FIVE-GROUP staggered calendar: on activation the governor rolls each state into a group (random placement), with a ‚â§3-per-first-three-groups cap and a bump-to-Group-5 overflow sink; there is no `nominationMechanism`/`nominationMethod` era-selector field in `types.ts` (the primary runs the same single sort regardless of era).
