> **‚òÖ‚òÖ‚òÖ Batch-62 change (5-thread ZERO-MINT / SHARPEN-ONLY batch ‚Äî `5b162cf5`-third-party-system/#48+DH-55+#228+#4 (the DESIGNER-AUTHORITATIVE single-post third-party lifecycle: spawn-gate ‚Üí nominee-criteria ‚Üí event-spawn ‚Üí win-and-absorb realignment; RESOLVES DH-55's open decision), `5500e143`-trump-arrest-scandal-pardon/#122+#327+#292+#189+#140+#330 (the arrest‚Üíoffice-lock-UNLESS-pardoned ruling + the crime-type PARDON AUTHORITY-SPLIT + the designer's candidacy-BLOCK BAR + the acquitted/convicted next-election modifier), `27711296`-biden-era-events/#169+#206+#221+#258 (a per-president-midterm name-templated flavor-speech class + a multi-era `eras[]`-on-scripted-`EraEvent` availability edge case); I [tech-lead] code-verified EVERY brief-listed claim against `src/` HEAD 2026-07-01 and am the build-state authority; ‚òÖ this is a ZERO-MINT batch ‚Äî NO new gap IDs, and NO new ¬ß8 debt rows (b61's max #251 stands; every b62 delta SHARPENS an existing owner already covered by a shipped debt/divergence row ‚Äî see the NO-NEW-ROW justification below); FOLDS/SHARPENS #48/DH-55 (the win-and-absorb realignment RESOLVES DH-55 toward absorb-the-winner; party count STAYS 2) + #122 (pardon crime-type AUTHORITY-SPLIT: federal‚ÜíPresident, state‚ÜíGovernor + the office-lock-release use-case) + #327/#330 (the barred-from-RUNNING candidacy-BLOCK variant ‚Äî the INVERSE of #330's "may still RUN" band) + #292/#189 (the acquitted-bonus/convicted-malus per-pol next-election modifier) + #169/#206/#221 (the president-name-templated flavor-speech class + `eras[]` on `EraEvent` + the missing `future` Era value); updated game-mechanics ¬ß30.52 (.1/.2/.3). NO new keystone, NO re-sequence ‚Äî top-of-queue framing UNCHANGED: QW0 ‚Üí K0/#296 ‚Üí K2 ‚Üí K3/K4 + scenarioBoot ‚Üí E1; the keystone queue does NOT change. ‚òÖ This is the leanest batch to date: THREE DESIGN/Q&A threads (one designer-authoritative lifecycle statement, one designer spot-ruling, one playtester content list), NO live playtest, ZERO mints, ZERO new debt rows ‚Äî pure re-verification of already-owned surfaces. ‚òÖ‚òÖ THE LOAD-BEARING b62 FINDING for the planner: **the DESIGNER has now RESOLVED the two biggest OPEN questions on already-parked epics** ‚Äî (1) DH-55's third-party permanence fork resolves to **absorb-the-winner** (permanence = the winning independent's party ABSORBS one of the two majors via an event; the count stays 2, so `PartyId` need NOT become open-ended ‚Äî a 2-slot-with-swap model suffices, NOT a dynamic party set), and (2) #122's pardon authority is now specified as a **crime-type split** (federal‚ÜíPresident / state‚ÜíGovernor). Neither resolution is near-term-buildable: the third-party lifecycle is ~0% built on top of a hard `'BLUE'|'RED'` binary (an architectural change), and the pardon-split rides the unbuilt arrest/office-lock feature. ‚òÖ‚òÖ‚òÖ The build-state CORRECTIONS + near-term calls the planner needs: (1) **THIRD-PARTY (#48/DH-55) ‚Äî the entire 4-part lifecycle is ~0% built; Phase 2.9.3 is a hardcoded no-op and the party model is a hard binary.** Phase `2.9.3` "Third Party" ships in `PHASE_SEQUENCE` (`phases.ts:41`) but its whole engine body is one line ‚Äî `case '2.9.3': addLog(snap, '2.9.3', 'election', 'No third-party challenge this cycle.'); return {};` (`engine.ts:70`) ‚Äî it never reads `partyPreference`/`enthusiasm`, never rolls, never spawns. `PartyId = 'BLUE' | 'RED'` (`types.ts:3`) is a hard binary: `enthusiasm[ideo] = { BLUE, RED }` (`Enthusiasm`, `types.ts` ~`:1415-1418`), the primary iterates `['BLUE','RED']` (`phaseRunners.ts:3728`), and the general hard-returns `null` if either the blue OR red candidate is missing (`phaseRunners.ts:3752-3754`) ‚Äî no third ballot slot. The gate INPUTS ship and are event-driven (`partyPreference` `types.ts:1570`/`:1093-1094`; the per-ideology `enthusiasm` table, both moved by `applyEffect` `phaseRunners.ts:3226-3233`), but **nothing reads the both-neutral spawn predicate.** `absorb`/`spawnThirdParty`/`merge.*party` = **0 party-realignment logic** in `src/` (the only `absorb`/`reabsorb` tokens are Rev-War flavor: `eraEvents1772.ts:300`, `GameOverScreen.tsx:10`); `EraEventResponseEffect` (`types.ts:1448-1457`) has no `spawnThirdParty` field. **‚òÖ SIZE: the third-party subsystem is a LARGE greenfield gated on an architectural party-model change (DH-55) ‚Äî PARK it on the party/realignment epic. The win-and-absorb realignment is the now-DESIGNER-RESOLVED tail of that epic (absorb-the-winner, count stays 2); do NOT build ahead of the party-model refactor. NOT near-term, NOT a keystone.** (2) **PARDON (#122) ‚Äî only a flavor exec action exists; no politician-targeted pardon, no governor path, no lock-lift.** The sole pardon in code is the flavor exec action `'Pardon political prisoners.'` (`phaseRunners.ts:3639`) which nudges `snap.game.partyPreference` by `¬±0.2` and **targets NO politician**; grep `pardon` across `src/` = exactly that 1 hit. `forceRetire` (`phaseRunners.ts:2723-2728`) REMOVES a pol (`retiredYear` + `recordRetirement` + `markPoliticianRetired`) ‚Äî it does not lock/unlock. The crime-type authority-split (federal‚ÜíPresident / state‚ÜíGovernor) and any office-lock RELEASE are 0% built. **‚òÖ SIZE: the pardon-split RIDES the unbuilt arrest/office-lock feature (#327's recoverable-ineligibility flag is the thing a pardon would RELEASE); it is a TEMPLATE + a role-gated pardon action, NOT a type primitive ‚Äî but inert until the office-lock flag lands. Rides the #327/#122 scandal/eligibility epic. NOT near-term, NOT a keystone.** (3) **‚òÖ CANDIDACY-BLOCK (#327/#330) ‚Äî the run-vs-appointment punishment axis is HALF-built: `forceRetire` removes, but there is NO "cannot run" flag.** `grep candidacyBlock|barred|ineligible|cannotRun|canRun src/` = **0 hits.** The engine can REMOVE a pol from play (`forceRetire`, `phaseRunners.ts:2723-2728`) but cannot bar a *living, un-retired* pol from STANDING FOR ELECTION while staying in play ‚Äî which is precisely the designer's BAR for building the arrest feature at all (`5500e143#POST 3`: "a legal block against him RUNNING (not just him choosing not to run)"). This is the **INVERSE of #330's punishment band** (#330 = barred from cabinet/military/judiciary but MAY STILL RUN); together they define a **run-vs-appointment punishment-state axis** ‚Äî two independent eligibility flags. **‚òÖ SIZE: the candidacy-block is a SMALL type-add (one `canRun`/`candidacyBlocked` flag on `Politician` + one filter in the primary `runPhase_2_9_1_Primaries` `phaseRunners.ts:3728-3746` + the general candidate build) ‚Äî but it is INERT until the arrest/Disgraced (#327) feature that would SET it lands, and it RIDES the #327/#330 punishment-axis epic. NOT near-term (no producer today), NOT a keystone.** (4) **‚òÖ NEXT-ELECTION MODIFIER (#292/#189) ‚Äî no per-pol one-shot carry-forward election modifier exists.** `grep acquit|convict|nextElection|electionModifier src/` = **0 hits.** Trait election bands are STATIC per-trait (`TRAIT_ELECTION_EFFECTS` `types.ts:738`; Controversial `presGeneral` ‚àíMEDIUM `types.ts:831`), applied uniformly; `calcStateVote` (`phaseRunners.ts:3685`) has **no event-seeded carry-forward term** ‚Äî nothing stores an acquit/convict outcome to bias a specific pol's NEXT race. **‚òÖ SIZE: a NEW stateful class ‚Äî a `Politician`-scoped one-shot `nextElectionModifier` (delta + expiry-on-consume) that `calcStateVote` reads and clears ‚Äî S as a primitive, but it needs a PRODUCER (the arrest acquit/convict roll) and RIDES the #189/#292 election-rework epic behind K0 (it binds at the same `calcStateVote` jitter surface as debt #1). NOT near-term, NOT a keystone.** (5) **‚òÖ CONTENT (#169/#206/#221) ‚Äî `EraEvent` has no `eras` field, the `future` Era value is absent, and there is no president-name templating.** The `decider:'president'` + zero-effect `responses[]` flavor-event SHAPE already ships (`eraEvents1856.ts:12,61`), but the `EraEvent` model (`types.ts:1466-1482`) is scheduled by a literal `year: number` and has **NO `eras` field at all** ‚Äî so a genuinely cross-era *scripted* event is unrepresentable, and there is **no president-name interpolation token**, so a flavor speech cannot be name-templated with the sitting president. By contrast `AnytimeEventTemplate.eras?: Era[]` (`anytimeEvents.ts:12`) already accepts a multi-band list (proven usages `:49,59,74,84,92,109,135,143`), but the `future` value it would need is absent ‚Äî `Era = 'independence' | 'federalism' | 'nationalism' | 'modern'` (`types.ts:1337`), grep for a `'future'` Era value = **0 hits**. Biden/modern content is ~0% reachable (only the 1772 & 1856 scenarios boot). **‚òÖ SIZE: the name-templating token + the `eras[]`-on-`EraEvent` field are XS-S primitives on the content model ‚Äî but INERT until the modern/future eras + a content corpus exist; they RIDE the content-engine (#221/#258) + era-model (K3/K4, #92/#206) work. The `future` Era value is the doubly-unbuilt hole #206 owns. NOT near-term, NOT a keystone.**

FOUR sources are DESIGN/RULESET threads (one is the AUTHORITATIVE CPU-rules changelog); NONE is a live playtest. ‚òÖ‚òÖ THE LOAD-BEARING b61 FINDING for the planner: **the shipped CPU is a set of small greedy heuristics that the designer-ratified changelog now supersedes on ALL FOUR core decisions** ‚Äî the shipped draft is a deterministic SCORE-MAX (no 25%/25-PV roll), the track-assignment is a ONE-LINE best-uncapped-skill heuristic (none of the 5-step procedure), early-removal DOESN'T EXIST (20-year-only), and relocation is a 0.3/0.1 per-pol gate (neither the old 20%/1 NOR the new 75%/5 19-priority list). This is the concrete numeric spec for the #20 CPU-coherence cluster ‚Äî but it is **wiring/replacement over EXISTING data paths (tracks, kingmakers, altState, seeded `chance`), NOT greenfield systems**, so it is sized as a MEDIUM refactor riding #20 + K0, not a keystone. ‚òÖ‚òÖ‚òÖ The build-state CORRECTIONS + near-term calls the planner needs: (1) **The CPU 4-decision spec (#232/#289/#38/#323) is ~0% built but is a REWIRE, not a build-from-nothing.** `pickBestForFaction` (`phaseRunners.ts:33-53`) is a deterministic `pvCache + (personalityMatch?25:0) + (eligIdeos?50:0)` score-MAX with the +50 ideology gate applying ONLY in the 1772 inaugural draft (`isExpansion1772 :38-43`) ‚Äî no 25% roll, no 25-PV threshold, off-ideology not even modeled post-1772. `bestAvailableTrack` (`:392-399`) is the ENTIRE track-assignment CPU logic (highest skill < 5 whose track has room, ties by `SKILLS` order); Pass 1 (`:422-452`) assigns track-less CPU pols age<50 and re-tracks 20-year finishers if age<60. Removal fires ONLY at `careerTrackYears >= CAREER_TRACK_MAX_YEARS` (=20, `types.ts:236`; `:427`) ‚Äî no +5%/4yr early-removal, no territory-status gate. Relocation (`runPhase_2_1_4_Relocations :623-679`) rolls `cpuGate.withAltState = 0.3` / `withoutAltState = 0.1` (`types.ts:245`) per pol then a thin-state heuristic (same-region ‚Üí fewest-residents ‚Üí id), capped at `RELOCATION_ATTEMPTS_PER_TURN = 5` attempts/turn (`types.ts:247`; `:577,656`) ‚Äî no 19-priority list, no likeable/Teflon filter, no kingmaker-pairing targeting. **‚òÖ SIZE: this is a MEDIUM rewrite of 4 existing pure-engine functions, K0-gated (every % is a seeded `chance` roll), riding #20 ‚Äî NOT near-term (the whole spec must land coherently), NOT a keystone.** (2) **‚òÖ The two committee/floor asides (#323) are the ONLY genuinely-MISSING systems in the CPU set: there is NO crisis-legislation category in the data model at all.** `runPhase_2_6_2_Committee` (`:3463-3496`) resolves each bill with a single chair pass-roll `clamp((sameParty?0.85:0.25) + cardVoteBias, 0, 1)` ‚Äî NO crisis branch (and no crisis-bill type/flag anywhere; "crisis" in `src/` = the `Crisis Manager`/`Crisis Gov` TRAIT + era-event copy). `runPhase_2_6_3_Floor` (`:3498-3580`) tallies each passed-committee bill INDEPENDENTLY with no cross-bill dedupe ‚Äî two same-topic bills both pass and both `applyEffect`. Both are 0% built. (3) **‚òÖ‚òÖ SECESSION (#58/#288/#336) ‚Äî the slavery flag is read by ZERO secession code; the CW is a YEAR-GATED, slavery-AGNOSTIC script.** `isSlaveState` (`types.ts:1329`) is written in the state data + at territory admission (`:3175`: `t.region === 'South' || t.region === 'Border'`) and read by EXACTLY ONE consumer ‚Äî the `StatesPage.tsx:20` "Slave?" display cell; NO engine code reads it. There is NO abolition mechanism (`GradualEmancipation` is inert flavor `types.ts:326`; `amendmentProcess` only sets the passage bar; no bill/law/amendment flips `isSlaveState`). `nullif`/`abominations` = **0 hits** ‚Äî #336's SC-only nullification path is entirely unrepresented. **‚òÖ SIZE: the secession/CW engine is a LARGE ~0%-built system (slavery gate + abolition mechanism + John-Brown point-of-no-return + CSA polity + #336 single-state exit) ‚Äî PARK it as greenfield, likely on the antebellum-endgame / Reconstruction epic; the flag‚Üísecession wiring is its first requirement.** (4) **‚òÖ SCOTUS (#270) ‚Äî 0% programmed, designer-confirmed.** `pendingCourtCases: SupremeCourtCase[]` (`types.ts:1587`) is initialized to `[]` in both scenarios (`scenario1856.ts:175`/`scenario1772.ts:95`) and NEVER read or pushed to; `SupremeCourtCase.effect?` (`types.ts:1555`) is NEVER populated or applied. `runPhase_2_5_3_Court` (`:3397-3415`) is a per-turn `chance(0.5)` over 4 HARDCODED title strings, ideology-headcount ‚Üí `partyPreference ¬±0.1` ‚Äî no cases, no per-case effect, cadence is per-turn (vs the designed 2-4-cases/half-term). **‚òÖ SIZE: the per-case effect field/model is a SMALL type-add (the `effect?` slot exists but needs Yea/Nay + points + a persistent-policy-flag target), but it is INERT until a case roster is authored and a cadence + conditional-trigger (precondition) field are added ‚Äî a small primitive on a mostly-CONTENT epic.** (5) **‚òÖ FUTURE-ERA (#258/#169/#206) ‚Äî the notation maps 1:1 onto the shipped 1772-only DSL; the block-edge is the one NEW primitive.** `Predicate`/`evalPredicate`/`ERA_GRAPH_1772` (`eraGraph.ts:1-47`, model imported from `eraEvents1772`) IS the `->` + `(other requirement)` + AND/OR/NOT model ‚Äî but 1772-ONLY (1856 uses hardcoded `if(year>=)`; > 1. **‚òÖ‚òÖ THE CPU-RULES CHANGELOG (debt #248, gaps #232/#289/#38/#323) RIDES #20 + K0 ‚Äî it is the designer-ratified NUMERIC SPEC for the CPU-coherence cluster, a MEDIUM rewrite, NOT near-term and NOT a keystone.** All four core decisions (draft 25%/25-PV gamble, 5-step track-assignment + territory-gated early-removal, 75%/5-pol 19-priority relocation) are wiring/replacement over EXISTING data paths (tracks, kingmakers, altState) ‚Äî but every % is a seeded `chance` roll, so it is K0-GATED, and it must land as ONE coherent replacement (piecemeal would leave the CPU incoherent). The two committee/floor asides (#323) are the ONLY net-new systems in the set (a crisis-bill category + a cross-bill dedupe guard) and can be their own small PRs within the epic.
> 2. **‚òÖ THE CONTESTED RELOCATION CAP (debt #248, gap #38) is a DESIGNER-RECONCILIATION (a B# candidate), NOT a code call ‚Äî flag it, recommend the framing, but it is the designer's decision.** b58's ruling was **4 state-movements (successes), alt-EXCLUDED, per HALF-TERM**; b61's changelog implies **75% √ó up-to-5 attempts per TURN** (fails counted, alt-INCLUDED) ‚Äî which AGREES with the shipped unit (`RELOCATION_ATTEMPTS_PER_TURN = 5` attempts/turn, `types.ts:247`) and nearly on value, but CONFLICTS with b58 on both unit (attempts vs successes) and window (turn vs half-term). **Recommended framing for the planner: adopt b61's per-turn-attempt cap (it matches shipped) and treat b58's "4 successes/half-term" as a SEPARATE success-cap decision to resolve ‚Äî but ROUTE this as a designer-decision (B#), do not let the roadmap silently pick one.** The b61 19-priority list is the DETAILED selection spec regardless of which cap wins; both live under #38.

Its first requirement is the flag‚Üísecession WIRING (gate the existing 1860/1861 events on slavery-active, not year); the #336 SC-only nullification exit (debt #247) is a sub-mechanic it absorbs. This is NOT near-term and does not move the queue ‚Äî it rides the same downstream antebellum/Reconstruction work already sequenced behind K0/K3/K4.
> 4. **‚òÖ SCOTUS (debt #250, gap #270) ‚Äî the per-case EFFECT+PRECONDITION FIELD is a near-term-ABLE SMALL type-add, but INERT until a case roster is authored.** The `SupremeCourtCase.effect?` slot (`types.ts:1555`) exists but needs Yea/Nay + points + a persistent-policy-flag target + a `precondition` for conditional cases; the cadence must move from per-turn to 2-4/half-term. Ship the field cheaply if a court epic is in flight, but it produces NOTHING until content lands ‚Äî so it is a low-priority type-add, not a feature. The amendment-reversal link needs the post-founding amendment mechanism (#39/#119/#64).
> 5. **‚òÖ FUTURE-ERA (debt #251, gaps #258/#169/#206) ‚Äî the event-BLOCKS-event NEGATIVE EDGE + the #169 president-action ban-lift are near-term-ABLE XS-S primitives on the EXISTING 1772 DSL, but they are LOW-VALUE until the future era exists (0%-built, no `future` Era value).** The block-edge unblocks mutually-exclusive event chains inside the 1772 graph today (a small win) and is a prerequisite the future-era content will need; add it opportunistically. Everything else future-era (the two bands, the content corpus, the second era's DSL adoption) rides the era-model epics behind K3/K4.

> **‚òÖ‚òÖ‚òÖ Batch-60 change (5-DIGEST batch ‚Äî `478ab80e`-polk-third-term-mandate/#335+#111+#128+#129+#130+#184+#292+#288 (a live 1840-antebellum PLAYTEST snapshot), `0bde31bd`-start-dates-roster/#92+#115+#186+#228+#206 (the AUTHORITATIVE-but-NOT-final designer 16-window roster), `0adcd14c`-speech-by-era-delegation/#185+#331, `29902e8e`-favorite-playtest-moments/#330+#184+#62+#84+#66+#270+#111+#154+#290+#1 (five FEATURE-WITNESSES of forum systems), `cb91e00e`-vacancy-filling-career-track/#154+#290; I [tech-lead] code-verified EVERY brief-listed claim against `src/` HEAD 2026-06-30 and am the build-state authority; THREE new debt rows #244-#246 (continue after b59's max #243); references this batch's ONE NEW gap row #335 (presidential MANDATE win-quality governing state) + FOLDS/SHARPENS #111 (term-limits STORED-but-NEVER-ENFORCED ‚Üí indefinite terms + the dropped 3rd-term trait + the per-justice SCOTUS election-challenge vote) + #128/#129 (Kingmaker national "+1" + breaks-on-presidency) + #130 (historical-death-date + elective/trait retirement) + #154/#290 (vacancy-fill CT-pull-benefits toggle + House-empty/Senate-generate split) + #184/#292 (platform ¬±5 swing + president's-party midterm ‚àí1) + #185/#331 (speech-by-era speaker rule + primary-era gate) + #270/#66 (SCOTUS election-challenge, distinct from policy cases) + #288 (event‚ÜíREGIONAL penalty; `EraEventResponseEffect` national-only ‚Äî 2nd instance of the wall) + #330 (Senate-Majority-Leader office+toolset ‚Äî UPGRADED) + #62/#84 (contingent-election-to-House) + #92 (canonical 16-window start-roster) + #1 (faction-vs-party); updated game-mechanics ¬ß30.50 (.1/.2/.3). NO new keystone, NO re-sequence ‚Äî top-of-queue framing UNCHANGED: QW0 ‚Üí K0/#296 ‚Üí K2 ‚Üí K3/K4 + scenarioBoot ‚Üí E1. ‚òÖ This is a FOLD-HEAVY batch ‚Äî **1 mint (#335) + 16 folds** onto already-sequenced epics; no source ships new behavior. ONE source is a **live GM PLAYTEST snapshot** (`478ab80e`, ~seven mechanics composing at once), ONE an **authoritative-but-explicitly-not-final designer roster** (`0bde31bd` ‚Äî "the start dates could change"), THREE are **feature-witness / design-poll** threads. ‚òÖ‚òÖ THE LOAD-BEARING b60 FINDING for the planner: **the forum MULTIPLAYER game was materially LARGER than the shipped single-player build** ‚Äî LBJ's Senate-Majority-Leader toolset, a party platform (+5 swings), House contingent elections, and per-justice SCOTUS election-challenges were all witnessed LIVE, and NONE of them ships (`grep majorityLeader|platform|plank|contingent src/` = 0). These are real designed features, not GM flavor ‚Äî but they are **multiplayer-scope / election-epic items, NOT near-term buildables**; PARK them on their epics behind K0. ‚òÖ‚òÖ‚òÖ The build-state CORRECTIONS + near-term calls the planner needs: (1) **#335 MANDATE is a genuine NEW governing state, 0% built** ‚Äî `grep -i mandate src/` = 0; `runPhase_2_9_4_PresidentialGeneral` (`phaseRunners.ts:3752-3814`) records the `ElectionResult` winner + per-candidate votes/pct but **NO margin / win-quality / mandate** field on the president, term, or `ElectionResult`. Small new state, but its consumer (how a mandate governs) rides the election/governing epics ‚Äî a near-term-able TYPE-ADD, not a near-term feature. (2) **‚òÖ #111 term-limits are STORED DATA THAT IS NEVER ENFORCED ‚Üí indefinite terms.** `termLimits` (`types.ts:1396`, `'two_terms'|'no_limits'`) is referenced ONLY in `constitutionalConvention.ts` (`:60-63,120-121,143`) + a `ConventionModal.tsx:11` UI LABEL + the 1856 default (`scenario1856.ts:189`) ‚Äî it is read by NEITHER `calcStateVote`, the primary (`runPhase_2_9_1_Primaries phaseRunners.ts:3725`, which sorts to the top-PV eligible age-35‚Äì80/command‚â•2 candidate), NOR the general. There is NO `termsServed` counter and NO re-run eligibility gate (`grep termsServed|third.?term|3rd.?term src/` = 0) ‚Üí Polk can run a 3rd/4th term indefinitely; adopting `two_terms` changes nothing. This is a **concrete SMALL-ish greenfield: a per-president `termsServed` counter + a primary/general eligibility check** ‚Äî see the near-term call below. (3) **‚òÖ #288 `EraEventResponseEffect` is NATIONAL-ONLY ‚Äî a 2nd instance of the same wall.** The struct (`types.ts:1448-1457`) carries only `meters`/`partyPreference`/`enthusiasm[]`/`interestGroups`/`domesticStability`/`diplomacy`/`startWar`/`text` ‚Äî NO per-state or per-region field, so a "‚àí1 in New England" event penalty (the John-Brown event `eraEvents1856.ts:84`, whose effects are all national) has nowhere to live; `calcStateVote` reads a static `state.bias` (`phaseRunners.ts:3697`). This is the SECOND instance of the national-only wall (the 1st was the missing slavery-flag WRITE channel). Requirement: a per-state/per-region enthusiasm-or-bias delta field on the effect struct. (4) **‚òÖ #330 Senate-Majority-Leader UPGRADED ‚Äî missing office PLUS its toolset.** `OfficeType` (`types.ts:1111-1133`) has `SpeakerOfHouse`+`SenateProTem` (Pro Tem grants only +1 Legislative, `phaseRunners.ts:1884`) but NO `SenateMajorityLeader`/`MinorityLeader` (`grep majorityLeader src/` = 0); the forum witnessed the office WITH an active leader-action toolset, so #330's scope grows from "missing office" to "missing office + LBJ toolset." (5) **#154/#290 vacancy-fill has ZERO fill logic** ‚Äî `vacateOffice` (`phaseRunners.ts:2446-2478`) only NULLS/filters office pointers; seats refill at the next election; no CT-pull path (Career Track `types.ts:43` pols exit only by aging out or CC appointment), no procedural replacement generator (`grep generate*Politician|randomPolitician src/` = 0), no House-empty/Senate-generate split. (6) **#62/#84 contingent election = 0%** ‚Äî 2.9.4 is winner-take-all higher-EV (`winner = blueEv > redEv ? blueCand : redCand`, `phaseRunners.ts:3782`; ties resolve to RED via `>`), no majority threshold, no House fallback, no tie handling. (7) **#92 start-roster: only 1772+1856 boot** on a 4-value `Era` enum ending at `modern` (`types.ts:1337`, no `future` band); no era-boundary table; boot is a hardcoded literal `GameState` (`scenario1856.ts:150-153`/`scenario1772.ts:70-73`). ‚òÖ The canonical 16-window roster (`0bde31bd` POST 2) is now the definitive SPEC for the scenarioBoot keystone (K3/K4) ‚Äî pin it as the boot target.
> **‚òÖ CODE-VERIFICATION RESULTS (every brief pin confirmed at `file:line`; I am the build-state authority ‚Äî NO digest/gap-log/¬ß30.50 claim contradicted on substance; ONE precision-fix flagged below):**
> - **‚òÖ #335 MANDATE ‚Äî a genuine NEW governing state, 0% built. CONFIRMED.** `grep -i mandate src/` = **0** (no field, set, or read path anywhere). `runPhase_2_9_4_PresidentialGeneral` (`phaseRunners.ts:3752-3814`) tallies per-state EV, declares `winner = blueEv > redEv ? blueCand : redCand` (`:3782`), builds an `ElectionResult` with per-candidate `votes`/`pct` (`:3786-3793`), and resets the cabinet ‚Äî it records **NO margin / win-quality / mandate state** at all. The forum's "won two elections but didn't have a mandate either time" (a governing modifier derived FROM the win's margin/turnout, distinct from the `partyPreference`/`enthusiasm` PRE-election levers and the #184 platform swing) has **no shipped representation and no downstream consumer**. ‚Üí the ONLY net-new gap this batch; a small new field on the president/term (and OPENs: binary-vs-graded, margin/turnout threshold, the governing effects). **New on #335.**
> - **‚òÖ‚òÖ #111 TERM-LIMITS STORED-BUT-NEVER-ENFORCED ‚Üí indefinite terms. It is referenced by **NO** election code ‚Äî NOT `calcStateVote`, NOT the primary (`runPhase_2_9_1_Primaries phaseRunners.ts:3725-3749`, which filters `partyId && !deathYear && !retiredYear && age 35‚Äì80 && command >= 2` then sorts by `pvCache + command*5 + traitBonus` ‚Äî an incumbent re-runs simply by re-winning the primary), NOR the general. There is **NO `termsServed` counter** and **NO re-run eligibility gate** (`grep termsServed|third.?term|3rd.?term src/` = 0). ‚Üí term limits are **built as DATA, unenforced**; a `two_terms` selection changes nothing without a NEW per-president `termsServed` counter + a primary/general re-run check. The **3rd-term trait** POST 3 describes was **designed-and-explicitly-dropped** (the GM abandoned it ‚Äî "hard to determine who would have that ability"), unbuilt. CONFIRMED.** `EraEventResponseEffect` (`types.ts:1448-1457`) fields = `meters?` / `partyPreference?` / `enthusiasm?[]` (ideology+party+delta, NOT region) / `interestGroups?[]` / `domesticStability?` / `diplomacy?[]` / `startWar?` / `text` ‚Äî **NO per-state or per-region field**. Requirement: a per-state/per-region enthusiasm-or-bias delta field on the effect struct (cross-ref #247 event-keyed realignment / #292 per-state `preferredIdeology`). **Sharpens #288 ‚Äî a small TYPE ADDITION, near-term-able as a primitive; see below.**

No procedural replacement generator anywhere (`grep generate*Politician|randomPolitician src/` = 0), no special-election / governor-appointment path. ‚Üí the build **coincidentally matches the poll's HOUSE answer** (leave empty) but for EVERY seat, and implements NONE of the CT-pull-with/without-benefits toggle, the bare-min+50/50-retire generator, or the House-empty/Senate-generate chamber split. **Sharpens #154 (vacancy-fill ladder) + #290 (the generator's design intent); a multiplayer-adjacent M-L item ‚Äî PARK.**

The per-justice uphold/challenge vote on a contested election is **distinct from #270** (policy cases), modeled under **#111**; contingent-election + SCOTUS-challenge co-occurred in the same 1840s playtest ‚Üí a designed **disputed-election resolution track**. (c) **Platform:** `grep platform|Platform|plank|Plank src/` = **0** ‚Äî no player-chosen platform producing a ¬±5 swing; nearest analog is the `partyPreference`/`bias` levers (`phaseRunners.ts:3389,3412`). **All three = designed forum systems with no build analog ‚Äî election-epic / multiplayer-scope; PARK behind K0.**

The build ships **2 of 16** (1772, 1856-default) on a **4-value `Era` enum ending at `modern`** (needs a `future` band for entries 15-16) with **no era-boundary table** (just one coarse `eraForYear` + per-scenario `currentEra` literals) and hardcoded boot literals. ‚Üí this roster IS the era-boundary table K3/K4 must encode; the era-beginnings-vs-per-term FORK (creator wants per-term starts IF authoring becomes cheap) means the parameterized boot must not assume era-only starts. Faction-vs-party sniping (#1) = a multiplayer negotiation layer, corroboration only. ‚òÖ Placement: ALL behind K0/#296 (every one is roll- or election-adjacent) and on their respective epics; none re-orders the top of queue.

> **‚òÖ CODE-VERIFICATION RESULTS (every brief pin confirmed at `file:line`; I am the build-state authority ‚Äî NO digest/gap-log/¬ß30.49 claim contradicted on substance; ONE sharpening + the record-fix confirmations flagged below):**
> - **‚òÖ‚òÖ #332 MID-GAME CONCON ‚Äî the founding substrate SHIPS, the mid-game path is 0%. CONFIRMED.** `makeConvention(year)` (`constitutionalConvention.ts:6-77`) builds the 7-article founding convention; its **SOLE** caller is `constitutional_convention_kickoff` (`phaseRunners.ts:3182-3186`, `snap.game.pendingConvention = makeConvention(...)`), reached only via the 1772 Annapolis graph path. The interactive loop ships end-to-end: `engine.ts:18-19` returns `needsPlayerInput:'convention'`; `GameContext.tsx:491-493` opens the `ConventionModal`; `autoFillCPUVotes`(`:81`)+`applyConvention`(`:127`) resolve it (`GameContext.tsx:580-582`). BUT the mid-game TRIGGER is absent on all four required axes: (a) the **`domestic` meter** (`types.ts:1403`, label `labels.ts:51`) is read/written ONLY by bill effects (`applyEffect`), era-event modulation (`phaseRunners.ts:2912-2947`), cabinet drift, and the "use the army" gov-action (`:3638`) ‚Äî **NOTHING reads a low/lowest `domestic` value to unlock/gate/fire a convention** (it is scored, never a system-trigger); (b) NO 5-Gov gate (`grep 'governing >= 5' src/` = 0); (c) NO 2/3-of-states convention threshold (the `2/3` that DOES exist is the unrelated Articles-passage `continentalCongress.ts:198` + the `'two_thirds'` amendmentProcess option value); (d) NO scenario boots into a `pendingConvention` (both builders hardcode a literal `GameState`: 1856 `phaseId:'2.1.2'` `scenario1856.ts:153`, 1772 `phaseId:'2.1.1'` `scenario1772.ts:73`). **‚òÖ `startConvention` is a DEAD postEffect union member (`types.ts:1478`) ‚Äî the dispatcher (`phaseRunners.ts:3056-3087`) handles ONLY `assembleCC` (`:3060`); every OTHER of the SIX union types (`startWar`/`unlockGovernors`/`unlockArticles`/`startConvention`/`endWar`/`addPolitician`) falls to the `default` "Unhandled postEffect type" log (`:3083`), and only `assembleCC` is emitted by any data (`eraEvents1772.ts:107`).** ‚Üí #332 is WIRING atop a reusable substrate, NOT greenfield: the article-voting + CPU auto-fill + historical-default fallback already ship; the deltas are the Dom-Stab trigger (a NEW consumer the meter lacks), a per-state 5-Gov "call a convention" gov-action (folds into #20), the 2/3-raced-vs-recovery threshold, and the boot-into-a-convention entry point (folds into #92/#115/#186/#228). **Sharpens #20; rides the scenarioBoot keystone.**
> - **‚òÖ‚òÖ TRAIT REWORK (#327/#189/#216) ‚Äî the SPLIT the digest proposes ALREADY SHIPS; the two DURABLE asks are BLOCKED by missing type-fields. CONFIRMED, + the 3 record-fixes hold.** `Scandalous` (`types.ts:101`) AND `Controversial` (`:103`) are both live in the `Trait` union with DISTINCT, fully-authored election bands (`:816-828` Scandalous / `:830-842` Controversial), each with an `opponentConditional` swap vs `Integrity` only. **The #189 ideology-SPLIT math is BLOCKED at the type level:** `TraitElectionRule` (`:727-736`) carries `{trait, context, magnitude, era?, opponentConditional?}` ‚Äî its ONLY conditional is `opponentConditional` (an OPPONENT-trait check), there is **NO electorate-ideology-conditional field**, so "penalize only with the out-of-line electorate, bonus with the aligned one" has nowhere to live. **The #327 Controversial‚ÜíScandalous ESCALATION is BLOCKED:** `tryGrantTrait` (`engine/traits.ts:41-59`) is conflict-REPLACE (d6-arbitrated remove-held + add-new, `conflictD6Threshold:4` `types.ts:638`) ‚Äî NOT a severity ladder; and there is **NO trait-acquisition timestamp** on `Politician` (`grep gainedYear|acquiredYear|whenGained src/` = 0), so the "When Gained" field the escalation + a "recent-scandal decays" rule need is absent. **#216 Teflon** ‚Äî `grep Teflon|Disharmonious|Illicit|Disgraced src/` = 0 (all absent from the 55-trait union). **The 3 RECORD-FIXES verified:** (1) the Flip-Flopper‚ÜíTwo-Faced RENAME does NOT ship ‚Äî BOTH coexist as distinct traits (`:98` Flip-Flopper, `:99` Two-Faced; Two-Faced conflicts `Predictable` `:677-678`, Flip-Flopper is in `CAREER_RANDOM_NEGATIVES` `:227`); (2) "Controversial = opposite of Integrity" is FALSE ‚Äî `TRAIT_CONFLICTS` pairs Integrity ONLY with `Corrupt` (`:663-664`); (3) the split/rename is the SHIPPED reality, so the thread (Jan-2025) pre-dates or is unaware of it. **Sharpens #189/#292 (the election-rework epic ‚Äî same `calcStateVote`/`TraitElectionRule` surface) + #327 (Disgraced ladder) + #216; NO in-place body edit needed ‚Äî all corrections recorded in ¬ß30.49.2.**
> - **‚òÖ‚òÖ #333 FOREIGN ANNEXATION ‚Äî the annexable MAP DATA ships; the conquest MECHANIC + the entire foreign-pol CONTENT model are 0% built. CONFIRMED.** Foreign nations are annexable seeds ‚Äî `region` enum includes `Canada|Caribbean|Latin America|Pacific|Atlantic` (`types.ts:1322`; also on `Politician.region`), ~103 foreign-region seeds in `expansionStates.ts`, each stamped with the FLAT placeholder `electoralVotes:4, bias:0, industries:{agriculture:2,trade:1}` (`:173-183`, EV at `:178`) ‚Äî no size/likelihood differentiation. `admitState` (`territories.ts:8-23`) is add-only and **IDENTICAL for US-territory and foreign** ‚Äî checks not-present, copies the seed, stamps `admissionYear`, pushes, logs "admitted to the Union"; there is **NO conquest/war/annexation path** (`grep annex|conquer|conquest|cede|divest src/` = prose/UI/flavor only). The bundled dataset has **ZERO foreign pols** (0 Trudeau/Laurier/Macdonald, 0 foreign home-states) and there is **no ~20-token generator** ‚Äî the only procedural generator is the flat 20-rookie stub with 15 hardcoded Anglo-American names (`phaseRunners.ts:181-182`, always `state: pick(stateIds)` `:203`) ‚Üí the #290 nation-aware-generation fold. **This is DISTINCT from the US alt-state gaps** (#38 carpetbagger `altState`, #55 53-state US roster, #269 US state-fission, #325 CA UC/LC split ‚Äî all US expansion). The digest's foreign-pol model = `0-2 real hand-authored (notability-gated, many zero) + ~20 token/nation, random-gen majority, depth ‚àù annexation-likelihood, post-early-release`. **Sharpens #290; SUPERSEDES the #60 roster-figure framing (Canada-conquest); DEFER (post-early-release).**

CONFIRMED.** OrangeP47's lever (maxed-spending ‚Üí sell a state ‚Üí one-off spending burst next turn, `53f0759d` POST 6) couples two systems the build keeps entirely separate: there is **NO sell/cede/divest path** for an admitted state (`grep sell|cede|divest|relinquish|dispose src/` = 0 state-sale path; territory is admission-only) AND there is **NO economy/spending/budget/treasury model** in `territories.ts` (both halves of the lever are absent). It is a Dec-2022 brainstorm with NO GM accept/reject (thin authority). Distinct from admission/annexation and from a war-loss cede. **PARK ‚Äî double-blocked on an economy model that doesn't exist; sits at the #42/#11 (spending-cap) √ó #178/#260 (territory) intersection.**

> **‚òÖ‚òÖ‚òÖ Batch-58 change (5-DIGEST batch ‚Äî `97aa3f6f`-state-of-rules-changelog/#45+#56+#85+#38+#189+#292+#296+#287+#327 (the AUTHORITATIVE designer changelog), `1ae348bb`-investigations-special-committees/#330+#327+#112+#206+#258+#221, `fb8070f3`-preprimary-conventions/#185+#71+#72+#183+#331, `df11a769`-trump-2-0-content-catalog/#329+#169+#221+#270+#258+#206+#20, `eb573c91`-1928-era-of-ideologies-duo/#92+#4+#1+#114 (corroboration-only); I [tech-lead] code-verified EVERY brief-listed claim against `src/` HEAD 2026-06-30 and am the build-state authority; FIVE new debt rows #236-#240 (continue after b57's max #235); references this batch's NEW gap rows #329 (bundle/omnibus bill) + #330 (congressional investigations/special committees) + #331 (nomination-mechanism era-selector + King Caucus) + sharpens #45/#56/#85 + #38 + #189/#292 + #296/#287 + #327 + #258/#206 + #169/#221/#270/#20 + #185/#71/#72/#183 + #1/#92/#114; updated game-mechanics ¬ß30.48. NO new keystone, NO re-sequence ‚Äî top-of-queue framing UNCHANGED: QW0 ‚Üí K0/#296 ‚Üí K2 ‚Üí K3/K4 + scenarioBoot ‚Üí E1. ‚òÖ THE HEADLINE for the planner: ONE source (`97aa3f6f`) is an **AUTHORITATIVE designer CHANGELOG** ‚Äî three already-LANDED rulings with canonical NUMBERS (not proposals) ‚Äî and it lands those numbers on items the queue ALREADY sequenced: **Change 1** (military-preparedness ‚Üí a FLAT per-tier battle bonus) sharpens the war track (debt #205 = #85 live war-casualty + #45/#56 greenfield war loop, debt #105/#124); **Change 2** (state-movement cap = 4 SUCCESSES/half-term, alt EXCLUDED) is a RE-MODEL of relocation (debt #38) that ESCALATES the standing BUG-0 (the "XS one-line `5‚Üí4`" framing is now WRONG ‚Äî see the correction below); **Change 3** (election trait ‚Üí 50%¬±1 / ability ‚Üí 20%√óability) is a CONCRETE K0/#296 dependent that FOLDS into the #184/#292 election-rework epic + the debt-#1 `Math.random` die-fix (SAME `calcStateVote` lines). The other three substantive sources are net-new DESIGN with 0% shipped: **#330** congressional investigations (a greenfield L system ‚Äî the designed PRODUCER of the dead `forceRetire` executor), **#331** the nomination era-selector + King Caucus (the human convention rulebook + the 12A P+VP ticket, on the empty 2.9.2 stub), **#329** the bundle/omnibus bill (a net-new bill TYPE atop the single-effect `Legislation`). ‚òÖ‚òÖ‚òÖ The build-state CORRECTIONS the planner needs: (1) **Change 1 is a NET-NEW coupling, not a TUNE** ‚Äî the Rev-War combat resolver reads NO preparedness meter today (`grep meters.military revolutionaryWar.ts` = 0); the `military` `NationalMeter` feeds only era-events/display/gov-action, never the to-hit roll. When built it lands in the NEW FLAT form (add the tier bonus to `target`/`baseTarget+diffMod`). (2) **Change 2 ESCALATES BUG-0 from "XS one-line edit" to a RE-MODEL** ‚Äî shipped counts ATTEMPTS (increment precedes the success roll), per-YEAR (yearly reset), alt-state INCLUDED ‚Äî THREE semantic mismatches vs the ruling's 4-SUCCESSES/half-term/alt-EXCLUDED; there is no half-term success counter in `src/` (grep = 0). (3) **`forceRetire` is RESOLVED but ZERO templates emit it** (re-confirmed from a 3rd angle, QW49) ‚Äî and **#330 is its designed PRODUCER**; the near-term #140 fix (add `{ kind: 'forceRetire' }` to the `scandal-federal-investigation` template `anytimeEvents.ts:258`, which already carries `Scandalous`+`pvHit`+`scandalScaled`) stands INDEPENDENT of the full #330 build. (4) **`vicePresidentId` is NEVER set by any election path** (re-confirmed) ‚Äî the 12A ticket is entirely unmodeled; `runPhase_2_9_4` sets `presidentId` and never touches VP.

> **‚òÖ CODE-VERIFICATION RESULTS (every brief pin confirmed at `file:line`; I am the build-state authority; NO digest/gap-log/¬ß30.48 claim contradicted on substance ‚Äî ONE standing-row CORRECTION to flag: BUG-0's size framing):**
> - **‚òÖ‚òÖ CHANGE 1 ‚Äî military-preparedness‚Üícombat is ENTIRELY UNBUILT; it is a NET-NEW coupling (NOT a tune of an existing term). CONFIRMED.** The combat resolver (`revolutionaryWar.ts:187-218`) reads no preparedness meter: naval `target = (secWar.admin + admiral.military) + admiral.military*10; win = d100() ‚â§ target + 30` (`:187-191`); ground `baseTarget = planning + general.military*10 + (frenchAlliance ? 25 : 0)`, `diffMod ‚àà {‚àí20,0,+15}`, `win = d100() ‚â§ baseTarget + diffMod` (`:212-218`). **`grep meters.military src/engine/revolutionaryWar.ts` = 0.** `military` IS a real `NationalMeters` field (`types.ts:1402`, label "Military Preparedness" `Meter.tsx:8`) but combat ignores it ‚Äî it feeds only era-event modifiers (`phaseRunners.ts:2925,2928`), the `milPower` display/threshold (`:3602`), and a "use the army" gov-action (`:3638`). ‚Üí there is NO preparedness term in combat to tune; the canonical NEW FLAT ladder (9 banded tiers ‚àí10‚Ä¶+20, immediate-defeat branch DELETED) must be ADDED to `target` at `:191` and `baseTarget+diffMod` at `:218`. **Sharpens war-casualty debt #205 (#85, the live `applyCasualties` tune+determinism row) + the greenfield 1856+ war loop (#45/#56, debt #105/#124).**
> - **‚òÖ‚òÖ CHANGE 2 ‚Äî relocation: shipped is ATTEMPTS/YEAR/alt-INCLUDED; ruling is 4-SUCCESSES/HALF-TERM/alt-EXCLUDED ‚Äî a 3-axis RE-MODEL. CONFIRMED, and it CORRECTS BUG-0's size.** `RELOCATION_ATTEMPTS_PER_TURN = 5` (`types.ts:247`). The accumulator increments the per-faction count at `phaseRunners.ts:579` **before** the success roll at `:584-585` (so a FAILED move still consumes budget = ATTEMPTS, not successes); the counter resets when `g.relocationAttempts.year !== g.year` (`:536`) = **per-YEAR**, not half-term; alt-state has **NO special-case** at the cap gate (`:577`) ‚Äî `altState` is consumed only on success (`:585`) and only affects the odds, so an alt move STILL counts. There is **no half-term success counter** in `src/` (grep = 0; `halfTermSummary.ts` tracks deaths/retirements, not moves). ‚Üí honoring POST 4 is a re-model: a per-faction **success** counter scoped to the **half-term**, capped at **4**, with **alt-state excluded** ‚Äî NOT the const flip the BUG-0 row plans. **Sharpens #38; ‚öÝ CORRECTS the standing BUG-0 / divergence #9 row** (`technical-guide.md:1903`, which says "One-line edit: `RELOCATION_ATTEMPTS_PER_TURN = 4` + a guard"; that closes only the VALUE axis ‚Äî the unit (attempts‚Üísuccesses) + window (year‚Üíhalf-term) mismatches remain, so BUG-0 is now S, not XS).
> - **‚òÖ‚òÖ CHANGE 3 ‚Äî election scoring is on a THIRD model (deterministic fixed-point bands + STILL opponent-conditional + NO ability term); the new 50%¬±1 / 20%√óability rolls REQUIRE seeded `chance` and supersede the `:3711` jitter. CONFIRMED.** `calcStateVote` (`phaseRunners.ts:3685-3723`) score = `50 + baseLean*5 + partyPref*5 + enthusiasm*2 + pv*0.1 + factionBias + traitBonus + (Math.random()‚àí0.5)*8` (`:3709-3711`), with `traitBonus` from `applyTraitElectionBonus` (`electionEffects.ts:16-39`). Three divergences from the ruling: (a) **traits are DETERMINISTIC fixed points** ‚Äî `TRAIT_ELECTION_BANDS = {SMALL:2,MEDIUM:4,LARGE:8}` (`types.ts:721-725`) added whenever held, no roll (e.g. Charismatic-presGeneral = flat **+4** every time vs the ruling's **50% chance of +1**); (b) **opponent-conditional rows STILL EXIST** ‚Äî exactly what the ruling removes: `applyTraitElectionBonus` swaps to `bumpedMagnitude` when an opponent matches (`electionEffects.ts:29-32`; Integrity SMALL‚ÜíMEDIUM vs Scandalous/Controversial/Corrupt, `types.ts:748-758`); (c) **NO Gov/Legis-ability term** in the state-vote scorer (`grep skills.governing|skills.legislative` over `:3685-3723` = 0). **‚òÖ The `Math.random()` jitter is at `phaseRunners.ts:3711`** (re-confirmed = the flagship determinism leak, debt #1); the rebuild's 50% / 20%√óability rolls **must use seeded `chance(...)` (K0/#296)** and the ¬±8 jitter is **superseded entirely** by the per-trait rolls. **Sharpens #189 (trait-scoring owner) / #292 (ability-input); CONFIRMS #287/#296 (the `Math.random` offender, line-anchored). FOLDS into the #184/#292 election-rework epic (debt #184) ‚Äî SAME `calcStateVote` lines.**
> - **‚òÖ‚òÖ #330 CONGRESSIONAL INVESTIGATIONS ‚Äî 0% built; the designed PRODUCER of the dead `forceRetire` executor. CONFIRMED.** `grep investigat|specialCommittee src/` returns only flavor strings (the `scandal-federal-investigation` template TEXT `anytimeEvents.ts:258,264` + an 1856 era-event response label `eraEvents1856.ts:97`) ‚Äî **no investigation phase, entity, 5-member panel, or guilt roll.** The only "committees" are the 4 bill-review committees `'Domestic'|'Foreign'|'Economic'|'Justice'` (`types.ts:1237`). It is the **governor-action TWIN of the #323 CPU bill-scorer**, and it RIDES three prerequisites: the #20 gov-action LIBRARY (the action set the selector picks from ‚Äî itself a greenfield L epic) + the governor-trait/expertise WIRING + a designer call on the **#294 DIRECTION CONFLICT** ‚Äî and sits behind K0/#296 (every bucket roll is seeded RNG). ‚òÖ‚òÖ‚òÖ The build-state corrections the planner needs: (1) **the predicate/prereq-gating MACHINERY is already SHIPPED** (`Predicate` + `evalPredicate` + `GraphNode.precondition`) ‚Äî but deployed in the **1772 era graph ONLY** (35 preconditions; 1856 + both anytime pools = 0); so #258 is now a **CONTENT-AUTHORING REQUIREMENT** (extend gating to 1856/anytime/future), NOT a new engine epic. (2) **`forceRetire` is RESOLVED in the engine** (`phaseRunners.ts:2723-2728`) but carried by **ZERO templates** ‚Äî so the #140 scandal‚Üíremoval near-term fix is purely adding `{ kind: 'forceRetire' }`/`death` to a scandal template's `effects[]`, no new mechanism. > **‚òÖ CODE-VERIFICATION RESULTS (every brief pin confirmed at `file:line`; I am the build-state authority ‚Äî TWO digest discrepancies to flag at the gate; all brief pins + game-master ¬ß30.47 + gap rows #327/#328 confirmed against `src/` HEAD; NO substantive contradiction):**
> - **‚òÖ‚òÖ CPU GOVERNOR-ACTION = a flat 30% governing-skill `bias` nudge ‚Äî CONFIRMED, reads NO traits/expertise/cards/crisis/industry (#20/#328).** `runPhase_2_5_2_Governors` (`phaseRunners.ts:3382-3392`) is the ENTIRE governor turn: 11 lines; for each state with a governor, `chance(0.3)` (seeded) ‚Üí `s.bias` nudged by `(gov.skills.governing ‚àí 1) * 0.05`, signed by party (`BLUE` ‚àí, else +), `clamp(..., -5, 5)` (`:3389`). It reads ONLY `gov.skills.governing` and `gov.partyId`. NO 25/25/50 rule, NO trait/expertise gate, NO industry/election-law/special-passion path. The building blocks DO ship as data/infra ‚Äî `Trait` union incl. Integrity/Controversial/Manipulative (`types.ts:62-117`), the 19-tag `Expertise` axis (`:182-192`, `EXPERTISE` array `:188-192`), `LOBBY_INDUSTRY` (`:398-414`), convention-only `termLimits` (`:1396`), and seeded `chance`/`pick`/`pickWeighted` (`phaseRunners.ts:9`) ‚Äî so #328 is WIRING existing pieces into a new selector, not greenfield infra. The selector still needs the #20 action LIBRARY to pick from (that IS greenfield).
> - **‚òÖ‚òÖ #294 DIRECTION CONFLICT ‚Äî CONFIRMED. The shipped `LOBBY_INDUSTRY` runs lobby‚áíindustry; the thread wants industry‚áílobby.** The ONLY consumer of `LOBBY_INDUSTRY` is the 2.1.8 alignment-drift pass (`phaseRunners.ts:1638-1656`): for each state, each faction with a local member, each held lobby card bumps the matching `s.industries[key]` +1 (capped at 5, `:1649`). That is **held-lobby-card ‚áí state-industry +1** ‚Äî the REVERSE of the thread's **governor-improves-industry ‚áí matching-lobby-card-POINTS** (Agriculture‚ÜíBig-Ag, Finance‚ÜíWall-Street). Same table (`LOBBY_INDUSTRY` `types.ts:398`), opposite causal arrow; the gov-action use case is unbuilt. ‚öÝ This is a DESIGNER CALL (industry‚áílobby, lobby‚áíindustry, or both-directional) before the gov-action-industry-expansion path is built.
> - **‚òÖ‚òÖ FUTURE era / scenario ‚Äî CONFIRMED 0% (#206); meters all present incl. Earth's-health=`planet` (#221).** `Era = 'independence' | 'federalism' | 'nationalism' | 'modern'` (`types.ts:1337`) ‚Äî **NO `future` value**; only `scenario1772.ts` + `scenario1856.ts` exist (the `'future'`/"future" grep hits are flavor strings in `expansionStates.ts:5`/`SettingsPage.tsx` only). All 7 `NationalMeters` are modeled (`types.ts:1399-1407`) including **`planet` (=Earth's-health, no new meter needed)**; `partyPreference` is the separate top-level field. So the future-era CONTENT is missing but the meter substrate it targets is complete.
> - **‚òÖ‚òÖ PREDICATE-GATING MACHINERY shipped, deployed 1772-ONLY ‚Äî CONFIRMED (#258 is now a CONTENT-AUTHORING requirement, not a new engine epic).** The serializable `Predicate` tree (`types.ts:1487-1504`) + interpreter `evalPredicate` (`engine/eraGraph.ts:12`) + the optional `GraphNode.precondition` (`eraEvents1772.ts:25`) are real and shipped; selection gates on it at `eraGraph.ts:114`. **BUT it is deployed in the 1772 era graph ONLY:** `grep -c precondition` ‚Üí `eraEvents1772.ts` = **35**, `eraEvents1856.ts` = **0**, `anytimeEvents.ts` = **0**, `anytimeNationalEvents.ts` = **0**. The GM's two no-prereq complaints (half-the-animals-extinct firing twice; citizens-in-space with no enabling state) are the exact symptom of un-gated content. Delta = extend the EXISTING machinery to 1856 + both anytime pools + future content (a once-only/`eventCompleted` guard for the repeat-firing case), as an authoring DISCIPLINE ‚Äî corroborates the b40-era #60/DH-60 territory-gating finding from a 4th angle.
> - **‚òÖ‚òÖ "Disgraced"/"Illicit"/"Disharmonious" ABSENT from the trait union; impeachment 0%; retirement age-only; Integrity-d6 not categorical (#327/#112/#130/#216) ‚Äî CONFIRMED.** `Trait` union (`types.ts:62-117`) = **55 traits** (awk count of `| '...'` lines); `grep disgrac|illicit|disharmon src/` = **0**, `grep impeach src/` = **0**, `grep expel src/` = **0**. Retirement is purely age-driven: `runPhase_2_4_1_Deaths` (`phaseRunners.ts:2351-2380`) rolls `retireRateFor(p.age) * cfg.retireMult` ‚Äî NO scandal/unpopularity/impeachment input (so vcczar's "already have most with retirement" describes the FORUM game, not the build). Integrity does NOT categorically block disgrace: `Integrity:'Corrupt'` is a `TRAIT_CONFLICTS` pair (`types.ts:663-664`) with `conflictD6Threshold:4` (`:638`), so during a scandal-scaled event `tryGrantTrait(p,'Corrupt')` displaces on a **d6**, logged "Integrity weathers the scandal ‚Äî Corrupt does not take, on a d6" (`phaseRunners.ts:2746-2750`) ‚Äî probabilistic, scandal-specific, NOT a 100% lock.
> - **‚òÖ‚òÖ `forceRetire` RESOLVED in the engine but ZERO templates carry it ‚Äî CONFIRMED (the #140 near-term fix is purely additive).** The effect kind is EXECUTED ‚Äî `case 'forceRetire': p.retiredYear = ...; recordRetirement(...); markPoliticianRetired(...)` (`phaseRunners.ts:2723-2728`), and the effect-walker logs `retireCause:'event'` when a template has it (`:2767,:2775`). But `grep forceRetire src/data/anytimeEvents.ts` = the **type declaration only** (`:28`) ‚Äî **no template's `effects[]` includes it**. `death` is wired to exactly 2 templates, both violence/accident (small-plane-crash `:170`, assassination `:236`); the scandal-* templates (240-386) grant `Scandalous`/`Corrupt` + `pvHit` and never remove. So vcczar's "random events that can remove controversial pols already exist" (`0dc883c4` POST 4) describes the DESIGNED game; the BUILD has the removal PRIMITIVE wired-and-working but no scandal/corruption‚Üíremoval EVENT ‚Äî the fix is adding `{ kind: 'forceRetire' }`/`{ kind: 'death' }` to a scandal template's `effects[]` + a trigger probability.
> - **‚òÖ‚òÖ INCUMBENCY ‚Äî `calcStateVote` has NO incumbency/prior-office term; `Politician` has no ousted flag; the code's `incumbencyAdvantage` is the faction-leader CHALLENGE (#287/#297) ‚Äî CONFIRMED.** `calcStateVote` (`phaseRunners.ts:3685-3723`) score = `50 + baseLean*5 + partyPref*5 + enthusiasm*2 + pv*0.1 + factionBias + traitBonus + (Math.random()-0.5)*8` (`:3709-3711`) ‚Äî NO incumbency, NO prior-office, NO returning-candidate term (a returning officeholder gets only office-weighted `pv*0.1`). `grep ousted|priorSeat|previousOffice|lostElection|officeHistory src/` = **0** ‚Äî `Politician` (`types.ts:1251-1291`) has `currentOffice` only. The ONLY `incumbencyAdvantage` in code is `LEADERSHIP_RULES.eraConfig.*.incumbencyAdvantage` (`types.ts:462/466/470/474/479`, values 30/20/15/8 by era), the faction-leader-challenge tenure term consumed at `phaseRunners.ts:2070` + `FactionLeaderPage.tsx` ‚Äî intra-faction leadership, NOT electoral seat incumbency. NOTE the `(Math.random()-0.5)*8` at `:3711` is the pre-existing determinism leak (debt #1, folds into K0).
> - **‚öÝ TWO DIGEST DISCREPANCIES TO FLAG AT THE GATE (neither changes substance):** (1) **trait-count drift between digests** ‚Äî `326c33dd` says the `Trait` union is **55** traits and `0dc883c4` says **60**; the code is **55** (awk count of `| '...'` lines over `types.ts:62-117`). Both agree "Disgraced"/"Illicit" are absent; only the roster size differs. Use **55**. No conflict, noted for cross-ref hygiene.

> **‚òÖ‚òÖ‚òÖ Batch-56 change (5-DIGEST batch, all DIGEST-ONLY ‚Äî `70a67176`-sc-nomination-confirmation/#66+#270+#326+#20 (+#52+#275), `0af55af3`-party-shift-inevitability/#4+#171+#298+#302 (+#314+#306+#263), `b15af728`-game-start-date/#86+#115+#186+#92 (+#263), `2b729148`-california-uc-lc-split/#325 (+#294+#295+#34+#55+#313), `92202e9a`-jeannette-rankin-suffrage/#238+#258 (+DH-14); I [tech-lead] code-verified EVERY brief-listed claim against `src/` HEAD 2026-06-30 and am the build-state authority; SIX new debt rows #225-#230 (continue after b55's max #224); references this batch's NEW gap rows #325/#326 + sharpens #66/#270/#20/#4/#171/#298/#302/#314/#306/#263/#86/#115/#186/#92/#294/#295/#34/#238/#258/DH-14; updated game-mechanics ¬ß30.46. NO new keystone, NO re-sequence ‚Äî top-of-queue framing UNCHANGED: QW0 ‚Üí K0/#296 ‚Üí K2 ‚Üí K3/K4 + scenarioBoot ‚Üí E1. ‚òÖ This batch adds NO shipped behavior; every delta is a DESIGNER-DECISION (open A/B/C fork) or a bounded ADDITION/FOUNDATIONAL-prereq onto already-shipped engines ‚Äî re-verified. ALL FIVE sources are DESIGN-Q&A / poll / deep-dive threads (no historian ran the batch). ‚òÖ‚òÖ THE HEADLINE for the planner: TWO of this batch's items are DESIGNER-DECISIONS that must route to vcczar BEFORE any build ‚Äî (A) the **SC auto-confirm-vs-always-vote fork** (`70a67176` POST 2/5-9; unresolved in-thread, weight leans always-vote; EITHER/OR-coupled to the automated court-DECISIONS #270 ‚Äî if you always-vote, you can't also auto-decide), and (B) the **post-1772 start-phase-entry A/B/C** (`b15af728`; team leans B = begin at 2.1.2). NEITHER is buildable until the user picks. Everything else is a bounded addition or a foundational prereq ‚Äî see the size ledger. ‚òÖ‚òÖ‚òÖ The build-state corrections the planner needs: (1) the party-shift INEVITABILITY ENGINE is SHIPPED (`factionCenter` + `resolveIdeologyShift`, NOT 0%); only its GATING is unbuilt. (2) the 1856 scenario is the DEFAULT New-Game and boots as a HYBRID (enters 2.1.2, pre-seated incumbents) ‚Äî the thread's "only 1772 ships" is STALE.

> **‚òÖ CODE-VERIFICATION RESULTS (every brief pin confirmed at `file:line`; I am the build-state authority ‚Äî FIVE line-drift / path mis-citations to flag at the gate, plus all 5 digests' shipped-state claims re-confirmed; NO digest/gap-log/¬ß30.46 CLAIM contradicted on substance):**
> - **‚òÖ‚òÖ SC nomination-CONFIRMATION ‚Äî CONFIRMED auto-pick, NO vote, NO age floor.** Justices are auto-REPLACED on retirement, never nominated-by-faction and never voted: the reactive filter is `!currentOffice && !deathYear && !retiredYear && partyId === president.partyId && skills.judicial >= 2`, sorted by highest `judicial`, top one seated and logged "confirmed" (`phaseRunners.ts:3661-3667`). `grep` confirms this is the ONLY justice-pick site. Retirement is `age >= 75 && chance(0.15)` (`:3655`) ‚Äî `age>=75` only TRIGGERS the vacancy, it NEVER gates the appointee, so a 20-yr-old J2 extremist is seatable TODAY (the live ‚òÖ7 bug + #326). The CABINET "mirror" is likewise auto-SCORED: `CABINET_SEAT_SCORING[seat]` (`:2167`) ‚Üí `scoreCabinetCandidate` + cross-party penalty ‚Üí sort ‚Üí `pick = scored[0]` set into seat + logged "confirmed" (`:2191-2198`), with NO confirmation vote. Court DECISIONS = a 50% coin-flip `chance(0.5)` (`runPhase_2_5_3_Court` `:3397`) over 4 hardcoded case titles (`:3398-3403`). So both the cabinet "mirror" #66 wants AND the SC nomination it would extend are un-voted today ‚Äî #66 (the cabinet committee‚Üífloor vote) is the unbuilt root.
> - **‚òÖ‚òÖ PARTY-SHIFT INEVITABILITY ENGINE ‚Äî CONFIRMED SHIPPED (correcting any "0%" read).** The ideology-drift-toward-faction-center engine is BUILT: `factionCenter(snap, factionId)` (`phaseRunners.ts:704-727`, living-member mean ideology index + expertise-lean bias, clamped 0-6) + `resolveIdeologyShift` (`:768-794`, runs only in phase `2.1.5`, drifts a subject toward the ACTOR faction's center via `stepToward`, one attempt/subject/turn, capped per turn). This IS the mechanism that makes the modern shift "practically guaranteed" (the digest's claim). What is UNBUILT is the GATING around it: the era-banded `eligibleIdeologies` ships **1772-inaugural-ONLY** ‚Äî per-faction `eligibleIdeologies: Ideology[]` (`factions1772.ts:12-22`, e.g. `fact_red_lw_1772` :19) read by `getEligibleIdeologies` (`:30`) behind the `isExpansion1772` gate (`scenarioId === '1772' && year === startYear`, `:38`); the 1892/2012 columns of the eligible-ideology MATRIX are absent. `grep canBeIndependent`=0 and `grep draftRestriction`=0 across `src/` ‚Äî neither the starting-party draft gate, the "can be independent" tag, nor the restriction settings toggle exists.
> - **‚òÖ START-DATE / SCENARIO-BOOT ‚Äî CONFIRMED 1856 ships, as a HYBRID, as the DEFAULT (correcting "only 1772 ships").** `scenario1856.ts` builds `phaseId: '2.1.2'` (`:153`, "start past draft for first turn"), with incumbents PRE-SEATED from the seed roster (president/VP/cabinet/court/general found by `currentOffice.type`, `:123-141`), `constitutionRatified: true` + `governorsExist: true` + `currentEra: 'nationalism'`. `NewGameScreen.tsx:25` defaults `useState<ScenarioId>('1856')` ‚Äî 1856 is the DEFAULT New-Game scenario, so the thread's "only 1772 ships" (POST 6) is STALE. Each scenario HARDCODES its own entry phase in its builder (`scenario1772.ts:73`='2.1.1', `scenario1856.ts:153`='2.1.2'); there is NO configurable A/B/C boot and no shared entry-phase constant. The 1856 boot matches NEITHER poll option A (start at draft) NOR B (start at 2.1.2-with-faction-leader): it skips the draft AND there is no 2.2.4 Party-Leaders step before nationalism play (2.2.4 is independence-skipped, `phases.ts:86-87`), so the shipped hybrid needs reconcile against whatever A/B/C the designer picks.
> - **‚òÖ CALIFORNIA UC/LC SPLIT-STATE ‚Äî CONFIRMED 0% shipped; `altState` is NOT it.** CA and TX exist as SINGLE states only: `tx` (`states1856.ts:24`, EV=4) / `ca` (`:35`, EV=4); no `upper_cal`/`lower_cal`/`west_texas`/`north_texas` anywhere in `src/`. The ~150-entry annexable registry (`expansionStates.ts`) has Mexican/western carve-ups but NO CA/TX split, and stamps a flat hardcoded `electoralVotes: 4` on every expansion state (`:178`). `State` (`types.ts`) has no `parentState`/`splitOf` field; no pol‚Üísplit assignment of any kind (no die, no quartile roll, no ‚â•4-core-office fill). ‚öÝ `altState` (`types.ts:1256`) is the UNRELATED relocation/carpetbagger secondary-home field ‚Äî region-distance-seeded once per pol (`altStateSeeded`) and consumed by `runPhase_2_1_4_Relocations` (`phaseRunners.ts:546,554,588,626-642,657-659`). Do NOT conflate it with the split-state system. (The `latin_columbia`/`LCO` entry `expansionStates.ts:46` is Latin America ‚Äî a coincidental "LC" abbreviation, not Lower California.)
> - **‚òÖ JEANNETTE-RANKIN SUFFRAGE ‚Äî CONFIRMED NO gender model at all.** `grep -i 'gender|female|suffrage|womens'` across `src/` = ZERO. `Politician` (`types.ts:1251`) and `ImportedDraftee` (`:1780`) carry no gender/sex attribute; there is no per-state women's-suffrage flag and no `women`/`suffrage`/`19th` in `src/data/`. The additive electoral-bias channel that COULD carry the proposed ‚àí5 exists: `calcStateVote` (`phaseRunners.ts:3684`) sums a `traitBonus` from `TRAIT_ELECTION_EFFECTS`/`TRAIT_ELECTION_BANDS` (`types.ts:738`). The existing DH-14 row (`technical-guide.md:1920`, `drums` ‚Äî "bill ideology impacts not era-aware ‚Üí Women's Suffrage in 1916 is era-wrong") is the suffrage-adjacent content debt this gender prereq sits beside.
> - **‚öÝ FIVE LINE-DRIFT / PATH MIS-CITATIONS TO FLAG AT THE GATE (none change substance):** (1) brief said `calcStateVote phaseRunners.ts:3685` ‚Äî the function signature actually starts at `:3684` (off-by-one). (2) brief implied `src/pages/NewGameScreen.tsx:25` ‚Äî the file is `src/components/NewGameScreen.tsx:25` (line correct, directory differs). (3) brief said the entry phase is hardcoded at `phases.ts:65-67` ‚Äî it is actually hardcoded in each SCENARIO builder (`scenario1772.ts:73`, `scenario1856.ts:153`); `phases.ts:64-66` is the `shouldRunPhase` 2.1.1 first-turn-draft special-case (related, not the entry-phase source). (4) brief said `scenario1856.ts:124-193` ‚Äî the pre-seat + game block is ~120-195 (phaseId is the `:153` line). (5) brief said `resolveIdeologyShift phaseRunners.ts:704-794` ‚Äî `:704` is `factionCenter`; `resolveIdeologyShift` itself starts at `:768` (the `704-794` span correctly brackets BOTH functions). All five are citation hygiene only ‚Äî every claimed BEHAVIOR is confirmed.

> **‚òÖ‚òÖ SEQUENCING RECOMMENDATION FOR THE ROADMAP-PLANNER (batch-56 deltas) ‚Äî top-of-queue framing UNCHANGED: QW0 ‚Üí K0/#296 ‚Üí K2 ‚Üí K3/K4 + scenarioBoot ‚Üí E1.** This batch adds NO keystone and re-sequences nothing at the top; it is mostly DESIGNER-DECISIONS + bounded additions/foundational-prereqs onto already-shipped engines.
> 1. **‚òÖ‚òÖ TWO DESIGNER-DECISIONS must route to the designer BEFORE any build ‚Äî they are the headline. (A) The SC AUTO-CONFIRM vs ALWAYS-VOTE fork (debt #225)** is unresolved in `70a67176` (weight leans always-vote) and is EITHER/OR-coupled to the automated court-DECISIONS (#270) ‚Äî decide them TOGETHER; the always-vote BUILD then rides #66 (the cabinet committee‚Üífloor vote) and the 2.3 confirmation rebuild (#275), NOT near-term. **(B) The post-1772 START-PHASE-ENTRY A/B/C (debt #228)** is a poll (leans B); the shipped 1856 hybrid matches NEITHER A nor B, so the ruling + the hybrid reconcile fold into the EXISTING scenarioBoot keystone (K3/K4 + scenarioBoot, #86/#115/#186) as one more knob ‚Äî not a new keystone.
> 2. **‚òÖ #326 (SC age / Judicial seating gate, debt #226) is the ONE clean near-term FIX this batch ‚Äî schedule it behind K0/#296.** It is a SMALL bounded patch to the live auto-pick under-age/extremist bug: add an age floor + Judicial-keyed eligibility table to the justice-appointment filter (`phaseRunners.ts:3661`). It is DISTINCT from the #225 vote fork (this is seating-eligibility, that is the vote) and pairs with #66's compromise-candidate rule to fully fix the ‚òÖ7 bug. Behind K0 because any "penalty under N" form is a roll.
> 3. **‚òÖ The PARTY-SHIFT INEVITABILITY ENGINE is already SHIPPED (`factionCenter` + `resolveIdeologyShift`) ‚Äî do NOT plan it as new work.** Only its GATING is unbuilt (debt #227): the later-era `eligibleIdeologies` matrix columns (#4 ‚Äî this is the 3rd witness), the "can be independent" draft-party tag, and the restriction settings toggle (#171/#263). All three RIDE the existing #4 draft-profile epic (b52 #202's generalize-the-eligible-set work) ‚Äî not a standalone item, not near-term.
> 4. **‚òÖ #325 (split-state pol-assignment, debt #229) RIDES the alt-states / `expansionStates` work + the #295 era-aware draft pool ‚Äî and is GATED on the split alt-states existing as real states + K0/#296 (the seeded assignment die).** Do not start it until UC/LC + the 4 TX splits exist as states with a `parentState`/`splitOf` field; then the per-draft-class die (CA 50/50, TX quartiles) + the ‚â•4-core-office fill are a bounded M. Use `src/rng.ts`, NOT `Math.random`. Same minimize-generated-pols design family as the b53 #313 rejection.
> 5. **‚òÖ‚òÖ The GENDER ATTRIBUTE (#238, debt #230) is a FOUNDATIONAL demographic prereq ‚Äî flag it EARLY, exactly like the b55 #319 backroom reconciliation.** It is small but MODEL-TOUCHING (the core `Politician`/`ImportedDraftee` types + the dataset generator + a save migration), and it BLOCKS the entire suffrage gate. Once it exists, the per-state women's-suffrage flag is a NEW #258 predicate class and the ‚àí5 rides the `calcStateVote` traitBonus channel. INERT until a 1916+ era/scenario ships (rides #92/#301; couples to the existing DH-14 era-aware-bill debt). Schedule the field with the other foundational type-touches; defer the suffrage GATE to the 1916+ era unit.
> 6. **‚òÖ K0/#296 is RE-CONFIRMED as the gate for the roll-based bits this batch:** the #326 under-age penalty roll (debt #226) and the #325 per-draft-class assignment die (debt #229). Keep QW0 ‚Üí K0/#296 ‚Üí K2 ‚Üí K3/K4 + scenarioBoot ‚Üí E1; nothing here re-orders the top of queue.
> - **‚òÖ One-line for the planner: keep QW0 ‚Üí K0/#296 ‚Üí K2 ‚Üí K3/K4+scenarioBoot ‚Üí E1; this batch adds NO keystone ‚Äî its headline is TWO DESIGNER-DECISIONS (SC auto-confirm-vs-always-vote #225, coupled to court-decisions #270, build rides #66/#275; post-1772 start-phase A/B/C #228, folds into the scenarioBoot keystone), ONE clean near-term fix (#326 SC age/Judicial seating gate #226, behind K0), ONE foundational prereq (#238 gender attribute #230, flag early like b55 #319), and TWO rides-existing-epic items (#227 party-shift gating on the #4 draft-profile work ‚Äî the inevitability ENGINE is already SHIPPED; #229 split-state on the alt-states + #295 + K0 work).**

This is a DESIGNER DECISION (ratify the forum 60/40 model OR ratify the shipped faction-center model) ‚Äî it BLOCKS the #247 geo-ideology data layer (the 60%-target needs a per-state preferred-ideology field that does NOT exist; only `bias: number` `types.ts:1324`, grep `preferredIdeology|geoIdeolog` = 0) and, if the forum model wins, is DOWNSTREAM of K0/#296 (the D-100√ó3 rolls draw seeded RNG) + needs the enthusiasm‚Üíshift wiring (un-built ‚Äî `Enthusiasm` `types.ts:1415` is read only by elections `:3696,3709`). ‚òÖ Second: #293 faction-difficulty is a SIZEABLE MODEL ADDITION (no faction√óera favorability term in `calcStateVote` `:3709`; `Faction.personality` is a 3-bucket `'LW'|'Center'|'RW'` `types.ts:1297` with no Red1‚ÄìRed5 axis; the `Era` enum's 4 values `types.ts:1337` can't represent Reconstruction/Gilded/New-Deal/post-1974) ‚Äî it DEPENDS on the era-granularity reframe (#92) + a Red1‚ÄìRed5 axis, NOT near-term. ‚òÖ Third: #317 (faction-leader-only-presidential-candidate NOT enforced ‚Äî `runPhase_2_9_1_Primaries` `:3725-3749` takes any command‚â•2 party member 35-80, no `factionLeaderOf`/Celebrity gate) is a SMALL code change BUT decision-gated (needs a human ruling that the rule is canonical, not table-shorthand). ‚òÖ Fourth: the modern catalog (#169/#206/#270/#316) is blocked by the structural no-modern-scenario prerequisite; #270's N-groups-per-ruling is a SMALL type-widening (`SupremeCourtCase.effect?` `types.ts:1555` ‚Üí a Yea/Nay LIST), the new Environmentalists/Big-Business interest groups are small enabling adds (absent from `InterestCardId`/`LobbyCardId` `types.ts:310-320`), #316 is author-time-only (no code surface). ‚òÖ Fifth: #318 inter-party cooperation REWARDS is a real new mechanic (legislation passes by tallies only; must model DEFECTION); #315 draft skill-tie tiebreak is small (`pickBestForFaction` `:33-53` scores PV+ideo only); the Era-Evo "corporate flavor" line is content-engine-adjacent (model corps as `{homeState, industrySector}` TAGS, NOT an entity ‚Äî `EraEvent` `types.ts:1466` has no `isFlavor`/category field; > **‚òÖ CODE-VERIFICATION RESULTS (every brief pin confirmed at `file:line`; I am the build-state authority ‚Äî NO contradictions to flag this batch: all 5 digests, game-master ¬ß30.44, and gap rows #314-#318 are accurate against `src/` HEAD):**
> - **‚òÖ‚òÖ #314 CPU ideology-shift MOVEMENT rule ‚Äî the SHIPPED `2.1.5` system CONFIRMED + the per-axis DIVERGENCE confirmed.** `resolveIdeologyShift` (`phaseRunners.ts:767-809`) steps `p.ideology = to` where `to` comes from `ideologyShiftOdds(... center ...)` ‚Üí `stepToward(fromIdx, actorCenter)` (`:740-741,:752-753`) and `center = factionCenter(snap, actorFactionId)` (`:773`) ‚Äî **TARGET = actor faction's mean-ideology center, NOT state-preferred(60)/party-enthusiasm(40).** Odds: `IDEOLOGY_SHIFT_ODDS.attempt = { self: 0.65, opposed: 0.15, ffRisk: 0.5 }` (`types.ts:255`) + CPU gates `cpu: { selfGate: 0.3, selfBudget: 3, opposedGate: 0.1, opposedScan: 10 }` (`:261`) ‚Äî **30%/65% & 10%/15%, NOT 20%-via-D100√ó3.** Cap `if ((counts[actorFactionId] ?? 0) >= IDEOLOGY_ATTEMPTS_PER_TURN) return null` (`:778`), `IDEOLOGY_ATTEMPTS_PER_TURN = 5` (`types.ts:263`) ‚Äî **the STALE "5", not canonical 3.** Two-faced: `if (kind === 'opposed' && chance(ffRisk)) { p.flipFlopperPenalty += 1 }` (`:790-792`) ‚Äî an **OFFENSIVE flip-flop INFLICTED on the victim on success**, NOT a self-avoidance gate. Passive drift (`:886-930`) rates `drift: { faction: 0.08, stateBias: 0.04, stateBiasMin: 1.0, residual: 0.01 }` (`types.ts:254`) toward faction-center / state-`bias`-sign ‚Äî the closest "D100√ó3, one move" SHAPE analog but per-pol drift, not the CPU action.
> - **‚òÖ #314 data layer + enthusiasm wiring ‚Äî CONFIRMED ABSENT.** `grep preferredIdeology|leastPreferred|mostPreferred|geoIdeolog|stateIdeology src/` ‚Üí **ZERO.** `interface State` (`types.ts:1318-1335`) carries only `bias: number` (`:1324`) ‚Äî a single scalar (positive = red, `calcStateVote` `baseLean` `:3697`), no most/least-preferred ideology pair ‚Üí no data home for the 60%-target (sharpens #247). `Enthusiasm` (`types.ts:1415`) is read by `calcStateVote` (`:3696,:3709`) + the EnthusiasmPage meter UI but NO shift/drift path consults it ‚Üí the 40%-target is un-wired.
> - **‚òÖ #293/#92 faction-difficulty ‚Äî CONFIRMED no surface.** `calcStateVote` (`phaseRunners.ts:3685-3713`) score = `50 + baseLean*5 + partyPref*5 + enthusiasm*2 + pv*0.1 + factionBias + traitBonus + (Math.random()-0.5)*8` (`:3709-3711`) ‚Äî `baseLean = state.bias` (per-state STATIC), **NO faction-ideology √ó era favorability term**; era enters only via `applyTraitElectionBonus(..., { era, ... })` (`:3708`). `electionFactionBias` (`:1539-1561`) is card-driven (clamped ¬±3), no era/ideology weight. `Faction.personality: 'LW' | 'Center' | 'RW'` (`types.ts:1297`) ‚Äî **3-bucket, NO Red1‚ÄìRed5 axis**; `Faction` (`:1293-1304`) has no `difficulty`/`favorability`/per-era field (the only `difficulty` is the Rev-War battle tier `types.ts:1363`). `Era` = `'independence'|'federalism'|'nationalism'|'modern'` (`types.ts:1337`) ‚Äî **the calibration's eras (Reconstruction/Gilded/New-Deal/post-1974) CANNOT be expressed.** Lobby acquisition gate `ALIGNMENT_RULES.interestCardBucket[proxy] === bucket` (`:1798-1801`) ‚Äî coarse 3-bucket match, no Red1-vs-Red5 ease; modern lobby names are interest-group STRINGS `WallStreet`/`BigOilGas`/`BigAg`/`RWMedia`/`BigTech`/`MilitaryIndustrial` (`factions1856.ts:24-31`), NOT `LobbyCardId` values (`types.ts:316-320` = 1772/1856 historical set only).
> - **‚òÖ #317 faction-leader presidential gate ‚Äî CONFIRMED NOT enforced.** `runPhase_2_9_1_Primaries` (`phaseRunners.ts:3725-3749`) filters `p.partyId === partyId && !deathYear && !retiredYear && age >= 35 && age <= 80 && command >= 2` (`:3729`), ranks `pvCache + command*5 + totalBonus` (`:3733`) ‚Äî **NO `factionLeaderOf` gate, NO `Celebrity`-trait gate.** The faction-leader machinery EXISTS (`Politician.factionLeaderOf` `types.ts:1290`; the 2.2.3 leader election `:1969-2018`) but is **NOT referenced anywhere in the primary** ‚Äî confirmed unwired to candidacy.
> - **‚òÖ #318 / #315 ‚Äî CONFIRMED.** Legislation passes by vote tallies (no cross-aisle logrolling reward/defection model anywhere); Speaker/PPT auto-appointed to the top-PV majority member (`runPhase_2_2_1` `:1866-1888`) ‚Äî no contest/concession (#318). `pickBestForFaction` (`:33-53`) scores `p.pvCache + (ideoMatch ? 25 : 0) + (eligIdeos?.includes ? 50 : 0)` and `scored.sort(...)` (`:45-52`) ‚Äî **no skill-tie tiebreak, no admitted-state check**; territory-origin draftees implicitly exist (`recordDraftPick` `:55-75` retains `state` with no admitted-state filter) so #315's rule is satisfiable (#315).
> - **‚òÖ #270/#169/#206/#316 modern content ‚Äî CONFIRMED.** Only `scenario1772.ts` (`:69`) + `scenario1856.ts` (`:149`) exist; the `modern` `Era` value (`types.ts:1337`) has **no scenario** (#169/#206). Court `runPhase_2_5_3_Court` (`:3397-3415`) = `chance(0.5)`/turn, 4 hardcoded strings, justice-headcount, ¬±0.1 `partyPreference`. `SupremeCourtCase` (`types.ts:1548-1556`) has a **single `effect?: EraEventResponseEffect`** (`:1555`) ‚Äî needs a Yea/Nay LIST for WV-v-EPA's N-groups-per-ruling (#270); `pendingCourtCases` seeded `[]`, never read. `InterestCardId` (`types.ts:310-314`) + `LobbyCardId` (`:316-320`) have **no Environmentalists, no Big Business** (only the `planet` national meter `:1399-1407` is green-adjacent) ‚Äî both genuinely new (#316/#206). `EraEventResponseEffect` (`:1448-1457`) carries `meters?`+`interestGroups?`+`enthusiasm?` ‚Äî the dual-payload channels exist; the case SHAPE + the new groups are missing. No gov/pres-action system (`grep govAction|presAction|executiveAction src/` = 0). #316 inclusion bar = author-time, no code surface.
> - **‚òÖ #221/#248/#294/#261 Era-Evo flavor ‚Äî CONFIRMED 0% shipped.** `EraEvent` (`types.ts:1466-1477`) is a single uniform shape (`id/year/title/description/responses/decider/...`) with **NO `isFlavor` flag, NO category/subtype field**; `grep isFlavor|flavorEvent|corporation|companyId|industrySector src/` = **ZERO**. `State.industries: Record<string, number>` (`:1328`) keys are ad-hoc (shipping/finance/tobacco/cotton/manufacturing ‚Äî no steel/oil/auto/tech/media sector taxonomy). ‚öÝ The #294 TERMINOLOGY COLLISION is real: "era-evo" means an industry/lobby UNLOCK SCHEDULE on #294 vs a FLAVOR-EVENT CATEGORY in `f1209123` ‚Äî same label, different construct; keep distinct when building.
