> **‚òÖ CODE-VERIFICATION RESULTS (every brief pin confirmed at `file:line`; I am the build-state authority ‚Äî ONE gap-ID MIS-CITATION to flag at the gate, plus all 5 digests' shipped-state claims re-confirmed):**
> - **‚òÖ‚òÖ SECESSION / CIVIL-WAR / RECONSTRUCTION engine ‚Äî ~0% shipped, CONFIRMED.** `State.isSlaveState: boolean` (`types.ts:1329`) is WRITTEN at statehood (`isSlaveState: t.region === 'South' || t.region === 'Border'`, `phaseRunners.ts:3175`) and **read by NO secession/CW logic** (only the display `StatesPage.tsx`) ‚Äî confirms #288's ships-but-unread. `State.region` (`types.ts:1322`) is a coarse fixed enum (`'Northeast'|'Midwest'|'South'|'West'|'Border'|'Caribbean'|...`) with **NO "Upper South"/"Deep South"/"Southwest"** granularity the politician-secession stay-% table needs (#58). The ONLY secession mechanic that ships is the PR6 **"Secession Winter" EVENT** ‚Äî cabinet-officer defection rolls + a meter-band multiplier (`secessionWinterBand` `phaseRunners.ts:3001`, defections `:2974`) that, on N‚â•2 defectors, injects `out.startWar = { name:'American Civil War', against:'Confederate States' }` (`:2980-2981`). That war becomes a **NAME-ONLY generic `War`** in `snap.wars` via `applyEffect` (`phaseRunners.ts:3240-3253`) ‚Äî `{ id, name, enemy, startYear, warScore:0, generals, battles:[] }`, **no battle engine, no theaters, no two-theater win condition.** The actual battle engine `revolutionaryWar.ts` is **single-front** (`groundWinsNeeded`/`currentGroundWins`, win/loss at `:254-263`), runs ONLY on `game.revolutionaryWar` (gated `phases.ts` 2.7.2), and the `RevolutionaryWar` type (`types.ts:1371-1387`) has **NO `theater`/`isCivilWar` field**. The loyalty traits **`Union Loyalist`/`Southern Unionist` are ABSENT** from the `Trait` union (`types.ts:62-117` = 55 traits); `Provincial` exists ONLY as an election-modifier (`types.ts:107`, bands `:916-922`), NOT the POST-9 "100% secession-or-loyalty by region" rule. `grep -rE 'readmission|[Rr]econstruction|militaryDistrict|secessionConvention' src/` ‚Üí **ZERO** ‚Äî no Reconstruction phase, no readmission plans, no #324 citizenship-strip ladder.
> - **‚òÖ‚òÖ CPU PRESIDENTIAL-CONVENTION system ‚Äî ~0% shipped, CONFIRMED.** 2.9.1 Primaries (`runPhase_2_9_1_Primaries` `phaseRunners.ts:3725-3749`) filters each party to `command >= 2 && age 35-80`, sorts ONCE on `pvCache + command*5 + traitBonus`, and **picks the top one** ‚Äî no factions-running-candidates, no delegates, no nominators, no minor/celebrity path. 2.9.2 Conventions is a **one-line log stub** ‚Äî `engine.ts:69`: `case '2.9.2': addLog(... 'Party conventions ratify the primary winners.'); return {};`. `grep` of `src/types.ts` for `delegate/ballot/momentum/darkHorse/presidentialPromise/threshold/keynote/platform/runningMate` ‚Üí **nothing** (only Continental-Congress delegates + the `ConstitutionalConvention`). `vicePresidentId` (`types.ts:1568`) is **never SET by the election flow** ‚Äî only read for display + cleared on VP-vacate (`phaseRunners.ts:2450`); the pres-general (`:3752`) runs a head-to-head EV race with NO running mate and resets the cabinet to null post-election. ‚òÖ `pendingConvention` is the CONSTITUTIONAL Convention (`constitutionalConvention.ts`), a wholly separate founding-era system ‚Äî do NOT conflate.
> - **‚òÖ STAT-SYSTEM reconciliation ‚Äî CONFIRMED.** `SkillKey` (`types.ts:24-41`) = **7 keys** `admin/legislative/judicial/military/governing/backroom` vs canon's **6 abilities** (Backroom is a `CareerTrack` `:50`, `TRACK_SKILL.Backroom='backroom'` `:202`, NOT a 0-5 ability) ‚Üí code has 7 of the canonical 6 (#319; `command` is a separate `Politician.command` field `:1281`). `Expertise` (`types.ts:182-192`) = **19 tags, EXACT match** to canon (#320); all 3 canonical USES wired but `EXPERTISE_IDEOLOGY_LEAN` carries a lean on only **3 of 19** tags (`types.ts:421-425`, faction-center calc `phaseRunners.ts:717-718`), the re-elected-governor expertise grant is **unimplemented**, and the gov-action-gating USE has **no consumer** (no gov-action system). `Trait` union = **55** (`types.ts:62-117`) vs canon **57** ‚Äî a DIFFERENT roster (41 shared / 15 canon-only incl. `Easily Overwhelmed` [referenced in ability-loss design but absent from the union ‚Äî a dangling ref], `Lawful/Cop`, `Southern Unionist`; 14 code-only) (#216/#320). `LobbyCardId` (`types.ts:316-320`) = **15 scenario-flavored lobbies**, `LOBBY_EXPERTISE` (`:373-391`) keys only **7 of 15** vs the canonical 18 modern lobbies (#321). Cabinet: `OfficeType` (`types.ts:1111`) + `cabinetSeatsForYear` (`:1196`) ship the Big-4 + Navy/Interior/PMG/General/Admiral/Ambassador but MISS the Cabinet-Level tier (FBI/CIA-DNI/Fed-Chair/UN-Amb/NSA), the special non-meter Ministers (Indian Sovereignty / Confederate States / Northern Confederacy / Western America), and Key Advisor; confirmation is auto-scored, not a Committee/Floor vote (#66).
> - **‚òÖ 1892 + CABINET-ENTHUSIASM ‚Äî CONFIRMED.** No 1892/Progressivism scenario ‚Äî only `scenario1772.ts:69` + `scenario1856.ts:149`; the `Era` enum = 4 values `'independence'|'federalism'|'nationalism'|'modern'` (`types.ts:1337`), no Gilded/Progressive band (#92/#301). `Enthusiasm` (`types.ts:1415-1418`) = per-IDEOLOGY‚Üíparty (`{ [ideology]: { BLUE; RED } }`, ‚àí5..+5), built once at scenario start, moved ONLY by era-event effects ‚Äî there is **NO per-FACTION enthusiasm value** (`Faction` `types.ts:1293-1303` has no enthusiasm field), **NO interest-group-card‚Üífaction binding for enthusiasm, NO appointment-tier 20/10/5% roll** anywhere in the engine (#124). No rejected-confirmation‚ÜíControversial+pref-shift branch (`grep reject/Controversial` in `src/engine` finds only resolver-bool comments) (#199).
> - **‚òÖ AUTOMATION / LINGERING ‚Äî CONFIRMED.** `runPhase_2_5_1_Lingering` (`phaseRunners.ts:3260`) does **part (1) ONLY** ‚Äî cabinet-skill-driven per-meter drift + per-seat expertise bonus + per-trait modulation + national-debt update; **NO volatility, NO tax/tariff decay, NO meter‚Üímeter cross-effects** (the designed 4-part spec is 3 parts short, #134). `runPhase_2_2_2_Committees` (`phaseRunners.ts:1894`) grants the **4 chairs** command+1/legislative+1/expertise but has **NO small-chance +1-ability roll, NO per-member (non-chair) pass** ‚Äî though the chance-gated +1 PRIMITIVE exists in career-track `rollThreshold` (`phaseRunners.ts:299`), committee just doesn't use it (#322). No CPU bill-SELECTION scoring formula (#323, distinct from #74's vote heuristic). `src/rng.ts:1-5` still uses `Math.random` (un-seeded) ‚Äî every roll-based spec above is gated on K0/#296.
> - **‚öÝ ONE GAP-ID MIS-CITATION TO FLAG AT THE GATE (game-mechanics ¬ß30.45 sub-headers):** the ¬ß30.45.2 header titles the CPU-convention spec **"(#319)"** and the ¬ß30.45.4 header titles the cabinet-enthusiasm calculator **"(#322)"**. Per the canonical gap-log (`game-context.md:1332-1336`), **#319 = the backroom 7th-ability discrepancy** and **#322 = the committee +1-ability roll** ‚Äî NOT the convention spec / cabinet-enthusiasm. The CPU-convention spec sharpens the EXISTING **#185** (+#71/#72/#181/#183/#20/#74/#10); the cabinet-enthusiasm calculator sharpens the EXISTING **#124** (+#199). The ¬ß30.45 intro at `game-mechanics.md:24001` itself lists the correct sharpen-IDs (#185 / #124), so the two child sub-headers are internally inconsistent with both the intro and the gap-log ‚Äî a header-only mis-label, not a content error. I use the gap-log numbering throughout this block.

> **‚òÖ‚òÖ SEQUENCING RECOMMENDATION FOR THE ROADMAP-PLANNER (batch-53 deltas) ‚Äî top-of-queue framing UNCHANGED: QW0 ‚Üí K0/#296 ‚Üí K2 ‚Üí K3/K4 + scenarioBoot ‚Üí E1.** This batch adds NO keystone and re-sequences nothing at the top; its single most-valuable output is a RECONCILIATION that REMOVES a would-be build. Explicit calls:
> 1. **‚òÖ‚òÖ THE #307/#308-vs-¬ß29.5/#62/#191 RECONCILIATION (the top call) ‚Äî #308 is NOT a standalone House-representation build; it FOLDS into the already-tracked #62/#191/¬ß29.5 cluster.** The census thread's flat **3/2/1 influential-Rep count is the Apr-2022 ORIGIN spec, SUPERSEDED by the `housepoll` 1-10 EV-derived ladder already documented in ¬ß29.5** (#219/#55). Code-verified the mapping: 3.0.24's (EV‚àí2)√∑count vote-power = ¬ß29.5's `floor(stateUSReps/focusReps)` + remainder‚Üíhighest-`legis` (one formula generalized off the ladder); 3.0.24's ahistorical-seat bias (add 25/25/25/25, subtract protect-the-bias-seat) = the #191 deviant-bias engine; the single weighted delegation vote = #62. **#307's Big/Med/Small is the EARLY size-label form of the ¬ß29.5 ladder, NOT a net-new state-size primitive** ‚Äî build it AS the ladder, not a parallel 3-bucket class. So the planner should NOT mint a new #307/#308 House-representation epic: route the count to ¬ß29.5, the per-seat bias to #191, the delegation vote to #62. The ONLY genuinely-new deltas this thread adds are the **era-gated House CAP** (post-1929 `Cap + states√ó2`, DC/Moon/Mars-excluded reconciliation ‚Äî no const today) and the **output-consumer WIRING** (the census recompute must DRIVE the ¬ß29.5 ladder + #191 seats each decade, which today don't recompute at all) ‚Äî both rolled into debt #207.
> 2. **‚òÖ The CENSUS ENGINE internal order REFINES b52's: state-population primitive (#305, debt #206) ‚Üí census recompute (#34) ‚Üí state-size (#307) ‚Üí House-representation (#308 = the ¬ß29.5 ladder).** #305 is the FOUNDATIONAL prerequisite (established b52 ‚Äî `State` has no `population` at all); the #34 recompute (debt #207) is downstream of it; #307 size-classification is a derived output of the recompute (build it AS the ¬ß29.5 ladder per call 1); #308 House-representation is the ¬ß29.5/#191/#62 cluster the recompute WIRES into. ‚òÖ The census PROBABILISTIC rolls ‚Äî the Step-3 10%/25% industry %s and the ahistorical-seat 25/25/25/25 bias ‚Äî sit behind **K0/#296** (seeded RNG) for reproducibility/tunability. The era-gated 435 House cap is a NEW const + reconciliation step inside the recompute. Large PV impact (EV drives the EC tally) ‚Äî the recompute must run BEFORE the election phase reads `electoralVotes`.
> 3. **‚òÖ The GOV-ACTION OVERHAUL (#20, debt #209) is a real GREENFIELD epic ‚Äî and the designer's own "token afterthought" admission makes it the explicit priority, esp. for CPU.** Trait-gating (reads `gov.traits`) + industry‚Üîgovernor-EXPERTISE coupling (reads `gov.expertise` ‚Üí writes `State.industries` ‚Äî the FIRST governor‚Üíindustry path, and the SAME axis surfaced on #294) + an extra action + a tunable success rate. The axes (Expertise tags, `State.industries`) both ship + are unconnected. ‚òÖ The CPU selection pass is DOWNSTREAM of the deterministic-CPU/K0 work (the designer wants it "esp. for CPU"); the player-facing depth is contested (vcczar would automate SC/Gov/Mil). Sequence on the gov-action/CPU track, behind K0. The (B) expertise‚Üíindustry coupling overlaps #294's expertise axis ‚Äî build once.
> 4. **‚òÖ The SC CASE GENERATOR (#270, debt #210) is buildable on EXISTING types ‚Äî relatively SELF-CONTAINED.** `EraEventResponseEffect` already carries meters+interestGroups+enthusiasm, so the per-side payload needs no new effect plumbing ‚Äî the build is the GENERATOR + a Yea/Nay dual-effect case shape + `points` + a 25%-chance meter wrapper + populating/consuming the dead `pendingCourtCases`. ‚òÖ Its 25%-chance meter rolls + random name/meter/group draws are behind **K0/#296**. Mostly independent of the content-engine epic (it's a court-phase + a small type change). M.
> 5. **‚òÖ The SMALL items: #309 abolition‚Üíappointed-gov (M, content-engine-adjacent) + #310 event-target/name-template (S-M, touches the event type) + #311 tutorial (S-M, UI) + #312 state-history log (S, UI).** #309 rides the content engine (abolition as a Legis-Prop/amendment) + adds an appointed-governor path; #310 extends the event record (`target` + `{name}`-fill) + ties #258; #311/#312 are low-priority UI/onboarding-lane adds (no engine, no K0). None re-sequence the top of queue.
> 6. **‚òÖ #313 (territorial offices) is REJECTED ‚Äî NO build sequencing.** Logged so it is not re-proposed (designer "very vehement against it," 3-4√ó proposed). Re-open ONLY if all three deferral conditions are met. The three salvageable sub-gaps (pre-statehood territory gameplay; no statehood-admission ACT, ties #43; low-`domestic` independence trigger) are FLAGS, not builds ‚Äî do not pursue absent a fresh design ruling.

> **‚òÖ CODE-VERIFICATION RESULTS (every brief pin confirmed/corrected at `file:line`; I am the build-state authority ‚Äî and TWO brief/gap-log claims are HALF-WRONG, flagged ‚òÖCORRECTION below):**
> - **#297 leadership‚Üíelections is PV-ONLY ‚Äî CONFIRMED, with the shipping vehicle MORE built than the brief implied.** Faction-leader/party-leader are ROLES that grant PV: `FactionLeader: 8` / `PartyLeader: 14` office-prestige (`pv.ts:25-26`) + `p.factionLeaderOf != null ‚Üí +8` (`pv.ts:83`); fields are real (`Politician.factionLeaderOf` `types.ts:1290`, `Faction.leaderId` `:1301`, `Party.leaderId` `:1309`). PV reaches elections ONLY via `pv * 0.1` in `calcStateVote` (`phaseRunners.ts:3699,3709`) and `pvCache` in the primary scorer (`:3733`). NEITHER scorer reads `factionLeaderOf`/`leaderId` ‚Üí NO flat leadership-keyed term. NO focus-race concept (`grep focusRace|campaignFocus|donor` over `src/` = ZERO). **‚òÖ But the candidate shipping vehicle ‚Äî the additive trait-bonus channel ‚Äî is ALREADY FULLY WIRED, not just present:** `TRAIT_ELECTION_EFFECTS` (`types.ts:738`, 119 rows / 19 distinct traits), `TraitElectionRule` (`types.ts:727-736`), `TRAIT_ELECTION_BANDS = {SMALL:2,MEDIUM:4,LARGE:8}` (`types.ts:721-725`), `ElectionContext` (6 values, `types.ts:697-703`), and `applyTraitElectionBonus` (`electionEffects.ts:16`) is summed into BOTH the general scorer (`phaseRunners.ts:3708`) AND the primary scorer (`:3732`). So #297 reduces to ADDING table rows + (for focus-race) a per-faction designation/die input the channel doesn't yet read ‚Äî the engine plumbing exists.
> - **‚òÖCORRECTION #1 ‚Äî `Obscure` DOES have `TRAIT_ELECTION_EFFECTS` rows.** The brief AND gap-row #297's framing say "`Leadership`/`Obscure` have no trait-election-effects row." Verified: `Leadership` has ZERO rows (correct), but **`Obscure` has FIVE rows** (`types.ts:845-849`: presGeneral ‚àí4, presPrimary +2, house ‚àí4, governor ‚àí4, internalParty ‚àí2). So 10centjimmy's catch-all "not obscure AND Leadership" would have to RECONCILE with `Obscure`'s existing penalty rows, not assume a clean slate. (The game-master's ¬ß30.41.1 also loosely says "Obscure is in no leadership/election path" ‚Äî true only in the narrow sense that it isn't part of the LEADERSHIP path; it IS in the election-effects table.)
> - **NatSec + Guns content + Pres/Gov-Action primitives ‚Äî CONFIRMED 0% shipped.** `grep` over `src/` for govAction/governorAction/presAction/presidentialAction/policyGenre/legislationGenre/policyNode = **0 hits** (the gov-actions the digests cite are effectively unshipped ‚Äî confirmed; the only "gun" in `src/` is a `future-foot-gun` comment, `phaseRunners.ts:2303`). `Legislation` (`types.ts:1506-1520`) has only the 4-value `committee` (`Domestic|Foreign|Economic|Justice`, `:1513`) ‚Äî NO `subtype`/`policyGenre`/weapon-class/prereq field. The entire bill library is **8 generic `BILL_TEMPLATES`** (`phaseRunners.ts:3420-3429`), randomly drawn. So both catalogs corroborate the existing content-engine debt (b47 #172/#173, b49 #186) with TWO new genres.
> - **The counter/repeal predicate ‚Äî CONFIRMED ABSENT.** `Predicate` (`types.ts:1487-1504`) variants = `all`/`any`/`not`/`yearAtLeast`/`yearAtMost`/`eventCompleted`/`eventChose`/`meterAtLeast`/`meterAtMost`/`interestAtLeast`/`diplomacyAtLeast`/`warActive`/`warOutcome`/`stateAdmitted`/`officeControlledByPlayer`/`rosterHasSkill`/`flag`. There is **NO law-active / policy-passed / policy-repealed / agency-exists / policy-active-in-state-B leaf.** The Privacy Amendment ("disable most National Security legislation") is the corpus's clearest policy-DISABLES-policy demand; the Guns manufacturer-liability shield ‚Üî open-to-lawsuits is the opposed-counter specimen; state reciprocity needs a cross-state policy-active linkage `Predicate` entirely lacks (only `stateAdmitted` is cross-state-ish). All unrepresentable today. Folds straight into the b49 debt #186 active-policy-tracking keystone-within.
> - **The weapon-class taxonomy + the Drugs substance-class enum ‚Äî BOTH greenfield (‚òÖCORRECTION #2 to the brief's "sibling of THE Drugs substance-class enum").** `grep` for substanceClass/weaponClass/marijuana/narcotic over `src/` = **0 hits.** There is **no shipped Drugs substance-class enum** for the weapon-class taxonomy to be a sibling OF ‚Äî b49 debt #187 logged the Drugs substance-class enum as itself a DESIGNED, un-built sub-model. So the weapon-class taxonomy (handgun/long-rifle/shotgun/machine-gun/assault-weapon/ghost-gun/bump-stock/large-cap-magazine) is a sibling-BY-DESIGN of the (also-unshipped) Drugs substance-class enum ‚Äî both are NEW data tiers the content model would need, not one extending an existing one.
> - **The "leave gun laws to the states" DESIGN-ASSERTED-SHIPPED discrepancy ‚Äî CONFIRMED no gun-devolution policy exists.** The only "states-rights" construct in `src/` is the `StatesRights` **ideology card** (`IdeologyCardId`, `types.ts:323,361`; on factions in `factions1772.ts:16`, `factions1856.ts:5-6`) ‚Äî a faction-alignment tag, NOT a gun-specific (or any-genre) devolution POLICY. vcczar's POST 6 "The game has that" refers to the master design list / federalism ideology framing, not shipped Guns content. Log as DESIGN-ASSERTED-SHIPPED, build-UNVERIFIED.
> - **#300 firearms-tech combat tree ‚Äî CONFIRMED 0% built + DOWNSTREAM of K0.** The ONLY combat system is `revolutionaryWar.ts`. Ground target = `planning + generalScore(military√ó10) + (frenchAlliance ? 25 : 0) + diffMod`, then `win = d100() <= baseTarget + diffMod` (`revolutionaryWar.ts:212-218`). Naval target = `planning + adminScore(military√ó10) + 30`, `win = d100() <= target + 30` (`:186-192`). There is **NO weapon-tech/equipment term in either target**, and **NO military-tech tier field** on the `RevolutionaryWar` interface (`types.ts:1371`, 16 fields, none weapon/tech/tier) or anywhere on the snapshot. Both rolls use `d100()` from `rng.ts` ‚Üí so a tier-scaled success term MUST draw from the seeded generator (#296) to be tunable: **#300 is DOWNSTREAM of K0.** Cross-link the `145db158` weapon-testing power-bump axis ‚Äî design as ONE military-tech subsystem, not two (the firearms ladder is the deterministic-tier sibling of that stochastic power-bump).
> - **#74 CPU vote model ‚Äî CONFIRMED flat-affiliation, no faction-score term.** `voteCC` (`continentalCongress.ts:211-215`) decides each delegate aye/nay by a flat probability keyed on sponsor relationship: `sameFaction ? 0.92 : sameParty ? 0.6 : 0.2`, nudged by `cardVoteBias(...)` over the bill's interest cards (`:214`). NO computation of the bill's projected effect on the voting faction's SCORE ‚Äî identity is the proxy. The pro-historical bias exists ONLY in the era-event graph (`pickAIResponse` first-response default + `aiBias` lookup, `eraGraph.ts:85-103`) ‚Äî NOT in the Convention CPU: `preferredOption` (`constitutionalConvention.ts:102-123`) picks each article by `partyId` + `ideology` + flat `chance()` coin-flips, no historical weighting, no faction-score input. The historical-bias √ó faction-score COMPOSITION is undefined (the open question for the deterministic-CPU spec).
> - **#301 scenario structural flexibility ‚Äî CONFIRMED recorded-but-INERT.** `legislature: 'bicameral'|'unicameral'` IS stored (`constitutionalConvention.ts:8-15`, `ConstitutionalArticles.legislature` `types.ts:1390`, written by `applyConvention` `:137` with `?? 'bicameral'` fallback) but is **NEVER READ for gameplay** ‚Äî the only consumers are the `ConventionModal.tsx:5` UI label, the `applyConvention` write-back, and the CPU's own `preferredOption` choice (`:105`); **no House/Senate construction branches on it** (`grep unicameral` over `src/engine`/`src/pages` finds only the convention-setup option + the CPU choice ‚Äî zero gameplay reads). 1856 hardcodes `'bicameral'` (`scenario1856.ts:183`). `State.region` is a FIXED closed 10-value enum (`types.ts:1322`), statically assigned, consumed-not-configurable. `termLimits` offers only `two_terms|no_limits` (`constitutionalConvention.ts:60-66`) and the executive article tops out at elected/congressional/council (`:18-24`) ‚Äî no appointed-for-life option, and `termLimits` is not evidently enforced on presidential re-eligibility. Reproducible counterfactual runs need seeded RNG ‚Üí **#74/#301's CPU-faction-score + counterfactual half is DOWNSTREAM of K0/#296** and feeds the deterministic-CPU engine (#20, debt #191) already tracked.
> - **#298 draftee conformance ‚Äî CONFIRMED 0% built.** `recordDraftPick` (`phaseRunners.ts:55-79`) sets `p.factionId`/`p.partyId`/`p.draftedYear` but **NEVER mutates `p.ideology`** ‚Äî a drafted Moderate stays Moderate. The "draftees take on the card ideology" half of poll options #2/#3 is unbuilt.
> - **#298's gradual-drift cousin DOES ship.** `resolveIdeologyShift` (`phaseRunners.ts:767-794`) mutates `p.ideology = to` (`:789`) ‚Äî one graduated step toward the actor faction's center (`stepToward` `:741`), gated to phase 2.1.5, governed by `IDEOLOGY_SHIFT_ODDS`. So the design choice is INSTANT conformance-on-draft (#298) vs the shipped GRADUAL drift; the realism objection (instant is unrealistic) suggests the shipped drift may be the intended answer.
> - **#299 off-ideology vote penalty ‚Äî CONFIRMED 0% built.** `computePV` (`pv.ts:67-89`) has **NO ideology term** (skills √ó office-weights, command, traits, prestige, age ‚Äî nothing reads `p.ideology`); there is no per-pol vote-selection surface. The personal-ideology slot exists (`Politician.ideology` `types.ts:1266`) and is used in CC selection (`firstContinentalCongress.ts` `ideologyDistance`) ‚Äî but never touches elections/PV.
> - **#4/#171 draft-eligibility gate ‚Äî CONFIRMED partial (1772-inaugural ONLY).** `Faction1772.eligibleIdeologies: Ideology[]` (`factions1772.ts:7`, populated `:12-23`) is consumed by `getEligibleIdeologies` (`phaseRunners.ts:28`) + `pickBestForFaction` (`:33`) but ONLY when `isExpansion1772` (`scenarioId === '1772' && year === startYear`, `:38-39`); the soft two-tier filter is `strict = eligible.filter(...); pool = strict.length>0 ? strict : eligible` (`:41-44`). Every later draft-year + all of 1856 fall through to a soft `+25 PV` preference on the 3-bucket `personality` (`:45-52`) = option-#4-with-a-thumb-on-the-scale, NOT a hard gate. Base `Faction` (`types.ts:1293`) has no `eligibleIdeologies`. **‚òÖ TERMINOLOGY TRAP (heed):** this thread's "ideology card" = the 7-point PERSONAL-ideology slot (`Politician.ideology`), NOT the shipped `IdeologyCardId` named policy/alignment cards (`types.ts:322`) ‚Äî a distinct mechanic that reused the name.

> - **#296 (K0) ‚Äî CONFIRMED.** `src/rng.ts` exposes `d100()` (`:12`) and `rollVs(target): {roll, success, margin}` (`:16`) ‚Äî the EXACT d100-vs-threshold mechanic the CPU rules need ‚Äî but `rand()` (`:4`) and every wrapper (`d`, `d100`, `rollVs`, `pickWeighted`, `pick`, `shuffle`, `chance`, `uid`) call **`Math.random()`** directly. Header comment is the smoking gun: *"We use Math.random by default‚Ä¶ deterministic seeding can be plugged in later."* This is the existing debt #1/#2/#3 cluster's root; batch 50 ELEVATES debt #3 to the gating dependency for #20. Fixed **+25** French-alliance add on the ground target (`:214`, `war.frenchAlliance ? 25 : 0`); naval phase has a separate hardcoded **+30** "French edge" (`:191-192`). `navalWins` is incremented (`:202`) but **NEVER read into ground-battle success** (the `baseTarget` is `planning + generalScore + frenchAlliance` only) ‚Äî a dead counter for land battles. Difficulty mod is `+15`/`0`/`‚àí20` (`:216`), divergent sign/magnitude vs the forum Easy0/Mod‚àí10/Hard‚àí25.

> **‚òÖ‚òÖ‚òÖ Batch-49 change (5-digest batch ‚Äî `6801b9f4`-meter-interpretation/#292, `5261c8ff`-bailouts/#258+#237, `c9b97b58`-unfinished-presidencies/#283, `faf2d8b3`-drugs/#237+#221+#248, `b9651d6d`-VP-blocks/#194+#216+#279+#240+#15; I [tech-lead] re-pinned EVERY brief-listed claim against `src/` HEAD 2026-06-29 and am the build-state authority; SIX new debt rows #184-#189; updated gaps #292/#283/#258/#237/#221/#248/#262/#206/#66/#12/#124/#240/#216/#194/#279/#15; updated game-mechanics ¬ß30.39. NO new keystone, but #184 (#292) is a HEADLINE election-engine rework that RE-SCOPES E16 a THIRD time and folds debt #1/#71's `Math.random` die-fix in. ‚òÖ The HEADLINE is debt #184 (#292 election vote-score rework + meter‚Üíenthusiasm pipeline). Its KEYSTONE sub-part is the meter‚Üíenthusiasm DERIVATION at end-of-Lingering: it is the structural step the engine has never had, and it is UPSTREAM of #12 (bill‚Üímeter) and #124 (cabinet‚Üímeter) ‚Äî without it those two never reach elections. Second: debt #186 (#258) ‚Äî the content-PREDICATE extension (meter-BAND + policy-active/NOT-active + EVENT-active leaves + a `PolicyNode` content model), the b47 #172/#173 content-engine track's batch-49 corroboration from two new genres (Bailouts/Drugs).):**
> **‚òÖ CODE-VERIFICATION RESULTS (every brief pin confirmed/corrected at `file:line`; I am the build-state authority):**
> - **`calcStateVote` `phaseRunners.ts:3685-3723` ‚Äî CONFIRMED.** Per-candidate `score = 50 + baseLean*5 + partyPref*5 + enthusiasm*2 + pv*0.1 + factionBias + traitBonus + (Math.random()-0.5)*8`. State lean is `baseLean = ¬±state.bias` (CONTINUOUS/graduated, `:3697`), NOT a flat +5. `enthusiasm*2` (`:3696/:3709`) is applied UNCONDITIONALLY ‚Äî no state-ideology gate. **MISSING: incumbent +1, media-card +1, president's-party midterm ‚àí1** (grep `incumbentBonus|midterm|mediaCard|presParty` = 0 hits). Die at `:3711` uses raw `Math.random` (violates the deterministic-RNG rule; = debt #1, folds into K0).
> - **Meter‚Üíenthusiasm DERIVATION ‚Äî CONFIRMED ABSENT (the keystone gap).** `game.enthusiasm` is a STATIC authored table (`buildEnthusiasm()` per scenario, e.g. `scenario1772.ts:19`) mutated ONLY by era-event effects via `applyEffect` (`phaseRunners.ts:3229-3233`). `runPhase_2_5_1_Lingering` (`phaseRunners.ts:3260-3290`) drifts the 7 METERS but NEVER touches `enthusiasm`; nothing runs "at end of Lingering" to derive enthusiasm from meters. grep `refreshEnthusiasm|computeEnthusiasm|enthusiasmFromMeters|deriveEnthusiasm` = 0 hits. Meters and enthusiasm are decoupled.
> - **`State.preferredIdeology` ‚Äî CONFIRMED ABSENT (#292 subsumes it).** `State` (`types.ts:1318-1335`) has `bias: number` (`:1324`) only ‚Äî a single signed BLUE/RED scalar, NO ideology-preference field. So neither the enthusiasm-only-if-state-ideology GATE nor the +1 ideology-match bonus (75% design / guaranteed forum) can be expressed; the build has NEITHER variant.
> - **Era-gated QoL/Planet meters ‚Äî CONFIRMED INVERTED.** Both `quality` + `planet` are permanent `NationalMeters` fields (`types.ts:1399-1407`) seeded LIVE from turn 1: 1772 `quality -1, planet 5` (`scenario1772.ts:15-16`); 1856 `quality 0, planet 4` (`scenario1856.ts:14-15`). No unlock/era-gate. `NationalMeters` has 7 meters; the design's 8th "Party Pref" is the SEPARATE `game.partyPreference` scalar (NOT a meter).
> - **Assassination `src/data/anytimeEvents.ts:222-238` ‚Äî CONFIRMED (note path: `data/`, NOT `engine/`).** TWO templates, both `eras: ['nationalism','modern']`: `assassination-attempt-survived` (weight 2 ‚Üí `grantTrait Charismatic` + `pvBump +8`) and `assassination-killed` (weight 1 ‚Üí `death`) ‚áí implied ~67% survive-and-BUFFED / ~33% death. The **‚àí1-all-abilities WOUND branch is wholly absent** ‚Äî `AnytimeEventEffect` (`anytimeEvents.ts:21-28`) has `skillBump`/`commandBump` but NO uniform-all-abilities-wound kind. Era-varying via per-era `fireMult` (`ANYTIME_EVENTS_RULES.eraConfig`, `types.ts:1076-1085`) + per-template `eras[]`. Target selection is UNIFORM-random (no office/notability weighting); Hale/Frail NOT consulted for assassination. `grep -i pineapple src/` = 0 hits (no "Pineapple Primary").
> - **`economic` band reconcile ‚Äî CONFIRMED.** `NationalMeters.economic` is a RAW `number` (`types.ts:1401`); bands are UI-ONLY display strings in `Meter.tsx:15` = `Depression ¬∑ Severe Recession ¬∑ Recession ¬∑ Slowdown ¬∑ Sluggish ¬∑ Stable ¬∑ Growth ¬∑ Expansion ¬∑ Boom ¬∑ Strong Boom ¬∑ Roaring`. The authors' "Stagnant"/"Panic" are NOT in this scale ‚Äî author bands MUST compile to numeric `meterAtMost` thresholds. `Predicate` (`types.ts:1487-1504`) has only numeric `meterAtLeast`/`meterAtMost` ‚Äî NO meter-band, policy-active, or event-active leaf. `GraphFlagId` = `'loansEnabled' | 'warVictoryGuaranteed'` (`types.ts:1109`).

> **‚òÖ Boot-state correction up front: a single-tier `Politician.skills` (`types.ts:1271`) + single `command` (`:1281`) is the WHOLE shipped stat model ‚Äî there is NO `peakYear`, NO midway/peak/rookie tier on either `Politician` or `ImportedDraftee` (`:1780`), and the dataset pipeline (`scripts/legislatorsToDataset.mjs:319`) authors exactly ONE stat set per row. So the 1960 boot "everyone on rookie stats" failure (`3b46b086` HEADLINE 2) is structural: the multi-tier model the designer describes does not exist anywhere ‚Äî model, data, and boot-back-fill are all unbuilt. This is why #289 is the headline and why its dataset-authoring sub-part is the long pole.**

> **‚òÖ CODE-VERIFICATION RESULTS (I confirmed/corrected each brief-listed pin against HEAD ‚Äî file:line):**
> - **‚úì CONFIRMED ‚Äî `Politician` carries ONE `skills` block + one `command`; NO peak/midway/rookie tiers, NO `peakYear`.** `Politician` (`types.ts:1251-1291`): `skills: Skills` (`:1271`), `command: number` (`:1281`), `careerTrack`/`careerTrackYears` (`:1279-1280`). A `grep` for `peakYear|midway|midpoint|rookieStat` in `src/` = ZERO (every "peak" hit is "Speaker"/"speak"). `ImportedDraftee` (`types.ts:1780-1793`) likewise: one `skills` (`:1788`), one `command` (`:1789`). **The 3-tier model (#289) is wholly absent.**
> - **‚úì CONFIRMED ‚Äî `CAREER_TRACK_MAX_YEARS = 20` (`types.ts:236`) vs the thread's 16-yr "maxed".** In-game development IS built: the lingering pass advances `careerTrackYears += 2` and calls `rollThreshold` every 4 career-years up to the cap (`phaseRunners.ts:459-461`), plus mentor acceleration (`:466-467`). But the cap is **20**, while `3b46b086` POST 9 (Vols21, citing the sheet) says **"16 years which follows the career path logic"** for the boot back-fill (‚â•16 ‚Üí peak, 8-15 ‚Üí midway, <8 ‚Üí rookie). **RECONCILE before authoring the boot: 16-yr sheet figure vs 20-yr code cap.** Boot applies NO stat progression today (no age-based back-fill exists) ‚Äî only the in-game accrual path is built (#289 DELTA-1).
> - **‚úì CONFIRMED (data-side blocker for #289) ‚Äî the DATASET pipeline authors ONE stat set, no peak/midway fields.** `scripts/legislatorsToDataset.mjs:319` CSV header = `...,admin,legislative,judicial,military,governing,backroom,command,traits,expertise,party,wikiUrl,loyalty` ‚Äî a SINGLE stat block + single command, NO peak/midway/rookie columns, NO `gender`/`race`. `seedDataset.mjs` emits the same single-set shape (`legislatorsToDataset.mjs:97-110` tenure‚Üíone-skill-set heuristic). The shipped `public/standard-draft-classes.json` rows confirm it (one `skills`+`command` per row; grep for `"peakYear"`/`"gender"`/`"race"` = 0). **So #289 is not just a `Politician`-type change ‚Äî the author-time pipeline must be regenerated to emit midway+peak+peakYear per row. This is the LONG POLE.** (The `races` map in the script is election-RACE tallying, not ethnicity ‚Äî `:169-195`.)
> - **‚úì CONFIRMED ‚Äî procedural fallback generator is a flat uniform stub on `Math.random` (#290).** `runPhase_2_1_1_Draft` source precedence (comment `phaseRunners.ts:143-146`): imported ‚Üí standard ‚Üí random rookie. The fallback (`:177-238`) builds `factions.length*2 ‚âà 20` rookies with **15 hardcoded national first-names + 15 last-names** (`:181-182`), **uniform `pick(ideologies)`** over all 7 (`:186`), **random `pick(stateIds)`** (`:203`), **`partyId: null`** (`:205`), `interests: []` (`:217`), and **`Math.random`** for every roll (`:188-197`, `:203`, `:210`) ‚Äî a determinism violation vs CLAUDE.md (the seeded `src/rng.ts` is NOT wired into this legacy path). **NO per-state quota, NO party split, NO gender/race/region.** `State.region` EXISTS (`types.ts:1322`, read by relocation/Secession-Winter) but the generator never reads it. **NO `gender`/`race`/`region` field on `Politician` (`:1251`) or `ImportedDraftee` (`:1780`).**
> - **‚úì CONFIRMED + ‚òÖ CORRECTED the brief's debt hypothesis ‚Äî `game.nationalDebt` SHIPS but is NOT a `MeterKey`; the Predicate union has NO debt leaf (#258/#237 Credit/Debt).** `game.nationalDebt: number` (`types.ts:1573`) is scenario-seeded ($0 in 1772 `scenario1772.ts`, $32M in 1856 `scenario1856.ts:161`) and mutated in EXACTLY one place ‚Äî a passive turn-end paydown `nationalDebt = max(0, nationalDebt ‚àí meters.revenue¬∑$1.5M)` (`phaseRunners.ts:3376`). But `MeterKey = keyof NationalMeters` (`types.ts:1409`) = `revenue/economic/military/domestic/honest/quality/planet` ‚Äî **NO debt field**, so `meterAtLeast`/`meterAtMost` (`:1495-1496`) CANNOT gate on the debt, and the `Predicate` union (`:1487-1504`) has **no debt leaf at all**. The `dutch_recognition` era event (`eraEvents1772.ts:248`, "John Adams secures a large loan in Amsterdam") sets the `loansEnabled` graph flag (`phaseRunners.ts:3144`; `GraphFlagId = 'loansEnabled' | 'warVictoryGuaranteed'` `types.ts:1109`) ‚Äî a bespoke one-off, NOT a generic recognition predicate. The `revenue` meter HAS the named bands the laws want (Bankruptcy‚Ä¶Balanced‚Ä¶Surplus, display-only in `Meter.tsx:14`) but no predicate reads them. **So the brief's "no debt/budget state ships" is HALF-WRONG: the debt value + budget meter ship as raw numbers; what's missing is (a) the policy CONTENT layer and (b) a debt-queryable predicate leaf.**
> - **‚úì CONFIRMED ‚Äî CC has exactly 7 article votes, NO suffrage plank (#159).** `makeConvention` (`constitutionalConvention.ts:6-67`) builds votes for `legislature, executive, judiciary, slaveCompromise, amendmentProcess, presidentialEligibility, termLimits` ‚Äî seven, no women's-suffrage / voting-rights plank (`grep suffrage|women.*vote|right to vote` in `src/` = 0). The DomStab consequence has a ready write channel: `domesticStability?: number` is a first-class `EraEventResponseEffect` arm (`types.ts:1453`), and CC honors already grant command (`:158,:168`). **So #159 = add the 8th plank + wire its "enacted" branch to a `domesticStability` hit.**
> - **‚úì CONFIRMED ‚Äî `Ideology` is a fixed 7-point scale; `PartyId = BLUE|RED`; `Can be Independent`/`Can Party Flip`/`Independent` are NOT in the `Trait` union (#291/#279).** `Ideology` (`types.ts:5-12`) = `LW Populist‚Ä¶RW Populist` (7 values, `IDEOLOGY_ORDER` `:14`); **no non-ideological-populist axis or flag**. `PartyId = 'BLUE' | 'RED'` (`:3`) ‚Äî no INDEPENDENT value, no `caucus`/`switchYear`/`partySwitchDate` field anywhere. The `Trait` union (`:62-117`) has neither `Can be Independent` nor `Can Party Flip`; nearest is `Flip-Flopper` (`:98`), a NEGATIVE-electoral instability trait, semantically distinct. **#291 (ideology axis) is DEFERRED by the model's own author; #279 (can-flip/can-independent traits + Independent caucus + switch-date) is the interim trait/party-model work.**
> - **‚úì CONFIRMED ‚Äî CJCS / Joint-Chiefs / military-prep meter all absent; build ships 1772+1856 ONLY (#49 is modern-era-gated).** `grep jointChief|CJCS|chairman` = "Speaker" only; no military-prep meter. BUT the admin-on-appointment PRECEDENT the CJCS hybrid (POST 12) wants already exists: `OFFICE_ADMIN_GRANT` (`types.ts:597-605`) grants +1 admin per cabinet seat on appointment, layered via `ABILITY_EARN_RULES.cabinetConfirmAdmin` (`:565,:595`). So the "military skill + admin-gained-on-appointment" CJCS formula reuses a shipped pattern ‚Äî but the whole modern-era cabinet/officer apparatus (and the CJCS seat) is unbuilt. **#49 is gated on modern-era scenarios EXISTING.**
> - **‚úì CONFIRMED (Washington-Rule-can-fail, #153/#104) ‚Äî the rule is a normal contested election; nothing forces a win.** Presidential resolution (`phaseRunners.ts:3755-3814`) is a strict EV tally over `calcStateVote(...,'presGeneral')` ‚Äî a Washington-Rule-eligible candidate runs as a NORMAL candidate and can LOSE (matches `e86d8661`'s "first unsuccessful Washington Rule candidate," Arnold 4th). **So #153/#104's "Washington-Rule can fail" is ALREADY TRUE in code ‚Äî this is a verify/XS, not a build.** The eligibility GRANT (Military Leadership + Celebrity ‚Üí presidentially eligible) is the documented bootstrap (game-mechanics.md); the election itself was never a coronation.

> **‚òÖ‚òÖ‚òÖ Batch-47 change (5-digest batch ‚Äî `de64de1a`-currency, `5d7c29ee`-slavery, `9c383f22`-northern-secession, `d62ea397`-infrastructure, `1bf19872`-taxation; the digests code-verified most claims and I [tech-lead] re-pinned each against `src/` HEAD 2026-06-28; FIVE new debt rows #171-#175; updated gaps #258/#237/#221/#248/#262/#206/#66/#56/#58/#156/#284, NEW #288; updated game-mechanics ¬ß30.37. NO new keystone, NO re-sequence. ‚òÖ The HEADLINE is debt #171 (#288 slavery-flag UNIFICATION + flag-write effect channel + secession-eligibility input) ‚Äî a CONCRETE, CONTAINED, mostly-INDEPENDENT buildable that unblocks the slavery-GATED half of the Civil-War cluster; and debt #172 (#258 active-institution/active-policy Predicate class) ‚Äî the CONTENT-ENGINE KEYSTONE move, with the Currency tree as the implementable spec.):**
>
> **‚òÖ CODE-VERIFICATION RESULTS (every digest/¬ß30.37 pin confirmed or corrected at `file:line` ‚Äî I am the build-state authority):**
> - **`BILL_TEMPLATES` `phaseRunners.ts:3420-3429` = 8 GENERIC templates ‚Äî CONFIRMED.** The 8 are Tariff Increase / Tariff Reduction / Homestead Act / Internal Improvements / Naval Expansion / Fugitive Slave Act Strengthening / Personal Liberty Law / Pacific Railroad Bill. **ONLY `Tariff Increase` (`:3421`, `revenue:+1`) and `Tariff Reduction` (`:3422`, `revenue:-0.5`) touch the `revenue` meter** (Internal Improvements `:3424` and Pacific Railroad `:3428` touch `revenue` NEGATIVELY too as a build COST, but neither is a tax/currency instrument). **CONFIRMED: NONE of the ~37 Taxation / Currency / etc. genre rows from ¬ß30.37 exist** ‚Äî there is no income-tax, excise, tariff-schedule, gold-standard, greenback, national-bank, or coinage bill in the build. The 8 templates are picked at random (`pick(BILL_TEMPLATES)` `:3444`) with a flat `effects: EraEventResponseEffect` payload ‚Äî there is NO genre/subtype/prereq/effect-catalog dimension.
> - **`Predicate` union `types.ts:1487-1504` + `evalPredicate` `eraGraph.ts:12-47` = ERA-EVENT-ONLY ‚Äî CONFIRMED, NO 7 missing classes.** The 18-arm union is: `all`/`any`/`not` (combinators), `yearAtLeast`/`yearAtMost`, `eventCompleted`/`eventChose`, `meterAtLeast`/`meterAtMost`, `interestAtLeast`, `diplomacyAtLeast`, `warActive`/`warOutcome`, `stateAdmitted`, `officeControlledByPlayer`, `rosterHasSkill`, `flag`. **CONFIRMED ABSENT (all 7 ¬ß30.37 predicate classes):** (1) active-institution/active-policy (no program-state read), (2) named economic-meter BAND (`meterAtLeast`/`meterAtMost` read the RAW number ‚Äî see next pin), (3) program-state, (4) generic-war (`warActive` `:31` reads `revolutionaryWar?.active` ONLY ‚Äî RevWar-scoped, NOT a generic `wars[]` check), (5) slavery-flag (no per-state flag clause), (6) policy-chain, (7) sequential segment-built. The `flag` clause (`:45`) reads `snap.game.graphFlags?.[id]` ‚Äî a GAME-GLOBAL `Partial<Record<GraphFlagId, boolean>>` (`types.ts:1634`) with only TWO values (`GraphFlagId = 'loansEnabled' | 'warVictoryGuaranteed'`, `types.ts:1109`) ‚Äî NOT per-state, NOT policy-mutable from an effect.
> - **`economic` is a RAW number, NO named Panic/Depression bands ‚Äî CONFIRMED.** `NationalMeters` (`types.ts:1399-1407`) = `revenue, economic, military, domestic, honest, quality, planet`, all `number`. There is no band-classifier const, no Panic/Recession/Depression enum, no threshold table. The Currency tree's "economic-meter-band" predicate (¬ß30.37) would be the FIRST band abstraction over a meter.
> - **`warActive` is RevWar-only; Reconstruction-active is UNREPRESENTED ‚Äî CONFIRMED.** `evalPredicate`'s `warActive` (`:31`) and `warOutcome` (`:32`) both read `snap.game.revolutionaryWar?` exclusively. The generic `wars: War[]` array (with `startWar`/`endWar`) has NO predicate reader. There is NO `reconstructionActive` / occupation / martial-law state field anywhere (the b45 #154 martial-law-bias overlay is still unbuilt).
> - **‚òÖ #288 PINS ‚Äî all THREE disconnected slavery reps + the missing flag-write channel CONFIRMED:** (i) `State.isSlaveState: boolean` EXISTS (`types.ts:1329`) and is populated per-state (TN=`true` `states1856.ts:26`; DE=`true` `:14`), but is **INERT for secession** ‚Äî grep shows it is read only by display/data paths, never by the defection runner. (ii) `SLAVE_STATES_1856` (`types.ts:1152-1155`, the 15-state hand-rolled set) is used ONLY by John Brown event modulation (`phaseRunners.ts:2942`, `SLAVE_STATES_1856.includes(secWar.state)`). (iii) `runSecessionWinterDefections` (`phaseRunners.ts:3028-3054`) keys defection off **`state.region`** (`stateRow?.region ?? 'Northeast'` `:3037` ‚Üí `LOYALTY_REGION_BASE`), NOT either slavery rep ‚Äî the comment at `types.ts:1151` ("Secession Winter loyalty decay reads state.region directly, not this set") and at `:1322` (the `region` field is a multi-purpose enum) confirm the intent. So THREE disconnected representations of "is this a slave state" coexist (`isSlaveState` / `SLAVE_STATES_1856` / `region==='South'|'Border'`). **`EraEventResponseEffect` (`types.ts:1448-1457`) has NO flag-write channel** ‚Äî its arms are `meters`/`partyPreference`/`enthusiasm`/`interestGroups`/`domesticStability`/`diplomacy`/`startWar`/`text` ONLY; an era-event response CANNOT today set a per-state flag (e.g. "this state abolishes slavery"). This is the missing write half of #288.
> - **Static flavor cards flagged as CONTENT-MIGRATION targets (CONFIRMED static, not data-driven policy):** `Internal Improvements` (`phaseRunners.ts:3424`) + `Pacific Railroad Bill` (`:3428`) are hardcoded `BILL_TEMPLATES` rows; `Public Works Program` is a hardcoded era-event RESPONSE (`eraEvents1856.ts:72-73`, the Panic-of-1857 `r3` option, flat meter+IG payload); Crittenden is a FACTION (`factions1856.ts:12`) + a seed politician (`politicians1856.ts:58-59`), NOT a policy. None carry a genre/subtype/prereq ‚Äî they fold into the #237/#221 catalog when it lands.

> **‚òÖ‚òÖ‚òÖ Batch-46 change (5-digest batch ‚Äî `a938ac91`-gov-actions/#258, `b7acabd2`-House-incumbency/#287, `9f2ab25f`-Environment, `215f0676`-July-reel, `243dc711`-Sept-reel; the digests code-verified most claims and I [tech-lead] re-pinned each against `src/` HEAD 2026-06-28; NINE new debt rows #162-#170; updated gaps #258/#221/#237/#248/#262/#66/#206/#270/#273/#277/#34/#58/#157/#191/#250/#62/#20/#284, NEW #286/#287; updated game-mechanics ¬ß30.36. NO new keystone, NO re-sequence. ‚òÖ The HEADLINE is debt #162 (#258 governor-identity/trade Predicate clause) ‚Äî the highest-LEVERAGE content-engine-adjacent item this batch: a CONTAINED extension of the shipped `Predicate` union that UNBLOCKS scripted Gov-Actions AND era-gated legislation; and debt #163 (#287 +2 House incumbency) ‚Äî a near-term QW buildable NOW.):**
> **‚òÖ Civil-War baseline correction up front: the shipped war name is `'American Civil War'` (not `'Confederate States'`) ‚Äî `out.startWar = { name: 'American Civil War', against: 'Confederate States' }` (`phaseRunners.ts:2981`). `'Confederate States'` is the `against:` STRING. The whole Civil-War subsystem today = an ABSTRACTION (4-cabinet-seat loyalty defections + a 0.3‚Äì2.0 meter multiplier + a `startWar` flag, war FORCED at N‚â•2); the territorial/institutional DEPTH is designed-but-unbuilt ‚Äî see debt #165.**
>
> **‚òÖ CODE-VERIFICATION RESULTS (I confirmed/corrected each pinned claim against HEAD):**
> **(1) ‚úì CONFIRMED ‚Äî the Civil-War abstraction baseline ships exactly as digested.** `eraEvents1856.ts` carries `johnBrown1859` (`:82`, "John Brown's Raid on Harpers Ferry"), `southern-secession-threat` (`:107`), and `secession-winter` (`:136`). The resolver (`phaseRunners.ts`): `runSecessionWinterDefections` (`:3028-3054`) iterates the FOUR cabinet seats `[SecretaryOfTreasury, SecretaryOfWar, SecretaryOfInterior, SecretaryOfState]` (`:3029`), applies `LOYALTY_REGION_BASE √ó LOYALTY_IDEOLOGY_MULT` decay, and defects any seat whose post-decay `loyalty < LOYALTY_DEFECTION_THRESHOLD` (nulls the seat, vacates `currentOffice`, grants `'Traitor'`, logs), storing `event.secessionDefectionCount`. `secessionWinterBand` (`:3001-3016`) maps `(responseId, N, pres)` ‚Üí a **0.3‚Äì2.0 meter multiplier** (r1 Reinforce 0.3/0.6/1.0/1.5; r2 Diplomatic; r3 Acquiesce flat 2.0). The modulation site (`:2971-2983`) multiplies every meter delta by the band and, **when `N >= 2 && !out.startWar`, injects `out.startWar = { name: 'American Civil War', against: 'Confederate States' }`** (`:2980-2981`) ‚Äî civil war is unavoidable at 2+ defections regardless of response. `applyEffect`'s `startWar` branch (`:3240-3253`) pushes the `War`. **Baseline CONFIRMED; the only correction is the war NAME above.**
> **(2) ‚úì CONFIRMED ‚Äî `Predicate` (`types.ts:1487-1504`) has NO governor-identity/ideology/trait clause and NO diplomatic/trade-RELATIONSHIP clause.** The 18 variants are `all/any/not`, `yearAtLeast/yearAtMost`, `eventCompleted`, `eventChose`, `meterAtLeast/meterAtMost`, `interestAtLeast`, `diplomacyAtLeast` (a ONE-SIDED `{nation,value}` scalar gate ‚Äî NOT a bilateral trade relationship), `warActive`, `warOutcome`, `stateAdmitted`, `officeControlledByPlayer`, `rosterHasSkill`, `flag`. `evalPredicate` (`eraGraph.ts:12-47`) interprets exactly these ‚Äî no governor branch, no trade branch. **So both #258 clauses (a `governorIs`/`governorIdeology`/`governorTrait` family AND a trade/relationship clause) are genuine NEW union members + new `evalPredicate` cases. CONTAINED, additive.**
> **(3) ‚úì CONFIRMED ‚Äî `calcStateVote` has NO incumbency term (#287).** The score (`phaseRunners.ts:3709-3711`) is `50 + baseLean*5 + partyPref*5 + enthusiasm*2 + pv*0.1 + factionBias + traitBonus + (Math.random()-0.5)*8` ‚Äî one function for ALL `ElectionContext` values (`presGeneral`, `house`, ‚Ä¶); the `ctx` arg only steers the trait-bonus table (`applyTraitElectionBonus`), never adds an incumbency bonus. `'house'` is NOT special-cased anywhere in the score. **#287 = add an incumbency term to this function gated on `ctx === 'house'`. ‚öÝ DO-NOT-CONFLATE: `incumbencyAdvantage` (`types.ts:462-479`) lives inside `LEADERSHIP_RULES.eraConfig` (`types.ts:438`, the const that opens at `:438`) alongside `baseFireChance`/`ideologyTriggerWeight`/`patronageTriggerWeight` ‚Äî it is the FACTION-LEADER-CHALLENGE success config (`:2070` `clamp(0.5 + edge - cfg.incumbencyAdvantage/100, ‚Ä¶)`), UNRELATED to House elections.**
> **(4) ‚úì CONFIRMED ‚Äî `grep contingent|faithless` = ZERO in `src/`; the contingent-election RESOLVER is absent AND so is 3rd-party / faithless EC output (#62).** `runPhase_2_9_4`-class presidential resolution (`:3755-3814`) is a strict TWO-candidate blue-vs-red EV tally: per-state `calcStateVote(...,'presGeneral')`, `if (blue.pct > red.pct) blueEv += s.electoralVotes; else redEv += ‚Ä¶`, `winner = blueEv > redEv ? blueCand : redCand`. NO House-decides fallback, NO EC-tie handling, NO `electoralVotes:[...]` row for any 3rd ticket, NO faithless-elector subtraction. **NB: the Sept digest's "5 faithless electors / 3rd-party Everett ticket / sub-50% popular winner" (POST 19) is NARRATED / Sheets-SIM color ‚Äî line 67 describes the lived run, line 68 confirms the CODE is null. So faithless-elector and 3rd-party-EC output do NOT exist in shipped code; #62 is a fully designed-but-unbuilt subsystem (3 trigger paths + a House resolver).**
> **(5) ‚úì CONFIRMED ‚Äî no `govAction` system; `GovernorsPage.tsx` is READ-ONLY (#20).** `grep govAction|governorAction|gov_action` = ZERO. `GovernorsPage.tsx` is 34 lines, a pure `<table>` over `snapshot.states` (governor name/party/ideology/`skills.governing`), with ZERO `onClick`/`<button>`/`dispatch`/`mutate`. The industry-boost nudge (`phaseRunners.ts:1638-1653`, PR7 Lobby‚ÜíIndustry +1) fires on **faction-holds-a-lobby-card with a living member residing in the state** ‚Äî it reads `f.lobbyCards` + member residency, and **never reads `s.governorId`** (#20: the boost ignores the governor). A random-stat draftee generator (`:188-198`, `skills[k]=Math.floor(Math.random()*2)`, `skills[boost]=2+‚Ä¶`) is the closest existing analog to a "roll-random-expertise" Gov-Action ‚Äî but it uses `Math.random` (would need K0 seeding). **#20 is greenfield: no govAction state, no selection logic, no UI surface.**
> **(6) ‚úì CONFIRMED ‚Äî `electoralVotes` is FIXED for the run; no reapportionment (#34).** The ONLY `electoralVotes =` write in all of `src/` is `constitutionalConvention.ts:210` (`s.electoralVotes = Math.max(3, (s.ccDelegateSlots ?? 2) + 1)` ‚Äî the one-time colony‚Üírepublic transition inside `if (conv.ratified)`). NO census/population EV-mutation producer exists; `admitState` (`territories.ts:8-23`) copies the seed's EV verbatim. **#34 (census/EV reapportionment over time) is a genuine new schema+sim subsystem and is the precondition for the House lose-a-seat redistricting (#250/#191).**
> **(7) ‚úì CONFIRMED ‚Äî the #286 fall/scandal/illness event machinery + its RATE config exist; the gap is TUNING, not a missing wire.** Anytime-event categories live in `anytimeEvents.ts` (`illness-acute/chronic/epidemic`, `scandal-financial/sexual/verbal`, `:6-9`) + `anytimeNationalEvents.ts` (`civic-executive-scandal`); resolution + scandal scaling in `phaseRunners.ts:2503-2748` (`SCANDAL_CATS`, `scandalScaled` invariants, `scandalMult = tpl.scandalScaled ? cfg.scandalMagnitudeMult : 1`). The RATE knobs are `ANYTIME_EVENTS_RULES` (`types.ts:1073-1095`): `baseFireChance:0.05`, `nationalBaseFireChance:0.70`, and per-era `eraConfig.{fireMult,nationalFireMult,scandalMagnitudeMult}` (independence 0.8/0.9/0.5 ‚Üí modern 1.1/1.1/1.3). **‚öÝ There is NO human-vs-CPU asymmetry knob today ‚Äî the rates are global/per-era only. So #286's "possible human-vs-CPU asymmetry" is a NEW dimension, not a re-tune. This is a DATA-TUNING pass on the existing rate config, DISTINCT from the #130 natural-death mortality tables (`MORTALITY_RULES` `types.ts:485`).**

> **‚òÖ CODE-VERIFICATION RESULTS (I confirmed/corrected each pinned claim against HEAD):**
> **(1) ‚úì CONFIRMED ‚Äî `expansionStates.ts:173-183`: EVERY alt-state/territory seed is flat `electoralVotes:4, bias:0`.** `EXPANSION_STATES = DEFS.map(...)` hard-codes `electoralVotes: 4, bias: 0, industries:{agriculture:2,trade:1}, isSlaveState:false, admissionYear:0` for ALL ~150 `DEFS` rows ‚Äî no per-state census, no per-state lean. (Anchors #280/#281/#277.)
> **(2) ‚úì CONFIRMED + the b44 claim CORRECTED ‚Äî `constitutionalConvention.ts:210` sets EV UNIFORMLY and NEVER reads `slaveCompromise`.** The ONLY shipped EV rewrite is the colony‚Üírepublic transition inside `if (conv.ratified)`: `for (const s of snap.states) { s.isColony=false; s.electoralVotes = Math.max(3, (s.ccDelegateSlots ?? 2) + 1); }` (`:208-211`). `slaveCompromise` appears ONLY at `:34` (article option), `:111` (article-effect switch), `:140` (persist default `'three_fifths'`) ‚Äî **none touch `electoralVotes`.** So the b44 "slave-compromise rewrites EV" wording was IMPRECISE; the slave-compromise plank has no EV effect in shipped code. `:52-58` = `presidentialEligibility` article (natural_born / any_citizen). `:189` = `chance(0.5)` governor-approval proxy (`govApproves = gov ? gov.partyId==='RED' || chance(0.5) : stateLeans`); `:192` = `conv.ratified = approve >= 9` (the 9-governor ratification). All confirmed.
> **(3) ‚úì CONFIRMED ‚Äî `eraEvents1772.ts:216-310`: Carlisle ‚Üí `dominion_autonomy` scripted game-over IS shipped.** `carlisle_commission` (`:216-236`) carries `leadsTo: ['dominion_autonomy']`, response `b` = accept; `dominion_autonomy` (`:305-310`) `precondition: { any: [chose('carlisle_commission','b'), chose('conciliatory_resolution','b'), chose('colonial_war_weariness','b')] }`, effect `triggersGameEnd: true`. Terminal endings `lost_war` (`:296-301`) + `dominion_autonomy` both end the game. Confirmed shipped.
> **(4) ‚úì CONFIRMED + DH-83 RESOLVED as forum-memory ‚Äî `revolutionaryWar.ts:87`: senior general/admiral ARE excluded from casualties.** `const candidates = [...participants].filter((p) => p.id !== war.seniorGeneralId && p.id !== war.seniorAdmiralId)` (`:87`) ‚Äî casualty victims are drawn ONLY from `candidates`, so the senior general/admiral CANNOT die in battle in shipped code. The DH-83 "senior general died in battle" playtest note is therefore **forum-memory / a SIM/Sheets-run artifact, NOT shipped behavior** (same class as DH-81/DH-82's Sheets-vs-code split). ‚òÖ The REAL adjacent issue is unchanged: casualty victim selection at `:89` and `:97` uses **`Math.random`** ‚Äî a determinism leak (the SAME war-track leak flagged in batch-44's ¬ß9, distinct from debt #1's `calcStateVote`); route through seeded `src/rng.ts` (debt #3/K0) before any war-model reproducibility work.
> **(5) ‚úì CONFIRMED ‚Äî `types.ts:1324` `bias: number` is a STATIC scalar on `State`; `calcStateVote` reads it at `phaseRunners.ts:3709`.** `State.bias` (`:1324`) is a single number, NOT a time/decade-indexed series. `calcStateVote` (`:3709`) computes `score = 50 + baseLean*5 + partyPref*5 + enthusiasm*2 + pv*0.1 + factionBias + traitBonus + (Math.random()-0.5)*8`, where `baseLean` derives from the static `state.bias`. Confirms #247 (time-indexed bias = a schema change to the election hot path) AND re-confirms the TWO live leaks on this exact line: `pv*0.1` (debt #85 election-PV term) + `(Math.random()-0.5)*8` (debt #1 determinism).
> **(6) ‚úì CONFIRMED ‚Äî `pendingCourtCases` (`types.ts:1587`) is ONLY ever `[]`.** Both scenario seeds set `pendingCourtCases: []` (`scenario1772.ts:95`, `scenario1856.ts:175`); NO producer anywhere in `src/` ever pushes to it. The shipped SCOTUS is the `chance(0.5)` coin-flip (`phaseRunners.ts:3397-3415`), which never populates this field ‚Üí #270 (SC-case schema+generator) confirmed 0% on the producer side.
> **(7) ‚úì CONFIRMED ‚Äî `impeach` (case-insensitive) = 0 occurrences in `src/`.** No impeachment subsystem of any kind ‚Äî confirms #273 is 0% shipped (and remains DESIGNER-DEFERRED "3.0 last", per the b44 #273 ratification gate).
> **(8) ‚úì CONFIRMED ‚Äî `TRACK_THEMED_TRAITS.Backroom` (`types.ts:212`) = `['Manipulative', 'Kingmaker', 'Numberfudger']` (DH-25).** The Backroom career track's themed-trait pool is exactly those three; the themed-trait roll (`phaseRunners.ts:336-339`, `CAREER_ODDS.themedByThreshold`) draws from it. (DH-25 Tweed-yield: if the digest expected a Tweed-archetype to surface a DIFFERENT trait via the Backroom track, the shipped pool is this fixed triple ‚Äî reconcile the expected-trait against this list; no engine bug, a content/pool-coverage observation.)
> **(9) ‚úì CONFIRMED ‚Äî `State` has NO `population` field and NO territory/state flag (#281).** `State` (`types.ts:1318-1335`) carries `electoralVotes/bias/...` + the 1772 fields `isColony?/colonySize?/ccDelegateSlots?` ‚Äî but **no `population`, no `isTerritory`**. `admitState` (`territories.ts:8-23`) is a straight `{...seed, admissionYear, governorId:null, senators:[], representativeIds:[], isColony:false}` copy ‚Äî NO census/eligibility/EV computation. So #281 (statehood-eligibility) is a genuine schema change.
> **(10) ‚úì CONFIRMED ‚Äî #12 the wiring EXISTS; the gap is in the BILL DATA, not the resolution path.** Passed bills DO call `applyEffect(snap, bill.effects)` at BOTH floor sites (`phaseRunners.ts:3516` CC era, `:3570` federal era), and `applyEffect` (`:3209-3235`) correctly clamps each meter delta into `game.meters[k]` (`-5..+5`). The PROBLEM is that `BILL_TEMPLATES` (`:3420-3429`) ship tiny meter deltas ‚Äî every effect is `¬±0.5` to `¬±1` on the `-5..+5` scale (e.g. Internal Improvements `quality:0.5, revenue:-1`; Pacific Railroad `economic:1, revenue:-1`); marquee/era-defining bills barely move the meter. The only PV reward is command `+1` (`addCommandPoint`, `:3511/:3566`) gated on `billImprovesAnyMeter` (`:3196-3207`) ‚Üí ‚âà `+10` PV via `computePV` (`pv.ts:74`, `command*10`). **Net: "marquee bills move meters ~0, only ~50 PV" is a DATA-TUNING gap (BILL_TEMPLATES magnitudes + a marquee PV/prestige reward), NOT a missing wire.** This is why #151 is BUILDABLE NOW with no decision gate.

You MAY pull the reshuffle pass forward as soon as bill-scoring (#20) + #6 are done,

**PARKING LOT (author before build ‚Äî rules don't exist yet):**
- **DH-1** filibustered-MUST-pass remedy (forced compromise vs shutdown clock).
- **Divergence #10 / #84** contingent-election rules (top-2 vs top-3,
  outgoing-vs-incoming Congress, deadlock side-effects).
- **DH-12** white-peace rules (negotiated peace spec).
- **DH-13** faithless-elector trigger cap.
- **DH-14** era-aware bill ideology impact tables.
- **DH-15** small/large-state action-impact multiplier.
- **¬ß25.9** Iron-Fist office-keyed trait names (6 candidates listed, but the
  exact split + cascade rules need a design call).
- **DH-25 (batch 6) ‚Äî career-track bootstrap rule (BLOCKS modern scenario
  shipping).** 3-yr-stale design discussion has no canonical rule for which
  existing pols start on career tracks at a mid-game boot. Zagnut's "1996+,
  1/track" houserule is on the table; Rodja hand-populated by GM ad-hoc.
  Author before `scenario1948` or `scenario2012` ships (Phase-2 #30).
- **DH-33 (batch 7) ‚Äî impeachment ruleset broken/outdated.** The mini-flow runs
  but the canonical rules are flagged non-functional and improvised (corroborates
  `hd`'s "impeachment super outdated" across a 2nd campaign). Author the rewrite
  before building it into the legislative-depth epic (Phase-2 #29).
- **‚òÖ DH-41 (batch 8) ‚Äî the general SCOTUS-ruling ‚Üí downstream-statute cascade
  is UNBUILT and was explicitly DEFERRED by `vcczar`.** Distinct from the *built*
  strike-a-single-law and overturn-an-amendment-on-review (#100): does a ruling
  that **contradicts a law on the books** auto-void/neuter that law, or leave it
  operative (today's default)? The Prigg-v-Pennsylvania ‚Üí Fugitive-Slave-Act case
  was raised and deferred (`tea1772` POST 124-126). **Author the cascade policy
  before building it.** Lands in the SCOTUS docket epic (Phase-2 #25) once decided.
  Cross-refs ¬ß24.4's *Pollock*‚Üíincome-tax coupling (a designed gate already).
- **‚òÖ DH-49 (batch 9) ‚Äî a POPULATION MODEL + House cap (un-blocks the census +
  Wyoming Rule).** The per-decade census EV reallocation (level (b) of the era
  model, ¬ß28.9) and the Wyoming-Rule apportionment (#34/#55) are
  **un-implementable without a real per-state population model + a House-size cap**
  ‚Äî neither exists today (`State.electoralVotes` is a static seed; there is no
  population field). **Author the population model + House cap before build**, OR
  size it INTO the census/apportionment epic (#34/#55) as its first sub-task. This
  is the one batch-9 item that is genuinely *new infrastructure*, not a relabel of
  existing work.
- **‚òÖ DH-54 (batch 9) ‚Äî impeachment / VP-vacancy fill was NEVER in the rules doc.**
  `nuke` (¬ß28.5): no impeachment trigger (a Watergate-analog went undetected ‚Üí
  pure upside) and VP-vacancy fill was "made up as we go." **Corroborates DH-33
  (`rep1800`) + `hd`** across a third campaign. **Author the impeachment +
  succession ruleset before building the institutional layer** (#112 / #61 /
  Phase-1 #10b + Phase-2 #29). Pairs with #105 (stat-collapse‚Üíforced-resignation).
- **‚òÖ DH-34 (batch 7) ‚Äî static era-biases ‚Üí the Red-unwinnable "AMPU 2.0" hole
  (a ROADMAP DECISION, not a parking-lot author task).** `State.bias` is a
  static per-era table that does not react to policy (abolish slavery ‚Üí the South
  should swing Red, but doesn't), so the Federalists are acknowledged-unwinnable
  1800-1840 and players quit. GM + designer: dynamic/policy-reactive biases are
  "too complicated / not part of the AMPU vision‚Ä¶ maybe AMPU 2.0." **The planner
  must DECIDE: ship static biases (accept the imbalance ‚Äî the cheap path the
  forum chose) vs. invest in policy-reactive biases (a large new system).** My
  call: **ship static for now** (it is the forum's own stance and the imbalance
  is a known-accepted property), revisit only if balance becomes a release
  blocker. Pairs with #21/#34 (bias as a field) + DH-29's solo-balance theme.
- **‚òÖ DELEGATE-CLASS ASSIGNMENT FORK (batch 11, NEW ‚Äî a human DECISION-GATE before
  E10's delegate-apportionment sub-PR).** `arkzag` opened a new fork distinct from the
  resolved #51: **who sets convention delegate classes** ‚Äî **AI-auto allocation**
  (Zagnut's house rule + a published 5-category EV√ó1‚Ä¶√ó4 formula) vs **player-rigged**
  (Ted: insiders rigging the convention is *intended* design ‚Äî the host-Governor
  advantage). **Practice did not converge** (the 1840 convention reverted to human-set).
  **Human picks AI-allocator vs player-rigged ‚Üí THEN E10's delegate-apportionment +
  E24's primary delegate apportionment build it.** Does NOT block the rest of E10 (the
  ballot loop / momentum / VP-rubric / 8-stage general are confirmed-ready). The
  convention-host analog of the #52/#18 forks.
- **~~‚òÖ #18 ENTHUSIASM STATE-SCOPE~~ ‚Üí ‚òÖ‚òÖ RESOLVED OUT (batch 15, `terror2000`) ‚Äî no
  longer a decision-gate; PROMOTED to ready-to-build.** Batch 11 RESOLVED the
  enthusiasm-shift *algorithm* (the ¬ß29.10 4-step reshuffle, matches `drums`) and left
  ONE open question ‚Äî which states a card's enthusiasm applies to (every-state-unless-
  penalized [Ted] vs ideology-leaning-only [V]). **Batch 15 CLOSES it:** Ted (running
  the 2000-start) reversed his own batch-10 reading to V/vcczar's 2022 canonical 2-layer
  model (`terror2000#POST 913-926`): **(a) a universal per-ideology METER modifier**
  (flat, BOTH parties, EVERY state, primary+general ‚Äî "unless penalized" GONE) **+ (b)
  the per-PARTY enthusiasm box** (the #51 reshuffle output), composed additively then
  ¬±3-capped. The three batch-10/11 variants are retired. **So #18 is now ready-to-build,
  S‚ÄìM, binding in `calcStateVote` (`phaseRunners.ts:3709-3711`)** alongside the ¬±3 cap +
  the reshuffle pass; layer (a) is the only new piece (a per-ideology meter‚Üímodifier
  table). This DECISION-GATE is CLOSED ‚Äî recount the Decision-gated bucket (#18 OUT;
  with #124-percentages also resolving OUT via batch-15's live 3-state tuning, the
  bucket nets ‚àí2). See ¬ß9.1.11(C) + game-mechanics ¬ß29.3.

**The most important calls for the planner (batch-7 leads; batch-8 HARDENS #0;
batch-9 ADDS the two-level era refinement + the NEGATIVE-SCOPE Cold-War finding +
CPU-AI-is-load-bearing):**
0. **‚òÖ ERA-MODEL REFRAME ‚Äî `advanceEra` becomes CONDITION-DRIVEN; this is a
   re-spec of K3/K4, NOT a new keystone (divergence #18 / ¬ß27.1 / #92).
   ‚òÖ BATCH-8: MULTI-SAVE PROVEN ‚Äî the highest-confidence architectural finding
   in the KB. ‚òÖ‚òÖ BATCH-9: TWO-LEVEL + structured-era-event-data requirement.**
   Two+ independent saves emit the IDENTICAL era-band sequence at identical dates
   (`tea1772` 1772-start + `rep1800` 1800-start + **`nuke` 1948-start** ‚Äî three
   start years, one deterministic sequence ‚Äî game-mechanics ¬ß27.1/¬ß27.2). The bands
   are deterministic game-state content-gates, not GM flavor. **No structural
   change from batch 7.** Batch 9 ADDS: the model has **TWO LEVELS that must both be
   built ‚Äî (a) point-banked Historical Eras with RULE-DELTAS** (the Era-of-Terror
   cabinet rework proves bands carry rule changes, not just content) **AND (b) a
   separate per-decade census** doing bulk EV-reallocation + state-bias re-lean +
   content-rotation (distinct from #68's per-era bias swap). PLUS **era-event data
   needs STRUCTURED `evDelta`/census fields** (DH-48 ‚Äî the Neocon census events were
   LOST as free-text flavor) + per-era completeness validation. **"Neocons" is a
   faction-rebrand, NOT a band ‚Äî do not model it as one.** All inside K3/K4.
   Verified: phases gate by `year % 4`/`year % 2` (`phases.ts:49-59`, keep for
   cadence); the ONLY era advance is the hard-coded `currentEra = 'federalism'` at
   `constitutionalConvention.ts:198`; no year‚Üíera derivation exists. `advanceEra(snap)`
   watches an `era.advanceWhen` condition; content gates on `game.eraBand` + a new
   `territoryOwned` predicate, **not the calendar**. **RECONCILES #68 + ¬ß26 + ¬ß27.1
   into ONE era system.** Sub-bands are content-band markers, not new enum values
   (my call: markers first). **#102: the per-era banking is also the DUAL-scoring
   scoreboard (per-era + cumulative).** See ¬ß9.1.5 + the updated K3/K4 rows.
0b. **‚òÖ‚òÖ NEGATIVE SCOPE (batch 9) ‚Äî do NOT build a Cold-War subsystem; do build ONE
   generic war engine + diplomacy (¬ß9.1.8 / ¬ß28.2).** Verified: only
   `src/engine/revolutionaryWar.ts` exists ‚Äî no generic and no Cold-War engine. The
   "Nuclear Age" has NO nuclear/MAD/NATO/space-race/military-branch mechanics in the
   design; the Cold War is the **generic war engine RELABELED + diplomacy + content.**
   The real items: (i) **ONE generic war engine** (Phase-1 #3) that **RESOLVES**
   (DH-47: wars never end today ‚Äî Korea ran ~2 decades) and ideally gets army/navy/air
   **branches**; (ii) the **diplomacy subsystem** (8 relation meters + ambassador
   actions; DH-46 add downward pressure; DH-45 fix the USSR-collapse trigger chain).
   Cold-War content = data on top of those. **This prevents the biggest scope-creep
   risk in the modern era.**
0c. **‚òÖ CPU-faction AI is LOAD-BEARING (batch 9, #114 / ¬ß28.12).** The designer's
   strongest corpus statement: **the APP is built for 1-human-vs-9-CPU** (multiplayer
   "goes off the rails"; the points system is for CPUs, not humans). The forum game
   is team-MP with CPU backfill ‚Äî **the app is a solo adaptation of a multiplayer
   game**, so the entire multiplayer apparatus must be CPU-AI-driven. **No re-sequence
   (K5 stays after K0+K2), but it ELEVATES K5 + the handler suite (E9) to a
   first-class Phase-1 system** ‚Äî the difference between a playable solo game and not.
   See ¬ß9.1.3. **Senate pass-threshold RESOLVED IN CODE = simple majority**
   (`phaseRunners.ts:3562`); the design question (60%/cloture?) stays open for the
   human (debt #27).
1. **‚òÖ‚òÖ RECONSTRUCTION SOLO-BLOCKER is a hard BUILD REQUIREMENT on E3b
   (DH-29 / ¬ß23.4 / #57).** GM-verified (`rep1800` POST 9170): the historical
   Strict/Ironclad readmission plan can NEVER pass with CPU factions ‚Üí **solo
   Reconstruction is UNRESOLVABLE**, which dead-ends the 1856-arc as a winnable
   solo scenario (and AMPU is single-player). **E3b's definition-of-done must
   include a CPU-passable readmission path** ‚Äî my rec: (1) a CPU default-vote
   bias for the flagged historical plan (K5 handler #2) **+** (3) an
   era-boundary auto-resolution backstop (K3). The readmission half of E3b
   should land AFTER K5 handler #2 or carry the era-boundary auto-resolution as
   its self-contained fallback. **Ties E3b to the CPU handler suite (K5 / E9).**
   See ¬ß9.1.6.
2. **‚òÖ IDEOLOGY-AS-CIRCLE is FOUNDATIONAL (a central-helper refactor) behind an
   era-gated flag (divergence #19 / ¬ß27.7 / #99).** Verified: `IDEOLOGY_ORDER`
   (`types.ts:14`) is LINEAR and distance is open-coded at 10+ sites
   (`factionCenter` `phaseRunners.ts:715`, `stepToward` `:740`, conversion
   adjacency `:993-1003`, sponsor `:3548`, `firstContinentalCongress.ts:120`,
   + 3 UI pages); **no central helper exists**. Add `ideologyDistance(a,b,
   circular)`, migrate the sites (behavior-preserving while the flag is off),
   gate the wrap on `GameState.ideologyIsCircular?`. **Place the helper +
   migration EARLY-ish (Phase-1 #5b)** ‚Äî cheap + additive while the flag is off,
   and every later ideology consumer calls it from day one. Not a keystone; M
   total (XS-S helper + migration; M for the flag + conversion-adjacency). ¬ß9.1.7.
3. **New early-republic subsystems (Phase-1 #4/#4b) + two grounding
   corrections.** 12A legislature-elector toggle (#20/#93 ‚Äî a NEW
   legislature-majority resolution branch; `senatePre17` does NOT model this);
   slavery-flag + Cohens (#94 ‚Äî **`State.isSlaveState` ALREADY EXISTS at
   `types.ts:1329`**, shrinking the refactor to the abolition-toggle + the
   Cohens rule-modifier); Second Bank recharter clock + Bank War exec-action
   (#95); statehood-by-bill ORGANIZE‚ÜíADMIT two-step + unorganized-territory
   draft gate (#95, the SAME `territoryOwned` predicate as the era reframe).
4. **Era-events-predating-start MERGED into BUG-1 + event-scheduler-min-floor
   (DH-30) added as a quick-win.** `rep1800` LIVE-confirms BUG-1 (the
   LA-Purchase-dropped-at-1800-start episode is a 2nd instance) ‚Äî **same bug,
   merged**. DH-30 (min = 20% of the era's max, round down; spill to the 5
   generic anytime events) is its companion on the same scheduling surface;
   both land in the era-event work / before `scenario1788`.

**Carried from batch 6 (still leads):**
1. **APOCALYPSE meter-driven endgame lands in Phase 1, sized M.** Verified:
   the only endgame model shipped is event-driven (`EraEvent.triggersGameEnd`
   ‚Üí `phaseRunners.ts:2871` ‚Üí `game.gameEnded`); no meter-watcher, no
   countdown anywhere. The model is meter-agnostic (the Populism Planet
   Health 10-yr clock is one configured row); the `planet` meter ships and
   ticks every era; the sink (`game.gameEnded`) is shared with the event-
   driven path. **Folds in with the meter-model generalization (Phase-1 #6)**
   ‚Äî same tick site (`runPhase_2_5_1_Lingering`), same termination sink, M
   sizing. Architecturally significant (NEW endgame surface) but cheap to
   ship because the meter and the sink both exist. See ¬ß9.3 #14 + ¬ß9.1.4 +
   ¬ß9.6 Phase-1 #6.
2. **K4 introduces the `BootSheet` schema as the cross-cutting build
   constraint.** Three documented mid-government boots (1788 designed / 1856
   shipped / 2012 designed in `pop`) share ONE shape ‚Äî pre-built faction
   roster (5 Blue + 5 Red) + per-faction archetype politicians + era-tuned
   ideology/interest/lobby decks + sitting government keyed to start year +
   **state roster keyed to `{era, startYear}`, NOT era alone** (divergence
   #17 ‚Äî same `modern` enum has 50+DC fresh-boot AND 53-state Wyoming-Rule
   continuation) + EXPLICITLY EMPTY at boot (no faction leaders, no career-
   track pols, no inherited PV/legacy/Kingmaker pairs). **Build the schema
   ONCE in K4**, instantiate per era. **Era identity is data configuration,
   not code paths.** Plus a **Senate-class verifier (DH-24)** + a
   **`TRAIT_CONFLICTS` validator (DH-27)** at the boot pipeline. See K4 +
   ¬ß9.3 #17.
3. **K2 + E16 + E17 refactors ‚Äî divergences #15, #16, and the conditional-
   vote-rules primitive (`pop` POST 1111) fold cleanly:**
   - **K2 adds `requires?: AmendmentPredicate` from day one** (divergence
     #16): one field on `GameAction<Ctx>` + one filter step in the picker
     reading `game.amendments.passed`. Cheap if early, expensive if retrofit
     across 6 libraries. Same `requires:` mechanism gates bill catalog +
     gov action rows; predicate is at the registry-row level.
   - **E16 cabinet refactor gains the dynamic seat list** (divergence #15):
     verified `cabinetSeatsForYear` (`types.ts:1196`) is pure derived with
     NO mutable state; refactor to BOOT SEED only + `GameState.cabinetSeats:
     SeatSpec[]` + `Legislation.createsCabinetSeat?`. Same code area as the
     retention refactor ‚Äî marginal additional cost.
   - **E17 Iron-Fist split + CPU handler #2 consume the conditional-vote-
     rules primitive** (`Faction.factionLeader.compelledVoteRule?: Predicate
     ‚Üí Vote`): handler #2 (legislation) consults it BEFORE the ¬ß25.6
     heuristic; handler #4 (cabinet) consumes it for auto-AYE-own-picks AND
     SCOTUS within-1-step auto-AYE (¬ß26.6.1). Promotes a ¬ß25.9 sub-effect to
     a first-class CPU primitive.
4. **K5 (`CpuController` scaffold) is a NEW keystone ‚Äî late-keystone, after K0
   + K2, parallel with K3/K4 + federalism** *(carried from batch 5)*. The
   shipped engine has **no agent-decision pass at all** (3 thin stubs); the
   ¬ß25 spec'd 15 subsystems have nowhere to live without it. K5 itself is
   ~120 lines (orchestrator + handler interface + tie-breaks + 2 `repair()`
   backfills + 1 determinism test). It **unlocks ~15 follow-on handler PRs**
   that parallelize across contributors. **K5 is a force-multiplier, not a
   scenario gate** ‚Äî federalism + the 1856-arc epic can ship with stubbed
   handlers and upgrade together. See ¬ß6.6.1 + ¬ß9.1.3.
5. **Cabinet-confirmation system (DH-23) is XS-S, NOT the M many digests
   imply** *(carried from batch 5)* ‚Äî because **the broken system isn't
   built yet**. `runPhase_2_3_1_Cabinet` (`phaseRunners.ts:2158-2223`) is a
   one-step scored pick with no Senate vote. So the fix is "build the
   confirmation step in the right shape from day one" (default-AYE baseline
   + Iron-Fist Maj-Leader auto-AYE-own-picks via the conditional-vote-rules
   primitive + lobby-maximizer Admin-weighting + the **batch-6 SCOTUS
   within-1-step auto-AYE** declarative rule for SCOTUS confirmations),
   which is **CPU handler #4** (¬ß6.6.1). Lands as a sibling of the cabinet-
   retention refactor (Phase-1 #16, which now ALSO carries the divergence
   #15 dynamic seat list).
6. **What's READY now vs. what still needs design.** **READY (no design
   task remaining):** the 13 ¬ß25 subsections #25.1-#25.8 + #25.10-#25.14
   ‚Äî all 15 CPU handlers can be authored against ¬ß25 verbatim. Plus #79
   25/10/5 Justice drift, #80 ¬±3 swing cap, #82 veto override 2/3, #83
   midterm meter+enthusiasm, #85 5%/half-term retire-death. **Batch 6
   adds READY:** #86 scenario-boot schema (the per-era boot sheets are
   data), #87 era double-points (a per-era table), #88 APOCALYPSE clock
   (the 10-yr clock is parameterized), #89 dynamic seat list (refactor of
   shipped code), #91 amendment-toggled VP actions (one field on K2).
   **Batch 7 adds READY:** #92 era-content-band model + the condition-driven
   `advanceEra` boundary formula (¬ß27.1/¬ß27.2 spec the explicit steps); #93
   12A legislature-elector toggle (the state machine is specced); #94
   slavery-flag + Cohens (the rule is specced; `State.isSlaveState` exists);
   #95 statehood-by-bill organize‚Üíadmit + Second Bank recharter clock (specced);
   #99 ideology-as-circle (the metric is `min(|a‚àíb|,7‚àí|a‚àíb|)`). **Batch 8 adds
   to READY:** #100 SCOTUS-overturns-a-ratified-amendment + threshold-amendable
   (the Gov-requested judicial-review/repeal loop + the mutable-threshold field
   are specced, ¬ß21.3); #102 dual era-scoring (per-era + cumulative ‚Äî the K3
   banking table IS the scoreboard, ¬ß27.2); #105 stat-collapse‚Üíforced-resignation
   (a one-rule floor); #104 lone-ideology convention exploit (a convention-CPU
   guard, handler 9e). **NEEDS DESIGN
   (parking lot):** divergence #10 / #84 contingent-election rules (5 rulesets
   invented mid-thread, no canonical answer); ¬ß25.9 Iron-Fist split (the 6 child
   traits' exact names + cascade rules); DH-1 filibustered-MUST-pass; DH-12
   white-peace; DH-13 faithless-elector trigger; DH-14 era-aware bill impacts;
   DH-15 small-state multiplier; **DH-25 career-track bootstrap (BLOCKER on
   modern scenario shipping)**; **DH-33 (batch 7) impeachment rewrite**; **‚òÖ DH-41
   (batch 8) the general SCOTUS-ruling ‚Üí downstream-statute cascade (UNBUILT,
   `vcczar`-deferred ‚Äî does a contradicting ruling void the law on the books?
   author the policy; the headline batch-8 author-before-build item)**; **DH-44
   (batch 8) the post-12A legislature-state vote count (Kingmaker votes vs
   Gov+Senators+focus-Reps headcount ‚Äî decide when building the 12A toggle #93)**;
   **the
   early-republic sub-band open Q (enum values vs content-band markers ‚Äî my
   call: markers first)**. **ROADMAP DECISION (not a parking-lot author task):
   DH-34 (batch 7) static-vs-policy-reactive era-biases ‚Äî the Red-unwinnable
   "AMPU 2.0" hole; my call: ship static.** These are PM/design tasks, not
   build tasks.
7. **(carried, batch 4) BUG-0 (relocation cap `5`‚Üí`4`) is the cheapest win
   in the whole roadmap ‚Äî do it first.** Verified:
   `RELOCATION_ATTEMPTS_PER_TURN = 5` at `types.ts:247`; one-line const
   edit, no migration. Plus: per-era point banking folds into K3; Civil-War
   / Reconstruction epic lands at Phase-1 #3b; #54 investigation committees
   is READY; DH-8 must-own for the convention epic (now CPU handler #5).
8. **(carried) K2 remains the second-most-important keystone after K0**
   (~6√ó leverage across 6 action libraries; **batch 6 adds the
   `requires?: AmendmentPredicate` field for amendment-gating**) and is a
   **hard prerequisite for K5** ‚Äî most CPU handlers pick from a registry.
   `scenario1788` before a fully-general `advanceEra` (¬ß9.1.1); federalism

---

## 10. Build / lint / test commands

- Dev server: `npm run dev`
- Production build (typecheck + build ‚Äî run before calling code done): `npm run build` (`tsc -b && vite build`)
- Typecheck only: `npm run lint` (`tsc -b --noEmit`)
- Preview built app: `npm run preview`
- Engine smoke tests: the `scripts/playtest*.ts` / `scripts/smokeTest*.mjs`
  harnesses run engine helpers headlessly (no React) ‚Äî the model for
  feature-level verification (the qa-tester role writes these).
- Regenerate the draft dataset: see ¬ß7.

**Definition of done** (from CLAUDE.md): `npm run build` passes **and** the change
is playtested in `npm run dev` ‚Äî build/typecheck prove correctness, not that the
game is fun or balanced (that judgment is human). Pushing/PRs are human-approved
checkpoints.
